purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call aims to harness the potential of disruptive technologies to deliver novel solutions to global challenges and accelerate progress toward achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). More research is needed on how disruptive technologies can have transformative, economic, or societal impacts, improving people's lives and the planet through contextually appropriate interventions. Please note! NWO funding can only be requested for projects addressing the following SDGs: Zero Hunger (SDG 2), Clean Water and Sanitation (SDG 6), Affordable and Clean Energy (SDG 7), Sustainable Cities and Communities (SDG 11).

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may submit an application if all applicants (= co-PI(s) and co-applicant(s)) have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations: Universities and universities of applied sciences (UAS) as referred to in Article 1.8 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act and universities listed in the Policy Rules for Universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; University medical centres by which is meant academic hospitals as referred to in Article 1.13 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act; Institutes affiliated to the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO; Netherlands Cancer Institute; The Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; Naturalis Biodiversity Center; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center; National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM); TO2-institutions (Deltares, MARIN, NLR, TNO,WUR); IHE Delft Institute for Water Education. Lectors employed at a university of applied sciences and researchers employed at a TO2-institution may also submit as a co-PI provided that they have at least a salaried position for the duration of the project. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment or the exception for lectors and TO2 institutes) may not submit a proposal. It could be the case that the applicant’s tenure track agreement ends before the intended completion date of the project for which funding is applied for, or that before that date, the applicant’s tenured contract ends due to the applicant reaching retirement age. In that case, the applicant needs to include a statement from their employer in which the research organisation concerned guarantees that the project and all project members for whom funding has been requested will receive adequate supervision for the full duration of the project. Applicants with a part-time contract should guarantee adequate supervision of the project and all project members for whom funding is requested. An application for funding from NWO may consider the following roles: National co-PI (mandatory): the applicant who submits the application to NWO and is the foreseen national project leader, in case a project is granted. National co-applicant (optional): national applicants in addition to the national co-PI with an active role and responsibility in realising the project and requesting funding from NWO. The national co-PI or co-applicant from a Dutch organisation may only request NWO funding for one project (part of a transnational consortium) in this call in which he or she is involved. See the full Call Documents on the NFRF website for more details and an overview of participating countries and the transnational eligibility requirements. These documents are available in English only. Please note that each project team must be eligible to receive funds from at least three Funding Organizations. To fulfill this requirement, the project team must include a minimum of three Principal Investigators, each eligible to receive funding from a different participating funding organisation. At least one of the three Principal Investigators must be eligible to apply to the NFRF program .

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The total NWO budget for this call is €3,800,000. A maximum of €760,000 can be awarded per project. The NWO budget modules (including the maximum amount) available for this Call for proposals are listed below. Apply only for funding that is vital to realize the project. A more detailed explanation of the budget modules and the financial details form can be found in the document: “Specification of eligible costs”. Money follows cooperation The budget module Money follows cooperation cannot be applied for in this call. Personnel Funding may be requested for salary costs of personnel contributing to the project. The amount depends on the type of appointment and the organisation where the personnel is employed. Personnel at a university in the Kingdom of the Netherlands, umc or a research organisation For personnel working at a university in the Kingdom of the Netherlands, university medical centre (umc) or another research organisation, as referred to in Article 1.1, first paragraph, subparagraphs c to h of the NWO Grant Rules salary costs can be claimed for the following positions: postdoc - at least 12 full-time months and at most 36 full-time months; Engineering Doctorate (Eng): Funding for the Engineering Doctorate (EngD) position can only be applied for if funding for a PhD student or postdoc is also applied for; non-scientific staff at (NSS) universities; research leave – for up to 5% of the grant amount. The rates are determined using the salary tables of UNL and NFU. Personnel of universities of applied sciences, TO2 institutes, educational institutions and other organisations It is possible to claim salary costs of personnel of universities of applied sciences, TO2 institutes, educational institutions and other organisations. The rates are determined using the Government Tariff Manual (HOT), Table 2 average total salary cost per salary scale, column 'Hourly rate productive hours, excluding VAT'. The salary scale of the requested position determines the rate from the HOT table. Students It is possible to engage students in the project if they are studying at a research organisation. You can enter the costs of these students as material costs within the project. There is no maximum number of students who can participate in the project. Funding may not be requested for PhD students. If students contribute as a secondary job alongside their study as student assistants, the rate according to HOT table 2, under 2.2 ‘average total salary cost per salary scale’, column 'Hourly rate productive hours, excluding VAT', scale 1 applies. Material Funding may be requested for all project-specific material costs. These costs are subject to a maximum of 30% of the grant amount allocated for personnel costs. Knowledge utilization Funding can be requested for activities that promote the use of knowledge from the research , in order to increase the societal impact of the research. It is mandatory to include an amount for knowledge utilisation. These costs are at least 5% and maximum 20% of the grant amount. Project management Funding can be requested for project management that is at most 5% of the total budget requested from NWO. This budget can only be used for activities that solely support the project for which the grant is requested. The applicant must satisfactorily justify this budget. At the Full proposal phase, it is required to submit a financial detail form separately to NWO. This form will be available via the NWO website. Do not hesitate to contact the national contact person in case of questions.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: 3 March 2026 (Canadian Time, 8:00 p.m. (eastern)): Deadline for submission of the required Notice of Intent to apply (NOI) in the portal of the NFRF.  Please check the website of the NFRF for exact details and ensure that you comply with all additional requirements in the annexes of the funders you are requesting funds from. 4 March 2026 (CET, 14:00): Deadline for the NOI additional information needed for NWO in ISAAC.  (On the right-hand side of this website it is stated “pre-proposal”; this is the NOI-additional information deadline). This concerns some information regarding the project (same as in the NFRF convergence portal) as well as a signed authorized letter to confirm the positions of the Dutch co-PI and Dutch co-applicants. Important! Due to maintenance, ISAAC will not be available on 3 March between 5:30 pm and 11:59 pm (CET). We apologize for the inconvenience. Applicants (= co-PI(s) and co-applicant(s)) requesting funding from NWO are required to submit an authorised mandatory signed letter from their organisation in which their position, tenured/tenure track or salaried position for the duration of the project is confirmed. Please check ISAAC for exact details. 9 June 2026 (Canadian Time, 8:00 p.m. (eastern)): Deadline for submission of the required Letter of Intent to apply (LOI) in the portal of the NFRF. Please check the website of the NFRF for exact details. 3 November 2026 (Canadian Time, 8:00 p.m. (eastern)): Deadline for submission of the full proposals in the portal of the NFRF. Please check the website of the NFRF for exact details. 4 November 2026 (CET, 14:00): Deadline for the full proposal additional information needed for NWO in ISAAC. This concerns some information regarding the project as well as a NWO budget form and a signed statement on the knowledge security policy. Applicants from Dutch organisations are required to submit a mandatory NWO budget form in ISAAC in the Full proposal stage. In addition, the co-PI must declare by means of a signed statement that the proposal has been drawn up in accordance with the knowledge security policy of the research organisation and that the officer responsible for knowledge security within the research organisation has been consulted for this purpose. This statement must be submitted at the full proposal stage in ISAAC.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria There are seven selection criteria for this, namely Fit-to-program, Interdisciplinarity, Equity, diversity and inclusion in research practice, Strength of the Project Consortium, High risk – novelty of approach/scientific rigour, High reward – anticipated societal impact, Feasibility – capacity to execute the project. The criteria for the LOI stage are: fit to program (pass/fail), interdisciplinarity (pass/fail), strength of project consortium (30%), high risk (25%), high reward (25%), feasibility (20%). The criteria for the full proposals are: Fit-to-program (pass/fail), EDI in research practice(pass/fail), Interdisciplinarity (pass/fail), Strength of project consortium (20%), High risk (25%), High reward (25%), Feasibility (30%). Procedure To apply to this call, teams must submit a notice of intent to apply (NOI) by March 3, 2026. This is a mandatory step in the application process. The NOI is used for administrative purposes and is not assessed. After the NOI deadline has passed, the research team will immediately be able to work on their Letter of Intent to apply (LOI). The team must submit the Letter of Intent to apply (LOI) by June 9, 2026. Submitted LOIs will be evaluated, for the purpose of identifying selected teams to be invited to the full application stage.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: See the full Call Documents on the NFRF website for more details and an overview of participating countries and the transnational eligibility requirements. These documents are available in English only. Please note that each project team must be eligible to receive funds from at least three Funding Organizations. To fulfill this requirement, the project team must include a minimum of three Principal Investigators, each eligible to receive funding from a different participating funding organisation. At least one of the three Principal Investigators must be eligible to apply to the NFRF program . Please be aware! Applications submitted to NFRF are subject to Canada’s Policy on Sensitive Technology Research and Affiliations of Concern. Dutch applicants should familiarize themselves with the list of universities, research institutes or laboratories connected to military, national defence, or state security entities that could pose a risk to Canada’s national security, to avoid possible research security issues.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The International Joint Initiative for Research Harnessing Disruptive Technologies to Address Global Challenges call will support research projects focused on harnessing disruptive technologies to address at least one of the Sustainable Development Goals or another recognized global challenge. Projects may be focused on developing or implementing a disruptive technology, or research on the impact of a disruptive technology on society and/or economies. NWO funding can be requested for projects addressing the following SDGs: Zero Hunger (SDG 2), Clean Water and Sanitation (SDG 6), Affordable and Clean Energy (SDG 7), Sustainable Cities and Communities (SDG 11). Projects must be interdisciplinary, integrating information, data, techniques, tools, perspectives, concepts or theories from two or more disciplines or bodies of specialized knowledge. Projects must involve disciplines from at least two of the following three areas: natural sciences & engineering; social sciences and humanities; and health and life sciences. Projects are expected to include appropriate stakeholders and end-users in the co-development and co-leadership of the project. Engagement with other sectors, as appropriate, will help identify barriers to implementation and develop strategies to overcome them. A trans-sectoral approach, involving the academic, research, economic (businesses), societal (governmental and non-governmental organizations), and/or community sectors, as appropriate, ensures relevant outcomes and positions research for uptake and use, which will maximize impact in the long-term. Non-academic stakeholders may be included as team members or collaborators. For NWO funded research the NWO Grant Rules apply. In accordance with the NWO Grant Rules , the project that NWO funds must be carried out in accordance with the nationally and internationally accepted standards for scientific conduct as stated in the Netherlands Code of Conduct for Research Integrity (2018) and the Global Code of Conduct for Equitable Research Partnerships . In case research is conducted in fragile states, it is advised to consult the Security Guidelines for field research in complex, remote and hazardous places . By submitting the proposal, the applicant commits to these codes. In the case of a (possible) violation of these standards during a project funded by NWO, the applicant should immediately inform NWO of this and should submit all relevant documents to NWO. More information about the code of conduct and the policy regarding research integrity can be found on the website: Scientific integrity | NWO Applicants are required to submit a mandatory NWO budget form in the Full Proposal stage. For full details of the general NWO funding process, please refer to the NWO website . Information about lodging an objection at NWO is available on our website . NWO strives to achieve an inclusive culture in which there is no place for conscious or unconscious barriers due to cultural, ethnic or religious background, gender, sexual orientation, health or age ( www.nwo.nl/en/diversity-and-inclusion ). Compliance with the National Knowledge Security Guidelines In the National Knowledge Security Guidelines, the Dutch knowledge sector (including NWO) and various government departments have laid down guidelines for those within research organisations who are involved in international collaboration and who need to weigh up the opportunities and (security) risks involved. Self-regulation by the knowledge sector is central to the approach to knowledge security within the Netherlands. NWO expects applicants to comply with the knowledge security policy of their research organisation. If NWO receives indications that a proposal or an allocated project entails knowledge security risks, NWO may request the applicant or project leader to provide insight into the risk mitigation measures. In addition, NWO may decide to include further conditions in the award letter to protect knowledge security. The National Knowledge Security Guidelines can be found on the website of the Dutch government: Home | National Contact Point for Knowledge Security (loketkennisveiligheid.nl) . The co-PI must declare by means of a signed statement that the proposal has been drawn up in accordance with the knowledge security policy of the research organisation and that the officer responsible for knowledge security within the research organisation has been consulted for this purpose. This statement must be enclosed with the complete proposal. The NWO data management protocol applies to projects funded by NWO. NWO processes data from applicants received in the context of this Call in accordance with the NWO Privacy Statement, Privacy Statement | NWO . The system used by the Canadian New Frontiers in Research Fund contains optional questions regarding race and ethnicity. These questions are part of the standard questions asked in Canada and cannot be removed. We ask all applicants applying funding from NWO not to fill in these questions, since according to our legal regulations we are not allowed to ask these questions. Not filling in these questions will have no affect on the assessment of your proposal. Furthermore, submission of financial and substantive reports at national level is required in accordance with the rules of NWO.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Applicants (co-PI(s) and co-applicants) applying for funding from NWO must submit a mandatory, signed letter from their organization confirming their position, whether it is a permanent position, an appointment with the prospect of a permanent position, or a paid position, for the duration of the project. Applicants from Dutch organizations must submit a mandatory NWO budget form in ISAAC during the full proposal phase.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Open Mind call aims to support out-of-the-box research ideas that offer sustainable solutions to global societal challenges through the power of technology. Whether you are pioneering new ways to transform education, build disaster-proof communication systems, empower people with disabilities, or advance environmental sustainability - we want to hear from you! Open Mind focuses on original, creative research ideas with meaningful and lasting impact. We’re looking for high-risk high-gain ideas with the potential to be groundbreaking, either within your field or beyond. Your proposal should be bold, and challenge conventional approaches. The Open Mind program supports one-year projects aimed at bringing these creative ideas to life, with the goal of creating meaningful and lasting impact.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers (including scientific personnel like postdoc and PhD with a master’s degree) from the following research institutes can apply: Universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands, University medical centres, Institutes affiliated to the Academy (Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (Koninklijke Nederlandse Akademie van Wetenschappen (KNAW) or NWO, the Netherlands Cancer Institute, the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics, Nijmegen, the Naturalis Biodiversity Centre, the Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL), the Princess Maxima Centre.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: NWO domain Applied and Engineering Sciences has a total budget of 1.000,000 euro available. A maximum of 10 Open Mind applications will receive funding, with a maximum of 100,000 euro (inclusive of Dutch VAT) each for the period of maximum one year. The applicant and co-applicant can apply for funding for personnel and material.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: An (online) information session is scheduled on January 22 2026 14:00 CET. The deadline for submitting proposals is March 10 2026, before 14:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The research proposal should be in line with the aim of Open Mind and contribute to solutions for global societal challenges. The applications submitted will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: 1. Boldness (40%): Novelty and Innovation, Creative Vision, High-Risk High-Gain. 2. Quality (30%): Clarity, Methodological Soundness, Realistic Risk. 3.  Impact (30%): Relevance and Urgency, Gap in Existing Solutions, Meaningful Impact. Procedure NWO assigns an independent multidisciplinary Assessment Committee consisting of representatives from science and society, featuring a mix of creative minds. A maximum of 20 proposals will be selected for a pitch-interview session to be evaluated by the Assessment Committee during the annual NWO innovation festival TEKNOWLOGY on 18th June 2026, which requires applicants to be physically present. A maximum of ten Open Mind 2026 winners will be awarded funding during the closing session of TEKNOWLOGY. Applicants who receive funding are requested to present the obtained results at the following annual TEKNOWLOGY in 2027.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applications must be submitted through the Isaac application and reporting system. If you do not have an Isaac account yet, then please create one at least one workday in advance. More information about writing a proposal and which forms you need to use can be found in Isaac. Please download and use the forms in Isaac. Below you will find examples of the required forms.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In order to be able to submit an application: The application form must be completed. A sketch should be provided regarding the research proposal. A link to the video should be given in the application form. The budget form must be completed. In case of a temporary appointment, a Statement of appointment must be provided. If the above is complete and you want to submit your application, please save the application form as a PDF file and upload it together with the completed Financial planning (as Excel file) in ISAAC

title: BrainHealth Call 2026-1 - Biological, social and environmental factors in brain health
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research
characteristics.Themes: Health Preventie Health Care Innovation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Brain disorders are a leading cause of disability and mortality globally, placing a great burden on people living with brain disorders, their families, social relations, professional and informal carers, as well as healthcare systems and national economies as a whole. It is thus crucial to identify the factors that influence brain health throughout life, from prenatal stages to advanced age, as well as methods to monitor them, influence them, or both. Brain health is dynamic and shaped by biological, social and environmental factors at every stage of life. Advancing the scientific knowledge about these factors may enable positive changes in brain health trajectories and lead to opportunities for prevention and recovery along the life-course. Applications for this call, should address how biological, social and environmental factors affect the trajectory of neurological, mental and sensory disorders across the lifespan.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Each proposal must include minimal three(in their country or region) admissible legal entities from at least three different countries. Maximum three consortium partners can be from the same country. Maximum total number of partners in a consortium is six, excluding partners participating with own expenses. Researchers from Dutch universities, universities of applied sciences and other knowledge institutions can submit applications to NWO. See the NWO Annex and the call for proposals for further information. A grant application (i.e. the Dutch part of a consortium) has a single main applicant (i.e. Dutch partner or coordinator). National co-applicants may be part of the application. An applicant can only apply for NWO funding for one project (as part of a European consortium).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: NWO and the Brain Foundation Netherlands, will provide a total of 2 million euro in funding for this call. A maximum of 400,000 euro can be applied for at NWO for a project proposal in this call. The maximum project duration is 3 years. Available NWO budget modules are: Personnel (Postdoc, Non-Scientific Personnel, Research leave), Material costs, Knowledge utilisation and Project management.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline pre-proposal (obligatory): 10 March 2026, 14:00 CET . Deadline for full proposals: 30 June 2026, 14:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications are assessed on the following criteria: Excellence Impact Quality and efficiency of the implementation A full description of the assessment criteria can be found in the Call for proposals. Procedure Eligible pre-proposals will be reviewed using the evaluation criteria via a written peer review process. Based on that, a ranking list will be established according to which the Call Steering Committee will decide on full-proposal invitations. An international interdisciplinary Peer Review Panel will evaluate the eligible full proposals based on the above-mentioned evaluation criteria and establish a ranking list. In this evaluation, the Peer Review Panel will take into account the assessments of patient reviewers, as well as recommendations provided by the ethics committee. Based on the advice of the panel and the available budget, a financing decision is taken by the participating national and regional financers. For NWO, the Executive Board subsequently decides on the allocation of Dutch projects.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: https://www.brainhealth-partnership.eu/

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The application process will be in two stages: pre-proposals and full-proposals. At both stages, one joint proposal document shall be prepared by the consortium (in English) and submitted by the coordinator to the transnational online application portal, hosted by the EP BrainHealth Joint Call Secretariat. The individual research partners in a consortium will be funded by the respective national/regional funding organisation(s). Eligibility criteria are the matter of individual partner funding organisations and additional national/regional regulations, requirements and processes may apply. The national annex of NWO can be found on this page (Tab 2. Prepare).

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Proposal form The proposal (prep-proposal and full proposal) is submitted through the online submission portal of the partnership.  The proposal does not need to be sent to NWO separately. Budget form It is recommended to use the NWO budget template in the pre-proposal stage to confirm eligibility of budget items. For full proposals, it is mandatory to submit the NWO budget form on a national level to NWO before the deadline for full proposals (share per e-mail: brainhealt@nwo.nl ).

title: GroenvermogenNL: Hydrogen & Hydrogen Carriers: Import, Conversion, Transport & Storage
programme: Transport and Storage of Hydrogen GroenvermogenNL NGF - GroenvermogenNL
target_groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: Transport and Storage of Hydrogen GroenvermogenNL NGF - GroenvermogenNL

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this Call for Proposals is to implement the second funding phase of GroenvermogenNL Work Package 2: Hydrogen & Hydrogen Carriers: Import, Conversion, Transport & Storage. This Call for Proposals focuses on breakthroughs and innovations with technical and economic potential for large-scale import, conversion, transport, and storage of hydrogen and hydrogen carriers. One consortium will be established through workshops. Research areas are: 1.            Liquid Hydrogen (LH2) import, transport & storage, 2.            Hydrogen carrier import, conversion, transport & storage, 3.            Development and optimization of port infrastructure, supply chains, and conversion hubs.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The proposal can be submitted by a consortium existing of: Researchers from universities, universities of applied science, TO2 institutions and other research organisations. Employees of businesses and other public and private organisations provided that: They have a tenured position for at least 0.6 fte; Their organisation has demonstrable Research & Development (R&D) activities in the Netherlands; They hold a master’s degree.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: Consortium building takes places during two multiple-day workshops. The workshops are mandatory for the main applicant of the consortium. Co-applicants, cofinanciers and collaborating partners preferably also take part in the workshops.  Signing up for the workshops can be done via ISAAC.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum total of €13,000,000 can be requested from NWO for the grant application in this Call for Proposals. NWO will fund a maximum of 80% of the project, and a minimum of 51%. You will therefore need to arrange other funding for this call. This can consist of an own contribution by enterprises and civil society organisations and cash or in-kind co-funding from private parties. See chapter 3.2 of the call for proposals for more conditions. Projects may last for a maximum of six years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Workshop registration deadline: Tuesday 10 March 2026, 14:00 CET. Deadline for full proposal: Thursday 9 July 2026, 14:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The proposal will be assessed on the basis of three criteria: Comply with the Framework for State aid for research and development and innovation for research organisations, Quality of the consortium, Quality of the research plan. Procedure The procedure fort his Call for proposals consists of 3 steps: Registration of organisations for the workshops (deadline 10 March 2026, 14:00 CET), Workshops (1 – 3 April/5 – 7 May 2026), Submission of the grant proposal (deadline 9 July 2026, 14:00 CEST). After submission, the proposal will be assessed by the selection committee, which will advise the NWO Domain Science Board about whether or not to award funding. The decision of the Domain Science board is expected in the fourth quarter of 2026.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Groenvermogen

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Organisations that want to participate in the grant proposal within this Call for proposals are required to submit an Application form Workshops to participate in the procedure. For each organisation, a maximum of one Application form Workshops may be submitted. This means a maximum of one Application form Workshops for the entire organisation at the highest level (e.g. at university level and not at faculty or department level). The main applicant submits the proposal via the NWO web application ISAAC. During the assessment process, NWO will communicate with the main applicant.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: In this call for proposals casco-workshops are organised to guide the process of forming a consortium and defining the main lines of the grant application. Participants do not attend the workshop in a personal capacity, but represent their organisation.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The following must be included as mandatory attachments when registering for the workshop: Registration form for the workshops. The following must be included as mandatory attachments with the complete applications: Budget form, Declaration of general terms and conditions under the GBER, Declaration co-funding (only if applicable), Guarantee of continuity in project supervision (only if applicable), Confirmation of own contribution to investments (only if applicable).

title: NWO Talent Programme Vici Science 2026
target_groups: Diversity Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: Diversity Researchers from Abroad

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Vici target group consists of researchers at the stage of consolidation and further development of their leadership/research group. The goal of the Vici grant is to contribute to the researcher's development in this area.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Vici is aimed at senior researchers with academic qualities that clearly exceed what is customary within the international peer group, as well as established leadership- and mentorship skills. Researchers may only submit an application if they have obtained their PhD in the fifteen years preceding the benchmark date of this round, 1 March 2026. An extension to the above career-dependent submission period may be possible under specific circumstances (see Section 3.4.2 of the Call for proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The maximum grant is 1.5 million euros.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for pre-proposals is March 10, 2026, 14:00:00 hours CET . The deadline for proposals is September 8, 2026, 14:00:00 hours CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessment criterion for the pre-proposals is: Quality of the researcher The assessment criteria for the proposals are: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%) Scientific and/or societal impact (25%) Procedure submission of the preproposal; admissibility check of the preproposal; assessment of the preproposal; provisional advice assessment committee; possibility of filing a request for the correction of a factual error; decision-making preproposal; submission of the full proposal (only possible if you have received a positive decision on the preproposal); admissibility check of the full proposal; assessment of the full proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; and meeting of the assessment committee; decision-making. There is a single submission point for the Domain Science. Applications from the Earth Sciences, Astronomy, Chemistry, Computer Science, Life Sciences, Physics and Mathematics can be submitted to the Domain Science. Vici applications are assessed in a domain-wide competition: applications from different disciplines will be compared in competition. The domain uses broad-based assessment committees of academics to advise on the merits and prioritisation of the applications. Depending on the number of submissions and the underlying disciplines the assessment procedure within the Domain Science can take place in different expert panels/clusters. Applicants must first submit a pre-proposal. These are assessed by the assessment committee, which then makes a recommendation on whether or not to submit a proposal. Proposals are sent to external referees. Applicants are given the opportunity to respond to the referees’ reports (‘rebuttal’). Next, the proposals, the reviews and the rebuttals are assessed by the respective assessment committees. Based on the proposal, the referees’ reports and the rebuttal the assessment committee advises the accredited board of NWO. All proposals must be submitted via ISAAC, NWO's electronic application system. The only exceptions to this rule are proposals to the Health Research and Development domain. These proposals can only be submitted via Mijn ZonMw.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Optional literature list As of 2023, the Vici uses an optional literature list  of relevant scientific literature closely related to your research idea. NWO will use the literature list - along with your research idea, key words and specified discipline(s) - to search for potential external reviewers for your full proposal. Your literature list will not be submitted to the committee. Frequently asked questions On this page you will find an overview of the most frequently asked questions about the NWO Talent Programme, including questions about the set time limit to submit a proposal (and any exceptions to it): FAQ | NWO

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applicants may only submit a proposal after they have been informed that the board of the domain concerned has taken a positive decision on the applicant’s pre‐proposal. Applicants are not permitted to submit a proposal following a negative decision on the pre‐proposal. Pre‐proposals must be written in English and must include: A description of the candidate’s academic profile (max. 1200 words); A description of the candidate’s key output (max. 10 items); The research idea (max. 150 words). Proposals must be written in English and must include: an outline of the research proposal and a completed motivation for the choice of institution; a completed section scientific and/or societal impact; a completed data management section; a budget form.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Mandatory annex to the preproposal: not applicable. Optional attachment to the preproposal: literature list for referees. mandatory annex to the full proposal: budget form (in Excel). Optional attachment to the full proposal (see section 3.5.6): declaration co-funding; statement exceeding maximum funding.

title: KIC Demand-driven Research by Consortia – XL
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Demand 2024-2027 KIC - Vraag voor consortia 24/27 - Demand Driven Research By Consortia - XL
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Demand 2024-2027 KIC - Vraag voor consortia 24/27 - Demand Driven Research By Consortia - XL

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this programme is to stimulate demand-driven public-private partnerships that contribute to impact for the economy, people and the planet. The focus is on the societal challenges described in the mission-driven innovation policy of the Dutch government. This funding scheme focuses on the consortium’s knowledge and/or development question. There are no discipline-related frameworks or predetermined themes: the research need only align with one or more KIAs. This gives consortia the freedom to design interdisciplinary research that addresses their own specific challenges.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications must be submitted by a consortium comprising: A main applicant: a researcher employed on a permanent contract or tenure track at one of the following organisations: Dutch universities, university medical centres, KNAW or NWO institutes, the Netherlands Cancer Institute (NKI), the Max Planck Institute in Nijmegen, Naturalis Biodiversity Center, ARCNL, or the Princess Máxima Center. Professors of universities of applied sciences (lectoren) may also act as main applicants; Co-applicant(s): researchers at the above-mentioned institutions or at other research organisations (such as foundations or associations) that meet NWO’s eligibility criteria; Co-funding partners and collaboration partners: contribute financially or in kind and/or play a substantive role in the project, but do not apply for NWO funding themselves. Please note: Participation of at least one private company (private for-profit party) is mandatory, as is the involvement of at least two different research organisations; TO2 institutes are not eligible to participate as applicants in this call.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The total grant ceiling for this Call for proposals is €14,000,000. Applicants may request a minimum of €1,400,000 and a maximum of €7,000,000 in NWO funding per proposal. NWO funding may cover up to 70% of the total project budget; the remaining 30% must be provided through mandatory co-funding. Funded projects may have a maximum duration of six years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting pre-proposals is Tuesday 10 March 2026, before 14:00:00 CET The deadline for submitting full proposals is Thursday 12 November 2026, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for submitting a request for review of (foreign) organisations is 4 working weeks prior to the stated submission deadlines. For pre-proposals, the deadline for submitting a request for review of (foreign) organisations is Tuesday, 10 February 2026 at 14:00:00 CET. For proposals, the deadline for submitting a request for review of (foreign) organisations is Thursday, 15 October 2026 at 14:00:00 CET. Additional submission conditions: The main applicant may only submit one proposal within this Call for proposals in the role of main applicant. In addition to this, a main applicant may participate as a co-applicant in no more than one other consortium in this Call for proposals; In this Call for proposals, a co-applicant may participate as a co-applicant in at most two consortia.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications will be assessed based on the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (25%) Expected impact and pathway to impact (25%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (20%) In the full proposal, the budget must be well-substantiated and balanced. It must also be clearly demonstrated that the required co-funding (at least 30% of the total budget, part of which must be private cash) has been formally committed through a signed co-funding statement. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: Assessment of KIA suitability and pre-proposal The process begins with a mandatory pre-proposal, which is assessed for its alignment with one or more of the Knowledge and Innovation Agendas (KIAs). If the proposal is initially deemed not to align, applicants will have the opportunity to respond to the KIA reviewer feedback (rebuttal). Only preproposals considered suitable will be invited to submit a full proposal. Assessment of the full proposal Following a positive outcome at the pre-proposal stage, applicants may submit a full proposal. These will be reviewed by external referees. Applicants will again have the opportunity to respond to the referee reports (rebuttal). Based on the full proposal, the referee feedback and the rebuttal, the assessment committee will draft a pre-advice. Interview and final assessment Consortia will be invited to an interview with the assessment committee. During the interview, the committee can ask further questions, including new questions not raised by referees. The consortium will have the opportunity to respond and discuss these during the interview. The committee will then form an integrated assessment and make a funding recommendation to the NWO Executive Board, which makes the final decision on the grant allocation.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Please refer to Section 3.3 of the Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The application must be written in English. The (pre-)proposals forms contain instructions on how to fill out the related questions. When writing your (pre-)proposal, please take into account that it will be assessed by both expert reviewers as well as the assessment committee with a broad range of expertise. Please only include the annexes specified in the Call for proposals and application form. Additional annexes are not allowed, and you will be asked to remove them.

title: CETPartnership Joint Call 2025
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Clean Energy Transition Partnership (CETPartnership) is a multilateral and strategic partnership of national and regional research, development and innovation (RDI) programmes in European Member States and Associated Countries. The CETPartnership aims to empower the clean energy transition and contribute to the EU’s goal of becoming the first climate-neutral continent by 2050, by pooling national and regional RDTI funding for a broad variety of technologies and system solutions required to make the transition. In this 2025-call of the partnership NWO funds research on (1) multi-vector interactions between the integrated energy system and industrial frameworks and (2) hydrogen and renewable fuels. Funded consortia bring together international partners from academia, companies and Industry organizations, local/regional governments, research organizations and NGO’s.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The CETPartnership Joint Call 2025 is structured by the joint call text and nine Call modules describing specific topics and/or challenges that applicants shall address in their project proposal. Applicants must choose and apply to a specific call module when submitting their project proposal. Applications need to be submitted by research consortia including teams from a minimum of three countries participating in the call (including a minimum of two from EU Member States or EU Horizon Europe Associated Countries). Specific additional requirements apply for the 2 call modules that are eligible for NWO funding: CM2025-01 Multi-vector interactions between the integrated energy system and industrial frameworks CM2025-05 Hydrogen and renewable fuels Full, associate and assistant professors, lectors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application. An applicant may only request NWO funding for one project (part of a European consortium) in this call of the CETPartnership. See the CETPartnership Call Documents for details on transnational eligibility criteria and participating countries. For scientists based in the Kingdom of the Netherlands, applying for NWO funding, the NWO eligibility criteria apply.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For research proposals a maximum of €400.000 can be applied for from NWO. Funding can only be requested for research on the following call modules: CM2025-01, CM2025-05. The maximum project duration is 3 years. No limit has been set on the total international consortium size (budget or partners), but per call module an indicative project size and minimum amount of consortium partners is described in the CETPartnership Call Documents per call module. The budget modules that are available within this Call for proposals are: Research Leave, Postdoc, Non Scientific Staff, Personnel TO2 institutes and universities of applied sciences, Benchfee for Postdoc positions, Material Costs, Knowledge Utilisation and Internationalisation. The maximum amounts for each module are stated in the Funding Organisation rules (part NWO) in the Call Documents. For further details, see the Call Documents at the CETPartnership website .

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Pre- Proposal (compulsory) submission deadline: 9 October 2025, 14.00 CET . Full proposal submission deadline: 12 March 2026, 14.00 CET . Proposals need to be submitted through the CETPartnership Application portal , the electronic submission system of the CETPartnership. Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit the completed ‘NWO budget template’ separately to NWO when uploading their full proposal via email

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals and full proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Excellence Impact Quality and efficiency of the implementation Please see the full CETPartnership Call Documents for the criteria including the sub-criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage consortia are invited to submit a pre-proposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage can submit a full proposal. Per individual call module each proposal will be evaluated by three independent experts. The proposals will be ranked per call module based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. At NWO the Executive Board will take this decision regarding the dedicated NWO budget.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information can be found on the CETPartnership website The application submission can be done via the CETPartnership Application portal, the electronic submission system of the CETPartnership.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit a completed ‘NWO budget template’ separately at NWO when submitting their full proposal. This form can be found below, under ‘Downloads for your application’. Applicants can submit the completed Budget Form to NWO via an email

information_about_application_on_externa.title: Information about application on external website
information_about_application_on_externa.text: Proposals have to be submitted through the CETPartnership website .

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad Collaboration with Industry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the NWA ORC 2026 call for proposals is to encourage research that is set up and carried out by inter- and transdisciplinary consortia that include representatives from the full breadth of the knowledge chain as well as relevant societal (public and/or private) partners, including citizens. The research will address so called wicked problems, focused on themes that are proposed by NWA-route networks and based on one or more of the 140 cluster questions.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals should be submitted by a consortium in which the various types of research in the knowledge chain (fundamental, applied and practice-oriented) are represented. There are four categories of participant within a consortium: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Cooperating partner(s); Co-funder(s) (optional). The main applicant submits the application on behalf of the consortium. More information about the four categories of participants as well as specific requirements regarding who can apply, can be found in paragraph 3.1 of the call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In this Call for Proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of collaborative workshops. The main applicant must have participated in both collaborative workshops on the relevant theme, or a substitute must have been present.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: In this round, proposals can be submitted that are based on a theme as described in Annex 7.5 of the Call for proposals. Project duration should be 4-8 years, and maximum available budget is €7,250,000. The budget is built up using the NWO-wide standardised building blocks, the so-called modules. Seven modules are available in this call: Personnel; Material; Investments; Knowledge utilisation; Project management. Extensive information about the modules can be found in section 3.2 and annex 7.1 of the call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The NWA-ORC 2026 round has two phases: Registration for and participation in collaborative workshops (mandatory for main applicants) Between 6 February and 17 March 2026, NWO organises collaborative workshops with the aim of bringing together interested parties (researchers and public and/or private social parties) around a specific theme, combining research ideas (joining forces) and forming broad innovative consortia. You can register for the workshops via this page . Submitting a proposal The deadline for submission of proposals via ISAAC is 6 October 2026, at 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis, contribution to a solution (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact  (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Each assessment criterion consists of several aspects. More information on the criteria can be found in paragraph 4.3 of the call for proposals. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: registration for collaborative workshops; participation in the collaborative workshops (mandatory for the main applicant or a deputy (see paragraph 3.3.2)); submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making. Detailed information about the procedure can be found in the Call for Proposals, Section 4.2.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The proposal is submitted by a knowledge-chain-wide, inter- and transdisciplinary consortium (see section 3.1 of the Call for Proposals). Research is based on themes proposed by and with support from the broad network of the NWA route networks and involves innovative, demand-driven research with the aim of achieving scientific and social breakthroughs.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: In this Call for Proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of collaborative workshops. The main applicant must have participated in both collaborative workshops on the relevant theme, or a substitute must have been present. The aim of the workshops is bringing together interested parties (researchers and public and/or private social parties) around a specific theme, combining research ideas (joining forces) and forming broad innovative consortia. You can register for the workshops via this page .

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To participate in the collaborative workshops, you must have read the Call for Proposals and registered via this page . After the collaborative workshops, you will find the forms for your proposal on this page and in ISAAC.

downloads.name: NWA-ORC 2026 | call for proposals

title: Demonstrator 2026
target_groups: Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Demonstrator programme focuses on facilitating and stimulating the application of research results from Dutch universities, research institutes or Universities of Applied Sciences. The programme aims at further developing the knowledge to the level of a Minimal Viable Product. In a Demonstrator project, the emphasis is on advancing the knowledge and expertise (moving from TRL/SRL 4 to 5) rather than continuing the research.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Full, associate and assistant professors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following organisations: Universities and universities of applied sciences established in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; University medical centers; KNAW and NWO institutes; the Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; NCB Naturalis; Advanced Research Center for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of € 200,000 (including VAT) can be requested for a proposal in this call. A Demonstrator project has a maximum duration of 18 months. The following budget modules are available within this call: Postdoc; Non-scientific staff (NSS) at universities; Replacement; Staff of universities of applied sciences; Material costs; Knowledge utilization; Patent maintenance costs.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: 19 March 2026 at 14:00:00 hours CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The proposals will be assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Basis and innovation (weighting factor 1); Societal impact (weighting factor 1); Project approach and Project team (weighting factor 1) For the specifications of the criteria please read the Call for proposals Procedure The proposals are evaluated in writing by an evaluation committee. Proposals are only eligible for funding if they do not score higher than 3.9 (good) for the assessment criteria 'Basis and innovation’, 'Societal impact' and 'Project approach and Project team'; the average of these three scores must not exceed 3.4 (very good). On the basis of the average grade according to these criteria, the proposals are ranked. The 11 highest-ranked applicants receive an invitation to present their Demonstrator proposal. The committee again prioritises these applications and gives a granting recommendation to the TTW board. The TTW board takes the ultimate granting decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Please note: for innovative academic start-ups, please take a look at the following instrument: Take-off For specific questions about this Call for proposals, please contact us by e-mail: demonstrator@nwo.nl

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: When writing a Demonstrator proposal keep the assessment criteria (paragraph 4.3.1 'Basis and innovation’, 'Societal impact' and 'Project approach and Project team') in mind and check if the formal conditions for submission have been met. For more information please refer to the Call for Proposals (paragraph 3.4.1).

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call we ask you to fill in the application form and if applicable provide us with a statement of ‘tenure track’ position when the applicant’s tenure track agreement ends before the intended completion date of the project.

title: Open Competition Domain Science XS 2026 packet 26-1

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The ENW-XS category is specifically intended to encourage curiosity-driven and bold research that involves the relatively rapid exploration of a promising idea. At the end of the ENW-XS project, researchers can decide for themselves whether or not to pursue this new line of research any further. One way in which this can be done is to submit a proposal within another funding programme in the Open Competition Domain Science (ENW-M and ENW-XL) or elsewhere.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers who have obtained a PhD can apply if they hold a salaried position for the duration of the application process and duration of the project for which the grant is being requested. ENW-XS is also open for postdocs. Read the conditions in section 3.1 of the call for proposals for the possibilities of requesting your own salary in ENW-XS. Applicants who had a proposal dealt with in the immediately preceding ENW-XS package (25-3; deadline 16 September 2025) may not make a submission Women especially are urged to apply.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For research proposals in ENW-XS, applicants may apply for sums of up to €50,000. There are no requirements concerning the type of research to be conducted. Also, there are very few restrictions on the subjects of proposals. For instance, they could involve the appointment of temporary staff, material costs, the costs involved in performing ‘citizen science’, and travel expenses for commuting to other locations to conduct experiments. Postdocs are permitted to request their own salary. The appointment of PhD students of physician assistants is subject to additional conditions. For details of these conditions, see Annex 7.1 to the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Three deadlines for submitting proposals for ENW-XS projects are specified for 2026. For the most up-to-date ENW-XS webpage please see www.nwo.nl/enw-xs-en Package Submission deadlines Decision anticipated by 26-1 13 January 2026, at 14:00:00 CET 19 March 2026 26-2 14 april 2026, at 14:00:00 CEST 25 June 2026 26-3 15 September 2026, at 14:00:00 CEST 19 November 2026 Indication of timetable with respect to next deadline: Deadline for submission of proposals 13 January 2026, at 14:00:00 CET Assessment of admissibility 13-30 January 2026 Disclosure of personal interests 15-22 January 2026 Proposals sent to assessors 3 February 2026 * NWO receives assessment reports 17 February 2026, at 14:00:00 CET NWO Domain Science Board reaches a decision 19 March 2026 Project start date No later than six months after the date of formal approval Project end date No later than 12 months after starting date * these dates are tentative and can differ with a few days, you will always get 10 working days for the assessment

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria High-risk/high-gain Is the proposed research innovative, highly speculative, and ground-breaking? Could the research proposal potentially have a scientific impact? Does the proposed research align with the aim of the Call, as detailed in Chapter 2? Feasibility of the research plan The objective, motivation and work plan must be clearly described. Does the methodology appear suitable for achieving the stated project objective? Is the research plan appropriately planned and are the activities sufficiently clearly included in the plan? Can the project be carried out with the requested budget? Proposals must be drawn up with a wide audience in mind, to enable assessors from all Domain Science disciplines to properly evaluate the research plan and the following assessment aspects. The assessors may award a lower ranking to any proposals that are drawn up in a way that is not accessible to those in other disciplines. Procedure All applications submitted for one of the deadlines are treated as a package. Per package the applications are separated into two clusters. Applicants choose in which of the following clusters their application is assessed: Life Sciences Physical and Exact Sciences Per cluster the applications are separated in group A and group B. The applications in groups A are assessed by the applicants in group B and vice versa. The NWO office will determine the final ranks of the applications based on the ranks submitted by the assessors. Two final rankings will be established per package; two for each cluster. There will be no opportunity for a rebuttal. The NWO Domain Science Board will decide whether to grant or refuse an application based on the results of the four rankings and the available financial means.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: When drawing up a proposal, be sure to make it anonymous: Applicants should include neither their own name nor those of any individuals in their research group; Applicants should not include details of any affiliations with institutes or knowledge institutions; Applicants should not include references to any publications of other types of output that they have (co-)authored; Applicants should not include the names of any partners (or potential partners). Applicants should be aware that they will be required to assess proposals from all Domain Science disciplines in the chosen cluster. Therefore, the own proposal must be written with a wide audience in mind.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Mandatory annexes: budget form (Excel) and information form. Optional annexes: declaration appointment PhD/Physician-researcher (see Annex 7.1 in the Call).

title: KIC Robust and resilient space infrastructure for a secure Netherlands
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Mission 2024-2027 Robust and resilient space infrastructure for a safe Netherlands

characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Mission 2024-2027 Robust and resilient space infrastructure for a safe Netherlands

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Space infrastructure is essential for communication, remote sensing, positioning, and time information, but it is also vulnerable to sabotage, espionage, and collisions with space debris from decommissioned or non-functioning satellites, as well as to natural disruptions. To strengthen the resilience of space infrastructure, it is important that the Dutch research community, industry, and government are well prepared and protect this infrastructure through innovative technologies, based on solid legal, ethical, and strategic security frameworks. This Call for proposals focuses on strengthening a robust and resilient space infrastructure for a secure Netherlands. This Call for proposals specifically invites research proposals addressing the following themes: Critical technologies to enhance the resilience and protection of space infrastructure (consortia are required to address this aspect in their proposal). Associated legal, ethical, and strategic security frameworks (consortia must explicitly describe in their proposal how these frameworks are integrated into the design, implementation, and application of their research).

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants other participants are involved as well. Four categories of participants can be distinguished in the consortium: Main applicant Co-applicant(s) Co-funder(s) Cooperation partner(s) A consortium must consist of at least one main applicant, one co-applicant, and two co-funders, possibly supplemented by one or more cooperation partners. The conditions for each participant are explained in more detail in the following sections. All consortium partners must play an active role in formulating the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: At 4th of February 2026, NWO will organize a matchmaking event for this Call for Proposals. Participation in this event is recommended but not mandatory.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For a proposal submitted in this Call for proposals, applicants can request a minimum of €1.000,000 and a maximum of €2,000,000 in NWO funding. NWO will fund up to 80% of the total project budget; the remaining portion must be covered through mandatory co-funding (see section 3.5.6). NWO will never fund less than 50% of the total project budget. Applicants and co-applicants may include costs for personnel, materials, investments, knowledge utilization, and project management.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting proposals is 2 April 2026 , before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting a request for the assessment of foreign organizations (see section 7.1.1) is 19 March 2026 , before 14:00:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The pre-proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: submission research idea (not obligatory) matchmaking; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making. An external, independent assessment committee will be assigned for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from the research field and society with knowledge of (partial aspects of) the topic.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: These conditions are: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1 and Section 7.1.1; the applying consortium contains two or more co-funders; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; all of the required annexes and forms are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals. the budget in the application has been prepared according to the terms of this Call for proposals (using the format made available which includes the most recent rates); the required co-financing has been correctly and fully committed through co-financing statements.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Compulsory annexes: budget form; declarations co-funding from co-funders (see Section 3.1.2 and 3.5.6); form ‘Statement and signature’; letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (if applicable, see Section 3.1 of the Call for Proposals ); confirmation of own contribution to investments (only if applicable, see section 7.1 of the Call for Proposals); Knowledge security statement. The proposal and the appendix(es) must be drawn up in accordance with the template provided by NWO. Annexes must be uploaded in ISAAC separately from the application. All of the annexes, except for the budget, must be submitted as PDF files (without encryption). The budget must be submitted in ISAAC as an Excel file. When submitting the proposal, the attached declarations co-funding should guarantee the full required amount of co-funding in line with the conditions of Section 3.5.6. Any annexes other than those above-mentioned are not permitted.
