title: NWA Resilient Digital Ecosystems
target_groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Interdisciplinary Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Interdisciplinary Collaboration
characteristics.Themes: Cybersecurity Artificial Intelligence Key Technologies Resilient Society Safety

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Digital interconnectedness is an integral part of Dutch society. Citizens, governments and businesses are closely intertwined in complex digital ecosystems, in which information, processes and services are continuously exchanged. This interconnectedness offers major advantages, but also makes society vulnerable. Disruptions, incidents or cyber attacks within a digital ecosystem can lead to serious societal disruption. The aim of this call is to develop socio-technical innovations and courses of action that increase the security and resilience of digital ecosystems. A security by design and security by default approach is strongly encouraged. Ecosystems in which the right technological and social choices are made from the outset enable participants to  make use of the ecosystem’s possibilities in a natural way and with minimal security risks. The Call for Proposals has a two-phase structure. In the first phase, research into sector‑specific digital ecosystems is funded. In the second phase, these insights are translated into cross‑sector guidelines for action for stakeholders within sectors and within government, brought together in one overarching project. The outcomes can also be used to scale up interventions and to shape new digital ecosystems that are secure and resilient by design.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications in the first phase of this call are submitted by a main applicant on behalf of a consortium. Within the consortium, four categories of participants are distinguished: Main applicant Co-applicant(s) Cooperation partner(s) Co-funder(s) (optional) A consortium should consist of at least a main applicant, a co-applicant and cooperation partner. In the second phase of this call, the main applicants of the research projects awarded in phase 1 will be invited to form a consortium for an overarching research project. In addition, co-applicants from the underlying research projects may be included in the consortium, insofar as their involvement contributes to the objectives of the overarching project. In this phase, the consortium may be expanded to include new cooperation partners and co-funders who were not involved in phase 1. The conditions are explained in more detail in the Call for Proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In order to increase the impact of the research, collaboration between researchers, researchers from other disciplines and partners from society, such as companies and societal organisations, is necessary. In this call, NWO supports the formation of a consortium by organising the following meetings: Matchmaking event (phase 1): on 23 April 2026, NWO will organise a matchmaking event for this call. Participation in this activity is recommended but not mandatory. Facilitating workshops (phase 2):  For the overarching project in phase 2, NWO is organising workshops to stimulate the development of the overarching application and the formation of the associated consortium. Participation in these facilitating workshops is mandatory: each phase 1 project must be represented by at least one project leader or work package leader. NWO will communicate in a timely manner with the phase 1 project leaders regarding the organization of these workshops. Additional information about consortium formation can be found in the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The available budget for this Call for proposals is €8,702,072 in total. An amount of €7,500,000 is available for the first phase. For each phase 1 project, a grant amount of at most  €1,875,000 can be applied for. Four or five applications are expected to be allocated within this phase. For the second phase, one overarching connection project can be awarded. The available budget for this phase 2 project is €1,202,072. More information about the available budget can be found in the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadlines: The deadline for submitting a request for assessment of organisations based in the Netherlands for phase 1 (see section 3.1.1) is 18 August 2026 before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting a request for assessment of foreign organisations for phase 1 (see section 7.1.1) is 18 August 2026 , before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting applications in phase 1 is 15 September 2026 , before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting a request for assessment of organisations based in the Netherlands for phase 2 (see section 3.1.1) is 18 September 2029 , before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting a request for assessment of foreign organisations for phase 2 (see section 7.1.1) is 18 September 2029 , before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting applications in phase 2 is 16 October 2029 , before 14:00:00 CEST. Meetings: The information meeting for phase 1 will take place on 19 March 2026 (not mandatory). The matchmaking for phase 1 will take place on 23 April 2026 (not mandatory). The facilitating sessions for phase 2 will take place in early 2029. NWO will communicate in a timely manner with the phase 1 project leaders regarding the organization of these workshops.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications submitted under this call will be assessed in both phases on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure Procedure phase 1 Assessment of (foreign) organisations 18 augustus 2026 Application deadline 15 September 2026 Interview selection, interviews, and assessment committee meeting November 2026 till January 2027 Board decision February 2027 – March 2027 Procedure phase 2 Facilitation session 1 April 2029 Facilitation session 2 May 2029 Assessment of (foreign) organisations 18 September 2029 Application deadline 16 October 2029 Assessment for consideration October 2029 Assessment by assessment committee November – December 2029

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: An application can only be submitted via the web application ISAAC. Applications that are not submitted via ISAAC will not be taken into consideration. As the main applicant, you are required to submit your application via your own personal ISAAC account. To prepare applications in phases 1 and 2, follow these steps: download the application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the funding instrument concerned); complete the application form; save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes; fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. Compulsory annexes: budget; declarations of commitment of cooperation partners; De minimis aid declaration (mandatory if applicable, see subsections 3.1.1 and 3.5.8); declaration co-funding from co-funders (mandatory if applicable); letter guaranteeing the continuity of the project supervision (compulsory if applicable, see paragraph 3.1); form 'Statement and signature'. “Knowledge security” statement

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: Matchmaking event (phase 1): on 23 April 2026, NWO will organise a matchmaking meeting for this call. Participation in this activity is recommended but not mandatory. Facilitating workshops (phase 2): For the overarching project in phase 2, NWO is organising workshops to stimulate the development of the overarching application and the formation of the associated consortium. Participation in these facilitating workshops is mandatory: each phase 1 project must be represented, with at least one project leader or work package leader in attendance. NWO will communicate in a timely manner with the phase 1 project leaders regarding the organization of these workshops.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the application form, we ask you to also submit the following annexes: Compulsory annexes: budget; declarations of commitment of cooperation partners; De minimis aid declaration (mandatory if applicable, see subsections 3.1.1 and 3.5.8); declaration co-funding from co-funders (mandatory if applicable); letter guaranteeing the continuity of the project supervision (compulsory if applicable, see paragraph 3.1); form 'Statement and signature'. “Knowledge security” statement The application and attachments must be drawn up according to the template provided by NWO.

title: KIC - Early detection of (emerging) outbreaks of infectious diseases
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 KIC - Missie 24/27 - Early detection of (emerging) outbreaks of infectious diseases
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 KIC - Missie 24/27 - Early detection of (emerging) outbreaks of infectious diseases
characteristics.Themes: Health Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this programme is to develop and refine AI-driven early detection systems. The focus is on using and combining existing non-biomedical data sources for the early detection of infectious disease outbreaks. Combining these data sources with existing biomedical surveillance, such as data from the veterinary domain, general practitioners or screening, is an important innovation. This will enable early detection of infection hotspots in the Netherlands and prevent disruptive epidemics. Applicants are also recommended to seek alignment with existing national and international initiatives and/or networks. Applicants are encouraged to develop a plan for data infrastructure, revenue models and a response plan and/or integrate these into existing plans. Therapy and vaccine development are not covered by the Call for proposals.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at a research organisation. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants two or more co-funders, and potentially one or more cooperation partners, are involved as well. All consortiumpartners should play an active role in the formulation of the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project. For more information: see section 3.1 in the Call for Proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: This Call for proposals stimulates the formation of consortiums by means of collaborative workshops. Attending the collaborative workshops is mandatory for main applicants. The objective of the collaborative workshops is to bring together potential applicants and their ideas, to encourage collaboration within broad, innovative consortia, and to limit the number of proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application, a minimum of €2.000.000 and a maximum of €3.500.000 of NWO funding can be applied for. With this, NWO finances maximally 90% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding (see Section 3.5.6). NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 6 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The dates of the collaborative workshops are 19 March 2026 and 16 April 2026. The deadline for submission of full applications is 6 October 2026, 14:00:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%)

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: For this Call for proposals, attending the collaborative workshops by the main applicant (or substitute) is mandatory. Full applications will be provided with peer review by referees. Applicants will be given the opportunity to submit a rebuttal to this. The applications will then be assessed by the assessment committee and and applicants will be given the opportunity to answer questions from the assessment committee during an interview . The assessment committee will present its advice to the NWO Executive Board for decision-making. In September 2027, the NWO Executive Board will adopt the final assessment of the applications.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: All the documents you need for your application can be found here. The submission of an application is done via the application and reporting system ISAAC. If you do not yet have a personal ISAAC account, please create one at least one working day earlier. Below you will find more information about writing a proposal and which forms you should use. You will need to use the forms that you can download below.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: For this Call for proposals, attending the collaborative workshops by the main applicant (or substitute) is mandatory.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In addition to filling in the application form when submitting the complete application, we also ask you to include a signed statement from the co-financiers. If applicable, a confirmation of contribution to investments (see section 7.1 in the call for proposals) and a guarantee for continuity in the project management (see section 3.1 in the call for proposals) must also be sent. At the time of submission, the enclosed letters of support must have pledged the full required co-financing in accordance with the conditions described in sections 3.1.2 and 3.5.6 in the call for proposals. Other types of annexes shall are not allowed at the stage of submission of complete applications. Attachments must be uploaded separately from the application in ISAAC. All attachments, with the exception of the budget form, must be uploaded as a pdf file. The budget form can be uploaded as an Excel file in ISAAC.

title: Research Infrastructure (RI): national consortia
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Applied Sciences Research

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Applied Sciences Research

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: With this instrument, NWO aims to strengthen the RI available to the research community in the Netherlands. The instrument focuses on designing innovative RI that is of (inter)national importance, which can be used to achieve innovations and breakthroughs with a high value for science, society, and business. Proposals may be submitted by national consortia from all academic disciplines. A broad group of relevant representatives in the field from across the Netherlands must be involved at an early stage (when writing the proposal) and given a relevant role in governance.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The main applicant submits the statement of intent and the completed proposal on behalf of the institution or consortium. There are four categories of participants for a consortium: Main applicant Co-applicant(s) Partner(s) (optional) Co-funder(s) (optional) Main applicants are employed by one of the following institutes: universities as referred to in Article 1.8(1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act and the universities listed in the Policy Rule Universities in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; university medical centres, meaning academic hospitals as referred to in Article 1.13(1) of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act; institutes affiliated to the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; Naturalis Biodiversity Center; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Centre. Co-applicants can be associated with research organisations mentioned above, and other research organisations mentioned in article 1.1, paragraph 4, of the NWO Grant Rules 2024 and that meet the cumulative conditions stated in the Call for proposals, section 3.1.1.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: For this call the forming of a consortium is necessary. Applicants are themselves responsible for finding partners. There are no obligatory meetings organised by NWO. The submitted letters of intent are published on the NWO website. In the case you see the possibility to cooperate with another party after seeing their proposal, you can request their contact details at NWO.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A minimum of € 1,500,000 and a maximum of € 10,000,000 (NWO contribution) can be applied for per RI project with a maximum project duration of 5 years. The own contribution of the applying consortium must be at least 25% of the total budget of the project application (NWO contribution + own contribution to the project). The total budget of a project application is therefore at least € 2,000,000. Funding can be requested for the costs of the development, purchase, building or upgrade of an RI. This funding programme is not intended for financing the research to be conducted with the RI. Only project-specific costs are eligible for funding. Capital investments can include the following types of costs: Personnel costs Investments Material costs IT costs

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting letters of intent (required) is 2 December 2025, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for applications is 24 March 2026, before 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals are assessed against four criteria: Scientific quality Impact Organisational and financial aspects Technical aspects The criteria have a weigh of 1/3, 1/3, 1/6, and 1/6, respectively. Procedure The assessment procedure consists of the following steps: Submission of statements of intent, deadline 2 December 2025; Submission of a full proposal, deadline 24 March 2026; Peer review by referees, April 2026 - August 2026; Submission of a rebuttal, September-October 2026; First meeting Assessment Committee (site visit selection), November 2026; Site visits, end of November-December 2026; Second meeting Assessment Committee, January 2027; Decision by the Executive Board of NWO, March 2027.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: NWO will test your statement of intent and application for conditions set out in the Call for proposals. Only if this meets the conditions it will be processed. Submitting a statement of intent is a requirement to submit an application.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Statement of intent Prior to submitting an application, the main applicant must announce the initiative digitally by submitting a statement of intent in ISAAC. The statement of intent is not assessed. Application For this call, we ask you to submit the following attachments in addition to filling in the application form. For all attachments, a mandatory format can be downloaded from this page or from the online application system ISAAC. The completed budget format in Excel; The completed and signed submission letter from the highest administrative body of the institution or organisation that acts as the official secretary of the consortium; For each applying institution, a signed letter of guarantee confirming the own contribution; For each co-funder, a Declaration of Co-funding; if necessary, declaration of appointment and project supervision.

title: Rubicon 2026-1
target_groups: International Collaboration Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Starting Researchers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: International research experience is often essential for building up one’s scientific career. Rubicon offers talented researchers who have completed their doctorates in the past year the chance to gain experience at a research institution outside the Kingdom of the Netherlands, as international research experience is likely to be an advantage at a later stage in the applicant’s academic career. Rubicon is open to all scientific disciplines for a research project at a foreign research institute. In some cases, there is an opportunity to apply for a research period at a research institute in the Kingdom of the Netherlands.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Postgraduates who are currently engaged in doctoral research or who have been awarded a doctorate in the twelve months preceding the relevant deadline can submit a proposal. Applicants who are still engaged in doctoral research may apply only if their supervisor provides a written declaration approving their thesis. Women especially are urged to apply. Applicants are eligible to apply if, before the start of the project, they have received a PhD from a research organisation mentioned under article 1.1 paragraph 1 of the NWO Grant Rules. The complete list of research organisations can also be found in the Call for Proposals. Applications must be submitted by individual researchers and not by pairs or teams of researchers. Applicants may only submit an application for a Rubicon grant once. You can find the complete list of the eligibility criteria in the call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A budget of 3,145,000 euro is available for this submission round. The available budget will be divided in proportion to the number of eligible applications between the units Science, SSH, AES and ZonMw. Applicants can apply for a period of up to 24 months at an excellent research institute outside the Netherlands. The minimum duration is 12 months. The eligible costs are your salary including fringe benefits, travel expenses and a limited amount of research costs. Any overhead costs such as housing, computers, infrastructure etc. are not eligible for funding. You can find the standard amounts in chapter 7 of the Call for proposals. It is not possible to combine a stay at several institutes. Fieldwork is allowed, depending on the nature of the research proposed.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for this round is Tuesday 24 March 2026 at 14:00:00 hours CET (2 PM sharp).

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The quality of the applicant The quality of the research proposal The quality of the host institute in the context of the match between the applicant/proposal and the host institute Scientific and/or societal impact Procedure Each application will be assessed within the realm of the relevant discipline(s) by a multidisciplinary committee based on the criteria mentioned above. NWO announces which applications have been granted about four months after the deadline of this round.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: When writing your proposal, please bear in mind that each application will be assessed with the realm of the relevant discipline(s) by a multidisciplinary committee. Please use the application form that you can download from this page. Only the required annexes will be accepted

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Preparing an application: Download the application form and fill it in; Save the application form as a pdf file and upload it in ISAAC (Science, SSH, AES) or MijnZonMw (ZonMw). The application form consists of: A research proposal written in English (max. 6 pages A4, 2,000 words). The word count includes footnotes, texts illustrations and tables, captions to illustrations and tables. References are excluded; A narrative CV of the applicant consisting of a narrative academic profile (400-700 words) and the key output (max. 10 items); One acceptance form of the intended host institute; Applications from applicants who have not yet been awarded a PhD must also be accompanied by a form approving their thesis. All forms will be made available on the websites of NWO and ZonMw at least two months before the deadline of this call.

title: Science for policy
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The objective of this NWA programme is to investigate how to optimise the government’s application of knowledge from research in order to improve understanding of complex societal challenges, how to address them more effectively and efficiently, and how to learn from these efforts. To this end, this programme considers current and possible new forms of interactions between the worlds of knowledge and policy, as well as the reinforcement of these forms of cooperation and what it is expected to yield: knowledge that can be utilised better and better knowledge utilisation. The insights and action perspectives that are needed in order to achieve this goal will be developed and tested through co-creation in the departmental policy labs . A policy lab is an experimental learning environment in which research proceeds parallel to the various phases of the policy cycle. Within this physical research environment, various parties (knowledge institutions, government bodies and civil society organisations) engage in co-creative collaboration to address a current departmental policy file.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: A consortium should consist of at least one main applicant, one co-applicant and one collaborative partner. Co-funding is not required. Additional conditions: The main applicant may submit only one proposal in this call in the capacity of main applicant. The main applicant may additionally participate as a co-applicant in another consortium no more than once. The main applicant (or a designated substitute) has participated in all three collaborative workshops. A co-applicant may participate as a co-applicant in a maximum of two consortia in this call. Individuals on zero-hours employment contracts are excluded from submission as co-applicants.

consortium_building.title: Consortium formation
consortium_building.text: NWO encourages and supports the formation of these collaborations by organising meetings or workshops for consortium formation. In this Call for proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through organising three collaborative workshops. The workshops offer the possibility to combine (research) ideas and to form broad innovative consortia (consolidation of strengths). It is subsequently up to the participants to enter into collaboration in inter- and transdisciplinary innovative consortia that span the entire knowledge chain, and to jointly develop the proposal. The main applicant (or a designated substitute) is required to participate in all three collaborative workshops.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The maximum budget to be applied for is € 4,630,000. Within this Call for proposals it is expected that a maximum of one proposal will be awarded funding. The duration of the project is 48 to 60 months. The applicant and co-applicants can request funding for the budget modules personnel, equipment, investments, knowledge utilisation, and project management.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for registering for the collaborative workshops is 04 September 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting proposals is 24 March 2026, before 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis, Expected impact and route to impact, Quality of the consortium, Quality of the research. The Call for proposals section 2.2.4 includes additional assessment criteria concerning the consortium, the policy labs , the supporting case studies, and the connectFing interdepartmental research. Procedure The procedure consists of two phases: Registration for and participation in the three collaborative workshops (mandatory for main applicants). Submission of a proposal. NWO will establish an assessment committee to assess the admissible proposals. No referees will be used for this funding round. NWO will compile a preliminary ranking based on the preliminary recommendations and the scores of the designated committee members. In principle, all consortia will be invited for an interview unless too many proposals have been submitted. In that case, a preselection will be made. At the end of the interviews, the committee will draw up a written recommendation on the quality and ranking of the proposals, which will be submitted to the Executive Board for approval.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: This NWA programme invites the design and implementation of participatory and interactive action research. The proposal should address the following three components in full: Departmental policy lab s Supporting case studies Connecting interdepartmental research The five departmental policy labs (at the Ministries of Defence; Justice and Security; Education, Culture and Science; and Housing and Spatial Planning/Infrastructure and Water Management) focus on existing files within the Dutch national government, in order to analyse the development and utilisation of knowledge, as well as to suggest and introduce improvements. The four supporting case studies can be from either within or outside the policy labs , but are focused on short-term policy initiatives and testing out smaller interventions. The connecting interdepartmental research brings together insights from the various policy labs and supporting case studies, and it provides a space within which the researchers, policy officers and other stakeholders can learn together and share experiences.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium formation activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: NWO is organizing three collaboration workshops. These workshops aim to bring together potential applicants and their ideas, encourage collaboration in broad, innovative consortia, and reduce the number of applications. The programme of each workshop will be shaped according to the theme of the call for proposals, the content descriptions of the policy labs and supporting cases, the expertise of the participants, and the expected impact of their contributions. While NWO supports mutual exploration, parties ultimately decide for themselves whether and with whom they wish to cooperate. During the workshops, participants exchange ideas, explore cooperation in consortia and make initial agreements for joint application development. Following the workshops, the consortia will further develop the applications.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the proposal form, NWO also asks you to send the following required documents: statements and signature, declaration of commitment cooperation partners (without co-funding), the budget form. If applicable, NWO also request that you send the following documents: declaration co-funding; letter guaranteeing the continuity of the project supervision.

title: Isaac Newton Group Telescopes 26b
programme: Observation time on the Isaac Newton Group of Telescopes

characteristics.Research programme: Observation time on the Isaac Newton Group of Telescopes

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Astronomers can apply for observing time at the Isaac Newton Group of Telescopes (ING) on La Palma. The ING consists of the 4.2 m William Herschel Telescope (WHT) and the 2.5 m Isaac Newton Telescope (INT).

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers in astronomy with a Dutch affiliation.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Dutch observing time at WHT for observations with WEAVE to be carried out between 1 November 2026 and 30 April 2027. or Dutch observing time at INT for observations with HARPS3 to be carried out between 1 August 2026 and 31 January 2027. WEAVE Proposals are invited for observations with WEAVE at WHT. In semester 2026B all three WEAVE observing modes are offered: MOS (multi-object spectroscopy), LIFU (large integral-field unit) and mini IFUs (mini integral-field units). MOS and mini-IFUs are available for the first time; mini-IFUs on a shared risk basis. Target of Opportunity and non sidereal observations can be applied for with WEAVE LIFU. The effective available Dutch observing time for open time proposals with WEAVE is approximately 90 hours per semester. HARPS3 Proposals are invited for the HARPS3 spectrograph at INT. HARPS3 is offered for the first time and on a shared-risk basis. Please note that in this semester the polarimetric mode is not offered. Approximately 14% of HARPS3 science time will be available for Dutch open-time proposals; 60% of HARPS3 science time will be devoted to the Terra Hunting Experiment (THE). For more information, see https://www.ing.iac.es/astronomy/instr.html .

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The next deadline is 24 March 2026. This is the deadline for: observing time with WEAVE@WHT to be carried out between 1 November 2026 and 30 April 2027. observing time with HARPS3@INT to be carried out between 1 August 2026 and 31 January 2027. Note : The ING issues two calls for proposals per year. The associated deadlines for Dutch open time proposals are yearly expected in the second half of March and in the second half of September.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals will be assessed by the ING Programme Committee of NWO. Because proposals for observing time come from many different fields of research, we recommend you to keep in mind that not all members of the Programme Committee are experts in your specific area of research. Please include the necessary background information and clearly formulate the relevance of your science question and how the requested data will contribute to answering this science question. Procedure The Programme Committee of NWO assesses proposals for Dutch observing time. International proposals are assessed by a different committee. In general the telescope organisation will inform you on the success of your proposal approximately three months after submission. Around the same time, you will also receive feedback from the Programme Committee.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Visit the ING-website (Isaac Newton Group).

title: KIC Collaborative business models for a circular economy
status_raw: Open calls Open for letter of intent
programme: KIC 2024-2027 KIC - Missie 24/27 - Collaborative business models for a circular economy
target_groups: International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.Status: Open calls Open for letter of intent
characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: KIC 2024-2027 KIC - Missie 24/27 - Collaborative business models for a circular economy
characteristics.Themes: Circulaire economie Sustainability Sustainable Cities Knowledge Utilisation Ruimtelijke en sociale transformaties Resilient Society Health Care Innovation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: With this Call for Proposals, we invite interdisciplinary and transdisciplinary consortia to investigate how collaborative business models can reconfigure value creation, risk sharing and financing in such a way that systemic barriers in the transition from linear to circular production systems are overcome. Three aspects are central to this: The creation of resilient business models and organisational forms for collaboration within circular production chains, networks and ecosystems. The balance of risks and (non-)financial values that this collaboration delivers. Financing models for collaborative business cases. All three aspects are closely intertwined. Research proposals should address them in a comprehensive way, but may choose to focus on one or two aspects. Business and financing models can be tested in different scenarios: in a linear economic system, during the transition phase, and in a fully circular system.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications are submitted by a main applicant and one or more co-applicants. An application is prepared by a consortium, which includes other participants in addition to the applicants. Four categories of participants in a consortium are distinguished: Main applicant Co-applicant(s) Co-funder(s) Cooperation partner(s) All participants in the consortium must play an active role in formulating the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: For this Call for proposals, NWO organises a matchmaking event on 27 January 2026 at Cultuur- en Congrescentrum Antropia (next to Driebergen-Zeist station).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The submission of a declaration of intent is mandatory for this Call for proposals in order to be eligible to submit a full proposal. By means of this declaration of intent you indicate that you intend to submit an application for this Call for proposals. The deadline for submitting declarations of intent is 24 March 2026, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for submitting full proposals is 26 May 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals will be assessed by the following four criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: matchmaking; submission of a declaration of intent; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The following conditions for submission will be assessed by NWO: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1; the main applicant has submitted a declaration of intent on the NWO website of the funding instrument before the set deadline; the applying consortium contains two or more co-funders; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; all of the required annexes and forms are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals; the budget in the application has been prepared according to the terms of this Call for proposals (using the format made available which includes the most recent rates); • the required co-financing has been correctly and fully committed through co-financing statements (see sections 3.1.2 and 3.5.6).

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: These documents are compulsory attachments to the application form: Declarations co-funding from co-funders (see paragraph 3.1.2 and 3.5.6 of the Call for proposals). A declaration needs to be filled in and signed by every co-funder (both in-cash and in-kind); Budget form; Form ‘Statements and signature’. The following attachment is compulsory if applicable: Letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (see paragraph 3.1 of the Call for proposals);

title: Knowledge-in-Action - Economic transition: the costs and benefits of (in)action
programme: KIN Kennis in ActieEconomische transitie: de kosten en baten van (n)iets doen
target_groups: Starting Researchers Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Knowledge Utilisation Interdisciplinary Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Starting Researchers Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Knowledge Utilisation Interdisciplinary Collaboration
characteristics.Research programme: KIN Kennis in ActieEconomische transitie: de kosten en baten van (n)iets doen
characteristics.Themes: Circulaire economie Sustainability Knowledge Utilisation Climate Ruimtelijke en sociale transformaties Resilient Society

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Knowledge-in-Action grant stimulates collaboration between science and practice to accelerate Economic Transition. This program brings together the costs of inaction and the benefits of action, where costs and benefits are defined broader than money alone: this call challenges societal partners and researchers to develop concrete interventions in co-creation and learning that contribute to an economy that puts wellbeing, ecology and justice at its core. Proposals can be submitted on specific sub-themes of the Knowledge-in-Action Agenda (chapter 2.2). The intended result is transformative knowledge and practical breakthroughs arising from action-oriented, cyclical processes of experimenting, researching and adjusting. This thematic call welcomes diverse approaches - from (trans)disciplinary, visual, design-oriented, entrepreneurial or very process-oriented, reflective or activist - as long as they unite practice and research to develop and share knowledge. The grant offers a unique opportunity to contribute to sustainable, just solutions for economic transition.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Who is this call for proposals for? Practitioner organisations experimenting within the desired transition Researchers who want to mobilise knowledge for transformative societal impact Pioneers who are already working on sustainable alternatives Anyone who wants to connect science and practice to drive climate transition Who can apply? Employees of knowledge institutions and societal organisations in the Kingdom of the Netherlands who want to contribute to transformative projects for economic transition. Collaboration is key. We believe that the best solutions arise when science and practice work together. Therefore, your team should consist of: at least one knowledge institution (such as universities, universities of applied sciences, university medical centres and other public and private knowledge institutions) at least one societal organisation (such as NGOs, non-profit foundations, municipalities, provinces, water authorities, safety regions, companies, self-employed persons and other public and private organisations) All project partners play an active role in developing the proposal and implementing the project. Everyone commits to the agreed time commitment and contributes their expertise.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Budget: Maximum € 50,000 per project What can be applied for: Salaries of project partners Materials and resources Temporary hiring of experts Other project activities Student assistants Not possible: Recruitment of new academic staff and basic facilities Creative approach welcome: We encourage non-traditional activities. Feel free to involve professionals from the creative sector. Desired duration: max. 9 months (longer duration must be well substantiated) Start: within 3 months of allocation More details can be found in chapter 3.1 of the call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Proposals can be submitted until 14 April 2026 14:00:00 CEST . Please note! Start submitting your proposal in time in our submission system Isaac and ensure that project partners who are not in the Isaac database register at least 10 working days before the deadline via relatiebeheer@nwo.nl . For the assessment of additional knowledge institutions, the assessment documents should be sent by e-mail to programmas@hetkin.nl no later than 15 working days before the submission deadline (i.e. no later than 24 March 2026 before 14:00:00 CET ). More information on preparation, mandatory annexes and submission requirements can be found in sections 3.3 and 3.4 of the call for proposals.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals are evaluated in terms of content on four main criteria (with sub-criteria), each of which must score satisfactorily: Relevance: Connection to Knowledge-in-Action agenda and transition questions. Quality: Clear (learning) objectives, well-founded approach, learning and reflective work process, knowledge use and diffusion, ensuring justice and sustainability. Co-creation en knowledge development: Integration of different knowledge types, active involvement of all project partners, action perspective for actors involved. Feasibility: Appropriate activities and budget, realistic timeline (duration >9 months requires substantiation), proportional resources compared to activity plan. For the full assessment criteria read section 4.2 of the call for proposals. Procedure The assessment procedure consists of the following steps: submission of the proposal consideration of the proposal pre-advice of the evaluation committee; meeting of the evaluation committee selection (if applicable); decision-making. Assessment: An external, independent assessment committee of science and practice experts evaluates proposals on their own merits with satisfactory (+1) or unsatisfactory (-1) for each sub-criterion. To receive a positive advice, each main criterion must be assessed with a sufficient rating (Sum of all sub-criteria is >0). Selection: In case of more proposals with a positive advice than available budget, additional selection will take place. For more information about the assessment see chapter 4.1 of the call for proposals.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information on the topic and the creation thereof can be found on the KIN website . There will be two online information sessions about this grant: on 9 march (11:00-12:00h) on 2 april (13:00-14:00h) You do not need to register; you can join via the link at the time of the session. This is an opportunity to ask your specific questions, listen to questions from others, or simply obtain some additional general information. Are you planning to submit a proposal? We encourage you to take a look at the LinkedIn group that was created around this topic. You can also express your interest or call upon others in this group. This way, everyone can see who wants to submit for which topic and new collaborations can potentially emerge. Information about application on external website Economic Transition: the Costs and Benefits of (In)Action - Het KIN

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The Knowledge-in-Action grant focuses on joint experimentation, investigating and learning in and with the field. Choose one sub-theme from the agenda and develop a concrete, action-oriented, transformative intervention with a clear learning objective and reflective approach that contributes to the transition. What alternatives and questions need to be explored in order to bring about the desired economic transition? Show transformative ambition: The committee is looking for projects that will bring about real change, not just optimalisation. Co-creation and knowledge development: Show how different types of knowledge come together, and how partners contribute to the proposal and its implementation. Be creative and concrete: Design-oriented, activist or creative approaches with clear impact are welcome. Write in an accessible style: Bear in mind that the committee is broadly composed of experts from different areas of science and practice.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, next to filling in the submission form, we also ask you to enclose a budget form, a signed commitment letter from all project partners and, if applicable, a signed declaration of knowledge institution without assessment and/or a de-minimis declaration.

title: KIC - Cooling needs in homes: effects, mitigation, adaptation
programme: Cooling needs in homes: effects, mitigation, adaptation Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC - Missie 24/27 -
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: Cooling needs in homes: effects, mitigation, adaptation Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC - Missie 24/27 -
characteristics.Themes: Circulaire economie Sustainability Sustainable Cities Energy Health Climate Preventie Ruimtelijke en sociale transformaties

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This Call for proposals addresses the current and future cooling demand in existing Dutch homes. The focus is on mapping this demand at the local level, identifying scalable, cost- and energy-efficient, and sustainable solutions to reduce cooling demand, and meeting the remaining demand. In doing so, the programme contributes to a comfortable and healthy indoor climate in homes. Research is needed into local heat variations, the effects of overheating, residents’ behaviour and differing cooling needs as well as the rising cooling demand across different housing types. To this end, sustainable heat mitigation and adaptation strategies for homes must be further developed and refined. An integrated approach is required, with the focus on residents, houses and the immediate surroundings. Researchers are invited to submit applications in collaboration with researchers from relevant disciplines and with relevant public and private parties.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The main applicant and co-applicant(s) must carry out the requested research within a consortium that always includes two or more co-financiers in addition to themselves, possibly supplemented by one or more collaboration partners. All participants in the consortium must play an active role in formulating the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project. Professors, university (senior) lecturers, lecturers and other researchers with a comparable position may act as main applicant or co-applicant. For the list of eligible organisations and conditions for employment, see section 3.1 of the Call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: To maximise the potential impact of the research, collaboration is required between researchers across disciplines combined with societal partners such as companies and civil society organisations. Within this Call for proposals, NWO supports consortium building by organising a matchmaking event, which takes place at the 25th of March 2026. It is encouraged to participate in the matchmaking event, however, this is not mandatory.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The total budget for this Call for Proposals is € 7.000.000. For an application, a minimum of € 600.000 and a maximum of € 2.300.000 of NWO funding can be applied for. With this, NWO finances maximally 80% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding (see Section 3.5.6). NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. In addition, the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: 25 March 2026: Live information session and matchmaking event NWO Utrecht 26 May 2026 14:00: Deadline for submitting a request for the review of foreign organisations 23 June 2026 14:00: Deadline proposals

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%); Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%); Quality of the consortium (30%); Quality of the research (30%). More information about the assessment criteria can be found in paragraph 4.3 in the Call for proposals. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: information session (optional); matchmaking (optional); submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making; An external, independent assessment committee will be assigned for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from the research field and society with knowledge of (partial aspects of) the topic.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: NWO will assess your application on all the conditions set out in this Call for proposals, including the conditions listed below. Your application will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. After submitting your application, NWO requests you to be available to implement any possible administrative corrections so that you can (still) meet the conditions for submission. These conditions are: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1 and Section 7.1.1 the applying consortium contains two or more co-funders the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; the budget in the application has been prepared according to the terms of this Call for proposals (using the format made available which includes the most recent rates); the required co-financing has been correctly and fully committed through co-financing statements (see sections 3.1.2 and 3.5.6); all of the required annexes and forms are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call you need to fill out the application form and the following compulsory annexes: budget form: this must be submitted in ISAAC as an Excel file and is set up according to the conditions of the Call for proposals; declarations co-funding from co-funders (see Section 3.1.2 and 3.5.6); letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (if applicable, see Section 3.1); form ‘Statements and signature’.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The objective of this fund is to enable research organisations to recruit top researchers and top talents from abroad who want to continue their research in the Netherlands due to growing threats to academic freedom. This will both boost and reinforce the Netherlands’ position at the forefront of international scientific research. The research carried out by these researchers will lead to scientific and social strengthening of the Netherlands and Europe. The research may be fundamental, applied or practice-oriented. There are no restrictions on the content.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Each of the research organisations in the Netherlands listed below may nominate a maximum of two candidates for each position via their board, for funding amounts of up to €1,000,000: public and private universities and the Open University; university medical centres; NWOi on behalf of NWO institutes (jointly); KNAW on behalf of institutes affiliated with KNAW (jointly). Each of the research organisations listed below may nominate a maximum of one candidate for each position via their board, for funding amounts of up to €1,000,000: Netherlands Cancer Institute; NCB Naturalis (Naturalis Biodiversity Center). The Netherlands Association of Universities of Applied Sciences can jointly nominate candidates for up to four positions, for funding amounts of up to €500,000. The boards of the research institutions should confirm the nomination by means of the required letter of submission. The technical submission in ISAAC should be made by a board member of the board of the nominating research organization, or a person mendated by this member. This person is the first point of contact for the programme.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of €1,000,000 in funding is available to the research organisation for each researcher; a maximum of €500,000 in funding is available to the research organisation for each researcher nominated by the Netherlands Association of Universities of Applied Sciences. The funding must be spent in five years. The funding must be used for the benefit of science in the Netherlands. The researcher enjoys broad freedom of spending, such as on personnel (scientific and non-scientific), equipment, investments, travel and accommodation expenses, knowledge utilization).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting nominations is provisionally set at 31 March 2026, 14:00:00 CEST . Submissions are accepted continuously.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria It should be clear from the nomination that the researcher fits within the defined target group. A top researcher is an established researcher (conducting fundamental, applied or practice-oriented research) at the stage of consolidation of their own research group. A top talent is a researcher who is ready to establish a research group or expand a recently established research group. It should be clear from the nomination that the researcher’s planned research activities are aligned with relevant strategic knowledge agendas in the Netherlands and/or the EU such as – but not confined to – strategic priorities of research organisations, NWA or KIC. Procedure The nomination procedure consists of the following steps: submission of the nomination; admissibility check of the nomination; assessment of the assessment committee; meeting of the assessment committee; rebuttal in case of a negative recommendation; decision making. For this fund, an external, independent assessment committee will be installed, consisting of representatives from science with knowledge of diverse academic fields. The task given to this assessment committee is to assess the submitted nominations and thereto pertaining documents, in light of its individual merits, based on the assessment criteria outlined in the Call for proposals.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: It is mandatory to write your nomination in English. More information can be found in the Call for proposals.

write_nomination.title: Write nomination
write_nomination.text: To write your proposal, you make use of the nomination form. This form can be downloaded from ISAAC or this webpage. The nomination form consists of (among others): A description of the quality of the researcher and their projected contribution to Dutch/European science (max. 3 pages); A description of the researcher’s 10 most important key outputs (max. 1000 words); The researcher’s CV (max. 4 pages). Furthermore, you are required to attach a letter of submission of the board of the nominating research institute. If applicable, in this letter a mandate can be given to another person to serve as the first point of contact for the nomination. Both the nomination and the letter of submission need to be written in English and make use of the NWO-supplied forms.

title: KIC AI for agriculture, horticulture, water and food 2026
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant AI for agriculture, horticulture, water and food 2026 KIC - Missie 24/27 - KIC 2024-2027
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant AI for agriculture, horticulture, water and food 2026 KIC - Missie 24/27 - KIC 2024-2027
characteristics.Themes: Biodiversity Circulaire economie Sustainability Energy Health Artificial Intelligence Key Technologies Food Water

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this research programme is to develop, apply, and expand AI methods that can contribute to finding solutions to urgent societal challenges within the agriculture, horticulture, water, and food sectors. AI technology can support these sectors, for example in improving and making production in agriculture and horticulture more sustainable, improving water management, allocation, and safety, and ensuring a healthy, nutritious, and safe food supply. The focus of research proposals within this programme can vary from the application and further development of existing AI algorithms to demonstrating a proof-of-principle that is relevant to the agriculture, horticulture, water, and food sectors. This programme also emphasizes the importance of considering relevant societal aspects and involving relevant stakeholders and end users.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period*) or a tenure track agreement at a research organisation. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants two or more co-funders, and potentially one or more cooperation partners, are involved as well. All consortiumpartners should play an active role in the formulation of the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project. For more information: see section in the Call for Proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In the period prior to the deadline for submitting the proposal, NWO facilitates a matchmaking event for this Call for proposals. Participation to this event is strongly recommended but is not compulsory to submitting an application. The matchmaking events in the KIC aim to bring together and connect researchers from different scientific disciplines and persons from societal organisations and companies.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The budget for this research programme is 7 million euro. NWO will fund each project within this programme with a minimum of 600,000 and a maximum of 1,840,000 euro. With this, NWO finances maximally 80% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding (in kind and/or in cash). Successful projects will have a maximum duration of six years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The matchmaking event will take place on 2 April 2026 . The deadline for submitting a statement of intent 30 June 2026. The deadline for submitting a full proposal is 13 October 2026 . The deadline for a request to review foreign organisations is 29 September 2026 .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The full proposal is assessed on the basis of four criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%), Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%). Procedure After submitting a proposal it is first assessed by referees. The applicant will be given the opportunity to write a rebuttal on the reports of the referees. On the basis of the proposal, the referee reports, and the rebuttal the assessment committee selects consortia for an interview. After the interview, the assessment committee advices NWO on the ranking of the research proposal.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The application should indicate how the proposed research answers the Call for proposals, which societal problem/challenge the project addresses, as well as what the goals, hypotheses, research methods and scientific feasibility of the project are. Also, in the proposal needs to be described how the consortium is built up, and how the research is carried out to aid in the application of the research results. The Impact Plan approach needs to be applied and described in the proposal.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In this program, obligatory annexes to a research proposal are: budget; declaration of cofunding; a ‘statements and signature’ form. If applicable, a guarantee for continuity in the project management (see section 3.1 in the call for proposals).

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The SSH Open Competition XS Call for proposals is specifically intended to encourage curiosity-driven and innovative research that involves the relatively rapid exploration of a promising idea. The characteristics of SSH-XS research are: the proposed research is innovative; it is not certain in advance whether the intended objectives will be achieved; the result of each project contributes to the advancement of existing science and new scientific insights.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers with a promising idea who obtained their PhD at least 5 years ago may submit an application if they have a paid appointment at one of the organisations mentioned in paragraph 3.1 of the Call for Proposals for at least the duration of the application process and the duration of the proposed project. The submission deadline for the round is the reference date of the graduation date. The graduation date on the PhD diploma (degree) is leading. Additional conditions: an SSH-XS application has a single applicant and no co-applicants; an applicant may submit a maximum of one application within the NWO/ZonMw Open Competition programme in the same calendar year (2026); applicants may not have an NWO OC XS project at the time of submission for which the end date has not yet passed; principal and co-applicants for an NWO/ZonMw Open Competition project other than an XS may submit an application no more than one year before the planned end date of their project.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals a maximum of 50,000 euro can be applied for. The minimum duration of the proposed project is 6 months and the maximum duration is 24 months. The institution where the project is carried out can make a (financial) contribution to the project. If this is done, a statement exceeding maximum funding must be submitted. Applicants are required to draw up a realistic budget and explain why the spending choices are relevant to the project. This explanation must cover all of the personnel requested. Please only apply for funding that is vital to realise the project. A more detailed explanation of the budget modules can be found in the annexe to this Call for proposals (Section 7.1).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: For 2026, three deadlines for submitting applications are specified: Round 1: 20 January 2026, before 14:00:00 CET - Closed . Round 2: 7 April 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST. Round 3: 15 September 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST. When you submit your application in ISAAC, you will also need to enter some details online. Therefore, please start submitting your application at least one day before the deadline of the round. Applications that are submitted after the deadline will not be taken into consideration.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applicants assess each other’s proposal through anonymous peer review. The assessors who rank the applications will focus on the following criteria. These criteria are not weighted. At the discretion of an assessor, both of these aspects may be used for the purposes of ranking. However, that ranking must be accompanied by a short written explanation to substantiate the given rank based on the assessment criteria listed below. Innovative aspect Is the proposed research innovative and groundbreaking? Does the proposed research contribute to existing and new scientific insights? Does the research proposal potentially have a scientific impact? Feasibility of the research plan The objective and work plan must be clearly described. Does the methodology seem suitable to achieve the indicated project goal? Is the planning of the research plan appropriate and are the activities included in the planning with sufficient clarity? Does the expertise of the research team  appear appropriate for the proposed research Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the selected panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria described above. The distribution of disciplines across the panels can be found in annex 7.3 of the Call for Proposals. The assessors may award a lower ranking to any applications that are drawn up in a way that is not accessible to those in other disciplines. Procedure Applications are divided into three panels. The applicant determines by which panel the application will be processed by means of the information form (see Section 4.2.4 of the Call for proposals). Applications processed per panel are divided into 2 groups, so that each panel consists of Group A and Group B. In principle, the classification takes place at the time of submission to NWO, taking into account the personal interests declared by the applicants. The assessors and applicants associated with a particular package of proposals do not meet. NWO will decide the final rankings of the applications, based on the rankings submitted by the assessors. Per round, 6 final rankings will be established – per panel one for Group A and one for Group B. There will be no opportunity to dispute the rankings. The SSH Domain Board will base its decision about awarding or rejecting the applications on the results of the 6 final rankings, the assessment reports and the available funding.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Frequently asked questions about the XS programme of the SSH Open Competition

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The research idea needs to be drawn up anonymously, which means the application form meets the following conditions: applicants should not include their own name nor the name of any individuals in their research group; applicants should not include details of any affiliations with institutes or knowledge institutions; applicants should not include references to any publications that they have authored or co-authored; applicants should not include the names of any (potential) partners. applicants do not mention any other information on the basis of which they can be identified. Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the selected panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Compulsory annexes to a proposal in this Call for proposals are the Budget form (Excel) and the Information form (pdf). If applicable, a statement exceeding maximum funding has to be submitted as well.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: For 2026, three deadlines for submitting applications are specified: Round 1: 20 January 2026, before 14:00:00 CET. Round 2: 7 April 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST. Round 3: 15 September 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST. When you submit your application in ISAAC, you will also need to enter some details online. Therefore, please start submitting your application at least one day before the deadline of the round. Applications that are submitted after the deadline will not be taken into consideration.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applicants assess each other’s proposal through anonymous peer review. The assessors who rank the applications will focus on the following criteria. These criteria are not weighted. At the discretion of an assessor, both of these aspects may be used for the purposes of ranking. However, that ranking must be accompanied by a short written explanation to substantiate the given rank based on the assessment criteria listed below. Innovative aspect Is the proposed research innovative and groundbreaking? Does the proposed research contribute to existing and new scientific insights? Does the research proposal potentially have a scientific impact? Feasibility of the research plan The objective and work plan must be clearly described. Does the methodology seem suitable to achieve the indicated project goal? Is the planning of the research plan appropriate and are the activities included in the planning with sufficient clarity? Does the expertise of the research team  appear appropriate for the proposed research Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the selected panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria described above. The distribution of disciplines across the panels can be found in annex 7.3 of the Call for Proposals. The assessors may award a lower ranking to any applications that are drawn up in a way that is not accessible to those in other disciplines. Procedure Applications are divided into three panels. The applicant determines by which panel the application will be processed by means of the information form (see Section 4.2.4 of the Call for proposals). Applications processed per panel are divided into 2 groups, so that each panel consists of Group A and Group B. In principle, the classification takes place at the time of submission to NWO, taking into account the personal interests declared by the applicants. The assessors and applicants associated with a particular package of proposals do not meet. NWO will decide the final rankings of the applications, based on the rankings submitted by the assessors. Per round, 6 final rankings will be established – per panel one for Group A and one for Group B. There will be no opportunity to dispute the rankings. The SSH Domain Board will base its decision about awarding or rejecting the applications on the results of the 6 final rankings, the assessment reports and the available funding.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Frequently asked questions about the XS programme of the SSH Open Competition

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The research idea needs to be drawn up anonymously, which means the application form meets the following conditions: applicants should not include their own name nor the name of any individuals in their research group; applicants should not include details of any affiliations with institutes or knowledge institutions; applicants should not include references to any publications that they have authored or co-authored; applicants should not include the names of any (potential) partners. applicants do not mention any other information on the basis of which they can be identified. Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the selected panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Compulsory annexes to a proposal in this Call for proposals are the Budget form (Excel) and the Information form (pdf). If applicable, a statement exceeding maximum funding has to be submitted as well.
