title: KIC System design in mechatronics and optomechatronics
programme: KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027 System design in mechatronics and optomechatronics
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027 System design in mechatronics and optomechatronics

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This Call for proposals aims to stimulate the optimisation of the design of (opto)mechatronic (OMT) systems. These systems contribute to the development and construction of high-tech instruments and machines that can address numerous societal challenges, such as improving the quality of healthcare and the efficiency of production processes. Researchers are invited to submit proposals for interdisciplinary research that combines fundamental and practice-oriented approaches. Applicants are encouraged to form broad consortia with partners from various sectors, including government institutions, TO2 institutes, societal organizations, and companies. This collaboration enhances the societal relevance and applicability of the research.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants other participants are involved as well. Four categories of participants can be distinguished in the consortium: Main applicant Co-applicant(s) Co-funder(s) Cooperation partner(s) A consortium must consist of at least one main applicant, one co-applicant, and two co-funders, possibly supplemented by one or more cooperation partners. The conditions for each participant are explained in more detail in the following sections. All consortium partners must play an active role in formulating the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: On 11 December 2025, NWO will organize a matchmaking event for this Call for Proposals. Participation in this activity is recommended but not mandatory

apply_for.title: Apply for
apply_for.text: For a proposal submitted in this Call for proposals, applicants can request a minimum of €600,000 and a maximum of €3,200,000 in NWO funding. NWO will fund up to 80% of the total project budget; the remaining portion must be covered through mandatory co-funding (see section 3.5.6). NWO will never fund less than 50% of the total project budget. Applicants and co-applicants may include costs for personnel, materials, investments, knowledge utilization, and project management.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting proposals is 21 April 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting a request for the assessment of foreign organizations (see section 7.1.1) is 7 April 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The pre-proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: matchmaking; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making. An external, independent assessment committee will be assigned for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from the research field and society with knowledge of (partial aspects of) the topic.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: These conditions are: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1 and Section 7.1.1; the applying consortium contains two or more co-funders; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; all of the required annexes and forms are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals; the budget in the application has been prepared according to the terms of this Call for proposals (using the format made available which includes the most recent rates); the required co-financing has been correctly and fully committed through co-financing statements.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Compulsory annexes: budget form; declarations co-funding from co-funders (see Section 3.1.2 and 3.5.6); form ‘Statement and signature’. letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (if applicable, see Section 3.1 of the Call for Proposals); confirmation of own contribution to investments (only if applicable, see section 7.1 of the Call for Proposals); Knowledge security statement. The proposal and the appendix(es) must be drawn up in accordance with the template provided by NWO. Annexes must be uploaded in ISAAC separately from the application. All of the annexes, except for the budget, must be submitted as PDF files (without encryption). The budget must be submitted in ISAAC as an Excel file. When submitting the proposal, the attached declarations co-funding should guarantee the full required amount of co-funding in line with the conditions of Section 3.5.6. Any annexes other than those above-mentioned are not permitted.

title: NWO Talent Programme | Vidi - Applied and Engineering Sciences (AES) 2025

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the NWO Talent Programme is to provide creative space for adventurous, talented, pioneering researchers to do research of their choice, develop their own line of research and further develop their talent.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications can be submitted by candidates who have obtained their PhD, irrespective of whether they hold tenured positions and of whether they come from the Netherlands or abroad. Candidates may only submit an application if they have obtained their PhD in the eight years preceding the benchmark date of this round, 1 October 2025. An extension to the above career-dependent submission period may be possible under specific circumstances (see Section 3.4.2 of the Call for proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of € 850,000 can be applied for within this Call for proposals. The maximum duration of the proposed project is five years. If the proposed research is of shorter duration, the maximum amount will be reduced accordingly. It is also possible to carry out the research part-time without extending the duration of the project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting preproposals is 4 november 2025, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for submitting full propoals is 9 april 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The preproposal submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criterion: Quality of the researcher. The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%); Scientific and/or societal impact (25%). Procedure submission of the preproposal; admissibility check of the preproposal; assessment of the preproposal; provisional advice assessment committee; possibility of filing a request for the correction of a factual error; decision-making preproposal; submission of the full proposal (only possible if you have received a positive decision on the preproposal); admissibility check of the full proposal; assessment of the full proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; provisional advice assessment committee; interview selection (ZonMw only); interview and meeting of the assessment committee; decision-making.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Embedding guarantee Since 2019 on NWO has asked candidates for an 'embedding guarantee' from the start of the application round for NWO Talent Programme Vidi funding. Researchers can only submit applications for Vidi funding with the support of the Dutch institution where they intend to conduct their research. It is expected to lead to fewer applications and to be more consistent with the research institutions’ personnel policy. The embedding guarantee format can be found under ‘Prepare’. Optional literature list As of 2023, the Vidi uses an optional literature list of max. ten pieces of relevant scientific literature closely related to your research idea. NWO will use the literature list - along with your research idea, key words and specified discipline(s) - to search for potential external reviewers for your full proposal. Your literature list will not be submitted to the committee. Frequently asked questions On this page you will find an overview of the most frequently asked questions about the NWO Talent Programme, including questions about the set time limit to submit a proposal (and any exceptions to it) and the embedding guarantee: FAQ | NWO

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The preproposal has to be written in English and contains an evidence-based curriculum vitae of the applicant, consisting of: a description of the applicant’s academic profile (1200 words); a description of the applicant’s key output (minimum 400 and maximum 700 words); short research idea (max. 150 words). NWO has recently updated the instruction for writing an EBCV . The updated instruction can be found in the application form for the Vidi. We would also like to draw your attention to the existence of an international digital platform to support narrative style CV writing ( PEP-CV ). The full proposal has to be written in English and contains: a description of the research proposal, a motivation for the choice of institution, and a description of the fit between the research proposal and the applicant’s academic profile (maximum 4000 words including in-text references); a section on scientific and/or societal impact (maximum 1000 words); a section on data management; and a budget form.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Mandatory annex to the preproposal: embedding guarantee. Optional attachment to the preproposal: literature list for referees. mandatory annex to the full proposal: not applicable. Optional attachment to the full proposal (see section 3.5.6): declaration co-funding; statement exceeding maximum funding.

Applications from the Earth Sciences, Astronomy, Chemistry, Computer Science, Life Sciences, Physics and Mathematics will be submitted to the Science domain. Candidates will therefore be interviewed and assessed by committee members from their own discipline and other disciplines in the Science domain.

title: NWA - Economic transformation in the Province of South Holland: space for an economy that supports broad prosperity
target_groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Interdisciplinary Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Interdisciplinary Collaboration
characteristics.Themes: Circulaire economie Cybersecurity Diversity and Inclusion Sustainability Sustainable Cities Energy Health Knowledge Utilisation Climate Artificial Intelligence Education Ruimtelijke en sociale transformaties Key Technologies Resilient Society Safety Water

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: In the province of South Holland is a need for insight and frameworks for action to accommodate economic structural change. Despite the province's status as one of Europe's most innovative economies, its growth rate lags behind similar areas. Moreover, not everyone benefits from the (broad) prosperity, and the distribution of scarce space in the province leads to complex trade-offs between economic, social and ecological interests. In order to work towards a sustainable, future-proof and competitive economy, South Holland faces the challenge of making tough choices at the intersection of economy, space and broad prosperity. The purpose of this call is to take an integrated approach to the issues and come to an actionable perspective on transformation that is widely supported.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The consortium should consist of at least one main applicant, one co-applicant and one collaboration partner. Main applicant Researchers may act as main applicants if they are permanently employed (and therefore have a paid employment contract for an indefinite period of time*) or have a tenure track agreement with one of the research organisations listed in the Call for proposals. Co-applicants Two types of co-applicants are possible in this Call for proposals: Co-applicants affiliated to research organisations; Co-applicants affiliated to civil society organisations. Co-applicants linked to civil society organisations can only act as co-applicants under application of the de minimis regulation (see the Call for proposals, section 3.2). Cooperation partners Cooperation partners are mandatory as partners in the consortium within this Call for proposals, because active involvement of civil society stakeholders is of great importance in developing knowledge about challenges and possible solutions. Societal stakeholders include both public and private organisations, and where relevant also citizens or a representation thereof. The organisation of a societal stakeholder is not a main or co-applicant or co-financier. Co-funders Co-funding is possible but not mandatory within this Call for proposals. Co-financiers are organisations that participate in the consortium and contribute cash and/or in kind to the project. Assessment of research organisations In this Call, it is possible for atypical research organisations to participate as main applicants (for national knowledge institutions ’RKI’s’) or co-applicants (for RKI’s and other research organisations). An assessment is mandatory for this. More information can be found in the Call for proposals, section 3.2.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: This Call for proposals deals with complex and challenging societal issues. Research into answers to these issues requires collaboration, for example between different research disciplines, practical and fundamental research, and with civil society organisations and companies. NWO encourages and supports the formation of these collaborations by organising consortium formation meetings or workshops. In this Call for proposals, consortium formation is stimulated by organising a Sandpit. The aim of a Sandpit is to establish cooperation in new collaborations and joint research proposals. Sandpits focus on collaboration in a single consortium consisting of the selected parties. During a Sandpit, the selected participants work jointly on site to develop a research project, under the guidance of external facilitators and content supporters. For participation in a Sandpit, selection takes place through an Expression of Interest. Only individuals invited by the NWO Executive Board can participate in the Sandpit.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Within this programme, a maximum of €2,800,000 of NWO grant can be requested for the proposal. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 5 years. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. The available budget modules (including the maximum amounts) are listed in the Call for proposals, section 3.3. The rates and an explanation of the budget modules can be found in section 7.1.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting Expressions of Interest is Thursday April 9th before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting the full proposal is Tuesday January 12 before 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Substantive assessment criteria for Expressions of Interest Participants (researchers, civil society actors and companies) are subject to the evaluation criteria as specified below: Scientific/ professional expertise (50%) Cooperation skills (50%) Substantive assessment criteria Proposal The application submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%), Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%). More information on the substantive assessment criteria can be found in the Call for proposals. Assessment For this NWA Call for proposals, an external, independent assessment committee will be appointed by the NWO Executive Board. Its members will be selected from the scientific community and the entire knowledge chain, including societal stakeholders and representatives from the target group, with expertise in the field. The assessment committee's task is to assess the Expressions of Interest and the proposal submitted on the basis of the assessment criteria. Each Expression of Interest and the proposal will be assessed on its own merits. Procedure The Sandpit method is applied in this Call for Proposals. To this end, interested individuals must submit an Expression of Interest. The Expressions of Interest will be assessed by the assessment committee, after which a decision on participation in the Sandpit will be taken by the NWO Executive Board. During the Sandpit, the participants will form a consortium and lay the foundations for the proposal. The proposal will be submitted to NWO and assessed by the assessment committee. An interview is part of the procedure.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: NWO is organising an information session on Monday 16 March 2026, between 14:00 and 15:00 CET, to explain the purpose of this Call for proposals and the procedure. You can register via the programme page.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Expression of Interest In an Expression of Interest, a potential participant in the Sandpit provides a concise description of their scientific/professional expertise and their competencies relevant to the Sandpit and the theme of the Call for Proposals. It is not required to formulate a research idea at this stage. The Expression of Interest will be assessed by the assessment committee. Proposal In the application, the consortium formulates the research proposal and the expected impact/route to impact using the Impact Plan Approach.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: This programme includes a Sandpit workshop. The aim of the Sandpit is to establish a new partnership, resulting in a single joint proposal. The Sandpit emphasises collaboration in a consortium to be formed, consisting of participants selected on the basis of the Expressions of Interest submitted (see section 4.1). During the Sandpit, the participants work together on site to develop the application, under the guidance of facilitators and content support staff hired by NWO. Participation is mandatory for the main applicant of the proposal.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, we ask you to complete and submit different forms for each phase. Phase 1 - Expression of Interest Expression of Interest form. Phase 3 - Proposal In addition to completing the proposal form, we also ask you to submit: budget; statements of support from cooperation partner(s); statement from co-funder(s) (mandatory if applicable); guarantee for continuity in project supervision (mandatory if applicable); de-minimis aid statement (mandatory if applicable); Statement and signature form.

downloads.name: NWA - Economic transformation in the Province of South Holland | call for proposal (in Dutch)

title: Restoration of ecosystem functioning, integrity, and connectivity (BiodivConnect)
programme: Rescuing Biodiversity to Safeguard Life on Earth
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad
characteristics.Research programme: Rescuing Biodiversity to Safeguard Life on Earth

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The European partnership ‘Rescuing Biodiversity to Safeguard Life on Earth’ (Biodiversa+) under Horizon Europe aims to contribute to bringing biodiversity in Europe back on a path of recovery by 2030. The partnership provides an overarching platform connecting national/local and European research and innovation programs and combines in-cash and in-kind resources, in line with the European Green Deal and the new EU Biodiversity Strategy for 2030. The fifth joint call of the partnership focuses on the topic ‘Restoration of ecosystem functioning, integrity, and connectivity (BiodivConnect)’. This call is focused on fostering innovative research to be integrated into nature restoration practices for interconnected and well-functioning ecosystems and habitats, with special consideration for long-term sustainability and future-proofing of restoration efforts, at scales from local to regional to cross-border and global, including their evaluation. The call covers restoration efforts for biodiversity in all types of ecosystems and habitats, in all regions of the world, supporting high-quality research and innovation projects on biodiversity and ecosystem restoration with holistic, systemic, and integrated approaches. To adequately address the key challenges of nature restoration, it is essential to support knowledge exchange and capacity building across disciplines and societal sectors, and acknowledge, appreciate and integrate multiple forms of knowledge. This call is arranged into three main topics that partly overlap. Project proposals can address one or more of these main topics. Topic 1: Setting restoration targets and measuring success Topic 2: Transferability and scaling of nature restoration efforts Topic 3: Resilience and sustainability of restoration efforts

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: An application for funding from NWO may consider the following roles: National main applicant (mandatory): the applicant who leads the application to NWO and is the foreseen national project leader, in case a project is granted. National co-applicant (optional): national applicants in addition to the national main applicant with an active role and responsibility in realising the project and requesting funding from NWO. Applications need to be submitted by research consortia including teams from a minimum of three countries participating in the call (including a minimum of two from EU Member States or EU Associated Countries). See the Biodiversa+ Call Documents for details and participating countries. Full, associate and assistant professors, lectors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application (i.e. participate in a consortium and request NWO funding) if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the institutions listed in Funding Organisation rules (part NWO) of the call for proposals. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal. An applicant may only request NWO funding for one project (part of a European consortium) in this call of the European Biodiversity Partnership. More information on the transnational eligibility criteria, and the eligibility criteria of other countries can be found in the Biodiversa+ Call Documents and Funding Organisation Rules.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For a research proposal in this round, a maximum of €325,000 can be applied for. The maximum project duration is 3 years. No limit has been set on the total international consortium size (budget or partners), the national funding agencies solely limit the budget that you can apply for at the national level. At NWO this maximum is €325,000. The budget modules that are available within this call for proposals are: Postdoc, Research leave, Personnel universities of applied sciences, TO2 institutes and other research organisations and students, Material Costs, Knowledge Utilisation and Internationalisation. The maximum amounts for each module are stated in the Funding Organisation rules (part NWO) in the Call Documents. For the budget module “Postdoc”, a one-off individual bench fee of €5,000 is added on top of the salary costs to encourage the scientific career of the project employee funded by NWO. An explanation of the budget modules can be found with the ‘Downloads for your application’ under the heading 'Prepare'.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline for submitting a pre-proposal (compulsory) is November 7th, 2025 at 12:00 CET . Please note: Prior to the submission of an application, NWO assesses on the basis of the above-mentioned conditions whether an organisation complies with Article 1.1, paragraph 4 of the NWO Grant Rules 2024 and may therefore participate as a national co-applicant. NWO performs this assessment to preclude the granting of prohibited state aid. The organisation of the prospective national co-applicant must provide the necessary documents no less than 10 working days prior to the submission deadline for pre-proposals (meaning no later than 24 October 2025, 12:00:00 CET) by email to biodiversa@nwo.nl Deadline for submitting a full proposal is April 14th, 2026 at 12:00 CEST . Proposals need to be submitted through the electronic submission system of Biodiversa+. More information can be found in the Biodiversa+ Call Documents. NB Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit the completed ‘NWO budget template’ preferably at the latest April 7th, 2026 separately to NWO when uploading their full proposal. The format for this form is available with the ‘Downloads for your application’ under the heading 'Prepare'.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Fit to the scope of the call Novelty of the research Impact Full proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Excellence; Quality and efficiency of the implementation; Impact. Please see the full Biodiversa+ Call Documents for the criteria including the sub-criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage consortia are invited to submit a pre-proposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage can submit a full proposal. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed by an independent Evaluation Committee consisting both of scientific and policy/management experts. The proposals will be ranked based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. At NWO the Executive Board will take this decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information can be found on the Biodiversa+ website Submitting an application can be done via the electronic submission system of Biodiversa+. Contact persons at partners Clara Superbie, Céline Billière and Sophie Germann Biodiversa+ Call Secretariat biodiversa.cs@agencerecherche.fr

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Applicants are required to submit a mandatory NWO budget form before the deadline for full proposals, preferably April 7th, 2026 at the latest. The completed budget form can be submitted to NWO via biodiversa@nwo.nl .

title: KIC Reducing critical raw materials use in the energy transition
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC - Missie 24/27 - Reducing critical raw materials use in the energy transition
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC - Missie 24/27 - Reducing critical raw materials use in the energy transition
characteristics.Themes: Circulaire economie Energy Resilient Society Safety

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The energy transition requires a rapid upscaling of technologies such as wind turbines, electrolysers, solar panels, and batteries. This transition currently depends on the availability of so-called ‘critical raw materials’, which are necessary to utilize these technologies. The aim of this program is to reduce the dependence on these scarce and geopolitically risky critical raw materials for the energy transition. As a result, this theme will contribute to greater certainty in the availability of materials and the progress of the energy transition by redesigning (parts of) the entire value chain. The focus is on reducing or replacing critical materials in the value chain. For this program, we consider both critical raw materials and strategic raw materials to be relevant, as both groups of materials face the same societal challenge. Specifically, we follow the most recent (2023) definition and lists from the CRMA to determine which materials belong to these groups.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The main applicant can submit the application together with one or more co-applicants. The public-private partnership consortium prepares the application, which involves not only the applicants but also other participants. The main and co-applicant(s) may carry out the proposed research within a consortium that includes, in addition to themselves, at least two or more co-financiers, possibly supplemented by one or more collaboration partners. All consortium partners are expected to play an active role in formulating the research questions as well as in the design and implementation of the project.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: NWO supports the formation of a consortium by organising a matchmaking meeting. The aim of matchmaking is to bring together potential applicants, researchers, collaboration partners and stakeholders from various fields and sectors to exchange ideas for, or needs for, research on the theme and impact described in the Call for Proposals. For this call, the matchmaking meeting will take place on 29 January 2026. Participation in this matchmaking is recommended but not mandatory.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of €750,000 and a maximum of €1,300,000 of NWO funding can be applied for in. With this, NWO finances maximally 80% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding. NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. The applicant and co-applicants may include costs for personnel, equipment, investments, knowledge utilisation and project management. The available budget modules (including the maximum amounts) are listed in paragraph 3.2 of the call text. Apply only for funding that is vital to realise the project.

line_of_research.title: Line of Research
line_of_research.text: This Call for Proposals requests proposals that focus on reducing the use of critical raw materials in the following areas of application of the energy transition: Electrolysis devices and electrolysis plants Wind turbines Photovoltaic energy Battery systems Other areas relevant to the energy transition, such as network infrastructure such as cables and transformers, power electronics, or the production of alternative fuels, etc. Applicants are asked to focus primarily on one area of application. Sufficient funding is available for at least one project in each of the five areas of application.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting full proposals is Tuesday 14 April 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications that NWO receives for this call will be assessed based on the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: matchmaking (not mandatory); submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; site visit; assessment committee meeting; decision-making

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: In your application, please describe how your proposed research aligns with the objectives of this call for proposals. Clearly outline the societal challenge your project aims to address, along with its goals, hypotheses, research methods, and scientific feasibility. We also invite you to showcase how your consortium is composed and how the partners will collaborate to maximize the practical relevance and impact of the research outcomes. To support this, the proposal should follow and describe the Impact Plan approach, ensuring a thoughtful strategy for translating research into realworld solutions.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call we ask you to fill in the application form and the following annexes: Mandatory: budget form; declaration co-funding: This template needs to be filled in and signed by each co-financer individually; form ‘Statements and signatures’. If applicable: letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (see paragraph 3.1 of the Call for proposals).

title: KIC Digital Twinning – Actual and accurate models for insight, optimalisation, and decision-making
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Digital Twinning: actual and accurate models for insight, optimisation, and decision-making
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Digital Twinning: actual and accurate models for insight, optimisation, and decision-making
characteristics.Themes: Artificial Intelligence Key Technologies

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This Call for Proposals focuses on the development of knowledge for Digital Twins and their application. The application of Digital Twins can enable people to better understand complex processes and gain insights for improvement, which increases productivity and promotes decision-making.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: This Call for proposals is open to proposals by researchers working at universities, universities of applied science, university medical center, and other knowledge institutions in the Netherlands. A consortium must contain at least one lead applicant, one co-applicant, and two cofunders, with the possible addition of one or more cooperation partners.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In the period prior to the deadline for submitting the proposal, NWO facilitates a matchmaking event for this Call for proposals. Participation to this event is strongly recommended but is not compulsory to submitting an application. Upon registration for the matchmaking it is possible to submit a research idea. The matchmaking events in the KIC aim to bring together and connect researchers from different scientific disciplines and persons from societal organisations and companies.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals one can request between € 1.500.000 and € 2.200.000 in funding from NWO. With this budget, NWO finances at most 80% of the total project budget; the remained of the budget must be incorporated through the obligatory cofounding. The applicant and co-applicants can submit for costs of personnel, materials, investment, knowledge utilization, and project management.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The matchmaking event will take place on February 3rd 2026 . The deadline for submiƫng a full proposal is April 14th 2026 . The deadline for a request to review foreign organisations is M arch 17th 2026 .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The full proposal is assessed on the basis of four criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure After submitting a proposal it is first assessed by referees. The applicant will be given the opportunity to write a rebuttal on the reports of the referees. On the basis of the proposal, the referee reports, and the rebuttal the assessment committee selects consortia for an interview. After the interview, the assessment committee advices NWO on the ranking of the research proposal.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: NWO will verify whether your application satisfies the following conditions: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1; the applying consortium contains two or more co-funders; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; all of the required annexes and forms are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals; the budget in the application has been prepared according to the terms of this Call for proposals (using the format made available which includes the most recent rates); the required co-financing has been correctly and fully committed through co-financing statements (see Sections 3.1.2 and 3.5.6).

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In this program, obligatory annexes to a research proposal are: budget; declaration of cofunding; declaration knowledge security; a ‘statements and signature’ form.

title: Driving Urban Transitions Call 2025
programme: Driving Urban Transitions to a sustainable future (DUT) Driving Urban Transitions to a sustainable future (DUT)
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration
characteristics.Research programme: Driving Urban Transitions to a sustainable future (DUT) Driving Urban Transitions to a sustainable future (DUT)

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The purpose of this Call for proposals is to support transnational research and/or innovation projects addressing urban challenges to help cities in their transition towards a more sustainable economy and functioning. Research projects which can be financed in this call are related to the following three transition pathways and in total seven topics: Circular Urban Economies (CUE) CUE topic 1 - Resilient green and social infrastructure CUE topic 3 - Public procurement to foster urban greening and circularity 15mC topic 2 - Reimagining parking – transforming urban parking policies 15mC topic 3 - Mobility policymaking in context of radical contestation PED topic 1 - Driving a just transition: PED strategies in social and subsidised housing PED topic 2 - Ensuring positive socio-economic impact: PEDs in local economies and energy markets PED topic 3 - PEDs in urban heating and cooling All consortia are asked to choose a main transition pathway and may indicate which other pathways are involved, if any.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The following countries will participate in this DUT call: Austria, Belgium, Croatia, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, The Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Republic of Korea, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Tunisia and Türkiye. Applications need to be submitted by research consortia involving, at least, three partners from three countries on this list that are eligible for funding by their respective Funding Agency. In addition, at least two eligible Applicants within a consortium must be from different EU Member States or Associated Countries eligible for EC co-funding in this Call. The added value of transnational collaboration should be clearly stated in the application. For applicants from Dutch universities and knowledge institutions, funding is available from NWO. Specifically for applicants from universities of applied sciences, funding is available from Taskforce for Applied Research SIA (Regieorgaan SIA). Please see Annex A of the Call for Proposals for details for both possibilities.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For research proposals in this round, consortia can apply for a maximum of € 325,000 in funding from NWO. Please note that funding from NWO is only available for strategic research within the framework of this call for proposals (for further explanation, see page 10-11 and Annex A in the call for proposals).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The submission deadline for pre-proposals is 17 November 2025, at 13:00 CET . The submission deadline for full proposals is 23 april 2026, at 13:00 CEST . For more information about the submission process, see the step “Prepare” on this website and the DUT call for proposals.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals and full proposals will be assessed according to specific evaluation criteria by using a common evaluation form. The criteria are: Excellence - Intellectual merit; Impact and user engagement Quality and efficiency of project implementation. Please see the DUT Call for proposals for the full criteria, including the sub-criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage, consortia are invited to submit a preproposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage will be invited to submit a full proposal. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed based on the criteria in the call text by an independent evaluation committee consisting of academics as well as practitioners and innovators. The proposals will be ranked in six ranking lists (based on Transition Pathway and research approach). Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. For NWO, the Executive Board will take this decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: For more information about the DUT partnership and the DUT Call 2025, please visit: https://dutpartnership.eu/dut-call-2025/ DUT Call secretariat: Call@dutpartnership.eu

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For this Call for proposals, a two-stage procedure will be adopted. In the first stage, consortia are invited to submit pre-proposals. A pre-proposal includes a description of the partners, concise project description of no more than 12 pages, and budget indications with limited details fulfilling national/regional requirements. The pre-proposals declared to be eligible according to the transnational and national/regional criteria will be assessed by an Expert Panel, consisting of recognised international experts in relevant fields. The decision for the invitation to submit a full proposal in the second stage will be jointly taken by the participating funding agencies.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Pre-proposals and full proposals must be prepared in English using the designated mandatory international proposal forms. These forms can be found on the DUT website and are linked through the Call for proposals. Applicants who apply for funding at NWO must submit a completed NWO budget form separately at NWO when submitting their full proposal (phase 2). This form can be found under ‘Downloads for your application’. Please send this form to: dutp@nwo.nl .

title: Scientific Meetings and Consultations 2026
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Starting Researchers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: NWO perceives a duty to encourage national and international knowledge networks as well as to promote all forms of monodisciplinary, interdisciplinary and transdisciplinary collaboration, along with collaboration throughout the knowledge chain and knowledge-building, as also indicated in NWO’s 2023-2026 strategy, ‘Science Works!’. The aim of the Scientific Meetings and Consultations programme is to promote academic networking and collaboration for scientific research within the exact sciences and physics, in a national or international context. This may also be in collaboration with other scientific domains. The programme encourages an exchange of research and ideas, as well as the formation of research communities. Through this low-threshold scheme, researchers can apply for financial support to organise (cross-disciplinary) scientific meetings and consultations for the formation and development of new teams and consortia.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Full, associate and assistant professors, other researchers with a comparable position, postdocs, and PhD candidates may submit an application if they have a paid position, for at least until the meeting or consultation for which funding is requested, at one of the following organisations (see extra conditions in section 3.1 of the Call for proposals): Universities as referred to in Article 1.8(1) of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act and universities listed in the Policy Rules for Universities in the Kingdom of the Netherlands University medical centres, by which is meant teaching hospitals as referred to in Article 1.13(1) of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act Institutes affiliated with the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO Netherlands Cancer Institute Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen Naturalis Biodiversity Center Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL) Princess Máxima Centre

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For a scientific meeting, a maximum contribution of € 15,000 is available. The maximum remuneration per participant is € 60. This must be matched by the organisers of the scientific meeting with at least € 25 per participant. For a scientific consultation, a maximum contribution of € 2,400 is available. The maximum remuneration per participant is € 60. A maximum of €15,000 is available for a scientific meeting and a maximum of €2,400 is available for a scientific consultation, with a maximum grant amount per eligible participant of €60. The organisation of the scientific meeting or consultation must match the amount of NWO funding requested by at least €25 ‘in cash’ per eligible participant. Participants eligible for funding are participants in the following categories: - Academic institutions (full, associate and assistant professors, postdocs and PhD students) Universities of applied sciences Business community Government Civil society The funding for both scientific meetings and consultations is intended for facilitation purposes only. This means that NWO will contribute only to costs relating to: Rental of facilities (room, studio, technical resources, computer, beamer) Accommodation of participants (coffee, tea, lunch, overnight stay) Travel and accommodation of guest speakers Setup and use of a digital platform on which all or part of the conference takes place, including recording costs

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Proposals for Scientific Meetings and Consultations must be submitted before the corresponding submission deadline. The submission deadline is determined by the date on which the meeting or consultation will take place (see table below). Scientific meetings and consultations can be proposed at the earliest 6 months before the (first) day on which the meeting or consultation will take place. Submission deadlines (deadline at 14:00:00 CE(S)T) Scientific meeting or consultation possible from: 6 January 2026 27 February 2026 3 March 2026 23 April 2026 19 May 2026 9 July 2026 14 July 2026 10 September 2026 1 September 2026 29 October 2026 3 November 2026 24 December 2026

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals submitted within this Call for Proposals will be substantively assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Suitability to the NWO Domain Science At least 50% of the intended and/or invited speakers are known*. At least 50% of the total intended and/or invited speakers work in one of the Science disciplines. At least 50% of the expected academic participants work in one of the Science disciplines. At least one applicant works in one of the Science disciplines. A scientific meeting or consultation should have a strong focus on Science research. The meeting or consultation’s main topic cannot be knowledge implementation for a patient population, diagnostics and health interventions, technology development or implementation, knowledge implementation in a social, cultural and societal context, or similar. * This does not apply to speakers chosen through a selection procedure, such as a call for abstracts. This information is necessary to be able to assess Science suitability. Suitability of the scientific meeting or consultation to the aim of the call. Procedure Applications can be submitted on a continuous basis. NWO reviews proposals that meet the formal requirements in order of receipt. NWO will determine this based on various administrative and technical criteria. NWO can only take your proposal into consideration if it meets these conditions. You will be given one opportunity to make these corrections, and you will be given five working days to do so. NWO does not seek external advice in relation to the assessment of a proposal for a scientific meeting or consultation. The office of the NWO Domain Science (ENW) assesses whether the proposal meets the assessment criteria and draws up advice on the basis of which a decision will be made. Next, the Board of the NWO Domain Science (ENW) reviews the procedure that has been followed and the advice of the office. Finally, it determines the final ratings and makes a decision about awarding or rejecting the proposals.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Do you have any other questions? Please contact us at wbo_science@nwo.nl

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Download the application form and the mandatory form with event information and budget below and provide all of the necessary information. Among other information, the application form should include the (provisional) programme of the scientific meeting or consultation, a description of the extent to which a scientific meeting or consultation contributes to one or more Science disciplines. If the proposal is multidisciplinary or interdisciplinary, the proposal form must include a description of the extent to which the meeting or consultation contributes to NWO’s other domains; Humanities and Social Sciences, Applied and Engineering Sciences, and Health Research and Development. Contributing to other domains is not required.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Save the application form as a pdf file and the budget form as an excel file and upload them both in ISAAC.

title: Partnership Water4All – Water and Health Call 4
programme: WATER4ALL – Water Security for the Planet
target_groups: International Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration
characteristics.Research programme: WATER4ALL – Water Security for the Planet

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The European Partnership ‘Water4All’ under Horizon Europe aims at systemic transformations and changes across the entire research – water innovation pipeline, fostering the matchmaking between problem owners and solution providers to ensure long term water security for all. It provides an overarching platform connecting national/local and European R&I programs, in line with the European Green Deal, Groundwater Directive, Water Framework Directive and the new EU Strategies for water management and Biodiversity for 2030. This fourth joint call of the Partnership focuses on the topic ‘Water and Health’, addressing four central topics: Waterborne contaminants and health risks: occurrence, behaviour, interactions, and vulnerability Innovative tools and technologies for water quality and exposure monitoring Water treatment and exposure mitigation Governance, socio-economic innovation, and policy integration for water and health

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications need to be submitted by research consortia including teams of at least a minimum of 3 countries participating in the call, including a minimum of two from EU Member States or EU Associated Countries. See the European Water4All Partnership Call for Proposals for details and participating countries. Full, associate and assistant professors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application (i.e. participate in a consortium and request NWO funding) if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the institutions listed in the Funding Organisation rules (NWO part) of the call for proposals. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal. , An applicant may only request NWO funding for one project (part of a European consortium) in this call of the European Water4All Partnership. More information on the transnational eligibility criteria, and the eligibility criteria of other countries can be found in the European Water4All Partnership Call for Proposals and Funding Organisation Rules.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For a research proposal in this round, a maximum of 325,000 euro can be applied for. The maximum project duration is 3 years. No limit has been set on the total budget for the project, the national funding agencies solely limit the budget that you can apply for at the national level. At NWO this maximum is 325,000. The budget modules that are available within this call for proposals are: Postdoc, Research leave, Personnel universities of applied sciences, Material Costs, Knowledge Utilisation, and Project management. The maximum amounts for each module are stated in the Funding Organisation rules (part NWO) of the Call for Proposals. For the budget module “Postdoc”, a one-off individual bench fee of 5,000 is added on top of the salary costs to encourage the scientific career of the project employee funded by NWO. An explanation of the budget modules can be found with the ‘Downloads for your application’ under the heading 'Prepare'.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline for submitting a pre-proposal (compulsory) is 13 November 2025, 15:00 CET . Deadline for submitting a full proposal is 29 April 2026, 15:00 CET . Proposals need to be submitted through the electronic submission system of the European Water4All Partnership. More information can be found in the European Water4All Partnership Call for Proposals. NB Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit the ‘financial details’ form separately to NWO when uploading their full proposal. The format for this form is available with the ‘Downloads for your application’ under the heading 'Prepare'.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following two criteria: Excellence, Impact. Full proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Excellence, Impact, Quality and efficiency of the implementation. Please see the full European Water4All Partnership Call for Proposals for the criteria including the sub-criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage consortia are invited to submit a preproposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage can submit a full proposal. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed by at least three external evaluators consisting both of scientific and policy/management experts. The proposals will be ranked based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. At NWO the Executive Board will take this decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information can be found on Water4All 2025 Joint Transnational Call | European Partnership Water4All . Applications can be submitted via the electronic submission system of the European Water4All Partnership. Contact persons at partners Sara Cella and Yasmine Iollo Ministry for Universities and Research, 2025 international call secretariat water4all2025_callsecretariat@mur.gov.it

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit a ‘financial details’ form separately at NWO when submitting their full proposal. This form can be found below, under ‘Downloads for your application’

information_about_application_on_externa.title: Information about application on external website
information_about_application_on_externa.text: Information about the partnership and more details about the 2025 Joint Transnational Call ‘Water and Health can be found on the Water4All website .

title: Use of space infrastructure for vegetation research
programme: Use of space infrastructure for Earth observation and planetary research
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration
characteristics.Research programme: Use of space infrastructure for Earth observation and planetary research
characteristics.Themes: Biodiversity Sustainable Cities Health Climate Food Water

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The GO programme aims to provide support to researchers working in the Netherlands in the substantial use of research infrastructure in space for high-quality scientific research. This GO Call for proposals is open to interdisciplinary scientific research focused on the use of space infrastructure for vegetation research. The focus is on the use of various types of satellite data, such as hyperspectral data and in-situ data, for research in the fields of biodiversity and ecosystems.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: There are two categories of consortium applicants: Main applicant Co-applicant(s) Proposals are submitted by a main applicant on behalf of the consortium, which consists of three or more co-applicants in addition to the main applicant, that are affiliated with at least four different institutes.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: The first phase of this Call for proposal comprises two consecutive preparatory workshops on 13 and 27 February. The preparatory workshops aim to promote optimal networking around the theme and stimulate collaboration. The workshops offer the opportunity to combine ideas and form an innovative consortium (pooling of resources). This contributes to focus and reduces competition and application pressure. It is then up to the participants to start a collaboration. Interested researchers are encouraged to participate in the workshops, as they offer opportunities to meet potential new partners and develop ideas for the research proposal. A facilitator and subject experts from NWO and NSO will be present at the workshops. Participation in both workshops is mandatory for the main applicant; for co-applicants, participation is strongly recommended but not a requirement for submission.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of €3,200,000 may be requested for a project. The maximum duration of the proposed project is six years. The applicants may claim costs for personnel, equipment, investments, and knowledge utilisation.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for registration for the first preparatory workshop is 10 February 2026 , 14:00:00 CET. The deadline for registration for the second preparatory workshop is 24 February 2026 , 14:00:00 CET. The deadline for submitting proposals is 7 May 2026 , before 14:00:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Coherence of the research programme and alignment with the objective of the Call for proposal (25%), Scientific quality (30%), Quality of the consortium (25%), Scientific and/or societal impact (20%). Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: preparatory workshops on 13 and 27 February 2026; submission of the proposal, deadline 7 May 2026; admissibility of the proposal; pre-assessment by assessment committee interview; assessment of the proposal and advice; decision-making in June/July 2026.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For this call, your application must fit within the objective of the GO programme. You should therefore consider in good time whether your application fits within this objective. If you have any doubts about this, please contact one of the contact persons for the programme well before the deadline. They can advise you regarding the connection of your proposal with this call.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: It is mandatory for the main applicant to participate in both workshops

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Mandatory: Application form and budgetform Optional: statement of appointment and project supervision

title: KIC - Heterogeneous development solutions for semiconductors & scaling of quantum technologies
programme: Heterogeneous development solutions for semiconductors & scaling of quantum technologies
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Heterogeneous development solutions for semiconductors & scaling of quantum technologies

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call for proposals aims to fund fundamental and applied research in two areas: semiconductors and quantum technologies. In the field of semiconductors, the focus is on heterogeneous development solutions, with challenges in the following areas: chiplets, advanced packaging, and the metrology required for this. In the field of quantum, the focus is on expanding knowledge in the field of manufacturing and scaling up quantum systems. This Call for Proposals also encourages crossovers between semiconductor and quantum technologies.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period*) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations: universities and universities of applied sciences; university medical centres; institutes affiliated to the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; Naturalis Biodiversity Center; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); TO2 institutes** Princess Máxima Center. * Researchers employed by a university of applied sciences may also apply as the main applicant with a fixed-term, paid contract. ** Researchers from TO2 institutes can only apply as co-applicants.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: For an application in this Call for Proposals, a minimum of €600,000 and a maximum of €3,200,000 can be requested in NWO funding. NWO will fund a maximum of 80% of the total project size; the remainder of the project budget is provided through mandatory 20% co-funding (in-cash, in-kind or a mix thereof). NWO will never fund less than 50% of the total project size. During the preparation phase, applicants must choose their preferred theme: "semiconductor technologies" or "quantum." Of the total budget, €5.925.000 is reserved for proposals related to semiconductors and €5.925.000 for quantum-related proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: 2 March 2026 – Matchmaking (optional), 7 May 2026 14:00:00 CEST - Deadline request for foreign organisations, 21 May 2026 14:00:00 CEST - Deadline proposals.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for Proposals will be assessed based on the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Expected impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: matchmaking (optional); submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; initial advice from the assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making. An external, independent assessment committee will be assigned for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from science and practice with knowledge of the field.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applicants must submit their proposal in English and using the application form. NWO will assess your application against all the conditions set out in this Call for proposals, including the conditions listed below. Your application will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. Make sure to check the requirements before submitting.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: There are no mandatory consurtium-building activities but applicants are encouraged by NWO to join the matchmaking event.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the application and budget form, we also ask you to include a signed declaration co-funding. If applicable, an embedding guarantee may also be requested.

title: DeepNL Top-up Call: Opportunities for Integration and Impact

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call aims to make targeted steps towards integration, upscaling, application and/or outreach, aimed at making results from the DeepNL programme accessible and usable for professionals and other knowledge users. Examples include tools for assessing or predicting subsurface properties, or activities that strengthen outreach to stakeholders and society. Projects can contribute to insights into gas production in Groningen or connect these to future mining and storage activities.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers are eligible if they are or were involved as (co-)project leader, postdoctoral researcher or PhD candidate in an ongoing or completed DeepNL project. Professors and researchers that have a PhD may submit an application if they have a paid position for at least the duration of the application process and the duration of the research project for which the grant is being requested at one of the organisations mentioned in the Call for proposals. Applications are submitted by one main applicant, and optionally with one or more co-applicants. An applicant may be involved in a maximum of two proposals, of which only one proposal in the role of main applicant.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Proposals can be submitted between 6 January 2026, 14:00:00 CET, and 22 September 2026, 14:00:00 CEST , as long as the grant ceiling has not yet been reached. Within this period, there will be three intermediate submission deadlines for assessment: 10 February 2026, before 14:00:00 CET 7 May 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST 22 September 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of €50,000 can be requested for each project for a period of 12 months. The total budget for this call is €500,000.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications will be assessed on the basis of two main criteria: Alignment with the aim of the Call for proposals (weighting 50%) Quality of the project plan (weighting 50%) Procedure The application procedure consists of four steps: admissibility of the proposal; initial advice from the assessment committee; assessment committee meeting; decision-making. NWO first assesses whether the application meets the administrative and technical requirements. Proposals are processed in order of receipt. The submission date and time of an application that meets all the administrative conditions is decisive in determining the order of applications to be awarded. An assessment committee draws up a written funding recommendation for each proposal, based on the assessment criteria. The board of the NWO Domain Science will take a decision about awarding or rejecting the proposals based on the advice from the assessment committee and the available budget.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the application form, we also ask you to submit a completed budget form. If the proposal focuses on application of knowledge and results (area 3 in paragraph 2.1 the Call for proposals), it is mandatory to submit a letter of support from the collaboration partner(s).
