title: Do-ReACT: Further Development and Regulatory Acceptance of Chemical Test Models
programme: Do-ReACT: Further Development and Regulatory Acceptance of Chemical Test Models
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration
characteristics.Research programme: Do-ReACT: Further Development and Regulatory Acceptance of Chemical Test Models
characteristics.Themes: Health Climate Preventie Safety

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Further development and validation of New Approach Methodologies (NAMs) is required to bridge the gap between the research  phase and the implementation of safety assessments for chemical substances. Before they can be used as standard methods for safety testing and in a regulatory context, NAMs must be validated. The aim of the “NWA Do-ReACT” Call for Proposals is to contribute to the validation of existing non-animal testing methods that focus on determining the hazards and/or risks of exposure to chemical substances for humans, animals, and the environment. This will promote acceptance of these methods and the results obtained from them. The ultimate goal is to bring these NAMs as close as possible to achieving international regulatory acceptance. This Call for Proposals also explicitly aims to gain insights into the validation process.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are to be submitted by the main applicant on behalf of the consortium. The main applicant has participated in both collaborative workshops. There are four categories of participants within a consortium: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Cooperation partner(s); Co-funder(s) (optional). A consortium should consist of at least a main applicant, a co-applicant and cooperation partner. More information about the four categories of participants as well as specific requirements regarding who can apply, can be found in paragraph 3.1 of the Call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: This Call for Proposals supports the formation of consortia through a series of two collaborative workshops. The two consecutive workshops form a single session, focusing on the exchange of ideas, exploring collaboration in consortia with other participants, and making the first agreements for jointly elaborating the application. Following the workshops, the formed consortia will further develop the applications. Participation in both workshops is mandatory for lead applicants.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Only consortia focused on the further development and validation of existing methods for the safety assessment of chemical substances can apply for funding within this Call for Proposals. These methods have been developed previously in research projects, which have already provided insight into their feasibility and potential impact. The Call for Proposals is aimed at funding a single project. A maximum grant of € 3,440,000 can be requested for the project. Various different methods can be further developed in the project. In this way, the project can be a learning community where knowledge is developed from different cases and where overarching research focused on generic insights and recommendations is also possible.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: This Call for Proposals consists of two phases: Registration for and participation in the collaborative workshops (mandatory for main applicants); Submitting a proposal. On 14 January and 4 February 2026, ZonMw will organise collaborative workshops with the aim of bringing together interested parties (researchers and public and/or private social parties) around a specific theme, combining research ideas (joining forces) and forming broad innovative consortia. The deadline to register for the collaborative workshops is 7 January 2026. The deadline for submitting applications is Tuesday 12 May 2026 , before 14:00:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for Proposals will be assessed based on the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%), Expected impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%), Each assessment criterion consists of several sub-criteria, which can be found in the call for proposals, section 4.3. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: registering for the collaborative workshops; participating in both collaborative workshops (mandatory for the main applicant or a deputy (see section 3.3.1)); submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre advice assessment committee; if neccessary: interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The proposal must be submitted by a knowledge chain-wide and interdisciplinary consortium (see section 3.1 of the Call for proposals). The consortium may consist of experts from humanities and social sciences as well as exact sciences, experts in Dutch and/or European legislation, civil society organisations and experts from the industry. This Call for proposals is open only to consortia that focus on the further development and optimisation of existing methods. These are methods developed by previous research projects that accumulated knowledge about their feasibility and potential impact. Proposals need not focus exclusively on individual animal-free methods. Combinations of various animal-free methods (test batteries) are also covered by the objective of this Call for proposals.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: Please read the Call for proposals before submitting a proposal. Proposals must be submitted through MijnZonMw. The deadline is 12 May 2026 (14:00:00 CEST).

downloads.name: Do-ReACT: Further Development and Regulatory Acceptance of Chemical Test Models | call for proposals

title: Museum Grants
target_groups: Collaboration with social partners

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with social partners

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Museum Grants programme funds research to promote talent development and scientific research in the museum field. Research results should directly benefit museum research practice. The programme focuses on personal development, which is made possible by creating space for research by museum employees who are motivated to conduct scientific research. This also contributes to scientific research into museum collections and the development of the field of museum studies. The programme is open to all museum employees who identify with this and who meet the requirements to apply. The programme is therefore also open to museum employees who are not curators. The Museum Grants programme not only strengthens the position of museological research within the scientific domain, but also promotes collaboration and knowledge sharing between museums and research institutions.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Employees of a museum institution or the Netherlands Institute for Art History (RKD) may act as the main applicant. Each application must involve one researcher affiliated with a research organisation as a supervisor of the research. The main applicant must meet the following requirements: have a permanent or temporary position at the museum from which the application is being made, at least for the duration of the research; hold a master's degree (HBO or WO). The museum of the main applicant must be registered in the Museum Register, or be in the process of registration with the Museum Register, if the institution is located in the European part of the Netherlands. For museums in the Caribbean part of the Kingdom of the Netherlands, registration in the Museum Register does not apply, but the condition described in section 3.1.1.1 applies.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Applicants may apply for a grant of 10.000 to 50.000 euros. The research project has a maximum duration of 18 months. The museum finances at least 20% of the total project budget in cash or in kind.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting an application is 12 May 2026, 14:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications submitted under this Call for Proposals will be assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Scientific quality (50%), Relevance to society and museums (25%), Quality of the applicant (25%). The criteria are specified in the Call for Proposals, Section 4.3. Procedure An external, independent review committee will be set up for this Call for Proposals. After you have submitted your proposal, NWO will determine whether it will be considered based on a number of administrative and technical criteria. Your proposal will then be submitted to several members of the review committee (the preliminary advisors) for their comments. The committee will weigh up the arguments of the preliminary advisers in a meeting. Following the discussion, the committee will draw up a written recommendation to the board of the NWO Social Sciences and Humanities domain on the quality and prioritisation of the proposals.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: To prepare your application, please follow these steps: Download the application form from the online application system ISAAC or from the NWO website (on the website of the relevant funding instrument); Complete the application form; Save the form as a PDF and submit it to ISAAC with the required attachments; Enter the information requested online in ISAAC.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To prepare your application, follow these steps: download the application form from the ISAAC online application system or from the NWO website (on the website of the relevant funding instrument). complete the application form; save the form as a PDF and submit it with the required attachments in ISAAC; enter the information requested online in ISAAC. Obligatory attachments consist of: completed budget form; letter of recommendation from the supervising co-applicant; employer's statement; if applicable: statement of Algemene Voorwaarden Algemene Groepsvrijstellingsverordening (AGVV); if applicable: proof of involvement in the Museum Register registration process; if applicable: documentation supporting the assessment of the museum institution in the Caribbean Netherlands;

title: Perspectief round 2025-2026
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: NWO Domain AES invites researchers and users (companies, societal organisations) to jointly submit new, challenging research projects within the application-oriented and technical sciences that generate economic and social impact in thematic areas relevant to the Netherlands.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Scientists, companies and social organisations can apply a comprehensive research project concerning new technology through consortia. Such a project usually consists of four to eight work packages which approach the overarching theme from various research directions, with an explicit focus on application. At least three knowledge institutions and four users must be represented in the consortium. In addition to academic parties, TO2 institutes and universities of applied sciences can also apply for funding under certain conditions. For each program, users must contribute to the program costs of at least 30%, of which at least 10% in cash. More information about the conditions can be read in the call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A total budget of 27 million euros is available for this Perspectief round. The minimum monetary value of the NWO contribution for each project is 2 million Euros. The maximum NWO contribution for each project is 5 million Euros. More information about the conditions can be read in the call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Online information session: Tuesday September 2nd, 2025 – 15:00-16:00, registration via ttw-perspectief@nwo.nl Submission of statement of intent: Tuesday October 7th, 2025 - 14:00:00 CEST Submission of pre-proposal: Tuesday November 25th, 2025 - 14:00:00 CET Submission of proposal: Tuesday May 19th, 2026 - 14:00:00 CEST

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessment committee assesses the pre-proposals and proposals on the three criteria Technology development, Scientific quality and Societal impact. These three assessment criteria carry equal weight. Procedure Submitting a Perspectief application has three phases: Phase 1: Deadline for submitting statement of intent on website: Tuesday 7th October 2025, 14.00:00 CEST (see tab 3. Submit) Phase 2: Deadline for submitting pre-proposal: Tuesday 25th November 2025, 14.00:00 CET (see tab 3. Submit) Phase 3: Deadline for submitting full proposal: Tuesday 19th May 2026, 14.00:00 CEST (see tab 3. Submit)

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Changes compared to previous round: In this Perspectief Round, no separate assessment of suitability within the KIA will be carried out. The contribution to the missions from the KIA must be described in the pre-proposal and the proposal and will be assessed by the assessment committee on the basis of the criterion “Societal impact”. Technology development has been added as a separate assessment criterion, and a quality requirement applies in both phases (minimum required score 4). In the pre-proposal, the contribution to a prioritised key enabling technology from the NTS is part of this criterion. In the pre-proposal phase, a referee's report will be used for the Technology development criterion. This report will be provided by a team with expertise in the field of key enabling technologies and the NTS.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: A pre-proposal should contain an outline describing the research lines, the pathway towards societal impact, and the type and number of work packages that would be required to achieve the project objective(s). Your pre-proposal should contain an estimate and justification of the financial resources required to achieve the proposed research objective.

title: Faculty of Impact 2026
target_groups: Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the Faculty of Impact is to offer talented researchers in all disciplines the opportunity to combine entrepreneurship with science. These researchers should have an innovative idea that can potentially create an impact on society. This can be a tool to stimulate behavioural change, a technological innovation, a new business model, et cetera. At this stage, however, there is no question of a functional product, service or policy, or of a commercial company. The idea must, however, have been validated by a pilot in a laboratory setting (corresponding to a Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of approx. 4) or by relevant stakeholders (corresponding to a Society Readiness Level (SRL) of approx. 4) [1] . The fellows will be given the opportunity during a two-year support programme to further develop and scale up their ideas. *See section 6.2 for a description of TRL and SRL levels. [1] See section 6.2 for a description of TRL and SRL levels.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: PhD, Engineering Doctorate (EngD) and Professional Doctorate (PD) students and Postdocs who have completed their PhD research, EngD programme, PD track or ongoing Postdoc project before the FoI programme start date (see section 4.2.8 for the start date). or Researchers with positions of university (senior) lecturer, professor or other researchers with independent research experience.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of € 210,000 subsidy can be applied for per project. The duration of the project is 24 months. The available budget modules are: Personnel: the applicant can declare own salary costs for 0.8 – 1.0 FTE. Material: financing can be requested for project-specific material costs. A maximum of €15,000 applies to these costs. The explanation of the available budget modules, including rates, can be found in section 6.4 of the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting full proposals is Tuesday 26 May 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantively assessed based on the following criteria: Quality of the idea; Social impact for the chosen theme; Quality of the applicant. Each of the three criteria is given equal weight and is given a score between 1 (excellent) and 9 (very poor). The quality requirement is then applied in this way: fellows are only eligible for funding when their score does not exceed 5.4 (good) for each of the criteria and the average of all criteria does not exceed 4.0 (very good). Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: submission of the statement of intent; admissibility of the proposal; interview selection ( optional ); interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making. An external, independent assessment committee will be assigned for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from science and practice with knowledge of valorisation and entrepreneurship. Note: do not assume the assessment committee members to be experts in the scientific field of the applicant.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: For more information about the Faculty of Impact, please refer to its own website: Facuty of Impact Contact persons at partners Frans Nauta, Faculty of Impact, hi@facultyofimpact.nl Bianca Oudshoff, Faculty of Impact, hi@facultyofimpact.nl

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: In the application form, the applicant must describe his/her idea and its social impact on the chosen theme. In addition, the applicant must motivate why he or she wants to participate in the Faculty of Impact program.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In addition to a written application, you must also include a link to a downloadable SURFDrive of SURFFilesender video link containing the short video. The video must not be longer than 3 minutes. There is no need to hire professional equipment or help. The video is judged on its content, not its quality. A quality equivalent to the HD quality of a mobile phone is acceptable. The video must be recorded in English. Keep a broad audience in mind: your well-educated neighbours should be able to understand your story. Check whether audio files or visual materials used are royalty-free. In addition, the applicant must upload four mandatory attachments to ISAAC in addition to the application itself: Budget; Letter of support from the applicant's supervisor; Letter of support from the organization's Knowledge Transfer Office; Letter of support from the department director.

title: Caribbean Research – PhD Scholarships 2025
target_groups: Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: Starting Researchers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This PhD scholarship programme aims to contribute to the overarching goals of the programme Caribbean Research: a multidisciplinary approach , to strengthen the local knowledge infrastructure and to stimulate scientific research focused on societal issues. More specifically, to increase and strengthen scientific and scholarly knowledge production on and about the Caribbean region. The knowledge and research experience gained should directly benefit local research organisations and society in the Caribbean region. In order to stimulate and support the main goals of this Call for proposals, there is a priority treatment for a maximum of three PhD positions meant for applicants with an (intended) main PhD trajectory at either the University of Aruba, University of Curaçao or University of St. Martin.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: PhD candidates are applicants themselves and submit a Statement of intent, pre-proposal and full application, in agreement with the intended responsible PhD supervisor and other supervisor(s) and intended research organisation. For applications the following general requirements apply: The applicant holds a university master degree (or equivalent), at the time of submitting the pre-proposal. An application can only be submitted by one applicant (not for another applicant, duos or (research) groups). The applicant does not already have a PhD title. At the time of submission of the Statement of intent, the applicant does not already hold a fully paid PhD position or receive full-time funding for PhD research, regardless of the subject of the research. There are two categories of scholarships that applicants can apply for, which are determined by the main conditions below: Category A – meant for applicants with an (intended) main PhD trajectory at either the University of Aruba, University of Curaçao or University of St. Martin. Category B – meant for applicants with an (intended) PhD trajectory at another research organisation than mentioned in category A. * Requirement category B: If you intend to obtain a PhD at a research organisation in the Netherlands, you must be born on one of the following islands: Aruba, Bonaire, Curaçao, Saba, Sint Eustatius, Sint Maarten (and currently reside outside of the above-mentioned islands). Or you must reside/be registered on the above-mentioned islands.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: In total a maximum of € 350.000 can be requested. This includes salary with a maximum duration of the proposed project between 48 months (1 fte) or equivalent of that up to a maximum of 72 months (0,65 fte). A maximum of 15% of the NWO grant amount allocated for salary of the main applicant can be requested for all project-specific material costs. And a maximum of 10% of the grant amount for material costs can be requested for research organisations abroad (i.e. outside the country of the main PhD appointment and outside the Kingdom of the Netherlands).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting letters of intent was Tuesday 24 June 2025, by 11:00:00 AST/17:00:00 CEST . The deadline for submitting pre-applications is Tuesday 16 September 2025, by 11:00:00 AST/17:00:00 CEST . The deadline for submitting full applications is Tuesday 26 May 2026, by 12:00:00 AST/17:00:00 CET. If you have not submitted a Statement of intent, it is not possible to submit a pre-proposal and participate in the application procedure.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The pre-proposal will be assessed using the following: Profile of the applicant: University education, Research experience, Motivation for PhD research, Embedding of the research, Training plan. The full application is assessed using the following criteria: Potential contribution and relevance of the research to science (40%) Scientific relevance of the research and the extent to which the proposed research has the potential to make an important contribution to the advancement of scientific knowledge and/or theory; The extent to which the proposed research contains (elements of) scientific innovation in terms of theory, methods and/or topic; Proposed scientific question (clarity of the hypothesis, research question(s), objective(s) and discussion). Research design: approach and methodology (30%) Research design is appropriate to the research question(s) and objective(s) Effectiveness of the research approach and methodology; Feasibility in terms of: work plan, available research time, available infrastructure and supervision; Embedding of the research(er): supervision and quality of the research group, applicants must be guided by an adequate research team, containing the relevant expertise, which will be frequently contacted. Potential impact and contribution to knowledge questions and current societal challenges in the Caribbean part of the Kingdom of the Netherlands in the short and long term (30%) Value and benefits of the research for the Caribbean region and positioning of the intended research in a wider societal context; Embedding of the research(er): effectiveness of the research (team) in relation to societal impact in the Caribbean region. Long-term vision of the applicant to ensure durability of the research in the Caribbean region. Procedure A science-wide assessment committee assesses and prioritizes the pre-proposals and full applications, without making use of external reviewers. The pre-proposals will be assessed based on a written assessment and committee meeting. All applicants that are invited to submit a full proposal on 24 February 2026 will be invited for an interview in March 2026. The assessment committee advises the Executive Board of NWO that will make the decision on the pre-proposals and full proposals in respectively October 2025 and the end of June 2026.

statement_of_intent.title: Statement of intent
statement_of_intent.text: What is a Statement of intent? An application starts with a Statement of intent, followed by a pre-proposal phase, a full proposal phase (if invited) and an interview. With a Statement of intent, you indicate that you intend to submit an application for this Call for proposals. The Statement of intent allows NWO to establish the expected number of applications. This Statement of intent is not part of the assessment procedure, however is compulsory and must be submitted before the submission deadline. In this Statement of intent we ask further information about the intended research organisation and supervision (responsible PhD supervisor (promotor), co-promotor and/or other (daily) supervisors) in relation to the intended research proposal. Please note that you are allowed to make adjustments in your intended research and supervisory team in the pre-proposal. Please note! When submitting the Statement of intent, you must complete two steps before the deadline: You must fill in details in ISAAC (title of the proposal and a short summary of the PhD research), You must send the required forms by email to phd-caribbean@nwo.nl You can find the forms for the Statement of intent under ‘Downloads for your application’. If you have not submitted a Statement of intent (and compulsory annex), it is not possible to participate in the application process.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The pre-proposal focuses on the profile of the applicant. In the narrative CV, the applicant is asked to describe their own qualities and motivation for conducting PhD research and the research in particular. In addition, the applicant is asked for key output, which can include both scientific and non-scientific activities that the applicant has developed. The activities should not be a summary or list, but they are asked to provide context. The full application focuses on an elaboration of the research idea. How does the proposed research potentially contribute to science, what is the potential impact and contribution to society in the Caribbean part of the Kingdom of the Netherlands? In addition, the research design is requested to be described in a realistic work plan, explaining step by step how the approach and methodology of the project is carried out, as well with respect to supervision of the research project.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this Call for proposals, we ask the applicant to complete the compulsory annex in addition to completing a Statement of intent. In the Statement of intent, we ask for additional information about the intended research organisation and supervision (promoter, co-promoter and/or other (daily) supervisors) in relation to the intended research proposal, which must also be signed by all parties. In the annex, we ask for additional personal information about, for instance, the applicant's country of birth and current place of residence. If you have not submitted a Statement of intent (and compulsory annex), it is not possible to participate in the application procedure. In the pre-proposal phase, in addition to completing the pre-proposal application form, we also ask you to complete two compulsory annexes: Supervision declaration and University declaration. Here, we ask for additional information about the structure of the supervision, such as the division of tasks and structure of the contact moments, and for additional information about the agreements made with the university, respectively. In the full application phase, in addition to the application form, we also ask you to complete a compulsory financial plan (Budget) of the project. When submitting, if necessary, the annex Statement appointment and project supervision can also be added, in which the intended institute/university indicates who will take over the supervision if the (co)promoter has to be replaced.

title: KIC Adaptivity in transitions
programme: KIC 2024-2027 KIC - Missie 24/27 - Adaptivity in transitions
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: KIC 2024-2027 KIC - Missie 24/27 - Adaptivity in transitions
characteristics.Themes: Sustainable Cities Energy Climate Ruimtelijke en sociale transformaties Key Technologies Food

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The objective of this Call for proposals is to develop insights, action perspectives and capacity for action that increase the adaptivity of systems and actors within transitions. Three transition contexts are linked to this central objective of the Call for proposals. Proposed projects must focus on at least one of these transition contexts; the focus may also be on a specific component of a transition context and/or on intersections between these transitions . Consortia must address both systemic adaptivity and actor-adaptivity, ensuring that actor-adaptivity is considered in connection with system-level dynamics . Consortia are also asked to clearly conceptualise and operationalise their approach to adaptivity, which underpins the proposed project.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Full, associate and assistant professors, lectors and other researchers with a comparable position may act as main or co-applicants. Eligible organisations and other conditions can be found in paragraph 3.1.1 of the Call for proposals. The main applicant and co-applicant(s) must carry out the proposed research within a consortium that always includes two or more co-funders in addition to themselves, possibly supplemented by one or more collaboration partners. All participants in the consortium must play an active role in formulating the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project. The main applicant (or their substitute) must have participated in both collaborative workshops (see also sections 3.3.1 and 4.2.2 of the Call for Proposals).

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: To increase the likelihood of research impact, collaboration between researchers, between researchers from other disciplines, and with societal partners such as businesses and societal organisations, is essential. In this Call for proposals, NWO supports the formation of a consortium by organising a collaborative workshop. A brief description of this method can be found below. More information on consortium formation is available on the NWO website. Participation in the collaborative workshops is mandatory for principal applicants (see also section 3.1 of the Call for Proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of €1,500,000 and a maximum of €2,000,000 of NWO funding can be applied for in. With this, NWO finances maximally 80% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding (see Section 3.5.6). NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 6 years. Further specific information can be found in Chapter 3 of the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Information session: 20 January 2026, at 14:00 CET Collaborative workshop day 1: 2 February 2026 Collaborative workshop day 2: 2 March 2026 The collaborative workshops cover two half-days in total and are mandatory for main applicants. For more information, see section 3.3.1 of the Call for proposals. Deadline for full application: 9 June 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals will be assessed by the following four criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) More information about the assessment criteria and sub-criteria can be found in paragraph 4.3 in the Call for proposals. Procedure information session (not mandatory) registration for the collaborative workshops; collaborative workshops (mandatory for the main applicant or a substitute, see section 4.2.2. of the Call for proposals); submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; pre-advice assessment committee; rebuttal; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Voorstel schrijven
write_proposal.text: NWO will assess your application against all the conditions set out in this Call for proposals, including the conditions listed below: These conditions are: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1; the main applicant or their substitute (see section 3.3.1) has attended both collaborative workshops (see sections 2.3, 3.3.1, and 4.2.2); the applying consortium contains two or more co-funders; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; all of the required annexes and forms are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, complete and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals. the budget in the application has been prepared according to the terms of this Call for proposals (using the format made available which includes the most recent rates); the required co-financing has been correctly and fully committed through co-financing statements (see sections 3.1.2 and 3.5.6);

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: The objective of the collaborative workshops is to bring together potential applicants and their ideas, to encourage collaboration within broad, innovative consortia, and to limit the number of proposals. The programme of the workshops will be designed according to the theme(s) of the Call for proposals, the expertise of the participants and the expected contribution to impact. Two collaborative workshops will be organised. Although NWO supports mutual exploration, the parties ultimately decide for themselves whether and with whom they want to collaborate. During the workshops, ideas are exchanged, cooperation in consortia with other workshop participants is explored, and initial arrangements for the joint development of the proposal are made. After the workshops, the consortia further develop the proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: These documents are compulsory attachments to the application form: Declarations co-funding from co-funders. This template must be completed and signed for each individual co-funder Budget form Form ‘Statements and signature’. The following attachment is compulsory if applicable: Letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (see paragraph 3.1 of the Call for proposals).

title: Open Competition Domain Science–XL round 2025–2026
programme: XL (voorheen OC ENW-GROOT) Open Competition ENW

characteristics.Research programme: XL (voorheen OC ENW-GROOT) Open Competition ENW

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Open Competition Domain Science (ENW)–XL grants are aimed at curiosity-driven fundamental scientific research with impact. The ENW–XL grants offer consortia the opportunity to establish, strengthen, and/or expand world-class, ground-breaking, challenging, and innovative lines of research (mono-, multi-, or interdisciplinary) within the research areas of the Domain Science. Collaboration in consortia through coordinated consolidation of strengths and areas of expertise creates added value compared to smaller individual projects. For this Call for proposals, your (pre-)proposal must fit within the NWO Domain Science. Consider at an early stage if the subject of your (pre-)proposal is within the Domain Science research areas. When in doubt, please get in touch with the contact person for this programme, who can advise you about the substantive connection between your (pre-)proposal and this Call for Proposals.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: A consortium consists of a minimum of two applicants from different research organisations. These applicants: are full, associate, or assistant professors, or a researcher with a comparable position, at a Dutch university, university medical centre, or NWO-approved research institute; and have a tenured position or tenure track agreement. A researcher can only be involved in one ENW‒XL (pre-)proposal in the round 2025‒2026 as main- or co-applicant. In addition to applicants, consortium partners can be included in the consortium. Consortium partners are involved in the project but do not receive direct funding. The restrictions for applicants do not apply to consortium partners. For a description of these and other conditions, please refer to the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an ENW‒XL project, a grant amount of at least € 1,000,000 and at most € 3,000,000 can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed project is six years. A minimum of four fixed-term academic positions (PhD student, postdoc) must be requested in combination with other budget modules. Budget for the following can be requested: personnel costs for PhD students, postdocs, and non-scientific personnel; material; investments to a maximum of € 500,000; knowledge utilisation. For a description of these and other conditions, please refer to the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting pre-proposals is Tuesday 16th September 2025, before 14:00:00 hours CEST . The deadline for submitting full proposals is Tuesday 9th June 2026, before 14:00:00 hours CEST . The ENW‒XL call is published once every two years.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals are assessed against the following two criteria: Scientific quality of the proposal (What; 50%); Quality of the consortium (Who; 50%). Full proposals are assessed against the following three criteria: Scientific quality of the proposal (What; 40%); Scientific and/or societal impact (Why; 20%); Quality of the consortium (Who; 40%). Procedure The assessment will take place in two phases: pre-proposals and full proposals. Pre-proposals Pre-proposals are not sent out for external review, but assessed solely by cluster assessment committees consisting of (inter)national experts. The clusters are formed on the basis of the research areas stated in the pre-proposals. NWO strives that each cluster assessment committee together harbours the expertise required to evaluate all pre-proposals in the cluster, but this also means that not every committee member is an expert on all topics in their respective cluster. As such, your pre-proposal should be sufficiently accessible to convince committee members of the quality of your pre-proposal even if they lack specific expertise in your research topic. The cluster assessment committees select the most promising pre-proposals per cluster via a graded assessment against the assessment criteria. Based on the advice of the cluster assessment committees, the Board of the NWO Domain Science decides which proposals are invited to submit a full proposal. Full proposals IMPORTANT: you may only submit a full ENW‒XL proposal if the board the of the NWO Domain Science has decided to invite you to submit a full proposal. In the second step, the full proposals are submitted to referees for assessment. Applicants will subsequently be given the opportunity to respond in writing to the referees' reports (the rebuttal). All consortia will be invited for an interview with one of the three assessment committees. Subsequently, these assessment committees rank the proposals via a graded assessment against the assessment criteria and advise the board of the NWO Domain Science about the quality of the proposals. Based on this advice and the available funding, the board decides which proposals are awarded.

writing_a_pre_proposal.title: Writing a pre-proposal
writing_a_pre_proposal.text: Before starting with a proposal, carefully read through the Call for proposals. A proposal consists of two parts: The scientific proposal, which consists of an abstract, a description of the proposed research, and a reference list. In the description of the proposed research, which has a length of ten to 15 pages, you should address what the research questions are, why these are important, what the scientific and/or societal impact is, how and by whom the project will be executed, and a justification of the project budget. The basic details of the main applicant, the co-applicant(s), and other consortium partners, as well as additional administrative details.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Download the application form, the overview projects and grant applications, the budget form, and, if relevant, the statement appointment and project supervision. Write the proposal in English, save as a pdf, and upload in ISAAC. When submitting your proposal in ISAAC, you will also need to enter some details online. Therefore, please start submitting your proposal at least one day before the deadline of this Call for proposals. The budget form must be filled out completely and submitted as an Excel file. For every proposal that is received, NWO assumes that the applicant has informed the host institution and that they accept the funding conditions of this programme.

title: Understanding and Preventing Overweight and Obesity (PREVENT-OO)
target_groups: International Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration
characteristics.Themes: Health Preventie Food

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: With this call, ERA4health aims to enable scientists in different countries to build a valuable collaboration on common interdisciplinary research projects focused on overweight and obesity with a clear translational research approach. Specifically, research projects awarded within this call will look into one of these two subtopics: Understanding causes of obesity and overweight and its complications with mechanistic research Prevention and public health strategies for critical transitional periods of life The outcomes of the funded projects will contribute to reducing the burden of overweight and obesity in Europe by providing a better understanding of yet understudied mechanisms underlying overweight and obesity. NWO solely funds projects based on subtopic 1. Applications are submitted to the joint call secretariat via the PT-Outline system. For Dutch participation in applications, submission of national documents is mandatory; see under ‘Preparation’.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications need to be submitted by research consortia including teams from at least three and maximally five countries participating in the call. Full, associate and assistant professors, lectors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application (i.e. participate in a consortium and request NWO funding) if they adhere to the regulations as stated in Annex I (part The Netherlands) of the PREVENT-OO call for proposals. An application for NWO funding has one national main applicant, responsible for scientific and financial management. National co-applicants within an application for NWO funding are also allowed. Each Principal Investigator can submit either one proposal as project coordinator or up to two proposals as simple partner. (i.e. the coordinator of a proposal cannot be partner in another proposal). More information of the eligibility criteria, including the requirements from other countries, can be found in the PREVENT-OO Call for proposals, which can be found on this website and the ERA4Health website.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A total of €2,500,000 is made available by NWO for Dutch participation PREVENT-OO. Per application, a maximum of €400,000 can be requested from NWO. The application concerns a project with a maximum duration of 3 years. Eligible costs for NWO funding can be found in PREVENT-OO, Annex I of the call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline for submitting a pre-proposal (compulsory) is January 21st, 2026 at 12:00 CET . Deadline for submitting a full proposal is June 11th, 2026 at 12:00 CEST . Proposals need to be submitted through the electronic submission system PT-Outline. More information can be found in the PREVENT-OO Call Documents. Please note: Applicants requesting funding from NWO must submit the completed NWO budget template separately to NWO when submitting the full proposal. The format for this form can be found under ‘Downloads for your application’ in the section ‘Preparation’.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Eligible Pre-proposals and full proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Excellence Impact Quality and efficiency of the implementation For the criteria impact and quality and efficiency of the implementation, there are subcriteria which are only assessed for the full proposals. Please see the full PREVENT-OO Call 2026 documents for more information on the criteria including the sub-criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage, consortia are invited to submit a pre-proposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage can submit a full proposal. The assessment of eligible pre-proposals and full proposals will result in two separate ranking lists for topic 1 and 2. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed by an independent Evaluation Committee consisting of experts from relevant scientific fields. The proposals will be ranked based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. At NWO the Executive Board will take this decision. Please note: NWO solely funds projects based on subtopic 1.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Submitting an application can be done via the electronic submission system of PT-Outline. More information can be found on the PREVENT-OO website Contact PREVENT-OO Joint Call Secretariat: Ingrid Pfeifer and Martine Batoux : Prevent-OO@agencerecherche.fr

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For more information and the application form see the PREVENT-OO Call 2026 website Don’t forget to also fill in the NWO budget form, and send it to era4health @nwo.nl upon submission of the full proposal.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For more information and application documents see the PREVENT-OO Call 2026 website Applicants requesting funding from NWO must submit the completed ‘NWO budget template’ separately to NWO when submitting the full proposal. The format for this form can be found under ‘Downloads for your application’ in the section ‘Preparation’. The budget form should be send to era4health@nwo.nl upon submission of the full proposal.

title: Doctoral grant for Teachers, 2nd round 2026
target_groups: Teachers

characteristics.For specific groups: Teachers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Teachers from primary, secondary, further vocational, higher vocational and special education can receive a grant for a PhD study. The aim is to directly apply the knowledge and research experience acquired for the benefit of educational practice. A higher number of practising teachers with a PhD also increases the quality of education and strengthens the connections between universities and schools. You can apply for a PhD grant for any research subject. However, you must be able to indicate the importance.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: You must hold a tenured position and you must be employed at an educational institute funded by the Dutch government.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: With the PhD grant a replacement teacher can be appointed for a period of 5 years for half of your contract hours, up to a maximum of 0.4 fte.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for the submission of proposals is Thursday 11 June 2026, 14:00 CEST. There are two submission rounds annually for the Doctoral Grant for Teachers, with submission deadlines in January and June.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Further information about the criteria can be found in the call for proposals. The quality of the candidate and the research proposal are assessed according to three criteria: Quality of the research proposal, Impact, Quality of the candidate. Procedure A science-wide committee will assess the proposals and will compile a ranking. The most promising candidates will be invited for an interview. The candidate will present the research idea to the selection committee. The Domain Board Social Sciences and Humanities of NWO will take the final granting decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information about the Doctoral Grant for Teachers can be found on the programme-website.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applicants are entirely free to choose the topic of their research; any research topic is eligible for consideration. The application will be assessed based on the criteria outlined in section 4.1 of the Call for Proposals. Applications may be written in either Dutch or English. In addition to the research proposal, the application form includes the applicant’s CV and a recommendation from the supervisor.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The application consists of: - The application form (including a recommendation from the supervisor); - a compulsory annex to the application: the employer’s declaration.

title: Treatments and adherence to treatment protocols (OH-TREAT)
programme: One Health Antimicrobial Resistance (OHAMR) One Health Antimicrobial Resistance (OHAMR)
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Researchers from Abroad
characteristics.Research programme: One Health Antimicrobial Resistance (OHAMR) One Health Antimicrobial Resistance (OHAMR)
characteristics.Themes: Sustainability Health Preventie Resilient Society Safety Food Water Health Care Innovation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Drug-resistant infections are a global problem that lead to higher mortality, slower recovery, and reduced food productivity. This first Call for Proposals of the European Partnership OHAMR invites international consortia to contribute to the success rates of patients/animals/plants affected by bacterial or fungal infections by offering new treatment options and reducing the risk of resistance.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications need to be submitted by research consortia including teams of at least a minimum of 3 countries participating in the call, including a minimum of two from EU Member States or EU Associated Countries. See the Call for proposals for details and participating countries. Researchers may submit an application (i.e. participate in a consortium and request NWO funding) if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the organisations listed in the national rules and requirements for NWO in the Call for proposals. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal. An applicant may only request NWO funding for one project (part of a European consortium) in this call. More information on the transnational eligibility criteria and the eligibility criteria of other countries can be found in the Call for Proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: There is an online matchmaking tool available to find potential consortium partners. Please find the Partner search tool on the OHAMR web page.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For a proposal in this call, a maximum of 400,000 euro can be applied for at NWO. The maximum project duration is 3 years. The budget modules that are available within this call for proposals are: Personnel, Material Costs, Knowledge Utilisation, and Project management. Please refer to the national rules and requirements for NWO for further details. An explanation of the budget modules can be found with the ‘Downloads for your application’ under the heading 'Prepare'.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline for submitting a pre-proposal (compulsory) is 2 February 2026, 13:00 CET . Deadline for submitting a full proposal is 17 June 2026, 13:00 CEST . Proposals need to be submitted through the electronic submission system of the European OHAMR Partnership: PT-Outline.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals and full proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following 3 main criteria: Excellence Impact Quality and efficiency of the implementation Please go to the Call for proposals for more information about the criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage consortia are invited to submit a preproposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage can submit a full proposal. Eligible full proposals will be assessed by external evaluators consisting both of scientific and policy/management experts. The proposals will be ranked based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. Full proposals recommended for funding will undergo an ethics review organized by OHAMR. At NWO, the Executive Board will take the national funding decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Joint transnational call 2026 - EUP OHAMR

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Proposals can be submitted under one of the three topics central to OH-TREAT: Identifying and developing new combination therapies using existing or innovative antimicrobial agents, or antimicrobial agents in combination with additional treatments to enhance drug efficacy and combat resistance. Developing tools and methods to improve adherence to treatment protocols. Assessing the impact of antimicrobial agents used in veterinary medicine and agriculture on the risk of AMR transfer to humans and the environment to develop policies for limiting certain antimicrobial agents for human use.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Budget form: mandatory during the full application phase, strongly advised is to have this checked by NWO for eligibility already during the pre-registrations via ohamr@nwo.nl If applicable: documents related to the assessment to prevent state aid via ohamr@nwo.nl .

title: Sustainable Blue Economy Partnership (SBEP) – Call 2025
target_groups: International Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration
characteristics.Themes: Biodiversity Circulaire economie Energy Ruimtelijke en sociale transformaties Resilient Society Water

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The SBEP call 2025 for proposals pools national and regional funding for the development of knowledge and innovation that boost the transition to a climate neutral, sustainable and productive Blue Economy. This SBEP Call 2025 co-funded call aims to support transnational research and innovation addressing the five priority areas below: Digital Twins of the Ocean (DTOs) at sub-basin scale; Transitioning the blue economy sectors, and the development of the coexistence and the marine multi-use infrastructures; Climate-Smart Planning and managing sea-uses at the regional level; Blue Bioresources; sustainable fisheries and aquaculture, and new bio-based products; Resilient Coastal Communities and Businesses. Projects in this co-funded call must be impact-driven contributions to the transformation into a blue economy for a more resilient future. Applications. Applications focus on EU and adjacent waters; the Atlantic and Arctic Ocean, the Baltic Sea, the Black Sea, the Mediterranean Sea, and the North Sea. Applications are submitted to the joint call secretariat via the European EPSS system. For more information, see: Funding opportunities | Bluepartnership . For Dutch participation in applications, submission of national documents is mandatory; see under ‘Preparation’.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications need to be submitted by research consortia including teams from at least three countries participating in the call. Full, associate and assistant professors, lectors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application (i.e. participate in a consortium and request NWO funding) if they adhere to the regulations as stated in Annex B: NATIONAL CONTACT POINTS AND NATIONAL/REGIONAL REGULATIONS (part The Netherlands) of the call for proposals. An application for NWO funding has one national main applicant, responsible for scientific and financial management. National co-applicants within an application for NWO funding are now also allowed. Researchers from TO2 institutions can also apply for this second call. Applicants may be involved in a maximum of two proposals as national main applicant and/or national co-applicant (as part of a European consortium). More information on the eligibility criteria, including the requirements from other countries, can be found in the SBEP Call 2025 Documents.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: To find European partners you can use the b2match platform between 15 September and 15 November. . This is not obligatory.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A total of €2,000,000 is made available for Dutch participation in the SBEP Call 2025. Per application, a maximum of €400,000 can be requested from NWO if there is one national main applicant, and up to €600,000 if there is both a national main applicant and a co-applicant. The application concerns a project with a maximum duration of 3 years. There is no limit on the size of the international consortium (neither financially nor in terms of the number of partners); the national funding organisations only impose a limit on the amount that can be requested nationally by each partner. The SBEP Call 2025 also allows for the inclusion of 'self-funded partners' in the international consortium. Eligible costs for NWO funding can be found in the SBEP Call 2025, Annex B: NATIONAL CONTACT POINTS AND NATIONAL/REGIONAL REGULATIONS. Please note: PhD positions cannot be applied for in this call, due to the maximum project duration of 3 years. Moreover, NWO funds project-related costs. Therefore overhead costs are not eligible for NWO funding.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline for submitting a pre-proposal (compulsory) is November 17th, 2025 at 15:00 CET . Deadline for submitting a full proposal is June 17th, 2026 at 15:00 CET . Proposals need to be submitted through the electronic submission system of SBEP More information can be found in the SBEP Call Documents. Please note: Applicants requesting funding from NWO must submit the completed ‘NWO budget template’ separately to NWO when submitting the full proposal. The format for this form can be found under ‘Downloads for your application’ in the section ‘Preparation’. It is recommended to share this document with NWO already at the time of submitting the pre-proposal, in order to receive feedback that can still be incorporated into the full proposal."

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Eligible Pre-proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following two criteria: Excellence Impact Eligible Full proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Excellence Impact Quality and efficiency of the implementation Please see the full SBEP Call 2025 Documents for the criteria including the sub-criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage, consortia are invited to submit a pre-proposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage can submit a full proposal. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed by an independent Evaluation Committee consisting of experts from relevant scientific fields. The proposals will be ranked based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. At NWO the Executive Board will take this decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information can be found on the SBEP website . Submitting an application can be done via the electronic submission system of SBEP . Don’t forget to also submit the mandatory NWO budget form. Contact SBEP Joint Call Secretariat Domnica Cotet – UEFISCDI sbep.call-secretariat@uefiscdi.ro

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For more information and application documents see the SBEP Call 2025 website: The Sustainable Blue Economy Partnership’s third joint transnational call | Bluepartnership Don’t forget to also fill in the NWO budget form, and send it to sbep@nwo.nl

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For more information and application documents see the SBEP Call 2025 website : The Sustainable Blue Economy Partnership’s third joint transnational call | Bluepartnership Applicants requesting funding from NWO must submit the completed ‘NWO budget template’ separately to NWO when submitting the full proposal. The format for this form can be found under ‘Downloads for your application’ in the section ‘Preparation’. It is recommended to share this document with NWO already at the time of submitting the pre-proposal, in order to receive feedback that can still be incorporated into the full proposal. The budget form should be send to sbep@nwo.nl .

information_about_application_on_externa.title: Information about application on external website
information_about_application_on_externa.text: Please find more information on the website The Sustainable Blue Economy Partnership’s third joint transnational call | Bluepartnership Contactpersonen bij partners (indien van toepassing) Contact SBEP Joint Call Secretariat Domnica Cotet – UEFISCDI sbep.call-secretariat@uefiscdi.ro

title: NWO Hop-On Call for Proposals for Researchers Based in Ukraine 2025
programme: NWO-NRFU Partnership Initiative NWO Hop-On Call for Researchers Based in Ukraine
target_groups: International Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration
characteristics.Research programme: NWO-NRFU Partnership Initiative NWO Hop-On Call for Researchers Based in Ukraine

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the call is to stimulate online participation of qualified researchers in Ukraine in ongoing NWO projects and to extend their professional network in the Dutch research community. The project team from the ongoing NWO project can provide consulting, information, mentorship and/or other assistance to the individual Ukraine-based researcher.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications can be submitted to NWO by the main applicant (principal investigator) of ongoing NWO scientific projects. In the application, the beneficiary's Ukraine-based research partner must always act as co-applicant. Ukrainian higher education institutions or research institutions may receive only two grants per institution in this Call for proposals. In this Call for proposals, an ongoing NWO project is specified as follows: a project from any Call for Proposals published by NWO in the Dutch government gazette, in which a positive granting decision was taken; a research project that has started and is ongoing, and as such complies with the granting conditions as stated in the granting decision.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of at most € 30,000 can be applied for. The grant provides financial support for a Ukraine-based research partner. The applicant may only include costs for one part-time or full-time Ukrainebased researcher for 6 to 24 months and for related material costs. Maximum costs for remuneration of one Ukraine-based research partner (full time equivalent workload) is € 1,250 per month, including requested material costs. For material costs, a maximum of € 375 per month per one full time equivalent workload may be requested. The material costs can be requested for project-specific material costs: project-related goods/services, implementation costs, and travel and accommodation costs for the personnel position applied for. The eligibility of material costs follows the NWO regulations for the associated NWO ongoing project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Proposals can be submitted continuously from 20 January 2026 until the deadline on 23 June 2026 at 14:00:00 CEST , or until the budget has been exhausted. Applications are to be awarded on a “first come – first served” basis.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Procedure Based on several administrative-technical criteria, NWO will determine whether or not your proposal will be taken into consideration. NWO will request a letter of recommendation (issued by the NRFU) for NWO’s consideration of the application for the Call for proposals. Applications are awarded on a ‘first come first served’ basis. To determine the order of receipt of applications, the exact date and time of submitting the admissible application (after a possible request for additions or changes) is valid.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: On Tuesday 25 November 2025, NWO is organising an online information session about the call. In addition to the online information session, NWO is organising a matchmaking event on 11 December 2025 to bring Ukrainian researchers and NWO project teams into contact with each other. Participation in these activities is recommended but not mandatory in order to submit an application. Registration via the registration form is mandatory to participate.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The application form includes a.o. a brief description of the contribution of the Ukraine-based research partner to the specified NWO project and a financial overview of the requested personnel and material costs.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to the completed application form, we also ask you to provide the following documents: CV of the Ukraine-based research partner (in English) A letter of support from the head of the beneficiary Ukrainian institution of higher education/research institution, including a clear statement that the researcher is located in Ukraine (on the official letterhead, in English) A scan-copy of the PhD/Doctoral diploma of the Ukraine-based research partner An official certificate verifying the Ukraine-based research partners’ position and the main place of employment at the Ukrainian institution of higher education/research institution (in Ukrainian)

title: KIC Integrated Biorefineries for a Circular Future
programme: Integrated Biorefineries for a Circular Future

characteristics.Research programme: Integrated Biorefineries for a Circular Future

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Call for proposals  ‘Integrated Biorefineries for a Circular Future – improving society and environment through sustainable industry’ is focussed on joint research between researchers, companies and societal organisations from both the Netherlands and the Brazilian federal state of São Paulo. The research for this programme is focussed on replacing fossil raw materials currently in use by the chemical industry with circular raw materials derived from biomass. Research should include multiple perspectives on biorefineries including: chemical engineering , (business-)economics, sustainability/environmental, social and legal. The research focuses on two important sub-themes: bio-based raw materials and conversion processes.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: We invite researchers to formulate research proposals together with researchers from relevant other disciplines and relevant societal organisations, including companies. Eligible consortia are composed of the following key members: one main applicant based at a research organisation in the (Kingdom of the) Netherlands, one main applicant based in the State of São Paulo and at least one private co-funder in the form of a company with a presence (main or branch office) in the Netherlands or Brazil. Proposals may also include one or more co-applicants. Researchers from the State of São Paulo, must consult FAPESP about their eligibility as soon as possible, but no later than 23 June 2026. The main applicants may only submit one proposal within this Call for proposals in the role of main applicant. See the call text for all terms and conditions.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In the period prior to the deadline for submitting a letter of intent, NWO will facilitate one or more online matchmaking activities, depending on the needs of the potential applicants. Participation in these activities is recommended, but not mandatory. The aim of the matchmakings is to bring together and connect researchers from different scientific disciplines and organisations. Further information about the meetings will be announced via the website.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The budget for this Call for proposals is €3,840,000 and is made available by NWO and FAPESP. Two project sizes are available: small-scale projects with a minimum of €140,000 and a maximum of €290,000 funding (up to €145,000 from NWO, and up to €145,000 from FAPESP); and large-scale projects with a minimum of €350,000 and a maximum of €700,000 funding (up to €350,000 from NWO, and up to €350,000 from FAPESP). NWO and FAPESP will finance at most 70% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory in cash and/or in kind co-funding of 30%. Including the mandatory 30% co-funding. NWO and FAPESP shall never jointly finance less than 50% of the total project budget. See the Call for proposals for more details. Project duration: Maximum 5 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Online matchmakings will take place in March and April 2026 (not mandatory) Deadline for submission of the mandatory Letter of intent is 23 June 2026 14:00:00 CET (via e-mail) and 23 June 2026 23:59:59 BRT (SAGe) Deadline for submission of Proposals is 20 October 2026 14:00:00 CEST (ISAAC) and 20 October 2026 23:59:59 BRT (SAGe)

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (25%) Envisaged impact and route to impact 25%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (20%) See section 4.3 of the Call for proposals for more details. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: online matchmaking (March/April 2026); letter of intent (23 June 2026); submission of the proposal (20 October 2026); admissibility of the proposal (November 2026); pre-advice assessment committee (Until the end of February 2027); rebuttal (March 2027); assessment committee meeting (April 2027); decision by the NWO Executive Board and FAPESP Board of Trustees (May/June 2027). Details about the procedure are described in chapter 4 of the Call for proposals.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Contactpersonen bij partners Luana Silva Soares, NWO-biorefinarias@fapesp.br

write_proposal.title: Write proposals
write_proposal.text: Applications need to address one of the two following topics: Biobased feedstock Conversion processes The proposal and the appendix(es) must be drawn up in accordance with the template provided by NWO.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Besides the application form we ask you to also submit a signed co-funding declaration. In a co-funding declaration the co-funder expresses financial support for the project and confirms the co-funding promised. Co-funding declarations from co-funders, which are mentioned in the application, are compulsory as attachments when submitting the proposal. The co-funding declaration must be signed by an authorised signatory of the co-funder. NWO provides a compulsory format for the co-funding declaration on the funding page of this Call for proposals on the NWO website and in ISAAC.
