title: Technology for innovative nuclear reactors and fuels
programme: Technology for Innovative Nuclear Reactors and Fuels Dutch nuclear energy research programme

characteristics.Research programme: Technology for Innovative Nuclear Reactors and Fuels Dutch nuclear energy research programme

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Nuclear energy is expected to play an important role in the energy system of the future as a stable, affordable and reliable supplement to wind and solar energy and as a low-carbon energy source. This call for proposals is part of the national nuclear energy research program and focuses on the development of Generation IV reactors, advanced modular reactors (AMR’s) and microreactors, and new fuel concepts. In addition, three cross-cutting themes are central to this call for proposal: reactor safety, materials research, and monitoring systems.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: For this call for proposals, one consortium submits a single joint application. There are four different categories of participants for the consortium: Main applicant Co-applicant(s) Co-funder(s) Collaboration partner(s) The main applicant and co-applicants may include professors, associate professors, professors of applied sciences, and other researchers with comparable positions from universities, universities of applied sciences, and TO2 institutions. A co-funder is a party that participates in the consortium and contributes cash co-funding and/or in-kind support to the project. A collaboration partner is a party that does not receive funding and does not provide co-funding for the application but is closely involved in the execution of the research and/or knowledge utilization.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: Consortium building takes places during two two-day workshops. The workshops are mandatory for the main applicant of the consortium. Co-applicants, cofinanciers and collaborating partners preferably also take part in the workshops. Signing up for the workshop can be done via ISAAC.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For the application under this call for proposals, a maximum of €6,100,000 can be requested from NWO. NWO funds up to 95% of the project. The remaining part of the project budget must be covered through mandatory co-funding, which can be provided either cash or in kind. Funding can be used for, among other things, personnel and material costs. The project may have a maximum duration of 6 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Workshop registration deadline: Thursday 29 January 2026, 14:00:00 CET . Deadline for full proposal: Tuesday 30 June 2026, 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The proposal will be evaluated based on the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Expected impact and pathway to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure The procedure for this Call for Proposals consists of three steps: Registration of organizations for the workshops (deadline: 29 January 2026, 14:00 CET) Workshops for consortium formation (2–3 March 2026, 30–31 March 2026) Preparation and submission of the application (deadline: 30 June 2026, 14:00 CEST) After submission, the application will be evaluated by the assessment committee, which will advise the NWO executive Board on the approval or rejection of funding. The NWO executive Board will make a decision in the fourth quarter of 2026.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: MMIP Kernenergie in een CO2-vrije energievoorziening in 2050 | Rapport | Rijksoverheid.nl

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Organisations who wish to participate in the application within this call for proposals are required to submit an Application form Workshops to participate in the procedure. For each organisation, a maximum of one Application form Workshops may be submitted. This means a maximum of one Application form Workshops for the entire organisation at the highest level (e.g. at university level and not at faculty or department level). The main applicant submits the proposal via the NWO web application ISAAC. During the assessment process, NWO will communicate with the main applicant.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: In this call for proposals casco-workshops are organised to guide the process of forming a consortium and defining the outline of the application. Participants do not attend the workshop on their own behave, but represent their organisation.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The applicaiton form for the workshops will be uploaded to this page around mid-December. The forms for the full application will be uploaded to this page around early March.

title: NGF: Direct Use of Hydrogen, GroenvermogenNL – Work Package 3, fase 2
FutureFoodS Call 2025
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This Call will be open to applications relating to one of the topics described below. Topic 1: Domestic food practices enhancing sustainable and healthy diets Topic 2: Towards diverse, sustainable and circular food processing systems Topic 3: Trust and transparency in sustainable food systems Please note that primary production activities such as the growing of food crops (including seafood), other elements of agricultural production and various specific aspects related to it, as well as nutrition- and lifestyle-related diseases are not in focus in this partnership. The focus of FutureFoodS is on issues post-harvest. Primary production aspects are covered in other Horizon Europe partnerships.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The following countries will participate in this call: Austria , Belgium, Bulgaria, Denmark, Germany, Estonia, Finland, France, Ireland, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Netherlands, Norway, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Spain, Sweden and Turkey Research consortia must be comprised of a minimum of three partners from at least three Member States or Associated Countries participating in the call and be eligible to request  funding from the FOs participating in this call  and providing funding for the selected topic. For applicants from Dutch universities and eligible knowledge institutions, funding is available from NWO – see the NWO section of the  Annex I of the Call for proposals for the possibilities. The added value of transnational collaboration should be clearly stated in the application.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For research proposals in this round, applicants to NWO can apply for a maximum of € 400,000 in funding.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The submission deadline for pre-proposals is 11 February, 2026, at 13:00 CET . The submission deadline for full proposals is 27 July 2026, at 13:00 CEST

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Full proposals will be evaluated against the following three equally weighted evaluation criteria: Excellence impact quality and efficiency of the implementation (for full proposals only). Please see the FutureFoodS Call for proposals ( https://futurefoods.ptj.de )  for the full criteria, including the sub-criteria Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage, consortia are invited to submit a pre-proposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage will be invited to submit a full proposal. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed by an independent evaluation committee.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information about this call is available at the websites https://www.futurefoodspartnership.eu , https://futurefoods.ptj.de For contact with NWO: futurefoods@nwo.nl For contact with the Call office: ptj-futurefoods@fz-juelich.de

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Pre-applications and full applications must be submitted through the online submission tool https://futurefoods.ptj.de .  Use the application forms available on the FutureFoodS website. In case of self-funded partners, a Letter of Commitment (see Annex E) should be submitted in the full proposal phase. Please read carefully the provisions of the NWO part of Annex I, where it is explained which additional documents should be submitted to NOW (at futurefoods@nwo.nl ) in case of national co-applicants to NWO, as well as the relevant deadlines. Applicants who apply for funding at NWO must submit a completed NWO budget form separately at NWO when submitting their full proposal. This form can be found under ‘Downloads for your application’. Please send this form to futurefoods@nwo.nl

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: The partners in the approved projects will be asked to sign a consortium agreement before the start of the projects. More information will be provided in the award letter.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: A two-phase procedure will be followed for this Call for Proposals. In the first phase, consortia will be invited to submit pre-proposals. A pre-proposal includes a description of the partners, a brief project description and budget indications as described in the detailed pre-proposal template in Annex C, and in other relevant documents available in the submission platform: https://futurefoods.ptj.de .  The pre-proposals that are eligible according to the transnational and national/regional criteria will be assessed by an Expert Panel, consisting of recognized international experts in relevant fields. The decision for the invitation to submit a full proposal in the second phase will be taken jointly by the participating funders and communicated to the main applicant of the transnational consortium.

title: KIC Unlocking Digital Twin Capabilities for Electricity Utilities
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC - Vraag voor partners 24/27 - Unlocking Digital Twin Capabilities for Electricity Utilities
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC - Vraag voor partners 24/27 - Unlocking Digital Twin Capabilities for Electricity Utilities

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: A Digital Twin (DT), or virtual representation of a process or system, can display the state and behaviour of its real-world counterpart in real time. This enables DTs to contribute to more efficient construction and a safer, more economical operation of the electricity system. However, DT technology for energy systems is still in its infancy. The current state of the technology does not yet meet the needs of grid operators and other stakeholders. There is an urgent need for new knowledge, expertise, technologies, processes, and solutions. This interdisciplinary research programme aims to increase knowledge and expertise in the field of DT technology. The focus is on the development of important building blocks, such as new methods, algorithms, architectures, and models. The intended result is to deliver working proof-of-concept solutions, which will ultimately be integrated into a demonstrable system.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations: universities as referred to in Article 1.8 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act and universities listed in the Policy Rules for Universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; university medical centres by which is meant academic hospitals as referred to in Article 1.13 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act; institutes affiliated to the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; Naturalis Biodiversity Center; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In this Call for proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of a synergy workshop. The aim of the synergy workshop is to form a new inter- and transdisciplinary consortium that develops a single application from different areas of knowledge and expertise in order to achieve maximum results and impact. Applicants submit a pre-proposal specified in one of the domains described in the Call for proposals. One application per domain will be selected. Lead applicants of selected pre-proposals are required to participate in synergy workshop. Co-applicants and collaboration partners of the selected pre-proposals are also invited to participate. During the workshop, available knowledge on as many aspects of a complex issue as possible will be shared. Facilitators hired by NWO supervise the formation of the consortium and the integration of the pre-proposals into one application to be submitted. Selection for participation in the synergy workshop will take place on the basis of the pre-submissions, see Call for proposals for more information.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: There is one call for proposals within this programme. NWO, Tennet, and Energy Innovation NL have made € 5,760,000 available for this call. It is expected that one proposal will be awarded funding. This proposal will be construed from various pre-proposals during the synergy workshops. The maximum duration of the proposed project is six years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline pre-proposals: 10 February, 2025 14.00.00 CET Synergy workshops: April - July 2026 Deadline application: 8 September, 2026 14.00.00 CEST

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria A preliminary -proposal should be focused on one of the six domains described in the Call for proposals. These proposals must clearly indicate which part of the issue outlined in the Call for Proposals they aim to address, while also leaving sufficient room for collaboration, adaptation, and synergy with the proposed research activities and insights from other preliminary -proposals. This flexibility is intended to enable the selected proposals to be further developed into a single, integrated application in phase 2. In phase 1, only a concise budget estimate is required, as the final project activities will be defined in phase 2. Procedure Pre-proposals will be reviewed by an assessment committee, which advises the NWO Executive Board. Based on this advice, the Board will decide which proposals proceed to the synergy workshops, with decisions communicated in March 2026. The workshops take place from April to July 2026, after which one joint proposal will be submitted. This proposal will be assessed, and the consortium will be interviewed in September 2026 to clarify the application. Following the interview, the final funding recommendation will be made to the Executive Board. The funding decision will be announced in January 2027.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Submitting a pre-proposal is mandatory for this Call for proposals. A pre-proposal is a concise application that outlines the main research topics the applicant and co-applicants intend to address, and how these contribute to the objectives and intended impact described in the Call for proposals. Each pre-proposal may address a maximum of one of the six domains outlined in chapter 2 of the Call for proposals. Both main and co-applicants may submit multiple pre-proposals, provided each addresses a different domain. Main applicants of selected pre-proposals are required to participate in the synergy workshop. A full proposal may only be submitted by one of the main applicants who submitted a pre-proposal in phase 1 and whose proposal has been selected by the NWO Executive Board to proceed to phase 2.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: In phase 2, synergy workshops will be used to form one large consortium. Participation in these workshops is mandatory for all pre-proposals selected in phase 1. The aim of the synergy workshop is to establish a new inter- and transdisciplinary consortium that brings together diverse areas of knowledge and expertise to jointly develop a single proposal, with the goal of achieving maximum results and impact. Selected existing or newly formed collaborations that submitted a preproposal will be brought together in the workshop. During the workshop, available knowledge will be shared across as many aspects of the complex issue as possible. Facilitators appointed by NWO will guide the consortium-building process and the integration of the pre-proposals into one final proposal.

fill_in_forms.text: Pre-proposal: Pre-proposal Form Guarantee of Continuity in Project Supervision (only if applicable) Full Proposal: Full Proposal Form Budget form declaration Co-funders ‘Statement and Signature’ Form Guarantee of Continuity in Project Supervision (only if applicable) Declaration Knowledge Security

title: Isaac Newton Group Telescopes 26A
programme: Observation time on the Isaac Newton Group of Telescopes

characteristics.Research programme: Observation time on the Isaac Newton Group of Telescopes

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Astronomers can apply for observing time at the Isaac Newton Group of Telescopes (ING) on La Palma. The ING consists of the 4.2 m William Herschel Telescope (WHT) and the 2.5 m Isaac Newton Telescope (INT).

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers in astronomy with a Dutch affiliation.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Dutch observing time at WHT for observations with WEAVE (LIFU mode) to be carried out between 1 May and 31 October 2026. Proposals are invited for observations with WEAVE@WHT’s large integral-field unit (LIFU) in low- and/or high-resolution modes. In semester 2026A it is also possible to apply for target-of-opportunity observations. The available Dutch observing time for open time proposals with WEAVE is approximately 100 hours per semester. Cannot be applied for via this call: Open-time observations with WEAVE in MOS mode. Once MOS mode is commissioned, ING may issue a special call for open-time proposals. Observing time with the HARPS3 spectrograph at the INT. ING expects to offer HARPS3 in open time in semester 2026A or 2026B. A dedicated delta-call will be issued following successful commissioning and science verification of the instrument. For more information, see https://www.ing.iac.es/astronomy/instr.html .

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The next deadline is 2 October 2025. This is the deadline for observing time with WEAVE (LIFU mode)@WHT to be carried out between 1 May and 31 October 2026. Each year, the deadline for submitting proposals for observing time in Semester A will be in the second half of September. Each year, the deadline for submitting proposals for observing time in Semester B will be in the second half of March.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals will be assessed by the ING Programme Committee of NWO. Because proposals for observing time come from many different fields of research, we recommend you to keep in mind that not all members of the Programme Committee are experts in your specific area of research. Please include the necessary background information and clearly formulate the relevance of your science question and how the requested data will contribute to answering this science question. Procedure The Programme Committee of NWO assesses proposals for Dutch observing time. International proposals are assessed by a different committee. In general the telescope organisation will inform you on the success of your proposal approximately three months after submission. Around the same time, you will also receive feedback from the Programme Committee.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Visit the ING-website (Isaac Newton Group)

title: KIC Data sharing for the energy transition: socio-technical challenges
programme: Data sharing for the energy transition: socio-technical challenges Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Data sharing for the energy transition: socio-technical challenges Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The transition towards sustainable energy changes the demands on the energy grid. Generating electricity is becoming more decentralised, and demand has to follow supply rather than vice versa. The necessary control mechanisms, models, policy and investment need to have a better availability of high quality data. In order to get the right data to the right stakeholder, new socio-technical insights are required to support sharing data in the realms of privacy, business-strategy, technology and behaviour. The aim of the call is to take away barriers that prevent the right stakeholders from having the right data in a quickly changing system. Removing these barriers should make sharing data a more structured, accessible and fair process.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants other participants are involved as well. The main and co-applicant(s) should conduct the proposed research as a consortium that always includes, beside themselves, two or more co-funders, and potentially one or more cooperation partners. All consortiumpartners should play an active role in the formulation of the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: For this Call for proposals, to promote and support consortium formation a matchmaking will be used as part of the Call procedure. This event takes place on Wednesday 24 September 2025. Additionally it is possible to submit a research idea, which NWO publishes on the website and uses to improve the matchmaking process. Research ideas submitted after Tuesday 2 September 2025 14:00:00 CEST will not be used for the matchmaking process. Participation in matchmaking activities is recommended but not required.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, one can apply for a minimum of €1.000.000 and a maximum of €2.200.000 of NWO funding. With this, NWO finances maximally 70% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding. NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments, knowledge utilisation and project management.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Matchmaking (recommended but optional) 2 September 2025 Deadline research ideas 24 September 2025 Matchmaking at NWO Utrecht Proposals 18 November 2025 Deadline proposals 19 November 2026 – February 2026 Referees consulted February-March 2026 Applicants can submit a rebuttal April 2026 Interview selection (if necessary) and interviews April 2026 Assessment committee meeting May 2026 Decision by the board

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The pre-proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis 20% Envisaged impact and route to impact 20% Quality of the consortium 30% Quality of the research 30% Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: submission of a research idea (optional); matchmaking session (optional); submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview selection (if necessary); interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: NWO will assess your application on the following conditions: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions; the applying consortium contains two or more cofunders; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in the Call for proposals; the required co-funding has been, after any request for addition or change, correctly and fully committed through declarations of co-funding; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the application budget of the proposal is drawn up in accordance with the conditions for this Call for proposals using the format made available which includes the most recent rates; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; all of the required annexes and forms, after a possible request to make additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals. the budget in the application has been prepared according to the terms of this Call for proposals (using the format made available which includes the most recent rates); the required co-financing has been correctly and fully committed through co-financing statements

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this Call for proposals we require aside from the application form a signed declaration of intent from the cofinancing parties. As potential errors in these letters have proven to be difficult to correct within the timing window for corrections, NWO offers to check these letters before the application deadline. If you wish to make use of this, please contact kic-energydatasharing@nwo.nl On this website you find the following documents: Application form: required. Declaration cofinancing: required for each co-financer. Declaration “statements and signature”: required. “Overzicht MMIPs en TRL niveaus”: only available in dutch, support document for filling in questions about MMIP’s and TRL level in “statements and signature”. For this call we generally recommend MMIP “2. Data science, data analytics and data spaces” in KIA Digitalisering, and MMIP “13. Systeemintegratie” in KIA Klimaat en Energie. Statement appointment and project supervision: in case a co-applicant does not have a permanent position, or the permanent position is foreseen to end due to e.g. retirement before the end of the project, required, otherwise no. Declaration research organisation: in case a foreign research organisation participates, required, otherwise no.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Take-off focuses on facilitating and stimulating activity and entrepreneurship from Dutch universities and research institutes recognized by NWO. This involves creating innovative new activities that follow knowledge development and utilization by researchers at these knowledge institutions. This may concern product, process, care or service innovation in the broadest sense of the word, within all scientific fields.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers from the following research institutes can submit applications: Dutch universities (or comparable positions at university medical centers); KNAW and NWO institutes; the Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; NCB Naturalis; Advanced Research Center for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center for Pediatric Oncology.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: NWO provides funding for Take-off feasibility studies in the form of grants with a maximum size of 60,000 euros for a maximum period of six months for the performance of a study into the feasibility for commercial application of specific innovative knowledge that has arisen within these research institutions.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Applications can be submitted continuously via ISAAC until December 10, 2026, at 14:00:00 CET at the latest . Unlike previous Take-off Phase 1 rounds, this Call for Proposals applies to the whole of 2026. Separate Calls for Proposals will no longer be published for spring and fall rounds. Applications can be submitted on an ongoing basis. Applications will be assessed in batches of 40, with 15 applications per batch being awarded funding, provided they meet the required quality standards. A maximum of five batches will be considered. There will no longer be a cluster classification in the assessment; applications will be assessed by a broad committee. See the Call for Proposals for more information.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The ranking criteria for Take-off WO phase 1 - feasibility studies are: Knowledge base and innovativeness knowledge that forms the basis of the business idea; innovative elements. Commercial potential application possibilities and added value. Team quality scientific expertise applicant(s) and support staff; entrepreneurial and commercial skills; team involvement in the implementation of the project plan. Quality of the project plan practical and economic approach; activity plan; project budget, broken down by project activity.

title: Open Technology Programme 2026
target_groups: Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with Industry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Open Technology Programme (OTP) encourages free and independent research aimed at the development and advancement of technology that has an impact on people and society. The OTP has no disciplinary boundaries and offers opportunities for interdisciplinary and multidisciplinary collaboration. An important principle is collaboration with relevant knowledge users from the public and/or private sector. This collaboration increases the likelihood that research results will actually be applied and generate social and/or scientific impact. The OTP is specifically intended for research activities and is not intended for proposals that are predominantly focused on other activities, such as investments, contract research or clinical trials.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations: Universities as referred to in Article 1.8 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act and universities listed in the Policy Rules for Universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; university medical centres by which is meant academic hospitals as referred to in Article 1.13 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act; institutes affiliated to the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; Naturalis Biodiversity Center; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of at most €950,000 can be applied for. For projects with an investment character, where more than € 150,000 in investments are requested, a maximum of € 1,050,000 can be applied for. For both projects with and without an investment character, co-funding is required if the total project costs exceed €700,000. The minimum required co-funding is 25% of the project costs above €700,000, with no distinction between co-funding in cash or in kind. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 6 year. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. Apply only for funding that is vital to realise the project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Applications can be submitted on a continuous basis between 12 January 2026 until 11 December 2026 , as long as the grant ceiling or the maximum number of applications submitted has not yet been reached.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals are assessed for scientific quality (50%) as well as impact (50%). Procedure Applications will be submitted to a jury. Each jury will consider 18 applications in order of receipt, provided they meet the conditions for submission. Applications are assessed by international independent referees. The research proposal, the referee reports and the rebuttal are scored by an independent multidisciplinary jury consisting of 10 to 12 members on the criteria ‘scientific quality’ and ‘impact’. A ranking is determined based on these scores. The Board of NWO Domain Applied and Engineering Sciences takes the final award decision based on the prioritization of the proposals.

changes.title: Changes
changes.text: Compared to the OTP 2025 Call for Proposals, a number of (administrative) changes have been made. These are listed below The aim of the programme has been refined (see Section 2.1), including the sub-aspect ‘fit within the OTP’, which is part of the assessment criteria (see Section 4.3); If a co-applicant from a foreign research organisation is involved in the application, the organisation must be assessed by NWO prior to submission based on a number of conditions (see Section 7.1.1). This assessment is required because, in such cases, funding is being requested for project-specific costs at a foreign research organisation under the budget modules for scientific personnel abroad, material and/or knowledge utilisation; It is possible to include a compensation for individuals who participate in the user committee meeting in an unpaid role, either in a personal capacity or as a representative of a foundation or association, as an expert by experience (see Section 7.1.2). For a full explanation see the Call for proposals.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The application should specify which problem or challenge the project addresses, as well as its goals, hypotheses, research methods, and the scientific feasibility of the project. Additionally, at least four users who will be part of the user committee must be included, and in the application should describe how the research can lead to scientific and/or societal impact should be provided.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this Call, in addition to completing the application form, please upload the following attachments: budget: always compulsory; declaration and signature of main applicant: always compulsory; declaration co-funding: compulsory if co-funding is involved; statement appointment and project supervision: compulsory if the applicant’s appointment does not cover the entire duration of the project (e.g., in the case of a tenure-track agreement); motivation for resubmission in Dutch or English: compulsory if the application is a resubmission of an application that was rejected in a previous OTP assessment procedure. Note: If a foreign co-applicant is involved in the application and funding is therefore requested for project-specific costs at a foreign research organization, please contact NWO well in advance of submitting the application via ttw-otp@nwo.nl . This is necessary to coordinate whether the foreign research organization must be assessed and may therefore participate as a co-applicant (see Section 7.1.1 Call for proposals).

title: KIC Societal Earning Capacity: Connection, Synthesis and Knowledge Utilisation
programme: KIA Societal Earning Capacity (KIC) Mission 2024-2027 Maatschappelijk Verdienvermogen Top-up Call: verbinding, synthese en kennisbenutting
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: KIA Societal Earning Capacity (KIC) Mission 2024-2027 Maatschappelijk Verdienvermogen Top-up Call: verbinding, synthese en kennisbenutting

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The KIC Societal Earning Capacity Top-up Call: Connection, Synthesis and Knowledge Utilisation aims to contribute to bring together, integrate and make knowledge available gained from current and/or completed KIC projects. Its purpose is to accelerate mission driven innovation and to create Societal Earning Capacity. This gained knowledge is not always fully integrated outside certain consortiums and ecosystems. Knowledge about mission-driven innovation is also still often scattered. This call is only open for current or completed projects from the following programmes: Mission-Driven Innovation Systems in a Regional Context KIC (2020); Collaboration in Mission-Driven Innovation KIC (2021); Realisation, Acceleration and Scaling-up of Mission-Driven Innovation KIC (2022)

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The project leads of a current or a completed project funded under the calls below are encouraged to submit an application. The ‘project lead’ is the person who took on the role of ‘project lead’ at the time when a proposal was submitted in the NWO administration system ISAAC for the current project. Nine projects which were funded under the following original three calls for proposals are invited to apply for this top-up call: Mission-Driven Innovation Systems in a Regional Context KIC (2020); Collaboration in Mission-Driven Innovation KIC (2021); Realisation, Acceleration and Scaling-up of Mission-Driven Innovation KIC (2022). The project lead of a current project funded under one of the three above-mentioned calls will take the role of main applicant when submitting a proposal. Additional conditions: Co-applicants are permitted. For the purposes of this top-up call, a consortium consists of a main applicant, one or more co-applicants and a partnering organisation. Consortiums who wish to submit a proposal to this top-up call may also collaborate and jointly request a higher grant.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: NWO facilitates physical workshops for this call during the Societal Earning Capacity programme days in October 2024, 2025 and 2026. The aim of the workshops is to bring together the consortiums of current projects to foster an exchange of ideas about their current findings.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Proposals submitted to this call can apply for a minimum amount in NWO funding of € 55,555 twice or a maximum of € 111,111 once. Every current project funded under an already allocated Societal Earning Capacity call may request up to a maximum of € 111,111; this may be applied for all at once or split between two different proposals valued at € 55,555. If two or more projects make a joint top-up application together, the allowable grant for a single project will be multiplied by the number of projects making the joint application. For more information see paragraph 3.2 ‘What can be applied for’ and annex 7.1 of the call text. The proposed project has a duration of at most 1 year. The grant will not cover activities that are already planned in the scope of allocated projects. Instead, the activities in this call for proposals must be distinct, newly initiated efforts.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: This Call for proposals has a specific lead time during which applications can be submitted continuously. During the term, three deadlines/collection times have been determined when applications will be assessed. Applications can be submitted continuously until January 21, 2027, before 2:00:00 PM CET. The pick-up times are: January 23, 2025, before 2:00:00 PM CET January 22, 2026, before 2:00:00 PM CET January 21, 2027, before 2:00 PM CET (also closing date of this Call for proposals)

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following (equally weighted) criteria: Consortium and collaboration (33%) Impact (33%) Plan (33%) For more information see paragraph 4.3 of the call text. Procedure The timetable for this Call for Proposals is shown below: Proposals collection date 1 October 2024 Programme day including workshop 23 January 2025 before 14:00:00 CET Deadline proposals collection date 1 March/April 2025 Decision by the board collection date 1 Proposals collection date 2 October 2025 Programme day including workshop 22 January 2026 before 14:00:00 CET Deadline collection date 2 March/April 2026 Decision by the board collection date 2 Proposals collection date 3 October 2026 Programme day including workshop 21 January 2027 before 14:00:00 CET Deadline collection date 3 March/April 2027 Decision by the board collection date 3

title: NWA Municipal policy for biodiversity restoration
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners
characteristics.Themes: Biodiversity Sustainable Cities Knowledge Utilisation Climate Resilient Society Water

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Biodiversity loss is an immense challenge for both science and society. Therefore, it is of great importance that biodiversity is not only taken into account, but also prioritised in policy and decision-making at national, provincial and municipal levels where necessary. The Call focuses on the development of knowledge that supports transformative change aimed at biodiversity restoration and conservation.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Pre-proposals and proposals are to be submitted by the main applicant on behalf of the consortium. There are four categories of participants within a consortium: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Cooperation partner(s); Co-funder(s) (optional). A consortium should consist of at least a main applicant, a co-applicant and cooperation partner. The conditions for each type of participant are explained in more detail in the Call for Proposals

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: Research on complex and challenging issues requires collaboration between research disciplines, between practice-oriented and fundamental research, and with businesses and social organisations. That collaboration takes place in consortia. NWO encourages and supports the formation of consortia through meetings and workshops. In this Call for Proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of matchmaking for pre-proposals and synergy workshops for a proposal. More information can be found in the Call for Proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of at most € 3,820,000 can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 5 years. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. Within this Call three pre-proposals will be selected. In the pre-proposal, applicants propose living labs. The selected applicants together submit one full proposal, which consists of an overarching research project and three living labs. In total, a budget of € 3,820,000 is available.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting pre-proposals is Tuesday 3 February 2026, before 14:00:00 CET. The deadline for submitting applications is Tuesday 2 March 2027, by 14:00:00 CET. The deadline for submitting a request for review of (foreign) organisations is 20 January 2026, by 14:00:00 CET for pre-proposals, and for applications 16 February 2027 by 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Phase 1 – pre-proposals: 1) Problem definition and analysis (40%) 2. Envisaged impact and route to impact (40%) 3) Quality of the consortium (20%) Phase 2 – proposals: 1) Problem definition and analysis (20%) 2. Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) 3) Quality of the consortium (30%) 4) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure Matchmaking meeting 6 November 2025 Matchmaking meeting Request assessment of (foreign) organisation(s) 20 January 2026 14:00:00 CET Deadline for submitting a request for assessment of (foreign) organisations for pre-proposals 16 February 2027 14:00:00 CET Deadline for submitting a request for assessment of (foreign) organisations for proposals Pre-proposals 3 February 2026 14:00:00 CET Deadline pre-proposals March 2026 Committee assesses pre-proposals April-May 2026 Applicants receive an advice about whether or not to elaborate on their pre-proposal and turn it into a proposal Synergy workshops July 2026 Synergy workshop session 1 October 2026 Synergy workshop session 2 December 2026 Synergy workshop session 3 Proposals 2 March 2027 14:00:00 CET Deadline proposals April - May 2027 Interview and assessment committee meeting May - June 2027 Decision by the board

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The submission of a pre-proposal is compulsory for this Call for proposals. In phase 1, pre-proposals have to be submitted via ISAAC. The pre-proposal covers the living labs as described in subsection 2.2.3. In addition, the pre-proposal should describe the method that will be used for the research, and outline what opportunities exist for collaboration with consortia working on the other research contexts. Finally, the pre-proposal describes which potential consortium members, such as collaboration partners and co-financiers, are still envisaged in phase 2. Prior to the submission deadline for pre-proposals, NWO will organise a matchmaking meeting on 6 November 2025 (see subsection 4.2.1 and the timeline in subsection 4.2.14). Additional information on the matchmaking meeting will be announced at a later date on the programme page of the NWO website. A pre-proposal must leave sufficient room for collaboration, adjustments and synergy with other pre-proposals so that in phase 2, the pre-proposals selected can be developed into one single integrated project. During phase 1, no detailed budget specifications will be requested, given that the definitive proposed project activities will only be finalised in phase 2. The main applicant and co-applicants of the selected pre-proposals will be invited to develop one single full proposal for phase 2.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: The main applicants whose pre-proposals have been selected are obliged to take part in the synergy workshops. If a main applicant has been unable to attend, for example in the event of force majeure as referred to in the NWO Force Majeure Extension Scheme, a replacement participant must have taken part in the synergy workshops. Co-applicants and collaboration partners of the selected pre-proposals are also invited to participate.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The pre-proposal should be drawn up in the template provided by NWO. The full proposal should be drawn up in the template provided by NWO. For a full proposal the following annexes are compulsory: budget; declarations of commitment of cooperation partners; Declaration of de-minimis support (mandatory if applicable, see paragraph 3.1.1 and 3.5.8); declaration co-funding from co-funders (mandatory if applicable); letter guaranteeing the continuity of the project supervision (compulsory if applicable, see paragraph 3.1); form 'Statement and signature'.

title: Working Together Towards an Inclusive Society: Implementing a Social Model of Disability
programme: Dutch Research Agenda (NWA) Working Together Towards an Inclusive Society: Implementing a Social Model of Disability
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research
characteristics.Research programme: Dutch Research Agenda (NWA) Working Together Towards an Inclusive Society: Implementing a Social Model of Disability

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This program aims to contribute to a more inclusive society that gives full and meaningful access to people with disabilities. This is based on a social perspective on disability, focusing on the design of society. Experiential knowledge, practical knowledge and scientific knowledge are linked. This identifies causes of exclusion, assigns responsibilities and formulates and tests possible solutions.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: An application must be submitted by a consortium. A consortium should consist of at least one main applicant, co-applicant and collaboration partner. All participants should play an active role in formulating the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project. Ongoing input from experts by experience throughout the project is mandatory. The conditions per participant are further explained in the Call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: Prior to phase I, a matchmaking meeting is organised. On the one hand, this method contributes to bringing together both researchers (from different disciplines) and (representatives of) experts by experience and other societal stakeholders. On the other hand, it encourages to team up per benchmark and to think strategically about cooperation. The matchmaking meeting will take place on Monday 27 October 2025.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: In phase I, €520,000 is available per benchmark project. In phase II, €785,000 is available for the overarching project. The intended duration of all projects together (phase I and phase II) is 5 years. The maximum duration for projects in phase I is 4 years and the maximum duration for projects in phase II is 3 years. This means that phase I and phase II will overlap for two years, allowing for interaction between phases I and II. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. The available budget modules (including maximum amounts) are listed in the Call for proposals. Apply only for what is essential to carry out the project. The rates and an explanation of these budget modules are given in Annex 7.1 of the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting applications in phase I is Tuesday 10 February 2026, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for submitting applications in phase II is Tuesday 8 February 2028, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for submitting a request for review of (foreign) organisations is 27 January 2026 by 14:00:00 CET of applications in phase I and 25 January 2028 by 14:00:00 CET of applications in phase II.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem posed and problem analysis Expected impact and route to impact Quality of the consortium Quality of the research Criteria 1 and 2 each carry a weighting of 20% in the overall assessment, and criteria 3 and 4 each carry a weighting of 30%. Procedure The deadline for submitting proposals in phase I is Tuesday 10 February 2026, before 14:00:00 CEST. For the assessment of the proposals, the committee will make use of pre-advices and interviews will be conducted. A final decision on funding by the NWO Executive Board is expected in June 2026. The deadline for submitting the overarching proposal is Tuesday 8 February 2028, before 14:00:00 CEST. For the assessment of the proposal, the committee will make use of pre-advices and an opportunity for response. A final decision on funding by the NWO Executive Board is expected in June 2028.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: An application is prepared by a consortium that has the necessary expertise, experience and practical knowledge required to carry out the project. The matchmaking event organised by NWO on 27 October 2025 can be used as starting point for this purpose. Here, potential collaborations on research ideas with other participants can be explored. Additionally, initial agreements for the joint development of the application can be made.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To prepare your application (phase II), please follow the steps below: download the application form from the online application system ISAAC or from the NWO website (on the website of the relevant financing instrument); complete the application form; save the form as a PDF file and submit it to ISAAC with any mandatory attachments; complete the information requested online in ISAAC. Mandatory attachments: budget form; declarations of support from cooperation partner(s); statement of co-funder(s) (obligatory if applicable); written confirmation of contribution to investments (obligatory if applicable, see section 7.1); guarantee for continuity in project supervision (obligatory if applicable, see section 3.1); de-minimis declaration (obligatory if applicable); form ‘Statement and signature’.

title: Computing Time on National Computing Facilities
programme: Computing Time on National Computer Facilities

characteristics.Research programme: Computing Time on National Computer Facilities

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Advanced computer systems are used for technical-scientific research where major computational and/or data-intensive problems need to be solved. In the Netherlands, various computer systems are available for national use and managed by SURF: the Snellius supercomputer, Data Processing (Grid/Spider), HPC Cloud (via SURF Research Cloud) . Through the call for proposals Computing Time on National Computer Facilities, researchers can apply for access to computing time and associated data services on these national computer facilities. Capacity can also be requested on the Dutch part of the pre-exascale supercomputer LUMI in consultation with SURF should the Snellius system no longer be sufficient.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations. Only for small computing time requests , the main applicant may also submit an application with a temporary employment contract, provided that his/her appointment lasts at least as long as the duration of the project for which an application is submitted; and The research organization guarantees the duration and completion of the computing time project. Universities and Universities of applied sciences, as referred to in section 1.8(1) of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act (WHW) and the universities listed in the Policy Rules for Universities in the Kingdom of the Netherlands ; University medical centres, by which is meant academic hospitals as referred to in section 1.13 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act (WHW); KNAW and NWO institutes; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; NCB Naturalis; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL). Princess Máxima Center. TO2 federation: Deltares, Maritime Research Institute Netherlands (Marin), The Netherlands Organisation for Applied Scientific Research (TNO), Netherlands Aerospace Centre (NLR) and Wageningen University (WUR). National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM); Royal Netherlands Meteorological Institute (KNMI); IHE Delft Institute for Water Education. Those with zero-hours contracts are excluded from submitting proposals Additional requirements: Applicants affiliated to other research organisations as referred to in Article 1.1(4) of the NWO Grant Rules, the organisation must meet cumulative conditions, see Call for proposals section 3.1 for more information.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Within this call, these are applications for large amounts of computing time: More than 1,000,000 SBU on the Snellius supercomputer (CPU and/or GPU), whether or not in combination with terabytes of project space and/or Data Archive (offline tape storage). More than 500,000 CPU core hours and/or 14,000 GPU hours and/or 100,000 TB hours of storage on the pre-exascale supercomputer LUMI, whether or not in combination with terabytes of online storage and/or offline storage. More than 1,000,000 CPU core hours or 10,000 GPU hours of Data Processing (Grid and/or Spider) and/or more than 200 TB of online data storage and/or more than 300 TB of Grid storage - tape (offline tape storage). More than 50,000 CPU core hours or 5,000 GPU hours on HPC Cloud (via SURF Research Cloud), whether or not in combination with terabytes of online storage and/or Data Archive (offline tape storage). The maximum duration of large computing time projects per application is two years. The maximum duration on the pre-exascale supercomputer LUMI is one year. Small amounts of computing time: These applications are intended primarily as short-term pilots to explore the system or to support small-scale studies, for example during the transition from local to national computer facilities. A researcher may submit a maximum of one computing time application per compute facility per calendar year directly to SURF. Furthermore, it is recommended that the research group's computing needs be combined as much as possible into one large group application. Small computing time applications can be submitted directly to SURF via the request portal https://www.surf.nl/en/small-compute-applications-nwo . These requests include: Up to 1,000,000 SBU on the Snellius supercomputer (CPU and/or GPU), possibly in combination with a maximum of 10 TB of project space and/or a maximum of 50 TB of data archive. Up to 500,000 CPU core hours and/or 14,000 GPU hours for pilot applications on the pre-exascale supercomputer LUMI, possibly in combination with up to 100,000 TB hours of storage. Up to 1,000,000 CPU core hours or 10,000 GPU hours of Data Processing (Grid and/or Spider), possibly in combination with up to 200 TB of online data storage and/or up to 300 TB of Grid storage - tape (offline tape storage). Up to 50,000 CPU core hours or 5,000 GPU hours on HPC Cloud (via SURF Research Cloud), possibly in combination with up to 2 TB online storage and/or up to 50 TB Data Archive (offline tape storage). The maximum duration small of computing time projects is one year. More information on how to apply can be found in the call for proposals under section 3.2.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: You can submit your proposal at any time. There are no intermediate deadlines.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications will be assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Project organization. Scientific aspects of the project. Need to access computer facilities and technical expertise. More information about the criteria can be found in the call for proposals Procedure Applications are assessed in order of receipt based on a the application, referee reports and rebuttal. This will lead to an advice concerning the allocation or rejection by the permanent assessment Committee for the Scientific Use of Supercomputers (WGS) to the Board of NWO Domain Science. The WGS meets six times a year to consider the then completed application files. More information about the procedure can be found in the call for proposals. For more information about the WGS-committee, see the Computing Time programme page

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: For more information on the available computer systems, you are directed to SURF (scroll down to ‘Compute’). For small amounts of computing time, applications for access to the above mentioned computer systems can be made directly to SURF . Have a look at the page researchprogrammes on computing time national computer facilities , to see the allocated projects so far.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: More information on the conditions for applicants can be found in paragraph 3 of the call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To prepare your application, please go through the following steps: download the application form that can be obtained from the online application system ISAAC or from the NWO website (on the website for this programme); you are expressly requested to use the most recent 2025 application form; application forms from previous years are outdated and will not be considered. The application form for 2025 can be found on the funding page for this programme on the NWO website. first consult one of SURF's advisers on the technical aspects of the application at servicedesk@surf.nl . complete the application form. select and complete the corresponding technical annexes of the application form; remove all irrelevant technical annexes of computer facilities that will not be used. save the form as a pdf file and submit it with any required annexe(s) in ISAAC. complete the information requested online in ISAAC. Mandatory attachment: If the application is a follow-up application, also submit a report from the previous project. Reports from previous projects are taken to the evaluation committee as additional information in the evaluation of the follow -up application. The report form can be found on the funding page for this program on the NWO website. The attachment should be prepared in accordance with the template provided by NWO. The attachment should be uploaded separately from the application in ISAAC. All attachments should be submitted as a pdf file (without security). Attachments other than those listed above are not permitted. It is mandatory to write your application in English. More information on the conditions for applicants can be found in section 3.3 of the call for proposals.

title: Open Access Books
target_groups: Diversity Interdisciplinary Collaboration Teachers Applied Sciences Research Starting Researchers Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Diversity Interdisciplinary Collaboration Teachers Applied Sciences Research Starting Researchers Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this funding instrument is to stimulate the Open Access publication of academic books that result from NWO funding by providing funds for the payment of Book Processing Charges. Open access means your book can be read, shared and cited digitally without readers having to buy it. Open access thus increases the chances of your work being widely read and having an impact. Open access publishing does not prevent a possible simultaneous publication of the printed book.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications for the Open Access book publication fund can only be submitted by project leaders or co-applicants of awarded NWO research projects. Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations: universities and universities of applied sciences (UAS) as referred to in Article 1.8 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act and universities listed in the Policy Rules for Universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; university medical centres by which is meant academic hospitals as referred to in Article 1.13 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act; institutes affiliated to the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; Naturalis Biodiversity Center; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal. It could be the case that the applicant’s tenure track agreement ends before the intended completion date of the project for which funding is applied for, or that before that date, the applicant’s tenured contract ends due to the applicant reaching retirement age. In that case, the applicant needs to include a statement from their employer in which the research organisation concerned guarantees that the project and all project members for whom funding has been requested will receive adequate supervision for the full duration of the project.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per research project, a maximum of € 10,000 is available for the Open Access publication of a peer-reviewed academic book that is the result of a research project funded by NWO and published by an academic publisher. Following Crossick (2015), NWO defines an academic book or monograph as: a long-form academic work or book on a single research topic, typically written by one or sometimes two authors. This includes (peer-reviewed) edited collections as well as critical editions. Excluded from this instrument are: Article Processing Charges for journal articles: these costs can be included in the grant application of the research project (note: NWO only reimburses APCs for full Open Access journals); Book chapters; Conference proceedings; Dissertations; Textbooks; Translations.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: You can submit your proposal at any moment during the year. There are no deadlines. You can apply so long as budget for this Call for proposals is available.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: The publication concerns an academic book, monograph or collected volume which is demonstrably the result of a research project funded by NWO that ended no longer than five years from the time of submission (to the Open Book Fund application). The publication has been subjected to a full, independent, external peer-review process and the manuscript is unconditionally accepted by the publisher. The publication mentions the support for research by NWO. The publication will be published under a Creative Commons license The publication will be provided with a Digital Object Identifier (DOI). The publication will be made available Open Access in the OAPEN library. NWO will not contribute more than €10,000 towards the publication fee, including Dutch VAT (if applicable). Procedure Applications are assessed by the NWO Office on the basis of the criteria set out above. Applications within this programme are not in competition with each other, and NWO intends to honour all applications that meet the conditions and criteria as long as the annual budget is sufficient.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The proposal consists of an application form and mandatory annexes (see: fill in forms).

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Download the application form from the electronic application system ISAAC or from the NWO website (at the foot of the grant page for this programme). Complete the application form (co-signed by the publisher). Add the mandatory attachments: A copy of the signed publishing contract (specifying the publication fee). The table of contents and summary of the book. Save the application form and the attachments as a single pdf file and upload it in ISAAC.

title: Van Moorsel and Rijnierse 2025
target_groups: International Collaboration Researchers from Abroad Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Researchers from Abroad Starting Researchers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Call for proposals Van Moorsel and Rijnierse is aimed at postdoctoral researchers within five years of obtaining their PhD. With this Call for proposals, NWO and the Van Moorsel and Rijnierse Foundation promote new and pioneering research on medieval Coptic Orthodox Christianity in Egypt and the Nile Valley. The Call for proposals stems from the bequest of Johanna Rijnierse (Sloten, 5 February 1917 - Leiden, 23 September 2013), administered by NWO and the Van Moorsel and Rijnierse Foundation.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The Call for proposals is open to international applicants who defended their PhD between 4 March 2020 and 4 March 2025 (the deadline of the round). The research must be undertaken at a university or knowledge institution in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (see section 3.1. in the Call for proposals). The applicant should have a permanent or temporary contract at the university, or the prospect of a temporary contract for the length of the project. To this end, the applicant should have a letter of support signed by the university in question.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of € 345,000 can be requested in this Call for proposals. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 3 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting applications is the 4 March 2025, 14:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The application will be assessed based on the following two assessment criteria: Scientific quality of the research proposal (50%) Quality of the researcher (50%) Procedure The application process consists of the following steps: submission of the application; consideration of the application; pre-selection (optional); preliminary assessment committee; interview; meeting of the assessment committee; decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The information on rules and guidelines (the explanatory notes) are embedded in the form. Please expand the ‘Explanatory Notes’ for information on terms, conditions and requirements. Note: on Apple computers the rules and guidelines can be made visible by selecting “Outline” under the “View” tab in Word. Also, the rules and guidelines are automatically visible when you open the form in Google docs.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Compulsory annexes: letter of support (see Call for proposals, section 3.5.6). statement exceeding maximum funding (if applicable, see Call for proposals, section 3.5.7).

title: Extreme Heat and Water Events: The Adverse Health Effects of Climate Change in Cities 2024

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This Call for proposals aims to fund collaborative research that investigates the health effects of climate change; i.e., how extreme heat and/or water events affect human health and wellbeing in an urban context. Researchers can choose to focus their applications on either extreme heat- or water-related events or a combination of both. The collaborative research should be disease-focused, encompass a broad perspective on the dynamics of cities, and advance scientific knowledge, as well as enhance knowledge exchange between researchers and stakeholders. Projects should not only investigate the health impacts of extreme heat and/or water events, but aim for innovative solutions, paying special attention to the lives and environments of high-risk and vulnerable populations. They should ideally be relevant to both the Netherlands and Brazil.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Consortia should be composed of: one main applicant based at an organisation in the (Kingdom of the) Netherlands, as listed in section 3.1.1 of the Call for proposals, one main applicant based in the State of São Paulo and, at least one collaboration partner from a public, semi-public or private practitioner organisation (for-profit or not-for-profit) from the Netherlands or from Brazil. Proposals may also include one or more co-applicants based at an organisation in the Netherlands as listed in section 3.1.2 of the Call for proposals, or based in the State of São Paulo.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of at most € 700,000 can be applied for from NWO and a maximum of     R$ 4,000,000 can be applied for from FAPESP. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 5 years. Reimbursable costs from NWO may include costs for personnel, equipment, knowledge utilisation and project management. Funding and costs requested by the São Paulo researchers to FAPESP must be in accordance with FAPESP norms for (i) Thematic research project or (ii) Young Investigator Award.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting full proposals is 4 March 2025, before 14:00:00 CET , at NWO and 4 March 2025 at 23:59:00 GMT-3 , at FAPESP .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The application will be assessed according to the following criteria: Quality of the research, Potential for societal impact, Quality of the consortium. All criteria carry equal weight. Procedure Applications should be submitted to both NWO (via ISAAC ) and FAPESP (via SAGe ). The procedure is carried out through the NWO application system ISAAC. The NWO main applicant and the FAPESP main applicant are both responsible for sharing the reviewer reports, information regarding the rebuttal and any further information with the rest of the consortium. The application procedure consists of the following steps: Submission of the proposal; Admissibility of the proposal; Peer review by referees; Rebuttal; Pre-advicing of the International Assessment Committee (IAC); Meeting of the International Assessment Committee (IAC); Decision-making by the Board of Trustees of FAPESP and, the NWO Executive Board. The application, reviewer reports, and the consortium's rebuttal will be submitted to an International Assessment Committee (IAC) for evaluation. NWO and FAPESP will jointly compose this committee, with experts on the subject of the Call. The task of the committee is to assess the applications and the relevant documents that are submitted, in conjunction with each other and with regard to both the respective merits of each application and the assessment criteria outlined in this Call for proposals. The NWO Executive Board and the Board of Trustees of FAPESP will assess the procedure followed as well as the advice from the committee and the advice of the FAPESP Executive Board. They will subsequently determine the final qualifications and make a decision over awarding or rejecting the proposals. The decision becomes definitive when the NWO Executive Board and the Board of Trustees of FAPESP have come to the same provisional decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: In case of questions regarding what can be applied for from FAPESP, please contact FAPESP at chamada-nwo@fapesp.br . In case of other questions, please contact the NWO policy officer, or send an email to Merian Fund Brazil Brazil-MF@nwo.nl .

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For the submission of the proposal, a standard form is available on the funding page of this Call for proposals on the NWO website. When writing the proposal, adhere to the questions stated on this form and the procedures explained in the explanatory notes. Please also adhere to the conditions for the maximum number of words and pages.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The application form consists of six sections. Each section contains further clarification and notes on how to fill out the related questions. When writing your proposal, please take into account that it will be assessed by both expert reviewers as well as the International Assessment Committee with a broad range of expertise. Please only include the annexes specified in the Call for proposals and application form. Additional annexes are not allowed, and will need to be removed from the application. Documents are only available in English.

information_about_application_on_externa.title: Information about application on external website
information_about_application_on_externa.text: FAPESP applicants should also consult the guidelines for São Paulo researchers published by FAPESP .
