purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The SSH Open Competition XS Call for proposals is specifically intended to encourage curiosity-driven and innovative research that involves the relatively rapid exploration of a promising idea. The characteristics of SSH-XS research are: the proposed research is innovative; it is not certain in advance whether the intended objectives will be achieved; the result of each project contributes to the advancement of existing science and new scientific insights.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers with a promising idea who obtained their PhD between 5 and 10 years ago may submit an application if they have a paid appointment for at least the duration of the application process and the duration of the proposed project. The submission deadline for the round is the reference date of the graduation date. The graduation date on the PhD diploma (degree) is leading. For round 1, the applicant must have obtained their PhD between 7 March 2015 and 6 March 2020. Extra conditions: an SSH-XS application has a single applicant and no co-applicants; an applicant may submit a maximum of one application in the SSH-XS, call round 2025; an applicant - when using the extension scheme - may not submit an application for multiple types of funding instruments within the NWO Open Competition programme during the same calendar year (2025); applicants may not be part of an XS project for which the end date has not yet passed at the time of submission. Please note: the extension scheme applies to the SSH Open Competition XS in 2025. For more information and the online application form for extension see: https://www.nwo.nl/en/extension-clause .

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals a maximum of 50,000 euro can be applied for. The minimum duration of the proposed project is 6 months and the maximum duration is 12 months. Applicants are required to draw up a realistic budget and explain why the spending choices are relevant to the project. This explanation must cover all of the personnel requested. Please only apply for funding that is vital to realise the project. A more detailed explanation of the budget modules can be found in the annexe to this Call for proposals (Section 7.1).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: For 2025, 3 deadlines for submitting applications are specified: Round 1: 6 March 2025, before 14:00:00 CET Round 2: 8 May 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST Round 3: 18 September 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST When you submit your application in ISAAC, you will also need to enter some details online. Therefore, please start submitting your application at least one day before the deadline of the round. Applications that are submitted after the deadline will not be taken into consideration.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessors who rank the applications will focus on the following criteria. These criteria are not weighted. At the discretion of an assessor, both of these aspects may be used for the purposes of ranking. However, that ranking must be accompanied by a short written explanation to substantiate the given rank based on the assessment criteria listed below. Innovative aspect Is the proposed research innovative and groundbreaking? Does the proposed research contribute to existing and new scientific insights? Does the research proposal potentially have a scientific impact? Feasibility of the research plan The objective and work plan must be clearly described. Does the methodology seem suitable to achieve the indicated project goal? Is the planning of the research plan appropriate and are the activities included in the planning with sufficient clarity? Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the relevant panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria described above. The SSH disciplines and associated (sub)disciplines are listed on the NWO website: https://www.nwo.nl/en/overview-panels-domain-social-sciences-and-humanities . The assessors may award a lower ranking to any applications that are drawn up in a way that is not accessible to those in other disciplines. Procedure The applicant determines by which panel the application will be processed by means of the SSH main discipline specified in the information form (see Section 4.2.3 of the Call for proposals). Applications processed per panel are divided into 2 groups, so that each panel consists of Group A and Group B. In principle, the classification takes place at the time of submission to NWO, taking into account the personal interests declared by the applicants. The assessors and applicants associated with a particular package of proposals do not meet. NWO will decide the final rankings of the applications, based on the rankings submitted by the assessors. Per round, 6 final rankings will be established – per panel one for Group A and one for Group B. There will be no opportunity to dispute the rankings. The SSH Domain Board will base its decision about awarding or rejecting the applications on the results of the 6 final rankings, the assessment reports and the available funding.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The research idea needs to be drawn up anonymously, which means the application form meets the following conditions: applicants should not include their own name nor the name of any individuals in their research group; applicants should not include details of any affiliations with institutes or knowledge institutions; applicants should not include references to any publications that they have authored or co-authored; applicants should not include the names of any (potential) partners. Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the relevant panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Compulsory annexes to a proposal in this Call for proposals are the Budget form (Excel) and the Information form (pdf).

title: Interdisciplinary Earth and Environmental Science with use of Atmospheric Instrumentation in Space
programme: Use of space infrastructure for Earth observation and planetary research
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration
characteristics.Research programme: Use of space infrastructure for Earth observation and planetary research

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This round of the GO programme focuses, within the framework of the general GO objective, on a defined theme within Earth observation research: the use of space-atmosphere instruments with significant Dutch involvement to address scientific issues that span multiple research areas (section  1.1.1 in the Call for proposals). Relative to the general objective of the GO programme (see section 1.1.1), the following are thus specified in this Call for proposals: infrastructure in space: these are atmospheric instruments with significant Dutch involvement; scientific research: it involves interdisciplinary scientific issues, which is understood to mean scientific issues at the interface between atmospheric research and one or more other (sub-)disciplines of Earth and environmental sciences such as land, water, oceans, ecology, ice, vegetation, soil.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations that are mentioned in section 3.1 of the Call for proposals. An applicatioon is submitted by a lead applicant and two or more co-applicants. Employees of organisations other than those listed above may be part of the consortium as collaborative partners but not as a lead or co-applicants, and cannot receive a grant under this Call for proposals. It could be the case that the applicant’s tenure track agreement ends before the intended completion date of the project for which funding is applied for, or that before that date, the applicant’s tenured contract ends due to the applicant reaching retirement age. In that case, the applicant needs to include a statement from their employer in which the research organisation concerned guarantees that the project and all project members for whom funding has been requested will receive adequate supervision for the full duration of the project. NB: the details can be found in the Call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: The preparatory workshops are designed to facilitate the creation of a joint research proposal from a submitting consortium (see section 2.1.2 in the Call for proposals). The preparatory workshops are planned for December 3, 2024 and January 23, 2025.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of at most €1.800.000 can be applied for. To promote interdisciplinary cooperation, a minimum of five scientific positions of PhD and/or postdoc type should be applied for per application, which should be appointed at a minimum of three different applicant research organisations participating in the consortium. Staff can only be appointed at institutions listed in section 3.1 in the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline application: March 6, 2025 at 2:00 pm CET

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Coherence and interdisciplinary nature of the proposed programme and alignment with the purpose of the Call for proposals (30%), Scientific quality (25%), Quality of the consortium (25%), Scientific and/or societal impact (20%). Procedure An external, independent assessment committee will be established for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from science with knowledge of the field. All applicant consortia are invited for an interview where they can present their research application to the assessment committee. In this manner, the principle of hearing and rebuttal is applied.  The interview is an important part of the assessment. Following the discussion, the committee draws up a written advice to the NWO Science Domain Board on the quality and ranking of the proposals. It bases this advice on the assessment criteria. The proposal as a whole must be rated at least 'very good' to be eligible for the grant.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Contact persons at partners J. Diekema (Netherland Space Office) tel.: +31 (0) 627062992 email: j.diekema@spaceoffice.nl

prepare.title: Prepare
prepare.text: Applications must be submitted through the Isaac application and reporting system. If you do not have an Isaac account yet, then please create one at least one workday in advance. More information about writing a proposal and which forms you need to use can be found in Isaac. Please download and use the forms in Isaac. Below you will find examples of the required forms.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For this call, your application must fit within the objective of the GO programme. You should therefore consider in good time whether your application fits within this objective. If you have any doubts about this, please contact one of the contact persons for the programme well before the deadline. They can advise you regarding the connection of your proposal with this call.

title: Cities Amidst Climate Change: Enhancing Physical and Social Resilience in Urban Areas
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Climate change is a global challenge that can only be addressed through transnational collaboration. This call is open to research that focuses on strengthening the physical and social resilience of urban areas. Research can focus on urban renewal and the transformation of urban areas, such as the Binckhorst (in the Netherlands) and Guangzhou’s Zhuhai district in China. Research can also focus on historical cities such as Utrecht and Xi’an that face the challenge of preserving their heritage while adapting to climate change. Growing urban populations require safe, healthy and inclusive living environments. Collaborative research should contribute to mitigating climate risks through renovation, reconstruction and redevelopment of urban areas in both the Netherlands and China.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Eligible consortia include Dutch and Chinese researchers, with main applicants, co-applicants from different organizations, and a public/private collaboration partner. Dutch side of the consortium: One main applicant, who will be the project leader on the Dutch side At least one co-applicant, who is of a different organisation than the main applicant; Please note: a different faculty or different department is not sufficient; A collaboration partner from a public, semi-public or private practitioner organisation (for-profit or not-for-profit) from either the Netherlands or China. Chinese side of the consortium: One main applicant, who will be the project leader on the Chinese side A minimum of two and maximum three teams from universities / institutes, or a partner from a public, semi-public or private practitioner organisation, who replaces one of the teams. The university or institute of the main applicant counts as one of the teams.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Consortia can apply for up to 700,000 euros at NWO and for a maximum of RMB 3,000,000 at NSFC. Different costs can be reimbursed from the NWO and NSFC grants, as specified in the Call for Proposals

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for applications is 11 March 2025, 14:00:00 CET at NWO , and 12 March 2025, 16:00:00 CST at NSFC .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The application will be assessed according to the following criteria: 1. Quality of the research proposal 2. Potential for societal impact 3. Quality of the consortium All criteria carry equal weight. Procedure Applications should be submitted to both NWO, via ISAAC, and NSFC via the Grants system. The assessment procedure will be conducted via the NWO application system, ISAAC. The Dutch main applicant is responsible for ensuring all correspondence regarding input during the assessment procedure is shared with the consortium. After the application has been found eligible, it will be submitted to external experts (reviewers) for consultation. The reports submitted by these reviewers will be made available to the consortium for response. The application, reviewer reports, and the consortium's response will then be submitted to an assessment committee for evaluation. NWO and NSFC will jointly compose the assessment committee, with experts on the subject of the Call. The committee will assess all applications in competition, according to the criteria specified in the Call for Proposals, and formulate a funding advice. The NWO Executive Board and the Board of NSFC will take a provisional decision based on the advice of the IAC regarding the applications to be awarded. The decision is final when both organisations have reached the same decision. Composition of committee/jury Please note that it is possible that not all committee or jury members mentioned on this page have assessed all pre-proposals or applications, for example in the context of the NWO Code for Dealing with Personal Interests. Please contact the round secretary if you have any questions about this. The composition of the assessment committee may change between the phase in which preliminary applications are assessed and the phase in which full applications are assessed. For this reason, committee members who have assessed preliminary applications may not sit on the assessment committee that assesses applications. Please contact the round secretary if you have any questions about this. Professor S. Davoudi – Technical Chair Professor A. Du Plessis Professor H. Duan Doctor F. Giliberto Professor Y. Han Doctor C. Kakderi Doctor T. Kershaw Professor H. Liao Professor A. Lord Professor J. Wang Professor J. Widén Professor W. Wu Professor B. Xi

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: In case of questions regarding what can be applied for from NSFC, please contact Xu Jin via the contact information in the Call for proposals. In case of other questions, please contact the NWO policy officer listed below, or send an email to nsfc-nwo@nwo.nl . Contact person by partners Mr XU Jin +86 10 62325351 xujin@nsfc.gov.cn

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For the submission of the proposal, a standard form is available on the funding page of this Call for proposals on the NWO website. When writing the proposal, adhere to the questions stated on this form and the procedures explained in the explanatory notes. Please also adhere to the conditions for the maximum number of words and pages.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The application form consists of six sections. Each section contains further clarification and notes on how to fill out the related questions. When writing your proposal, please take into account that it will be assessed by both expert reviewers as well as the assessment committee with a broad range of expertise. Please only include the annexes specified in the Call for proposals and application form. Additional annexes are not allowed, and you will be asked to remove them.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Open Mind call aims to support out-of-the-box research ideas that offer sustainable solutions to global societal challenges through the power of technology. Whether you are pioneering new ways to transform education, build disaster-proof communication systems, empower people with disabilities, or advance environmental sustainability - we want to hear from you ! Open Mind focuses on original, creative research ideas with meaningful and lasting impact . We’re looking for high-risk high-gain ideas with the potential to be groundbreaking, either within your field or beyond. Your proposal should be bold, and challenge conventional approaches. The Open Mind program supports one-year projects aimed at bringing these creative ideas to life, with the goal of creating meaningful and lasting impact.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers (including scientific personnel, PhD, postdoc) from the following research institutes can apply: Universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands University medical centres Institutes affiliated to the Academy (Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (Koninklijke Nederlandse Akademie van Wetenschappen (KNAW) or NWO the Netherlands Cancer Institute the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics, Nijmegen the Naturalis Biodiversity Centre the Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL) the Princess Maxima Centre.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: NWO domain Applied and Engineering Sciences has a total budget of 350,000 euro available. Seven Open Mind grants will receive funding, with a maximum of 50,000 euro (inclusive of Dutch VAT) each for the period of one year. The applicant and co-applicant can apply for funding for personnel and material.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: An (online) information session is scheduled on January 16 2025 13:00 CET . The deadline for submitting proposals is March 11 2025 , before 14:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The research proposal should be in line with the aim of Open Mind and contribute to solutions for global societal challenges. The applications submitted will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Your bold research idea (60%): Innovation and Uniqueness Clarity and Quality Realistic Risk Your meaningful impact (40%): Relevance and Urgency Gap in Existing Solutions Long-term Impact Procedure NWO puts together an independent multidisciplinary Assessment Committee consisting of scientists and other creative minds with unique perspectives. A maximum of 15 proposals will be selected to be evaluated by the Assessment Committee at the pitch session during the NWO innovation festival TEKNOWLOGY on 19th June 2025. Applicants should be present at this festival to pitch and discuss their proposal. During the closing session of TEKNOWLOGY funding will be awarded to a maximum of 7 proposals. Applicants who receive funding are requested to present the obtained results at the following annual TEKNOWLOGY in 2026.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Project proposals (maximum 5 A4 pages with sections 2-4 maximum 2 pages) should be written in English according to the format of the application form. A sketch of the research idea should be provided together with a link to a downloadable short English-language video (maximum 2 minutes) that clearly explains the problem and the proposed solution in an inspiring way. The assessment committee should be impressed by the scientific challenge, original and innovative research approach or method and expected societal impact. Be encouraged to think outside the box and find unique ways to convey your idea, making it as compelling as it is visionary. Audio and/or images used should be royalty-free. Please note that the video can be used for promotional purposes prior to and/or during NWO Innovation Festival TEKNOWLOGY 2025.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In order to be able to submit an application: The application form must be completed. A sketch should be provided regarding the research proposal. A link to the video should be given in the application form. The budget form must be completed. In case of a temporary appointment, a Statement of appointment must be provided. If the above is complete and you want to submit your application, please save the application form as a PDF file and upload it together with the completed Financial planning (as Excel file) in ISAAC

title: Demonstrator 2025
target_groups: Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Demonstrator programme focuses on facilitating and stimulating the application of research results from Dutch universities, research institutes or Universities of Applied Sciences. The programme aims at further developing the knowledge to the level of a Minimal Viable Product. This demonstrator version can be used to show a third party what the application perspectives of the underlying demonstrator knowledge are and that this is ready for transfer ("Show to Transfer”). The eventual commercialisation of the knowledge is realised at and by a commercial third party.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Full, associate and assistant professors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following organisations: Universities and universities of applied sciences established in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; University medical centers; KNAW and NWO institutes; the Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; NCB Naturalis; Advanced Research Center for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of € 160,000 (including VAT) can be requested for a proposal in this call. A Demonstrator   project has a maximum duration of 18 months. The following budget modules are available within this call: Postdoc; Non-scientific staff (NSS) at universities; Replacement; Staff of universities of applied sciences; Material costs; Knowledge utilization; Patent maintenance costs.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting applications is Tuesday 18 March 2025 at 14:00:00 hours CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The proposals will be assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Basis and innovation (weighting factor 1); Societal impact (weighting factor 1); Project approach and Project team (weighting factor 1) For the specifications of the criteria please read the Call for Proposals Procedure The proposals are evaluated in writing by an evaluation committee. Proposals are only eligible for funding if they do not score higher than 3.9 (good) for the assessment criteria 'Basis and innovation’, 'Societal impact' and 'Project approach and Project team'; the average of these three scores must not exceed 3.4 (very good). On the basis of the average grade according to these criteria, the proposals are ranked. The 11 highestranked applicants receive an invitation to present their Demonstrator proposal. The committee again prioritises these applications and gives a granting recommendation to the TTW Board. The TTW Board takes the ultimate granting decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Please note : for innovative academic start-ups, please take a look at the following instrument: Take-off For specific questions about this Call for proposals, please contact us by e-mail : Demonstrator | call for proposals 2025

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: When writing a Demonstrator proposal keep the assessment criteria (paragraph 4.3.1 'Basis and innovation’, 'Societal impact' and 'Project approach and Project team') in mind and check if the formal conditions for submission have been met. For more information please refer to the Call for Proposals (paragraph 3.4.1).

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call we ask you to fill in the application form and if applicable provide us with a statement of ‘tenure track’ position when the applicant’s tenure track agreement ends before the intended completion date of the project.

title: KIC Sustainable buildings inspired by nature
programme: Sustainable buildings inspired by nature Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Sustainable buildings inspired by nature Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Climate change is leading to more intense extreme weather conditions, which will result in more frequent long periods of heat and drought and heavy precipitation in short periods of time. This makes it necessary to regulate the climate in buildings in order to ensure user comfort and health, in which, in addition to heat retention, cooling and adaptation to (prolonged) dry and wet periods become more important. The purpose of this Call for proposals is to stimulate innovative research that contributes to making the existing building stock in the Netherlands more sustainable. The primary purpose of the research is to reduce energy consumption - and thus CO2 emissions – in buildings and to minimize the environmental footprint of building materials and sustainability solutions through nature-based and nature-inspired solutions. The added value of these solutions becomes greater when the solutions also contribute to circularity, biodiversity, living comfort and the health of occupants and users.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The main applicant and co-applicant(s) must carry out the requested research within a consortium that always includes two or more co-financiers in addition to themselves, possibly supplemented by one or more collaboration partners. All participants in the consortium must play an active role in formulating the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project. Professors, university (senior) lecturers, lecturers and other researchers with a comparable position may act as main applicant or co-applicant. For the list of eligible organisations and conditions for employment, see section 3.1.1. of the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of € 750.000 and a maximum of € 2.300.000 of NWO funding can be applied for in. With this, NWO finances maximally 80% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding (see Section 3.5.6). NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: 4 November 2024 Online information session 3 December 2024 Matchmaking NWO Utrecht 23 January 2025 14:00:00 CET Deadline statements of intent 20 March 2025 14:00:00 CET Deadline proposals

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) , Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%). Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: information session; matchmaking; submission of a statement of intent; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: NWO will assess your application on the following conditions: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions; the main applicant has submitted a statement of intent in ISAAC before the set deadline; the applying consortium contains two or more cofunders; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in the Call for proposals; the required co-funding has been, after any request for addition or change, correctly and fully committed through declarations co-funding; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the application budget of the proposal is drawn up in accordance with the conditions for this Call for proposals; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; all of the required annexes are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call you need to fill out the application form and the following compulsory annexes: budget form: this must be submitted in ISAAC as an excel file and is set up according the conditions of the Call for proposals; declarations co-funding from co-funders (see Section 3.1.2 and 3.5.6); letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (if applicable, see Section 3.1); form ‘Statements and signature’.

title: NGF: Hydrogen & green electrons for carbon-based chemistry
programme: GroenvermogenNL NGF - GroenvermogenNL Hydrogen & green electrons for carbon-based chemistry
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: GroenvermogenNL NGF - GroenvermogenNL Hydrogen & green electrons for carbon-based chemistry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This Call for Proposals intends to come to a grant proposal for the realization of work package 4 of GroenvermogenNL: “NGF: Hydrogen & green electrons for carbon-based chemistry. Through workshops, a consortium will be formed which focusses on innovations to make the carbon-based chemical industry more sustainable. Due to the use of green hydrogen and green electrons the greenhouse gas emissions of the chemical industry will decline.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers from universities, universities of applied and TO2 institutions. Employees of businesses and other public and private organisations provided that: They have a tenured position for at least 0.6 fte; Their organisation has demonstrable Research & Development (R&D) activities in the Netherlands; They hold a master’s degree.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: In this Call for proposals, a maximum of €35,317,355 can be applied for. This amount will be supplemented by the businesses and civil society organisations in the consortium with ‘Funding other than that provided by NWO’. ‘Funding other than that provided by NWO’ consists of: The own contribution, i.e. the non-fundable part of the eligible costs contributed by businesses and civil society organisations, plus; Possible in cash (excl. Dutch VAT) or in-kind co-funding. Financing can amongst other be used for personal and material costs.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline application workshops: Tuesday 12 November 2024, 14:00 CET Deadline proposal: Tuesday 25 March 2025, 14:00 CET

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessment of the full proposal is based on satisfying the ‘Framework for State aid for research and development and innovation’ and the GBER, as well as the quality of the consortium and the research plan. Procedure The procedure for this Call for proposals consists of 3 steps: Registration of organisations for the workshops (12 November 2024) Workshops (December 2024/January 2025) Developing grant proposal (deadline 25 March 2025) After submission, the proposal will be assessed by the selection committee, which will advise the NWO Domain Science Board about whether or not to award funding (End of June 2025).

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: https://www.groenvermogennl.org/en

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Organisations that want to participate in the grant proposal within this Call for proposals are required to submit an Application form Workshops to participate in the procedure (Step 1). For each organisation, a maximum of one Application form Workshops may be submitted. This means a maximum of one Application form Workshops for the entire organisation at the highest level (e.g. at university level and not at faculty or department level). The main applicant submits the proposal via the NWO web application ISAAC. During the assessment process, NWO will communicate with the main applicant.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The application form including the mandatory annexes will become available in the future.

title: Rubicon 2025-1
target_groups: International Collaboration Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Starting Researchers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: International research experience is often essential for building up one’s scientific career. Rubicon offers talented researchers who have completed their doctorates in the past year the chance to gain experience at a research institution outside the Kingdom of the Netherlands, as international research experience is likely to be an advantage at a later stage in the applicant’s academic career. Rubicon is open for all scientific disciplines for a research project at a foreign research institute. In some cases, there is an opportunity to apply for a research period at a research institute in the Kingdom of the Netherlands.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Postgraduates who are currently engaged in doctoral research or who have been awarded a doctorate in the twelve months preceding the relevant deadline can submit a proposal. Applicants who are still engaged in doctoral research may apply only if their supervisor provides a written declaration approving their thesis. Women especially are urged to apply. Applicants are eligible to apply if, before the start of the project, they have received a PhD from a research organisation mentioned under article 1.1 paragraph 1 of the NWO Grant Rules. The complete list of research organisations can also be found in the Call for Proposals. Applications must be submitted by individual researchers and not by pairs or teams of researchers. Applicants may only submit an application for a Rubicon grant once. You can find the complete list of the eligibility criteria in the call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A budget of 3,145,000 euro is available for this submission round. The available budget will be divided in proportion to the number of eligible applications between the units Science, SSH, AES and ZonMw. Applicants can apply for a period of up to 24 months at an excellent research institute outside the Netherlands. The minimum duration is 12 months. The eligible costs are your salary including fringe benefits, travel expenses and a limited amount of research costs. Any overhead costs such as housing, computers, infrastructure etc. are not eligible for funding. You can find the standard amounts in chapter 7 of the Call for proposals. It is not possible to combine a stay at several institutes. Fieldwork is allowed, depending on the nature of the research proposed.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for this round is Tuesday 25 March 2025 at 14:00:00 hours CEST (2 PM sharp) .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals will be assessed on the basis of the following evaluation criteria: The quality of the researcher The quality of the research proposal The quality of the host institute in the context of the match between the applicant/proposal and the host institute Scientific and/or societal impact Procedure Each application will be assessed within the realm of the relevant discipline(s) by a multidisciplinary committee on the basis of the criteria mentioned above. NWO announces which applications have been granted about four months after the deadline of this round.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: When writing your proposal, please bear in mind that each application will be assessed with the realm of the relevant discipline(s) by a multidisciplinary committee. Please use the application form that you can download from this page. Only the required annexes will be accepted.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Preparing an application Download the application form and fill it in; Save the application form as a pdf file and upload it in ISAAC (Science, SSH, AES) or MijnZonMw (ZonMw). The application form consists of: A research proposal written in English (max. 6 pages A4, 2,000 words). The word count includes footnotes, texts illustrations and tables, captions to illustrations and tables. References are excluded; A narrative CV of the applicant consisting of a narrative academic profile (400-700 words) and the key output (max. 10 items); Two recommendation forms; One acceptance form of the intended host institute; Applications from applicants who have not yet been awarded a PhD must also be accompanied by a form approving their thesis. Preparing an application Download the application form and fill it in; Save the application form as a pdf file and upload it in ISAAC (Science, SSH, AES) or MijnZonMw (ZonMw). The application form consists of: A research proposal written in English (max. 6 pages A4, 2,000 words). The word count includes footnotes, texts illustrations and tables, captions to illustrations and tables. References are excluded; A narrative CV of the applicant consisting of a narrative academic profile (400-700 words) and the key output (max. 10 items); Two recommendation forms; One acceptance form of the intended host institute; Applications from applicants who have not yet been awarded a PhD must also be accompanied by a form approving their thesis. All forms will be made available on the websites of NWO and ZonMw at least two months before the deadline of this call.

title: CETPartnership Joint Call 2024
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad Collaboration with Industry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Clean Energy Transition Partnership (CETPartnership) is a multilateral and strategic partnership of national and regional research, development and innovation (RDI) programmes in European Member States and Associated Countries.   The CETPartnership aims to empower the clean energy transition and contribute to the EU’s goal of becoming the first climate-neutral continent by 2050, by pooling national and regional RDTI funding for a broad variety of technologies and system solutions required to make the transition. In this 2024-call of the partnership NWO funds research on (1) system integration (renewable energy sources, storage)and flexibility of the energy system (2) integration of technologies for producing/storing, including concepts for heating and cooling into the built environment. Funded consortia bring together international partners from academia, companies and Industry organizations, local/regional governments, research organizations and NGO’s.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The CETPartnership Joint Call 2024 is structured by the joint call text and ten Call modules describing specific topics and/or challenges that applicants shall address in their project proposal. Applicants must choose and apply to a specific call module when submitting their project proposal.   Applications need to be submitted by research consortia including teams from a minimum of three countries participating in the call (including a minimum of two from EU Member States or EU Horizon Europe Associated Countries). Specific additional requirements apply for the 2 call modules that are eligible for NWO funding: CM2024-02 Energy system flexibility: renewables production, storage and system integration CM2024-10 Clean energy integration in the built environment Full, associate and assistant professors, lectors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application.   An applicant may only request NWO funding for one project (part of a European consortium) in this call of the CETPartnership. See the CETPartnership Call Documents for details on transnational eligibility criteria and participating countries. For scientists based in the Kingdom of the Netherlands, applying for NWO funding, the NWO eligibility criteria apply.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For research proposals a maximum of €400.000 can be applied for from NWO. Funding can only be requested for research on the following call modules: CM2024-02, CM2024-10. The maximum project duration is 3 years. No limit has been set on the total international consortium size (budget or partners), but per call module an indicative   project size and minimum amount of consortium partners is described in the CETPartnership Call Documents per call module. The budget modules that are available within this call for proposals are: Research Leave, Postdoc, Non Scientific Staff, Personnel TO2 institues and universities of applied sciences, Benchfee for Postdoc positions, Material Costs, Knowledge Utilisation and Internationalisation. The maximum amounts for each module are stated in the Funding Organisation rules (part NWO) in the Call Documents.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Pre- Proposal (compulsory) submission deadline: 21 November 2024, 14.00 CET . Full proposal submission deadline: 2 April 2025, 14.00 CET . Proposals need to be submitted through the CETPartnership Application portal (electronic submission system of the CETPartnership). Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit the completed ‘NWO budget template’ separately to NWO when uploading their full proposal.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals and full proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Excellence Impact Quality and efficiency of the implementation Please see the full CETPartnership Call Documents for the criteria including the sub-criteria Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage consortia are invited to submit a preproposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage can submit a full proposal. Per individual call module each proposal will be evaluated by three independent experts. The proposals will be ranked per call module based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. At NWO the Executive Board will take this decision regarding the dedicated NWO budget.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information can be found on the CETPartnership website . The application submission can be done via the CETPartnership Application portal (electronic submission system of the CETPartnership).

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit a completed ‘NWO budget template’ separately at NWO when submitting their full proposal. This form can be found below, under ‘Downloads for your application’. Applicants can submit the completed Budget Form to NWO via an email to cetpartnership@nwo.nl .

title: KIN Pact Network Grant
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The purpose of the KIN Pact Network grant is to support the creation of transdisciplinary networks focused on climate transitions. This applies both to the professionalisation of existing networks, and networks yet to be established. In this way, KIN aims to further stimulate the collaboration between different stakeholders and disciplines and to enhance the use of knowledge on climate transition themes.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Employees of all legal entities established in the Kingdom of the Netherlands can submit an application. A condition for receiving a funding is that the main and co-applicants of the network must be affiliated with the KIN Pact when submitting an application. The network must be transdisciplinary and consist of at least one knowledge institution and at least two societal organizations; these form the main and co-applicants. Transdisciplinarity means that the network consists of more than one thematic discipline and more than one type of organisation (knowledge institution, social organisation, government, companies, etc.). The KIN Pact network subsidy is explicitly not intended for networks with a focus on a single area of ​​expertise (mono-disciplinary) or conducting research.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of €25,000 in subsidy can be requested per application. Each network can submit one application. The subsidy must be used within one year after granting, this is not the same as the duration of the network. Examples of activities eligible for funding include; Organizing or co-hosting events and meetings; Developing products or services focused on knowledge brokering. Organizing community engangement (e.g., educational events and participatory workshops; arts and culture initiatives); Capacity building (e.g. field trips, training); Analyses on system barriers that impede (participants in) the network in effectiveness; Organizational or digital support for the network. No funding can be requested for scientific publications and activities that are already receiving funding (from KIN or elsewhere).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The dates of processing applications are: February 6, 2025 at 2:00:00 PM CE(S)T, April 8, 2025 at 2:00:00 PM CE(S)T, June 10, 2024 at 2:00:00 PM CE(S)T; also final deadline of this subsidy round.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this call will be assessed based on the following criteria: Alignment with the mission of the KIN and KIN Pact (50%), Appropriateness of activities and budget (50%). Procedure 6 February 2025 14:00 CE(S)T Processing deadline batch 1 Within one week of processing deadline Processing applications batch 1 Within two months of processing deadline Decision KIN steering committee batch 1 8 April 2025 om 14:00:00 uur CE(S)T Processing deadline batch 2 Within one week of processing deadline Processing applications batch 2 Within two months of processing deadline Decision KIN steering committee batch 2 10 June 2025 om 14:00:00 uur CE(S)T Processing deadline batch 3, and deadline of this call. Within one week of processing deadline Processing applications batch 3 Within two months of processing deadline Decision KIN steering committee batch 3

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Read more on KIN at www.hetkin.nl . Read more on the KIN Pact and how to become a member at www.hetkin.nl/pact .

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: An application consists of a concise plan describing the objectives of the network and the intended use of the grant, including a budget. The application must be written in Dutch or English.

title: NWO Talent Programme | Vidi - Applied and Engineering Sciences (AES) 2024

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the NWO Talent Programme is to provide creative space for adventurous, talented, pioneering researchers to do research of their choice, develop their own line of research and further develop their talent.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications can be submitted by candidates who have obtained their PhD, irrespective of whether they hold tenured positions and of whether they come from the Netherlands or abroad. Candidates may only submit an application if they have obtained their PhD in the eight years preceding the benchmark date of this round, 1 October 2024. An extension to the above career-dependent submission period may be possible under specific circumstances (see Section 3.4.2 of the Call for proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of € 850,000 can be applied for within this Call for proposals. The maximum duration of the proposed project is five years. If the proposed research is of shorter duration, the maximum amount will be reduced accordingly. Only request the amount that is required to execute the project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting preproposals is 5 november 2024, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for submitting full propoals is 8 april 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The preproposal submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criterion: Quality of the researcher. The full proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%); Scientific and/or societal impact (25%). Procedure submission of the preproposal; admissibility check of the preproposal; assessment of the preproposal; provisional advice assessment committee; possibility of filing a request for the correction of a factual error; decision making preproposal; submission of the full proposal (only possible if you have received a positive decision on the preproposal); admissibility check of the full proposal; assessment of the full proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; provisional advice assessment committee; interview selection (ZonMw only); interview and meeting of the assessment committee; decision-making.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Embedding guarantee Since 2019 on NWO has asked candidates for an 'embedding guarantee' from the start of the application round for NWO Talent Programme Vidi funding. Researchers can only submit applications for Vidi funding with the support of the Dutch institution where they intend to conduct their research. It is expected to lead to fewer applications and to be more consistent with the research institutions’ personnel policy. The embedding guarantee format can be found under ‘Prepare’. Optional literature list As of 2023, the Vidi uses an optional literature list of max. ten pieces of relevant scientific literature closely related to your research idea. NWO will use the literature list - along with your research idea, key words and specified discipline(s) - to search for potential external reviewers for your full proposal. Your literature list will not be submitted to the committee. Frequently asked questions On this page you will find an overview of the most frequently asked questions about the NWO Talent Programme, including questions about the set time limit to submit a proposal (and any exceptions to it) and the embedding guarantee.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The preproposal has to be written in English and contains an evidence-based curriculum vitae of the applicant, consisting of: a description of the applicant’s academic profile (1200 words); a description of the applicant’s key output (minimum 400 and maximum 700 words); short research idea (max. 150 words). Applicants who receive a positive decision for their pre‐proposals are invited to submit a full proposal. Applicants who receive a negative decision for their pre‐proposals may not submit a full proposal. This full proposal has to be written in English and consists of: a description of the research proposal, a motivation for the choice of institution, and a description of the fit between the research proposal and the applicant’s academic profile (maximum 4000 words on a maximum of 8 pages, including in-text references); a section on scientific and/or societal impact (maximum 1000 words); a section on data management; and a budgetplan.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Mandatory annex to the preproposal: embedding guarantee. Optional attachment to the preproposal: literature list for referees; Non-referees. mandatory annex to the full proposal: not applicable. Optional attachment to the full proposal (see section 3.5.6): declaration co-funding; statement exceeding maximum funding.

title: KIC Mission Digital Identities - A Foundation for Trust in the Digital World
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Mission 2024-2027 Digital Identities - A Foundation for Trust in the Digital World
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Mission 2024-2027 Digital Identities - A Foundation for Trust in the Digital World

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The proposed programme builds on the Breed Gedragen Programma Decentrale Technologie (Broadly Supported Decentralised Technology Programme) launched by the Top Sectors.  As cyber security and federative data systems are already covered by other research programmes, these elements are not included in this research programme. The aim of this Call for proposals is to generate knowledge and develop the most important elements of digital identities for individuals, legal entities and objects. The objective is to establish and maintain digital trust across society by developing and standardising digital identities. This will involve tackling not just challenges related to technological development and standardisation, but also societal challenges related to regulation and social acceptance.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Initiatives and proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants other participants are involved as well. Four categories of participants can be distinguished in the consortium: Main applicant, Co-applicant(s), Co-funder(s), Collaborating partner(s). In this Call for proposals it is mandatory to have a minimum of two co-funders participate in the project. The co-funders jointly contribute a net minimum amount of 20% of the total budget for the proposal.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The grant ceiling for the call for proposals totals € 7.000.000. For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of €600.000 and a maximum of €3.200.000 can be applied for in total. With this, NWO finances maximally 80% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding. NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting initiatives is Thursday 14 November 2024, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for submitting proposals is Tuesday 8 April 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Problem definition and analysis (20%), Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%). Procedure The submission of an initiative is mandatory for this Call for proposals. With an initiative you indicate that you have a potential contribution to or an idea for a (sub)project within this Call for proposals and intend to submit an application for this Call for proposals. Applications will be provided with feedback by referees. Applicants will have the opportunity to submit a rebuttal to this. Subsequently, the applications are assessed by the assessment committee. The assessment committee offers its recommendation to the NWO Executive Board for decision-making. In November 2025, the Executive Board determines the final assessment of the applications.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: The KIC call ‘Digital Identities - a foundation for trust in the digital world’ requires submission of an initiative. Submit initiative

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: You will find all of the documents that you need for your application here. Applications must be submitted through the ISAAC application and reporting system. If you do not have an ISAAC account yet, then please create one at least one workday in advance. More information about writing a proposal and which forms you need to use can be found below. Please download and use the forms below.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the application form when submitting the complete application, we also ask you to enclose the following mandatory attachments: budget form, declarations co-funding from co-funders (see Section 3.1.2 and 3.5.5), form ‘Statement and signature’, confirmation of institutional contribution to investment (compulsory if applicable, see Section 7.1 Call for proposals), letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (if applicable, see Section 3.1 Call for proposals). Other types of attachments are not accepted in this phase of submitting full proposals. Attachments should be uploaded to ISAAC separately from the proposal. All attachments, with the exception of the budget form, should be uploaded as PDF files. The budget form can be uploaded to ISAAC as an Excel file.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the NWO Talent Programme is to provide creative space for adventurous, talented, pioneering researchers to do research of their choice, develop their own line of research and further develop their talent.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications can be submitted by candidates who have obtained their PhD, irrespective of whether they hold tenured positions and of whether they come from the Netherlands or abroad. Candidates may only submit an application if they have obtained their PhD in the eight years preceding the benchmark date of this round, 1 October 2024. An extension to the above career-dependent submission period may be possible under specific circumstances (see Section 3.4.2 of the Call for proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of € 850,000 can be applied for within this Call for proposals. The maximum duration of the proposed project is five years. If the proposed research is of shorter duration, the maximum amount will be reduced accordingly. Only request the amount that is required to execute the project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting preproposals is 5 november 2024, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for submitting full proposals is 8 april 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The preproposal submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criterion: Quality of the researcher. The full proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%); Scientific and/or societal impact (25%). Procedure submission of the preproposal; admissibility check of the preproposal; assessment of the preproposal; provisional advice assessment committee; possibility of filing a request for the correction of a factual error; decision-making preproposal; submission of the full proposal (only possible if you have received a positive decision on the preproposal); admissibility check of the full proposal; assessment of the full proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; provisional advice assessment committee; interview selection (ZonMw only); interview and meeting of the assessment committee; decision-making. Applications from the Earth Sciences, Astronomy, Chemistry, Computer Science, Life Sciences, Physics and Mathematics will be submitted to the Science domain. The Science domain has a single submission window and six panels in which Vidi preproposals are assessed. You can indicate the panel of your choice in the application form. Within the Science domain you can choose between the following panels : Earth Sciences; Life Sciences; Chemistry; Physics and Astronomy; Mathematics; Computer Science. The full proposals are assessed in a domain-wide competition. Interviews will take place in three broad clusters. Candidates will therefore be interviewed and assessed by committee members from their own discipline and other disciplines in the Science domain.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Embedding guarantee Since 2019 on NWO has asked candidates for an 'embedding guarantee' from the start of the application round for NWO Talent Programme Vidi funding. Researchers can only submit applications for Vidi funding with the support of the Dutch institution where they intend to conduct their research. It is expected to lead to fewer applications and to be more consistent with the research institutions’ personnel policy. The embedding guarantee format can be found under ‘Prepare’. Optional literature list As of 2023, the Vidi uses an optional literature list of max. ten pieces of relevant scientific literature closely related to your research idea. NWO will use the literature list - along with your research idea, key words and specified discipline(s) - to search for potential external reviewers for your full proposal. Your literature list will not be submitted to the committee. Frequently asked questions On this page you will find an overview of the most frequently asked questions about the NWO Talent Programme, including questions about the set time limit to submit a proposal (and any exceptions to it) and the embedding guarantee.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The preproposal has to be written in English and contains an evidence-based curriculum vitae of the applicant, consisting of: a description of the applicant’s academic profile (1200 words); a description of the applicant’s key output (minimum 400 and maximum 700 words); short research idea (max. 150 words). Applicants who receive a positive decision for their pre‐proposals are invited to submit a full proposal. Applicants who receive a negative decision for their pre‐proposals may not submit a full proposal. This full proposal has to be written in English and consists of: a description of the research proposal, a motivation for the choice of institution, and a description of the fit between the research proposal and the applicant’s academic profile (maximum 4000 words including in-text references); a section on scientific and/or societal impact (maximum 1000 words); a section on data management; and a budgetplan.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Mandatory annex to the preproposal: embedding guarantee. Optional attachment to the preproposal: literature list for referees. mandatory annex to the full proposal: not applicable. Optional attachment to the full proposal (see section 3.5.6): declaration co-funding; statement exceeding maximum funding.

title: Biodiversity and Transformative Change (BiodivTransform)
programme: European Partnerships Rescuing Biodiversity to Safeguard Life on Earth
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Applied Sciences Research

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Applied Sciences Research
characteristics.Research programme: European Partnerships Rescuing Biodiversity to Safeguard Life on Earth

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The European partnership ‘Rescuing Biodiversity to Safeguard Life on Earth’ (Biodiversa+) under Horizon Europe aims to contribute to bringing biodiversity in Europe back on a path of recovery by 2030. The partnership provides an overarching platform connecting national/local and European research and innovation programs and combines in-cash and in-kind resources, in line with the European Green Deal and the new EU Biodiversity Strategy for 2030. The fourth joint call of the partnership focuses on the topic ‘Biodiversity and Transformative Change (BiodivTransform)’. The BiodivTransform Call aims at supporting interdisciplinary, transdisciplinary, and/or cross-sectoral research and innovation projects that will: Help understand trade-offs and cross-linkages between the worldwide crises of biodiversity loss, climate change and pollution. Identify, analyse, and comprehend transformation processes that may safeguard biodiversity by mitigating its threats and halt – even reverse – its decline, as well as the benefits it may provide to people.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications need to be submitted by research consortia including teams from a minimum of three countries participating in the call (including a minimum of two from EU Member States or EU Associated Countries). See the Biodiversa+ Call Documents for details and participating countries. Full, associate and assistant professors, lectors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application (i.e. participate in a consortium and request NWO funding) if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the institutions listed in Funding Organisation rules (part NWO) of the call for proposals. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal. An applicant may only request NWO funding for one project (part of a European consortium) in this call of the European Biodiversity Partnership. More information on the transnational eligibility criteria, and the eligibility criteria of other countries can be found in the Biodiversa+ Call Documents and Funding Organisation Rules.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For a research proposal in this round, a maximum of €325,000 can be applied for. The maximum project duration is 3 years. No limit has been set on the total international consortium size (budget or partners), the national funding agencies solely limit the budget that you can apply for at the national level. At NWO this maximum is €325,000. The budget modules that are available within this call for proposals are: Postdoc, Research leave, Personnel universities of applied sciences, Material Costs, Knowledge Utilisation and Internationalisation. The maximum amounts for each module are stated in the Funding Organisation rules (part NWO) in the Call Documents. For the budget module “Postdoc”, a one-off individual bench fee of €5,000 is added on top of the salary costs to encourage the scientific career of the project employee funded by NWO. An explanation of the budget modules can be found with the ‘Downloads for your application’ under the heading 'Prepare'.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline for submitting a pre-proposal (compulsory) is November 8th, 2024 at 15:00 CET . Deadline for submitting a full proposal is April 11th, 2025 at 15:00 CEST . Proposals need to be submitted through the electronic submission system of Biodiversa+. More information can be found in the Biodiversa+ Call Documents. NB Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit the completed ‘NWO budget template’ separately to NWO when uploading their full proposal. The format for this form is available with the ‘Downloads for your application’ under the heading 'Prepare'.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Fit to the scope of the call Novelty of the research Impact Full proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Excellence; Quality and efficiency of the implementation; Impact. Please see the full Biodiversa+ Call Documents for the criteria including the sub-criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage consortia are invited to submit a pre-proposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage can submit a full proposal. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed by an independent Evaluation Committee consisting both of scientific and policy/management experts. The proposals will be ranked based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. At NWO the Executive Board will take this decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information can be found on the Biodiversa+ website . Submitting an application can be done via the electronic submission system of Biodiversa+.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit a completed ‘NWO budget template’ separately at NWO when submitting their full proposal. This form can be found below, under ‘Downloads for your application’
