title: NWA Social dynamics in the energy transition. From practice to theory F2
programme: Social dynamics in the energy transition. From practice to theory Thema 2023 - Sociale dynamieken in de energietransitie
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Social dynamics in the energy transition. From practice to theory Thema 2023 - Sociale dynamieken in de energietransitie

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The knowledge this NWA programme seeks aims to develop must offer different levels of government ways of getting a grip on the unpredictability of such dynamics among end users, in order to better understand and hence better respond to them. If we want to accelerate the energy transition within the Dutch context – the ultimate urgent aim of this programme – we have a real need for practical hands-on knowledge about the exchange between, on the one hand, policy development and implementation by different levels of government (local, regional and national) and, on the other, the capacity of those in society to act. The programme seeks to deliver practical usable insights and prospects for taking action that different levels of government can use to develop inclusive and equitable policies that take account of the interests and concerns of end users. The main thrust is to sponsor research into social dynamics within the context of the government’s ambitions for the energy transition that adds to existing knowledge about the individual behaviour of end users.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Please mind: This is a closed application. Only main applicants who have submitted a pre-proposal that was considered by NWO may submit a full proposal. Pre-proposals and full proposals are to be submitted by the main applicant on behalf of the consortium. A consortium should consist of at least a main applicant, a co-applicant and cooperation partner. In phase 1 (pre-proposals), co-funders are excluded. In phase 2 (full proposal), co-funders are allowed, but not required. NWO will assess your application against the conditions listed below. Your application will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. After submitting your application, NWO requests you to be available to implement any possible administrative corrections so that you can (still) meet the conditions for submission. These conditions are: The main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1; The application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; The application form is, after a possible request to make additions or changes, complete and filled out according to the instructions; The application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; The application is received before the deadline; The application is written in English. Additional conditions for full proposals: The main applicant already submitted a pre-proposal that was accepted for processing by NWO and selected for phase 2; The full proposal must be submitted by a consortium consisting of at least one main applicant, one co-applicant and one partner; The proposal’s budget is drawn up in accordance with the conditions for this call for proposals; All the required annexes are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, complete and filled out according to the instructions, and drafted and submitted according to the conditions for this call for proposals. Where applicable: co-funders meet the conditions set in section 3.1.3; Where applicable: the co-funding has, after a possible request to make additions or changes, been committed correctly and fully by means of co-funding statements; The proposed project has a duration of at most 5 years.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For this Call for Proposals, a total of €2,855,000 is available. A maximum of one application will be awarded within this Call for proposals. This Call for proposals has two phases: Submitting a pre-proposal. Submitting a full proposal. For this call, a maximum of three pre-proposals will be selected. The pre-proposals must present practical case studies. The selected sub-consortia are required to submit a single full proposal, made up of an overarching research project and practical case studies. For this Call for proposals, in phase 1, a maximum of €500,000 can be requested per sub-consortium for research on practical cases. For the overarching research project (phase 2), a minimum of €1,355,000 is available.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting pre-proposals is 24 September 2024, before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline to apply a pre-proposal has passed. Only applicants who have been selected in phase 1 are allowed to apply for a full proposal. The deadline for submitting full proposals is 22 April 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The full application submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantively assessed according to the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Expected impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure Phase 1 – pre-proposals Information meeting (online); submission of the pre-proposal (for a practical case study); admissibility of the pre-proposal; assessment of the pre-proposal; decision-making and advice; invitation to participate in synergy workshops Phase 2 – Drafting and submitting a full proposal Phase 2a Participation in synergy workshops Phase 2b submission of the full proposal; admissibility of the fullproposal; pre advice assessment committee; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making by NWO Executive Board.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Only main applicants who have submitted a pre-proposal that was considered by NWO may submit a full proposal. The three knowledge gaps identified by this Call are: Drivers, catalysts and triggers of social dynamics Involving end users in an inclusive energy transition Interventions and prospective actions for adaptive policy development The complete proposal aims to acquire knowledge by means of practice-oriented research consisting of a maximum of three practical case studies (pre-proposal) that result in or contribute to addressing the knowledge gaps as identified. NWO will assess your application against the conditions listed below. Your application will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. After submitting your application, NWO requests you to be available to implement any possible administrative corrections so that you can (still) meet the conditions for submission. These conditions are: The main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1; The application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; The application form is, after a possible request to make additions or changes, complete and filled out according to the instructions; The application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; The application is received before the deadline; The application is written in English. Additional conditions for the full application: The lead applicant has submitted a preliminary application that has been considered by NWO and selected for Phase 2; The full application has been submitted by a consortium consisting of at least a lead applicant, a co-applicant and collaboration partners; The application budget has been prepared according to the terms of this Call for proposals; All required annexes are, after any request for additions or amendments, complete and completed according to the instructions and conditions of this Call for proposals prepared and submitted. If applicable: the co-financiers meet the conditions set out in section 3.1.3; If applicable: the co-financing has been correctly and fully committed by means of co-financing statements, after any request for additions or amendments; the proposed project has a maximum duration of 5 years.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this Call, you will need to upload the following forms in ISAAC: Full proposal: budget; declarations of commitment of cooperation partners (see section 3.1.2); declaration co-funding from co-funders (mandatory if applicable, see section 3.1.3); letter guaranteeing the continuity of the project supervision (compulsory if applicable, see paragraph 3.1). form 'Statement and signature'.

title: Driving Urban Transitions Call 2024
programme: Driving Urban Transitions to a sustainable future (DUT) Driving Urban Transitions 2022-2025

characteristics.Research programme: Driving Urban Transitions to a sustainable future (DUT) Driving Urban Transitions 2022-2025

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The purpose of this Call for proposals is to support transnational research and/or innovation projects addressing urban challenges to help cities in their transition towards a more sustainable economy and functioning. Research projects which can be financed in this call are related to the following three transition pathways and in total six topics: Circular Urban Economies (CUE) CUE topic 1 – Creating a new paradigm for urban water cycles​ CUE topic 2 – Circular models for regenerating, repurposing, protecting and conserving urban space for biodiversity​ Circular Urban Economies (CUE) 15mC topic 1 – Advancing urban mobility: innovations for inclusive and youth-centric mobility systems​ 15mC topic 3 – Evidence for the urban mobility transition: data and indicators for effective decision-making​ Positive Energy Districts (PED) ​PED topic 2 – Towards the climate-neutral city: PEDs, system integration and urban strategies​ ​PED topic 3 – Managing the urban energy transition: data management and decision support systems​ All consortia are asked to choose a main transition pathway and may indicate which other pathways are involved, if any.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The following countries will participate in this DUT call: Austria, Belgium, Brazil, Bulgaria, Chinese Taipei, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Italy, Japan, Latvia, Lithuania, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Republic of Korea, Romania, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Türkiye and United Kingdom. Applications need to be submitted by research consortia involving, at least, three partners from three countries on this list that are eligible for funding by their respective Funding Agency. In addition, at least two eligible Applicants within a consortium must be from different EU Member States or Associated Countries eligible for EC co-funding in this Call. The added value of transnational collaboration should be clearly stated in the application. For applicants from Dutch universities and knowledge institutions, funding is available from NWO. Specifically for applicants from universities of applied sciences, funding is available from Taskforce for Applied Research SIA (Regieorgaan SIA). Please see Annex A of the Call for Proposals for details for both possibilities.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For research proposals in this round, consortia can apply for a maximum of € 325,000 in funding from NWO. Please note that funding from NWO is only available for strategic research within the framework of this call for proposals (for further explanation, see page 11  and Annex A in the call for proposals).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The submission deadline for pre-proposals is 14 November 2024, at 13:00 CET . The submission deadline for full proposals is 24 april 2025, at 13:00 CET . For more information about the submission process, see the step “Prepare” on this website and the DUT call for proposals.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals and full proposals will be assessed according to specific evaluation criteria by using a common evaluation form. The criteria are: Excellence - Intellectual merit, Impact and user engagement, Quality and efficiency of project implementation. Please see the DUT Call for proposals for the full criteria, including the sub-criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage, consortia are invited to submit a pre-proposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage will be invited to submit a full proposal. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed by an independent evaluation committee consisting of academics as well as practitioners and innovators. The proposals will be ranked in six ranking lists (based on Transition Pathway and research approach) based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. For NWO, the Executive Board will take this decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information about this call can be found on the website of the Driving Urban Transitions partnership: the DUT partnership and the DUT Call 2024 Contact persons at partners DUT Call Secretariat: Call@dutpartnership.eu

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For this Call for proposals, a two-stage procedure will be adopted. In the first stage, consortia are invited to submit pre-proposals. A pre-proposal includes a description of the partners, concise project description of no more than 13 pages, and budget indications with limited details fulfilling national/regional requirements. The pre-proposals declared to be eligible according to the transnational and national/regional criteria will be assessed by an Expert Panel, consisting of recognised international experts in relevant fields. The decision for the invitation to submit a full proposal in the second stage will be jointly taken by the participating funding agencies.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Pre-proposals and full proposals must be prepared in English using the designated mandatory international proposal forms. These forms can be found on the DUT website and are linked through the Call for proposals. Applicants who apply for funding at NWO must submit a completed NWO budget form separately at NWO when submitting their full proposal. This form can be found under ‘Downloads for your application’. Please send this form to: dutp@nwo.nl .

title: Photonic Technologies
target_groups: Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The “Photonic Technologies” call is a six-year program with the aim to further grow the market and supply chains in photonics in the Netherlands. The current innovative photonic technologies that are moving from the labs to companies are based on research that has been performed in the Netherlands over the past 20-30 years. This Call for Proposals aims to fund early stage scientific research (Technology Readiness Levels between 1-4) with the aim to deliver breakthrough photonic materials, devices and systems. The program therefore focuses on photonic academic research in the fields of applied and theoretical physics, electrical engineering, chemical engineering, material and life sciences.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers from universities, universities of applied and TO2 institutions.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a total NWO contribution of at least €400,000 and a maximum of €1,200,000 can be requested. If the total project costs are higher than €700,000, a contribution to the project costs (co-financing) by (a) user(s) is mandatory, namely 25% of the excess above €700,000. This can be in-kind, in-cash or a mix of both.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline pre-proposal: 27 March 2025 om 14:00:00 CET Deadline proposals: 29 april 2025 om 14:00:00 CEST

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The application(s) submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Scientific quality (60%) Match of researcher/research team and institution(s) with proposed research (20%) Contribution of proposed research to the ambition and explicit goal of the Call for proposals (20%) Procedure 27 March 2025 at 14:00 Deadline statement of intent 29 April 2025 at 14:00 Deadline proposals May and June 2025 Referees consulted June 2025 Applicants can submit a rebuttal September 2025 Assessment committee meeting October 2025 Decision by the board

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In a later phase if necessary, we may also ask for a signed statement from the co-financiers and/or an embedding guarantee.

title: Partnership Water4All – Water For Circular Economy
programme: WATER4ALL – Water Security for the Planet LEEGMAKEN 1820
target_groups: International Collaboration Interdisciplinary Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Interdisciplinary Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad
characteristics.Research programme: WATER4ALL – Water Security for the Planet LEEGMAKEN 1820

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The European Partnership ‘Water4All’ under Horizon Europe aims at systemic transformations and changes across the entire research – water innovation pipeline, fostering the matchmaking between problem owners and solution providers to ensure long term water security for all. It provides an overarching platform connecting national/local and European R&I programs, in line with the European Green Deal, Groundwater Directive, Water Framework Directive and the new EU Strategies for water management and Biodiversity for 2030. This third joint call of the Partnership focuses on the topic ‘Water For Circular Economy, addressing four central topics: Enhancement of water circularity in industries, Urban water circularity, Resource recovery, and valorisation, Economic, environmental, and social implications of water reuse and recovered products.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Please note: The national regulations on 'Eligible Applicants' have been updated, please ensure you are working with the latest version when preparing your application. Applications need to be submitted by research consortia including teams of at least a minimum of 3 countries participating in the call, including a minimum of two from EU Member States or EU Associated Countries. See the European Water4All Partnership Call for Proposals for details and participating countries. Full, associate and assistant professors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application (i.e. participate in a consortium and request NWO funding) if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the institutions listed in the Funding Organisation rules (NWO part) of the call for proposals. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal. An applicant may only request NWO funding for one project (part of a European consortium) in this call of the European Water4All Partnership. More information on the transnational eligibility criteria, and the eligibility criteria of other countries can be found in the European Water4All Partnership Call for Proposals and Funding Organisation Rules.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply
what_to_apply_for.text: For a research proposal in this round, a maximum of €325,000 can be applied for. The maximum project duration is 3 years. No limit has been set on the total budget for the project, the national funding agencies solely limit the budget that you can apply for at the national level. At NWO this maximum is €325,000. The budget modules that are available within this call for proposals are: Postdoc, Research leave, Personnel universities of applied sciences, Material Costs, Knowledge Utilisation, and Internationalisation. The maximum amounts for each module are stated in the Funding Organisation rules (part NWO) of the Call for Proposals. For the budget module “Postdoc”, a one-off individual bench fee of €5,000 is added on top of the salary costs to encourage the scientific career of the project employee funded by NWO. An explanation of the budget modules can be found with the ‘Downloads for your application’ under the heading 'Prepare'.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline for submitting a pre-proposal (compulsory) is 13 November 2024, 15:00 CET . Deadline for submitting a full proposal is 29 April 2025, 15:00 CET . Proposals need to be submitted through the electronic submission system of the European Water4All Partnership. More information can be found in the European Water4All Partnership Call for Proposals. NB Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit the ‘financial details’ form separately to NWO when uploading their full proposal. The format for this form is available with the ‘Downloads for your application’ under the heading 'Prepare'.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Pre-proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following two criteria: Excellence, Impact. Full proposals will be reviewed on the basis of the following three criteria: Excellence, Impact, Quality and efficiency of the implementation. Please see the full European Water4All Partnership Call for Proposals for the criteria including the sub-criteria. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage consortia are invited to submit a preproposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage can submit a full proposal. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed by at least three external evaluators consisting both of scientific and policy/management experts. The proposals will be ranked based on the criteria in the call text. Based on the ranking and the budget available, the national and regional funding agencies will take the funding decision. At NWO the Executive Board will take this decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information can be found on the Water4All website . Applications can be submitted via the electronic submission system of the European Water4All Partnership. Contact persons at partners Anne Petzold and Simon Frank Water4All 2024 JTC - Joint Call Secretariat Project Management Agency Karlsruhe (PTKA) Mail: water4All.call2024@ptka.kit.edu

fill_in_forms.text: Applicants who apply for funding at NWO need to submit a ‘financial details’ form separately at NWO when submitting their full proposal. This form can be found below, under ‘Downloads for your application’

title: Use of space infrastructure for earth observation and planetary research (GO)
programme: Use of space infrastructure for Earth observation and planetary research

characteristics.Research programme: Use of space infrastructure for Earth observation and planetary research

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This funding instrument is intended to support high-quality scientific research that makes substantial use of infrastructure in space. The infrastructure can be both (current or past) existing and planned infrastructure. Within this objective, the GO programme is open for scientific research in the area of Earth observation and planets (and other objects) within our solar system. For this call, your proposal must fit within the objective of the GO programme. You should therefore consider in good time whether your proposal fits within this objective. If you have any doubts about this, please contact one of the contact persons for the programme well before the deadline. They can advise you regarding the connection of your proposal with this call.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations that are mentioned in section 3.1 of the Call for proposals. It could be the case that the applicant’s tenure track agreement ends before the intended completion date of the project for which funding is applied for, or that before that date, the applicant’s tenured contract ends due to the applicant reaching retirement age. In that case, the applicant needs to include a statement from their employer in which the research organisation concerned guarantees that the project and all project members for whom funding has been requested will receive adequate supervision for the full duration of the project. NB: the details can be found in the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A GO grant has a maximum amount of € 360,000. The grant must be used for a single temporary scientific position (a PhD student or postdoc) in combination with one of the other available budget modules.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting applications is 6 may 2023 at 14:00 hours CEST

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications are assessed on the basis of the following three criteria: Scientific quality of the proposal – What? (weight 60%) Appropriateness of the application to the GO programme (weight 10%) Scientific and/or societal impact – Why (weight 30%) Procedure Admissible applications are assessed by independent external experts (referees). The referees’ reports are sent to the applicants for a written rebuttal. The assessment committee ranks the applications on the basis of the assessment criteria and advises the Domain Science Board about the quality of the applications. Based on this advice and the available funding, the Domain Science Board decides whether to grant or reject the applications. The proposal as a whole and criteria 2 (appropriateness with the programme) must be rated at least 'very good' to be eligible for the grant. Parallel assessment trajectories will be established for Earth observation and planetary research. When NWO appoints the members of the assessment committees, it will do its best to choose members whose expertise fits the applications.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Applications in the area of earth observation research and in the area of planetary research are assessed in parallel trajectories. The total grant ceiling for this Call for proposals is 3.24 million euros The available budget will be allocated across the two themes, with each theme having a set grant ceiling. A total of 2.16 million euros is available for the theme of Earth observation, and 1.08 million euros is available for the theme of planetary research. The definition of ‘substantial use of space infrastructure’ is different for each theme. Please refer to the Call for proposals (see section 2.2).

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For this call, your application must fit within the objective of the GO programme. You should therefore consider in good time whether your application fits within this objective. If you have any doubts about this, please contact one of the contact persons for the programme well before the deadline. They can advise you regarding the connection of your proposal with this call.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The steps involved in writing your application are: download the application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the funding instrument concerned); complete the application form; save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes (see section 3.3 in Call for proposals); fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. Applicants are expected to have informed the research organisation where they work about submitting the application and that the research organisation accepts the grant conditions of this Call for proposals.

title: Open Competition Domain Science – XS package 25-2

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The ENW-XS category is specifically intended to encourage curiosity-driven and bold research that involves the relatively rapid exploration of a promising idea. At the end of the ENW-XS project, researchers can decide for themselves whether or not to pursue this new line of research any further. One way in which this can be done is to submit a proposal within another funding programme in the Open Competition Domain Science (ENW-M and ENW-XL) or elsewhere.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers who have obtained a PhD and/or hold a professorial position can apply if they hold a salaried position for the duration of the application process and the project. ENW-XS is open for postdocs. Read the conditions in section 3.1 of the call for proposals for the possibilities of requesting your own salary in ENW-XS. Applicants who had a proposal dealt with in the immediately preceding ENW-XS package (25-1; deadline 18 February 2025) may not make a submission.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For research proposals in ENW-XS, applicants may apply for sums of up to €50,000. There are no requirements concerning the type of research to be conducted. Also, there are very few restrictions on the subjects of proposals. For instance, they could involve the appointment of temporary staff, material costs, the costs involved in performing ‘citizen science’, and travel expenses for commuting to other locations to conduct experiments. Postdocs are permitted to request their own salary. The appointment of PhD students is subject to additional conditions. For details of these conditions, see Annex 7.1 to the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Three deadlines for submitting proposals for ENW-XS projects are specified for 2025. For the most up-to-date ENW-XS webpage please see www.nwo.nl/enw-xs-en Package Submission deadlines Decision anticipated by 25-1 18 February 2025, at 14:00:00 CET 17 April 2025 25-2 6 May 2025, at 14:00:00 CEST 10 July 2025 25-3 16 September 2025, at 14:00:00 CEST 20 November 2025 Indication of timetable with respect to next deadline: Deadline for submission of proposals 6 May 2025, at 14:00:00 CEST Assessment of admissibility 6 May – 22 May 2025 Disclosure of personal interests 8-15 May 2025 Proposals sent to assessors 27 May 2025* NWO receives assessment reports 17 June 2025*, at 14:00:00 CEST NWO Domain Science Board reaches a decision 10 July 2025 * these dates are tentative and can differ with a few days, you will always get 10 working days for the assessment

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria High-risk/high-gain Is the proposed research innovative, highly speculative, and ground-breaking? Could the research proposal potentially have a scientific impact? Feasibility of the research plan The objective, rationale, and work plan must be clearly described. Can the project be carried out within the maximum period of one year and within the requested budget? Does the proposed research align with the aim of the Call, as detailed in Chapter 2? Proposals must be drawn up with a wide audience in mind, to enable assessors from all Domain Science disciplines to properly evaluate the research plan and the following assessment aspects. The assessors may award a lower ranking to any proposals that are drawn up in a way that is not accessible to those in other disciplines. Procedure All applications submitted for one of the deadlines are treated as a package. Per package the applications are separated into two clusters. Applicants choose in which of the following clusters their application is assessed: Life Sciences Physical and Exact Sciences Per cluster the applications are separated in group A and group B. The applications in groups A are assessed by the applicants in group B and vice versa. The NWO office will determine the final ranks of the applications based on the ranks submitted by the assessors. Two final rankings will be established per package; two for each cluster. There will be no opportunity for a rebuttal. The NWO Domain Science Board will decide whether to grant or refuse an application based on the results of the four rankings and the available financial means.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: When drawing up a proposal, be sure to make it anonymous: Applicants should include neither their own name nor those of any individuals in their research group; Applicants should not include details of any affiliations with institutes or knowledge institutions; Applicants should not include references to any publications that they have authored or co-authored; Applicants should not include the names of any partners (or potential partners). Applicants should be aware that they will be required to assess proposals from all Domain Science disciplines in the chosen cluster. Therefore, the own proposal must be written with a wide audience in mind.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Mandatory annexes: budget form (Excel) and information form. Optional annexes: declaration appointment PhD or physician assistant (see Annex 7.1 in the Call)

title: Research into Collections with a Colonial Context
programme: Research into Collections with a Colonial Context
target_groups: Diversity International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: Diversity International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad Starting Researchers
characteristics.Research programme: Research into Collections with a Colonial Context

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: There are many questions surrounding objects and collections from a colonial context, including how they should be handled, their significance in their historical contexts and in modern-day changing societies, and questions about their repatriation to their country of origin. Research on such objects and collections requires specific expertise from and intensive collaboration with countries of origin, as this is essential to achieving true understanding of the collections and more equitable discussions. Therefore, this call aims to stimulate knowledge development, knowledge exchange and cooperation between researchers from countries of origin into museum collections and objects in the Netherlands with a colonial context, and researchers and museums in the Netherlands.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Exchange grant Researchers from organisations in countries or regions of origin of collections may submit an application if they have a paid position for the duration of the requested research at a public university or other public research organisation; and have at least a Master’s degree or comparable qualification. Consortium grant The applicant must be a full, associate or assistant professor, or other researcher with a comparable position and a PhD, with a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at a university or a research organisation in a country of origin. The university or research organisation must meet the following cumulative criteria: is a public institute and carries out its research independently; receives at least 50 percent public funding; is not-for-profit other than for the purpose of carrying out further research; its researchers enjoy freedom of publication in international scientific journals

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For Exchange grants, a maximum of €35,000 can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed research is twelve months. Funds can be requested for a living allowance, travel, and research costs, for a maximum of twelve months. For Consortium grants, a maximum of €350,000 can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed research is 60 months. Funds can be requested for personnel, such as PhD students, Postdocs and Non-scientific staff, research leave, material costs, knowledge utilisation, investments and internationalisation. More information on the budget modules and how to apply can be found in the call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The submission deadline for the first assessment round is on Thursday 6 March 2025, 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications will be assessed based on the following criteria: Quality of the research proposal Quality of the consortium Societal impact More information about the criteria can be found in the call for proposals. Procedure An external, independent assessment committee will be installed for this Call for proposals, consisting of international representatives from research and practice with knowledge of the field. Your proposal will be submitted for comments to several members of the assessment committee (the pre-advisers), who will provide a written substantive and reasoned first assessment of the proposal. The applicant then has the opportunity to form a response to address the committee’s remarks. Following a committee meeting, the committee draws up a written recommendation addressed to the NWO Executive Board about the quality and rankings of the proposals, which will determine the final qualifications and take a decision about awarding or rejecting the proposals.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Requirements and expectations around applications may be different in The Netherlands than in countries of applicants. Researchers may also not be familiar with aspects like NWO’s submission system or the budget modules. To help with this, NWO will organise several webinars that focus specifically on the administrative aspects, and organise question hours where consortia can ask questions and advice about completing the necessary forms. NWO will also organise matchmaking events for this call for proposals. More information on the matchmaking events and webinars can be found here at the research program page. NWO may require the applicant to submit additional documents, within ten working days prior to the submission deadline, to prove the stated requirements for applying are met.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the application form, we ask that you also submit (in PDF): Signed declarations of commitment from the organisations of the lead applicant, co-applicants and collaboration partners; If applicable: signed declarations ensuring continuity of project supervision; If applicable: signed declarations of co-funding. For applications for Consortium grants, a budget form should also be submitted (Excel).

title: NWA Knowledge development ecological effects of wind farms on sea
programme: Knowledge development ecological effects of offshore wind farms
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge development ecological effects of offshore wind farms

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This NWA programme focuses on effects of wind farms in the North Sea on ecological systems above and below water. With the Wozep and MONS programmes, the government is looking at these effects (for information on Wozep and MONS). Within this NWA programme, innovative and deeper understanding of essential and steering processes is being sought. Existing research with models will be transcended by further exploring assumptions made so far and adding new perspectives. This programme is complementary to the pathways ongoing within the MONS and Wozep multi‐year programmes. Insights from the research can form the basis for better‐informed, more integrated and future‐proof policy and decision‐making.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are to be submitted by the main applicant on behalf of the consortium. The main applicant has participated in the Sandpit (see Section 2.4.1, 3.3.1 and 4.2.2). There are four categories of participants within a consortium: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Cooperation partner(s); Co-funder(s) (optional). A consortium should consist of at least a main applicant, a co-applicant and cooperation partner.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In this Call for proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of a Sandpit. The aim of a Sandpit is to establish cooperation in new collaborations and joint research proposals. Sandpits focus on collaboration in a single consortium consisting of the selected parties. During a Sandpit, the selected participants work jointly on site to develop a research project, under the guidance of external facilitators and content supporters. For participation in a Sandpit, selection takes place through an Expression of Interest, see the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum grant amount of €5.200.000,‐ can be applied for. The applicant and co‐applicants may/can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation among other things. Co-funding is not compulsory within this Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting an Expression of Interest for participation in the Sandpit is Tuesday 6 May 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST . The Sandpit will take place from Monday 22  September 2025 up until and including Friday 26 September 2025. The deadline for submitting the proposal is T hursday 11 September 2025, before 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Assessment criteria Expression of Interest: Academic/professional attributes (40%), Personal competences and motivation (60%). Assessment criteria proposal: Problem definition and analysis (20%), Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%). Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: Submission of the Expression of Interest; Admissibility of the Expression of Interest; Selection of participants for Sandpit; Decision‐making; Participation in Sandpit; Submission of the proposal; Admissibility of the proposal; Pre-advicing assessment committee; Assessment committee meeting including interview; Decision‐making.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: NWO will assess your Expression of Interest for participation in the Sandpit against the conditions set out in this Call for proposals. Your Expression of Interest will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. Only individuals invited by the NWO Governing Board can participate in the Sandpit. It is important that participants attend the Sandpit in person. Invited participants who do not attend the Sandpit will be excluded from further proceedings unless there is force majeure, as referred to in the Force Majeure Extension Scheme .

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: In this Call for proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of a Sandpit. The Sandpit will take place from Monday 8 September 2025 up until and including Friday 12 September 2025. The aim of a Sandpit is to establish cooperation in new collaborations and joint research proposals. Sandpits focus on collaboration in a single consortium consisting of the selected parties. During a Sandpit, the selected participants work jointly on site to develop a research project, under the guidance of external facilitators and content supporters. For participation in a Sandpit, selection takes place through an Expression of Interest, see Section 4.2 of the Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For participation in the Sandpit, you must fill in the Expression of Interest form. You can download this form below.

NWA Knowledge development ecological effects of wind farms on sea | expression of interest form

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The SSH Open Competition XS Call for proposals is specifically intended to encourage curiosity-driven and innovative research that involves the relatively rapid exploration of a promising idea. The characteristics of SSH-XS research are: the proposed research is innovative; it is not certain in advance whether the intended objectives will be achieved; the result of each project contributes to the advancement of existing science and new scientific insights.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers with a promising idea who obtained their PhD between 5 and 10 years ago may submit an application if they have a paid appointment for at least the duration of the application process and the duration of the proposed project. The submission deadline for the round is the reference date of the graduation date. The graduation date on the PhD diploma (degree) is leading. For round 2, the applicant must have obtained their PhD between 9 May 2015 and 8 May 2020. Extra conditions: an SSH-XS application has a single applicant and no co-applicants; an applicant may submit a maximum of one application in the SSH-XS, call round 2025; an applicant - when using the extension scheme - may not submit an application for multiple types of funding instruments within the NWO Open Competition programme during the same calendar year (2025); applicants may not be part of an XS project for which the end date has not yet passed at the time of submission. Please note: the extension scheme applies to the SSH Open Competition XS in 2025. For more information and the online application form for extension see: https://www.nwo.nl/en/extension-clause .

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals a maximum of 50,000 euro can be applied for. The minimum duration of the proposed project is 6 months and the maximum duration is 12 months. Applicants are required to draw up a realistic budget and explain why the spending choices are relevant to the project. This explanation must cover all of the personnel requested. Please only apply for funding that is vital to realise the project. A more detailed explanation of the budget modules can be found in the annexe to this Call for proposals (Section 7.1).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: For 2025, 3 deadlines for submitting applications are specified: Round 1: 6 March 2025, before 14:00:00 CET (closed) Round 2: 8 May 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST Round 3: 18 September 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST When you submit your application in ISAAC, you will also need to enter some details online. Therefore, please start submitting your application at least one day before the deadline of the round. Applications that are submitted after the deadline will not be taken into consideration.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessors who rank the applications will focus on the following criteria. These criteria are not weighted. At the discretion of an assessor, both of these aspects may be used for the purposes of ranking. However, that ranking must be accompanied by a short written explanation to substantiate the given rank based on the assessment criteria listed below. - Innovative aspect Is the proposed research innovative and groundbreaking? Does the proposed research contribute to existing and new scientific insights? Does the research proposal potentially have a scientific impact? - Feasibility of the research plan The objective and work plan must be clearly described. Does the methodology seem suitable to achieve the indicated project goal? Is the planning of the research plan appropriate and are the activities included in the planning with sufficient clarity? Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the relevant panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria described above. The SSH disciplines and associated (sub)disciplines are listed on the NWO website: https://www.nwo.nl/en/overview-panels-domain-social-sciences-and-humani… . The assessors may award a lower ranking to any applications that are drawn up in a way that is not accessible to those in other disciplines. Procedure The applicant determines by which panel the application will be processed by means of the SSH main discipline specified in the information form (see Section 4.2.3 of the Call for proposals). Applications processed per panel are divided into 2 groups, so that each panel consists of Group A and Group B. In principle, the classification takes place at the time of submission to NWO, taking into account the personal interests declared by the applicants. The assessors and applicants associated with a particular package of proposals do not meet. NWO will decide the final rankings of the applications, based on the rankings submitted by the assessors. Per round, 6 final rankings will be established – per panel one for Group A and one for Group B. There will be no opportunity to dispute the rankings. The SSH Domain Board will base its decision about awarding or rejecting the applications on the results of the 6 final rankings, the assessment reports and the available funding.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The research idea needs to be drawn up anonymously, which means the application form meets the following conditions: applicants should not include their own name nor the name of any individuals in their research group; applicants should not include details of any affiliations with institutes or knowledge institutions; applicants should not include references to any publications that they have authored or co-authored; applicants should not include the names of any (potential) partners. Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the relevant panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Compulsory annexes to a proposal in this Call for proposals are the Budget form (Excel) and the Information form (pdf).

title: The Water-Food nexus in rainfed agri-food systems in Sub-Saharan Africa: socio-economics and governance

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call aims to enable scaling-up of soil moisture retention practices among smallholder farmers in rainfed agri-food systems* and as such helps to mitigate negative effects from increased severity of dry spells on agricultural production. The specific objectives are: Gain insights in the socio-economic barriers and opportunities in the enabling environment for scaling of water retention practices with a changing climate; Gain insights in and provide options for governance functions and governance attributes that support scaling-up of moisture retention practices; To strengthen cooperation among CGIAR researchers, Dutch researchers and local partners. Consortia can choose whether to focus on either the first or the second objective, always in combination with the third objective, or to focus on all three objectives at the same time. *rainfed agri-food systems include: cropping systems, mixed farming systems (e.g. with food-fodder-livestock) and pastoralist systems.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Pre-proposals and full proposals are to be submitted by the main applicant on behalf of the consortium. There are four categories of participants within a consortium: 1. Main applicant; 2. Co-applicant(s); 3. Cooperation partner(s); 4. Co-funder(s) (optional). A consortium should consist of at least a a member of the research staff of a CGIAR research centre – as main or co-applicant; a researcher from a Dutch knowledge institute – as main or co-applicant; a researcher from a knowledge institute from the country where the research takes place – as co-applicant; a stakeholder from a governmental organisation from the country where the research takes place – as cooperation partner. The conditions for each type of participant are explained in more detail in Section 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a maximum of €1,400,000 can be applied for in total. The maximum duration of the proposed project is four years. The budget modules (including the maximum amount) available for this Call for proposals are listed in Section 3.2 of the Call for Proposals. Personnel from CGIAR centres/Alliances use the budget module ‘Personnel at CGIAR centres’. Personnel from research organisations outside the Netherlands which are not CGIAR use the budget module ‘Money follows Cooperation’.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting pre-proposals is 15 October 2024, before 14:00:00 CEST . The deadline for submitting full proposals is 13 May 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The pre-proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Societal relevance of the proposed project (50% weighting) Scientific importance of the proposed project (25% weighting) Quality of the collaboration (25% weighting) The full proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria (carrying equal weight): Societal relevance of the proposed project Scientific quality of the proposed project Quality of the collaboration, capacity strengthening and value for money More information on the assessment criteria and the sub-criteria can be found in section 4.3 of the call for proposals. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: submission of the pre-proposal; − admissibility of the pre-proposal; pre-assessment by assessment committee; rebuttal; assessment committee meeting; decision-making; proposal development workshop; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; pre-assessment by assessment committee; rebuttal; − assessment committee meeting; decision-making. An external, independent international assessment committee will be assigned for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from science and practice with knowledge of the field.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The steps involved in writing your application are: download the application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the funding instrument concerned); complete the application form; save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes; − fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. The application must comply with the formal submission conditions listed in section 3.4 of this Call for Proposals and respond to the aims of the programme. Applicants have to make use of the instructions and explanatory notes in the application form.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Standard forms are available for both the preproposal and the full proposal. If applicable, we ask you to include ‘statement appointment and project supervision’ (see paragraphs 3.1 in the call for proposals) for the proposal as well as the full proposal. All attachments, with the exception of the budget form, should be uploaded as PDF files. The budget form can be uploaded to ISAAC as an Excel file. Pre-proposals Pre-proposal form; Narrative CVs of the main applicant and all co- applicants, and cooperation partners; If applicable: Statement appointment and project supervision. Full proposals - Full proposal form; Budget form (excel); Declaration of support; Draft consortium agreement; Narrative CVs of the main applicant and all co- applicants, and cooperation partners; If applicable, Declaration co-funding. Annexes must be uploaded in ISAAC separately from the application. The budget must be submitted in ISAAC as an Excel file. All of the other annexes, except for the budget, must be submitted as PDF files (without encryption). Any annexes other than those stated above are not permitted.

title: NGF: Towards the future Learning communities as drivers of the green hydrogen economy
programme: Towards the future. Learning communities as drivers of the green hydrogen economy National Growth Fund HCA Fase 2: Op weg naar de toekomst
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Towards the future. Learning communities as drivers of the green hydrogen economy National Growth Fund HCA Fase 2: Op weg naar de toekomst

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The key question is ‘How can we forge a responsive ecosystem – in which working, learning, and innovating are tightly intertwined – that integrates knowledge and skills development for hydrogen professionals (both present and future), to reduce time-to-job?’ The success of the hydrogen transition hinges on acquiring a great deal of new knowledge. Therefore, GroenvermogenNL invests not only in human capital but also in R&D, demonstrations, and pilot projects. To implement this new knowledge in the labour market, GroenvermogenNL links knowledge development with regional learning communities where working, learning, and innovation converge. In these learning communities, research findings are implemented and further refined. In this way, GroenvermogenNL achieves skills valorisation by valorising new knowledge from research and innovations through the development of skills among professionals and students. GroenvermogenNL encourages applying consortia to seek collaboration with regional learning communities, to foster skills valorisation. In the regions, these learning communities play a key part in fostering a responsive ecosystem. Thus, establishing and/or expanding learning communities is a crucial component of proposals in this Call for proposals.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Complete proposals must be submitted by the main applicant and several co-applicants. A proposal must be prepared by a consortium, which may include participants other than the applicants. There are four different categories of participant in a consortium: Main applicant, Co-applicants, Collaborative partners, Co-funders. The conditions for each participant are further explained in the following sections. Only main applicants and co-applicants applying under this Call for proposals are eligible to receive grants.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: In this call for proposals, the maximum total amount that can be requested per proposal, per region is: Region and maximum grant Northern Netherlands (Groningen, Friesland, Drenthe) € 4,450,000, Western Netherlands (Zuid Holland) € 4,450,000, Southwestern Netherlands (Zeeland) € 3,000,000, Northwestern Netherlands (Noord Holland, Utrecht, Flevoland) € 4,450,000, Southeastern Netherlands (Limburg) € 3,000,000, Eastern Netherlands Lifeport (Gelderland, Overijssel) € 4,450,000, Eastern Netherlands Brainport (Noord Brabant) € 3,000,000. The proposed project has a maximum duration of four years. See the call for proposals for more information.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: 15 October 2024, 14:00:00 CEST Workshop registration deadline Weeks 45 and 46 2024 Workshop day 1 Week 3 2025 Workshop day 2 13 May 2025, 14:00:00 CET Deadline for proposals May 2025 Assessment of conditions for submission by NWO July-September 2025 Preliminary advice by members of the assessment committee September-October 2025 Interview and Assessment committee meeting November 2025 Decision by the Board December 2025 Notification grant decision

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantively assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Criterion 1: Compliance with the R&D&I framework, Criterion 2: Problem definition and problem analysis (20%), Criterion 3: Quality of the consortium (40%), Criterion 4: Quality of the research plan (40%). Procedure An external, independent assessment committee will be appointed for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from science and everyday practice with knowledge of the field. This assessment committee is tasked with assessing the submitted proposals and the associated documentation, both in relation to one another and on their own merits, based on the assessment criteria set out in this Call for proposals (see Section 4.3.1). Given the expertise within the assessment committee, NWO has opted to use the provision in Article 2.2.4, paragraph 2, of the NWO Grant Rules 2017, to assess the proposals without involving referees.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Two Casco workshops will take place between 4-15 November 2024 and in week 3 of January 2025 as part of the call procedure. The first workshop will be organised regionally, while the second will take place at a central location. Participation in both workshops is mandatory for the Main applicant of the application. During these workshops, participating parties per region will work towards one regional Consortium and one proposal per region. Deadline for application is 15 October 2024, 14:00:00 CET.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Proposals can be submitted after participation in the workshops. Follow these steps when drawing up your proposal: Chapter 3: Conditions for applicants / NGF: Towards the future download the application form and the available forms for the mandatory annexes from the ISAAC online web application system or from NWO’s website (on the grant page of this grant instrument); complete the application form; complete and sign the mandatory annexes where applicable; save the application form as a PDF and save the annexes as described below; submit the application form in ISAAC, together with the mandatory annex (or annexes) complete the requested information online in ISAAC. Mandatory annexes: Budget form; Statement General Block Exemption Regulation (GBER) form(s) (see Section 3.5.1); Declaration Co-funding form(s) (if co-funding is involved, see Section 3.5.7); Statement Appointment and Project Supervision form(s) (if applicable, see Section 3.1.1); Statements and Signature form; Project Participants form. Annexes must be drawn up according to the template provided by NWO. Annexes must be uploaded in ISAAC separately from the proposal. All annexes, except for the Budget and Project Participants forms, must be submitted as PDF files (without encryption). The Budget and Project Participants forms must be submitted in ISAAC as Excel files. Annexes other than those listed above are not permitted. Your proposal must be written in English. Proposals can only be submitted via the ISAAC online application system. Any proposals that are not submitted via ISAAC will not be processed. As the main applicant, you are required to submit the proposal via your own personal ISAAC account. It is important to start work on your proposal in ISAAC in good time: if you do not yet have an ISAAC account, then you should create this in good time to avoid any potential registration problems; any new organisations must also be added to ISAAC by NWO; you also need to submit other details online.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: NWO will assess your proposal against the conditions listed below. Your proposal will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. You are asked to remain available after submitting a proposal to make any administrative corrections that may be needed, and thereby (still) meet the submission requirements. Chapter 3: Conditions for applicants / NGF: Towards the future These requirements are as follows: following any requests for additions or changes, the Workshop Registration Form must be completed as instructed; the main applicant and co-applicants must meet the conditions set out in Section 3.1; the proposal must meet the Declaration on Research Assessment (DORA) guidelines, as described in Section 4.1, and must be submitted through the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the proposal must be received before the set deadline; the proposal must be written in English; the proposed project has a maximum duration of four years; following any requests for additions or changes, the application form must be completed as instructed; following any requests for additions or changes, all of the mandatory annexes must be completed according to the instructions, and drawn up and submitted according to the conditions for this grant round. the proposal’s budget must be drawn up in accordance with the conditions of this Call for proposals; applicants who use the ICS rate system must give their consent for NWO to request their ICS rates from the RVO; the proposal must meet the conditions for the learning community as set out in this Call for proposals (see Section 2.1.3).

title: Diamond Open Access
target_groups: Diversity Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Diversity Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this programme is to support specifically Diamond Open Access for journals. Funding is for 2-year projects and aimed at existing journals that want to transition to Diamond Open Access. Editors, academic societies, foundations and associations that want to “flip” their existing academic journal(s) to a non-profit Diamond Open Access model can apply for funding in this Call for proposals.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applicants (main and co-applicants) must have a permanent or temporary contract (that covers at least the project duration) at one of the following organisations: universities and universities of applied sciences (UAS) as referred to in Article 1.8 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act and universities listed in the Policy Rules for Universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; university medical centres by which is meant academic hospitals as referred to in Article 1.13 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act; institutes affiliated to the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; Naturalis Biodiversity Center; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center. Personnel with a zero-hour appointment cannot apply. There are two categories of participants: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Additional conditions for participants: the main applicant must already be a member of the editorial board of the journal for at least one year, for which the application is submitted; a Diamond Open Access application has one main applicant and up to four co-applicants; the main applicant submits the application on behalf of the consortium (if applicable); the main applicant cannot be involved as a main applicant or co-applicant in other applications for the Call for proposals Diamond Open Access; journals must have a publication history of more than three years at the time of submission; for journals that seek to relaunch, the publication history of the journal that is left behind must be at least three years; (Trade) journals must conduct a form of academic quality control (peer review) in their editorial process. There are no co-financiers in this Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of € 50,000 can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 24 months. The grant will be transferred in the form of a lump sum and is intended to cover the costs related to the transition of an existing academic (peer reviewed) subscription-based (trade) journal to a non-profit Diamond Open Access model that ensures the sustainability of the journal after the project end date. The main applicant can budget costs for personnel and material costs. Appendix 7.1 explains which costs are eligible for funding in this Call for proposals. The main applicant should draft a realistic budget and explain how the budgeted costs are relevant to the project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting applications is 15 May 2025, before 14:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Alignment with the scope of this Call for proposals (30%). Feasibility of the proposal (40%): How realistic is the project plan? How relevant are the expertise and skills of the team for the execution of the project?) Completeness of identified risk and adequacy of risk management. Adequacy and substantiation of the budget. Open Access publishing standards (10%): To what extent have the applicants embraced and/or applied open access standards to the journal? How relevant and convincing are the plans for sustaining the journal after the end date of the project (20%)? Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: Submission of the proposal deadline 15 May 2025 Admissibility of the proposals / eligibility check by NWO May/June 2025 Rebuttal by applicants June/July 2025 Assessment committee meeting August 2025 Decision-making October 2025 An external, independent assessment committee will be assigned for this Call for proposals. The final decision on granting will be done by the NWO board, based on the advice of the assessment committee.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The proposal consists of an application form and a budget form. In the application form, you need to explain to what extent does the proposed project fit within the scope of this Call for proposals, the feasibility of your proposal and the long-term sustainability plans.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Please upload the following documents as separate files via ISAAC together with the application form, the completed budget form.

title: KIC Together for a better life with inherited heart disease: Innovations for early detection, risk prediction and therapy
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Partnerships (KIC 2020-2023) Together for a better life with inherited heart disease; Innovations for early detection, risk prediction and therapy (KIC)
target_groups: Collaboration with social partners Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with social partners Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Partnerships (KIC 2020-2023) Together for a better life with inherited heart disease; Innovations for early detection, risk prediction and therapy (KIC)

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this call for proposals is to fund research that provides insights and results that relevant parties will use to improve early detection, risk prediction and new treatments. Within this call are three inextricably linked sub-themes: early detection, risk prediction and therapy. Early detection: Hereditary heart diseases can cause severe symptoms or death suddenly and at a young age. Obduction and DNA testing of young deceased people are not always performed in the Netherlands, which requires improvement. Family members should also be better detected and invited for genetic counselling, with an emphasis on interdisciplinary collaboration. Risk prediction: current knowledge is insufficient to properly predict who will develop symptoms of hereditary heart disease and when. This makes treatment decisions complex and may lead to overtreatment or undertreatment. Better risk prediction can improve quality of life and optimise care. Greater understanding of genetic and non-genetic causes is needed, as well as better data infrastructure at national level. Therapy: Current treatments include lifestyle advice, medication and/or ICDs, but have drawbacks and are not always effective. Improving quality of life and developing new treatments, including gene therapies, is necessary. Innovative research can lead to improved survival rates and prospects for a cure for a broader group of patients.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Pre-proposals and full applications are submitted by a main applicant and one or more co-applicants. An application is set up by a consortium, in which other participants are involved in addition to the applicants. In this consortium the main applicant and co-applicants together represent at least two of the three research fields (humanities, natural sciences and social sciences), in which the natural sciences are always included. In addition, collaboration partners and potential co-funders should be included. For the full application, a consortium should consist of at least a main applicant, co-applicants, cooperation partners and at least two co-funders. In addition, at least two experts by experience, with relevant experience in the subject of the application, should always be part of the consortium.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The available budget for this call totals €7,816,000. The grant amounts to a maximum of 90% of the total project size. The minimum co-financing requirement is 10% of the total project budget (in kind/in cash) from a private party.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting sub proposals is 24 September 2024, at 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting the full application is 27 May 2025, at 14:00:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem posed and problem analysis, contribution to solution Expected impact and route to impact Quality of the consortium Quality of the research Criteria 1 and 2 each carry a 25% weighting in the overall assessment, criterion 3, a weighting of 30% and criterion 4, a weighting of 20%. Procedure The call consists of 2 phases. Sub-proposals are submitted in phase 1. After assessment by an external committee, the consortia of several sub-proposals are selected and invited to jointly develop one full proposal (phase 2). Development of the full proposal is supported by workshops. Details about the procedure are described in chapter 4 of the call text

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: In phase 1, sub-proposals are submitted. This is a concise application that outlines which sub-themes the applicant, co-applicants and collaboration partners want to address and how these contribute to the objectives and impact described in the call. In addition, the sub-application describes opportunities for collaboration with sub-proposals working on other sub-themes.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For the phase 1 sub-proposal, only the application form that has been made available must be completed. No attachments to the application can be submitted at this stage. The forms and templates for phase 2 full proposal will be shared with the applicants of the selected sub-proposals at the start of phase 2.
