title: Safety and Energy transition
target_groups: Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the Faculty of Impact is to offer talented researchers in all disciplines the opportunity to combine entrepreneurship with science. These researchers should have an innovative idea that can potentially create an impact on society. This can be a tool to stimulate behavioural change, a technological innovation, a new business model, et cetera. At this stage, however, there is no question of a functional product, service or policy, or of a commercial company. The idea must, however, have been validated by a pilot in a laboratory setting (corresponding to a Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of approx. 4) or by relevant stakeholders (corresponding to a Society Readiness Level (SRL) of approx. 4)[1]. The fellows will be given the opportunity during a two-year support programme to further develop and scale up their ideas. [1] See section 6.2 for a description of TRL and SRL levels.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: PhD, Engineering Doctorate (EngD) and Professional Doctorate (PD) students and Postdocs who have completed their PhD research, EngD programme, PD track or ongoing Postdoc project before the FoI programme start date (see section 4.2.8 for the start date). or Researchers with positions of university (senior) lecturer, professor or other researchers with independent research experience.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of € 200,000 subsidy can be applied for per project. The duration of the project is 24 months. The available budget modules are: Personnel: the applicant can declare costs for 0.8-1.0 personnel. Material: financing can be requested for project-specific material costs. A maximum of €15,000 applies to these costs. The explanation of the available budget modules, including rates, can be found in section 6.4 of the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting full proposals is Tuesday 03 June 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantively assessed based on the following criteria: Quality of the idea; Social impact for the chosen theme; Quality of the applicant. Each of the three criteria is given equal weight and is given a score between 1 (excellent) and 9 (very poor). The quality requirement is then applied in this way: fellows are only eligible for funding when their score does not exceed 5.4 (good) for each of the criteria and the average of all criteria does not exceed 3.4 (very good). Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: submission of the statement of intent; admissibility of the proposal; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making. Due to both the expertise present in the assessment committee and the small size of the grant, NWO has decided with regard to the assessment of these applications to exercise the option outlined in Article 2.2.4, paragraph 2, of the NWO Grant Rules 2024, to assess all applications without involving referees.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: For more information about the Faculty of Impact, please refer to its own website: www.facultyofimpact.nl

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: In the application form, the applicant must describe his idea and its social impact on the chosen theme. In addition, the applicant must motivate why he or she wants to participate in the Faculty of Impact program.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In addition to a written request, you must also include a link to a downloadable SURFDrive of SURFFilesender video link containing the short video. The video must not be longer than 3 minutes. There is no need to hire professional equipment or help. The video is judged on its content, not its quality. A quality equivalent to the HD quality of a mobile phone is acceptable. The video must be recorded in English. Keep a broad audience in mind: your well-educated neighbours should be able to understand your story. Check whether audio files or visual materials used are royalty-free. In addition, the applicant must upload four mandatory attachments to ISAAC in addition to the application itself: Budget; Letter of support from the applicant's supervisor; Letter of support from the organization's Knowledge Transfer Office; Letter of support from the department director.

title: KIC Greenhouses in transition: opportunities in water, energy and agriculture
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Partnerships (KIC 2020-2023) Greenhouses in Transition
target_groups: International Collaboration Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Partnerships (KIC 2020-2023) Greenhouses in Transition

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The call Greenhouses in transition aims to accelerate the transition from Controlled Environment Agriculture (CEA) to a circular and sustainable industry. As CEA is strongly linked to the water-energy-food nexus, the intended transition must go beyond sector boundaries on a technological and societal level. A focus on exclusively technological innovation in one sector is therefore not enough for this call. Inter- and transdisciplinary research is needed to arrive at cross-sectoral solutions. A regional, social and economic perspective is therefore required in addition to a technical perspective.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The full proposal in phase 2 is submitted by a consortium consisting of consortium partners based in the Netherlands and the United States, whereby the following conditions apply: the consortium exists of consortium partners who have been selected in phase 1 to submit an integrated full proposal; at least two main applicants, of which one NWO applicant NWO and one FFAR applicant (Dutch main applicant submits the application at NWO); at least one co-applicant at NWO and at least one cooperation partner and co-funder based in the Netherlands; at least one co-applicant with FFAR, and one cooperation partner and co-funder based in the United States; the consortium must be balanced in terms of participation consortium members who are based in the Netherlands and consortium members based in the United States. Applicants based in the Netherlands: Persons working at Dutch universities, KNAW and NWO institutes, and universities of applied sciences may act as applicants. Collaboration partners have an interest in the research results and can consist of governments, companies and other social parties. Cooperation partners cannot receive a subsidy for their own employees. Applicants based in the United States: A researcher working in a permanent position in a recognized academic institution or a publicly funded R&D institution/laboratory based in the U.S. See the call text for all terms and conditions.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: In phase 2, a maximum of € 5,475,140 is available to finance one project, of which €2,737,570 can be applied for by NWO applicants and $2,940,829 (approximately €2,737,570) can be applied for by FFAR applicants. Co-financing of 10% in cash/in kind is mandatory in phase 2. See the call text for details. Project duration: Maximum 5 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline for submission of full proposals in phase 2 is Thursday 5 June 2025, before 14:00:00 hours CEST (5 June 2025 before 08:00:00 EDT) in ISAAC.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The full proposal in phase 2 will be assessed against the following four criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Each criterion consists of multiple sub-criteria which are listed in paragraph 4.3.1 of the call text. Procedure In phase 2, selected sub-proposals jointly develop one full proposal. Development of the full proposal is supported by workshops. Details about the procedure are described in chapter 3 of the call text.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: In fase 2 an integrated full proposal will be submitted by a joint consortium consisting of the sub-applicants which were selected in phase 1 to develop a full integrated proposal. This full proposal integrates the three themes of the call: Themes: Theme 1: Coupling and designing technologies that bridge CEA, energy, water or urban planning sectors; Theme 2: Theme 2: Understanding societal complexity of embedding new cross-sectoral technologies/innovative approaches to enable sustainable social innovation Theme 3: Investigating the potential ecological effects of a new cross-sectoral technology/innovative approach The full proposal includes an impact plan, research plan, information on the consortium and a detailed budget (separate forms are used for the FFAR subsidy budget and the NWO subsidy budget).

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The forms and templates for phase 2 full proposal have been shared with the applicants of the selected sub-proposals at the start of phase 2.

title: NWA Key Enabling Methodologies (KEMs) - The utilisation of Key Enabling Methodologies in complex societal transition issues

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The programme ‘NWA Key Enabling Methodologies (KEMs) - The utilisation of Key Enabling Methodologies in complex societal transition issues’ enables research on Key Enabling Methodologies and contributes to the development of a framework for the coherent deployment of these methods and techniques in policy development for complex transitions. By examining the deployment of KEMs in the context of departmental policy-making from different perspectives, the working principle of the KEMs is examined through systematic reflection, learning about usability and thus sharpening and developing the methods.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: An application must be submitted by a consortium. A consortium should consist of at least one main applicant, co-applicant and collaboration partner. All participants should play an active role in formulating the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project. The conditions per participant are further explained in the Call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: This Call for proposals entails two phases: the submission of a research idea and participation in two collaborative workshops, and the full application submission phase. Following the submission of the research idea, two consecutive collaborative workshops will be organised by NWO. The person who submits the research idea will automatically be registered for the collaborative workshops. The workshops will take place on Wednesday 5 March and 26 March 2025 in the afternoon in Utrecht. Participation in the workshops is mandatory for the main applicant who wants to submit an application in phase 2. Should this person be unable to attend, a replacement must be arranged, working within the same organisation. All parties involved in a submitted research idea can apply for the workshops. In addition, the workshops are open to other interested parties. Parties not yet affiliated with a research idea before the workshop can view the submitted ideas on the NWO website. This allows them to determine whether affiliation with a research idea might be useful. Registration for the workshops is possible via the form on the programme page on the NWO website. Deadline for registration for the workshops is 20 February 2025 at 14:00:00 CET. Civil society organisations are specifically invited to submit a research idea and/or participate in the workshops. Submit research idea

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply
what_to_apply_for.text: A minimum of €2,850,000 and a maximum of €3,050,000 subsidy can be applied for per project. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 72 months/6 years. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. The available budget modules (including maximum amounts) are listed in the Call for proposals. Apply only for what is essential to carry out the project. The rates and an explanation of these budget modules can be found in Annex 7.1 of the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for the submission of research ideas is 13 February 2025, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline to r egister for the collaborative workshops is 20 February 2025, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for the submission of proposals is 12 June 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem posed and problem analysis, contribution to solution, Expected impact and route to impact, Quality of the consortium, Quality of the research. Criteria 1 and 2 each carry a weighting of 20% in the overall assessment, and criteria 3 and 4 each carry a weighting of 30%. Procedure The deadline for submitting proposals is 12 June 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST. For the assessment of the proposals, the committee will make use of pre-advices and interviews will be conducted. A final decision on funding by the NWO Executive Board is expected in October 2025.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: An application is prepared after the collaborative workshops, where research ideas were brought together, possible collaborations with other workshop participants were explored and where crucial initial agreements for joint development of the application were made.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: The (eventual) main applicant must have participated in both collaborative workshops.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To prepare your application, go through the following steps: download the application form from the online application system ISAAC or from the NWO website (on the website of the relevant funding instrument); fill in the application form; save the form as a pdf and submit it in ISAAC with any required attachments; complete the information requested online in ISAAC. Mandatory annexes: budget form; declarations of support from cooperation partner(s); statement of co-financier(s) (mandatory if applicable); guarantee for continuity in project supervision (obligatory if applicable, see section 3.1); form ‘Statement and signature’.

title: Knowledge security: scientific research in a geopolitical context
programme: Knowledge security: scientific research in a geopolitical context
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge security: scientific research in a geopolitical context

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The NWA programme 'Knowledge s ecurity: scientific r esearch in a g eopolitical c ontext' promotes research into developments around knowledge security and inherent dilemmas in the context of an evolving geopolitical landscape . The programme aims to contribute to the development of a conceptual framework for the study of knowledge security and yield insights into the international context and threats, the organisation and vulnerability of the knowledge sector , and the balance between opportunities and risks in knowledge security polic ies . These insights , together with tangible action perspectives , should help relevant stakeholders within the knowledge sector and government to develop balanced policies and deal with dilemmas . In this way, this programme aims to increase the resilience of the Dutch knowledge sector and bolster national security , in such a way that core academic values are being protected and promoted and society can continue to benefit from international cooperation.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: This Call for proposals consists of two phases. In phase 1, consortia submit a preproposal that addresses one of the four themes identified in the Call. In phase 2, the applicants of the selected preproposals jointly develop a proposal by participating in synergy workshops, after which the proposal is submitted by a main applicant who has been appointed in phase 2. A preproposal and application have to be submitted by a main applicant, on behalf of a consortium. A consortium should involve at least a main applicant, co-applicant and cooperation partner. All consortium members should play an active role in formulating the research questions and in preparing and executing the project. The requirements per consortium member are explained in further detail in paragraph 3.1 of the Call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In this Call for proposals, consortium building is encouraged through the use of synergy workshops. The aim of the synergy workshops is to encourage the formation of a new inter and transdisciplinary consortium that develops a single research project encompassing various areas of knowledge and expertise, thereby attaining maximum results and impact. To this end, different (existing or new) consortia and available knowledge on partial aspects of a complex issue, are brought together. Consortium building and the development of a joint proposal are done under the guidance of external facilitators. For participation in the synergy workshops, a selection is made on the basis of the preapplications, see sections 3.3 and 4.2 of the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Within this Call for proposals, a maximum of four preproposals will be selected. The preproposals consist of research proposals that address the four themes described in chapter 2. The selected applicants jointly submit one application, composed of an overarching work package and four thematic work packages. A total of €6,600,000 is available. At least €2,000,000 is available for the overarching work package. A maximum of €1,150,000 is available for each thematic work package. Depending on the number of preproposals selected, any remaining budget will be added to the overarching work package (phase 2). The budget for the preproposals (phase 1) is released  once the research proposal of the application (phase 2) has been approved. The proposed project has a duration of a minimum of 48 months/4 years and a maximum of 72 months/6 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting preproposals is Tuesday 24 June 2025 , before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting the proposal is Thursday 26 February 2026 , before 14:00:00 CET. Please note: only applicants of preproposals that have been selected in phase 1, are permitted to submit a proposal in phase 2.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Phase 1 – Preproposal: Problem definition and analysis (40%), Envisaged impact and route to impact (40%), Quality of the consortium (20%). Phase 2 – Proposal Problem definition and analysis  (20%), Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%). For a detailed description of the specific aspects that are distinguished within these substantive assessment criteria, please refer to paragraph 4.3.1 of the Call for proposals. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps, divided into two phases: Phase 1 – Preproposals information meeting (online); submission of the preproposal; admissibility of the preproposal; assessment and selection of the preproposal. Phase 2 – Drafting and submitting a proposal participation in synergy workshops submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making by NWO Executive Board. For a detailed description of the procedural steps, please refer to paragraph 4.2 of the Call for proposals.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: In this Call for proposals, four complementary themes have been identified within which knowledge gaps exist with regards to knowledge security: International context and threats Organisation and vulnerability of knowledge practice Balance (between opportunities and risks) and policy Action perspectives: increasing resilience In phase 1 of this Call for proposals, it is mandatory to submit a preproposal. A preproposal is a concise application addressing one of the four themes described above. The preproposal describes which knowledge questions the applicant and co-applicants want to address and how this contributes to the overall objectives and impact described in the Call for proposals. In addition, the preproposal describes which opportunities and leads the applicants anticipate for cooperation with the consortia working on the other themes. In phase 2 , one application is developed and submitted by a consortium consisting of parties that have submitted the selected preproposals. In this phase, the selected preproposals serve as starting point and are integrated into a single proposal. The proposal consists of an overarching work package in which an interdisciplinary conceptual framework is developed, which forms the basis to coherently conduct research within the four thematic work packages (preproposals).

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: NWO facilitates the design of the collaboration and integration of the selected preproposals by organising synergy workshops (phase 2). These consist of several sessions between November 2025 and January 2026 led by a facilitator appointed by NWO. It is mandatory for the main applicants of selected preproposals to participate in the synergy workshops in phase 2. It is possible to invite additional participants to the workshops, including stakeholders. The synergy workshops serve to support the joint writing of a single proposal that consists of an overarching work package and thematic work packages. Applicants are also expected to reserve sufficient time and capacity outside the workshops for further development of the research proposal, mutual coordination and writing of the application.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Phase 1 – Preproposals The steps involved in writing your preproposal are: download the preproposal form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the funding instrument concerned); complete the preproposal form; save the preproposal form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes; fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. In phase 1, there are no compulsory annexes. Phase 2 – Drafting and submitting a proposal The steps involved in writing your application are: download the application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the funding instrument concerned); complete the application form; save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes; fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. Compulsory annexes in phase 2: budget; declarations of commitment of cooperation partners; declaration co-funding from co-funders (mandatory if applicable); letter guaranteeing the continuity of the project supervision (compulsory if applicable, see paragraph 3.1); form 'Statement and signature'.

title: Venture Challenge Fall 2025
target_groups: Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Many scientific findings never reach the market. Researchers often do not have the experience and knowledge needed to set up a commercial organization and to come to a market-ready product or service. It is usually too early for private investors and experienced entrepreneurs to step in during this start-up phase, for example because the business case is not yet sufficiently clear to them. The Venture Challenge helps by developing a well-thought out business plan together with the researchers. This provides insight into what is needed to bring the invention to the market.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The Venture Challenge is intended for teams of three to four people who are planning to bring a product or service to the market as a new start-up. The product or service is based on a new technological discovery or a new application of existing technology. The team consists of the person who made the scientific discovery and the other founders of the start-up. If required, relevant advisors, such as a business developer, can be added. This call is focused on two themes: Medical Devices: A new product, process or service can be a new medical device, or a technology that improves or enables the discovery, development or use of medical devices. Other innovations within the Life Sciences & Health sector: A new product, process or service can be a new drug, new therapy, new diagnostic, or a technology for drugs or to improve the process of drug development.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: An edition consists of two three-day bootcamps and regular coaching sessions. Participants work under the supervision of experienced coaches, to set up or improve their business plan. The supervision can possibly be expanded with experts and entrepreneurs with a background in life sciences & health. The entire process takes eight to ten weeks. At the end, the participants pitch their plans in front of a jury, including experienced entrepreneurs and investors. The selected candidates pay a personal contribution and receive funding for participating in the Venture Challenge, to be spent in the upcoming edition. The personal contribution for participation per team is: 2,000 euros when 3 team members participate; 2,500 euros when 4 team members participate. There is budget for two editions, each with 5-6 teams. The following dates are important: Edition I 1-3 October 2025 – first bootcamp; 5-7 November 2025 – second bootcamp; 19 November 2025 – pitches for the jury. Edition II 26-28 November 2025 – first bootcamp; 21-23 January 2026 – second bootcamp; 11 Feburary 2026 – pitches for the jury.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Registration for the fall edition opens 6 may 2025 and closes on 1 July 2025 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria the commercial perspective of the new product or process or service; the innovative character of the new product or process or service; the perspective of the competitive position of the (future) Venture Challenge startup; the financial potential of the (future) Venture Challenge startup; the quality of the Venture Challenge team, considering the extent to which members complement each other, the members’ capacities and the allocation of roles within the team; the added value of participation in the Venture Challenge program for the (future) Venture Challenge startup. Procedure If NWO takes your application into consideration, then an interview can be part of the application process. In that case, you will be given the opportunity to explain your proposal to the advisory committee which NWO appoints to assess the applications. Based on the committee's advice, NWO will decide which VC teams will be awarded a grant. The timetable for the procedure is as follows: 1 July 2025 14:00:00 CET Deadline full proposals 25 August – 29 August 2025 Interviews 16 September 2025 Decision awards and rejections 17 September 2025 Announcement awards

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: In the application form you are asked about the goal of the startup, the proposition and to what extent it has been validated, a market analysis, competitive position, and the team.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Download the application form and complete the form. The maximum size of the application is 10 pages (excluding the ‘Innovatieadviesdienstverklaring’ mentioned below). If the application is submitted by a start-up, then a so-called ‘Innovatieadviesdienstverklaring’ from the start-up should be appended to the application. If the application is submitted by a team without a start-up, then each team member should complete and add an innovation advisory service statement. Please note: there are two different forms; which one you need to fill in depends on whether there is already a company established when you register for the Venture Challenge. You can read more in the ‘Toelichting’ (see the appendix to the form).

title: FutureFoodS Call 2024
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: FutureFoods has prioritised three topics for this first call. They reflect the need for new knowledge and innovations in society and the food sector. In each application one of the following three call topics can be chosen: Topic 1: The way towards sustainable and resilient food systems Topic 2: New foods – Fostering innovations in food design, processing and supply via demand and supply reorientation Topic 3: Empowering sustainable food choices – Enabling environments and dietary shifts

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The following countries will participate in this call: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Lithuania, Norway, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Spain, Sweden, The Netherlands, Türkiye. Research consortia must be comprised of a minimum of three partners from at least three Member States or Associated Countries participating in the call and be eligible to request funding from the FOs participating in this call and providing funding for the selected topic. For applicants from Dutch universities and eligible knowledge institutions, funding is available from NWO – see Annex I of the Call for proposals for the possibilities. The added value of transnational collaboration should be clearly stated in the application.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For research proposals in this round, consortia can apply for a maximum of € 400,000 in funding from NWO.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The submission deadline for pre-proposals is 15 January 2025, at 13:00 CET . The submission deadline for full proposals is 9 July 2025, at 13:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Full proposals will be evaluated against the following three equally weighted evaluation criteria excellence, impact and quality and efficiency of the implementation. Procedure The assessment in this call will be done in two stages. In the first stage, consortia are invited to submit a pre-proposal. Only consortia that were successful in the first stage will be invited to submit a full proposal. Eligible proposals – both in the first and in the second stage – will be assessed by an independent evaluation committee.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information about this call is available at this website For contact with the Call office: ptj-futurefoods@fz-juelich.de

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: A two-phase procedure will be followed for this Call for Proposals. In the first phase, consortia will be invited to submit pre-proposals. A pre-proposal includes a description of the partners, a brief project description and budget indications as described in the detailed pre-proposal template in Annex C, and in other relevant documents available in the submission platform: https://futurefoods.ptj.de . The pre-proposals that are eligible according to the transnational and national/regional criteria will be assessed by an Expert Panel, consisting of recognized international experts in relevant fields. The decision for the invitation to submit a full proposal in the second phase will be taken jointly by the participating funders and communicated to the main applicant of the transnational consortium.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: The partners in the approved projects will be asked to sign a consortium agreement before the start of the projects. More information will be provided in the award letter.

title: Hydrogen and green electrons for speciality and fine chemicals
programme: Hydrogen and green electrons for specialities and fine chemicals
target_groups: Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: Hydrogen and green electrons for specialities and fine chemicals

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This Call for Proposals intends to come to a grant proposal for the realization of work package 6 of GroenvermogenNL: “NGF: Hydrogen and green electrons for speciality and fine chemicals”. Through workshops, a consortium will be formed which focusses on innovations to make the synthesis of specialities and fine chemicals more sustainable. This will eventually result in a more sustainable Dutch chemical industry.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers from universities, universities of applied and TO2 institutions. Employees of businesses and other public and private organisations provided that: They have a tenured position for at least 0.6 fte; Their organisation has demonstrable Research & Development (R&D) activities in the Netherlands; They hold a master’s degree.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: Consortium building takes places during two multiple-day workshops. The workshops are mandatory for the main applicant of the consortium. Signing up for the workshop can be done via ISAAC.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: In this Call for proposals, a maximum of €19.317.355 can be applied for. This amount needs to be supplemented by the businesses and civil society organisations in the consortium with ‘Funding other than that provided by NWO’. ‘Funding other than that provided by NWO’ consists of: The own contribution, i.e. the non-fundable part of the eligible costs contributed by businesses and civil society organisations, plus; Possible in cash (excl. Dutch VAT) or in-kind co-funding. Financing can amongst others be used for personal and material costs.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline application workshops: Tuesday 13 March 2025, 14:00 CET Deadline proposal: Tuesday 10 July 2025, 14:00 CET

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessment of the full proposal is based on satisfying the ‘Framework for State aid for research and development and innovation’ and the GBER, as well as the quality of the consortium and the research plan. Procedure The procedure for this Call for proposals consists of 3 steps: Registration for the workshops (13 March 2025) Workshops (7-9 April 2025, 12-14 May 2025) Developing grant proposal (deadline 10 July 2025) After submission, the proposal will be assessed by the assessment committee, which will advise the board of the Domain Science about whether or not to award funding (November/December 2025).

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: GroenvermogenNL - Groenvermogen

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Organisations that want to participate in the grant proposal within this Call for proposals submit an Application form Workshops to participate in the workshops. For each organisation, a maximum of one Application form Workshops may be submitted. This means a maximum of one Application form Workshops for the entire organisation at the highest level (e.g. at university level and not at faculty or department level). The main applicant submits the proposal via the NWO web application ISAAC. During the assessment process, NWO will communicate with the main applicant.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The application form including the mandatory annexes will become available in the future.

title: ADM-LAB: Livings Labs for Adaptive Delta Management in Bangladesh
programme: ADM-LAB: Livings Labs for Adaptive Delta Management in Bangladesh Thematic Programming Dutch Research Agenda (NWA)

characteristics.Research programme: ADM-LAB: Livings Labs for Adaptive Delta Management in Bangladesh Thematic Programming Dutch Research Agenda (NWA)

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The ADM-LAB programme aims to enhance sustainable and knowledge-informed policies on Adaptive Delta Management (ADM) in Bangladesh under the Bangladesh Delta Plan 2100 (BDP2100). It seeks to secure past knowledge, integrate further knowledge development, and accelerate ADM implementation in both Bangladesh and the Netherlands. The programme focuses on delivering insights for policy and practitioners, creating an effective interface for knowledge interactions, and addressing challenges in ADM implementation. Furthermore, it emphasizes capacity building, interdisciplinary collaboration, and ownership in Bangladesh for effective ADM implementation.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications in Phase I are submitted by at least one main applicant, a co-applicant, a collaboration partner and optional co-funders. Only persons working at a Bangladeshi university or research organisation based in Bangladesh can act as main applicant. Co-applicants play an active role in the project and each application must include at least one co-applicant based in the Kingdom of the Netherlands. Organizational eligibility is assessed beforehand. Cooperation partners are crucial for knowledge sharing but cannot request funding for personnel or research costs. Letters of commitment are required from cooperation partners.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Applicants can apply for Living Labs (Phase I) and a Knowledge Management project (Phase II).   Phase I Living Lab applications can request up to €950,000, with a maximum project duration of 5 years. A minimum of €75,000 per living lab must be allocated towards the Knowledge Management project, facilitating research generalization and participation in Phase II.   Phase I involves three living lab projects focusing on four geographic (1. Coasts and Estuaries 2. Rivers 3. Urban 4. Haor and Wetlands) and thematic challenges within ADM.  Phase II allows for a maximum budget of €970,000, with a project duration of 4 years. Applicants should only request funding necessary for project realization, as detailed in the provided budget modules.  Phase II entails a knowledge management project to scale up insights from the living labs, facilitate stakeholder engagement, and ensure sustainability beyond the programme's timeframe. In Phase II, Phase I participants form a new consortium for a joint proposal for the overarching knowledge management project.  See the call text for details.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline for submission of proposals in Phase I is Tuesday 5 November 2024, 14:00:00 CEST in ISAAC. Deadline for submission of a knowledge management proposal in Phase II is Tuesday 2 September 2025, 14:00:00 CEST in ISAAC.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Phase I: The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Phase II: The application submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Alignment with the objective of the programme (40%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the proposal (30%) Each criterion consists of multiple sub-criteria which are listed in paragraph 4.3 of the call text. Procedure The call consists of two phases. Proposals for living labs are submitted in Phase I. After assessment by NWO and an external committee Phase I participants form a new consortium for a joint proposal for the overarching knowledge management project in Phase II.  Details about the procedure are described in chapter 4 of the call text.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Requirements and expectations around applications may be different in The Netherlands than in the country of applicants. Researchers may also not be familiar with aspects like NWO’s submission system or the budget modules. To help with this, NWO will organise a webinar that focuses specifically on the administrative aspects.  NWO will also organise collaborative workshops for this call for proposals. More information on the workshops and webinar can be found here at the research program page.  NWO may require the applicant to submit additional documents, within ten working days prior to the submission deadline, to prove the stated requirements for applying are met.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the application form, we ask that you also submit (in PDF): a budget form (Excel); form ' Statement and signature' signed declarations of commitment of cooperation partners If applicable: signed declarations ensuring continuity of project supervision; If applicable: signed declarations of co-funding.

title: Rubicon 2025-2
target_groups: International Collaboration Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Starting Researchers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: International research experience is often essential for building up one’s scientific career. Rubicon offers talented researchers who have completed their doctorates in the past year the chance to gain experience at a research institution outside the Kingdom of the Netherlands, as international research experience is likely to be an advantage at a later stage in the applicant’s academic career. Rubicon is open to all scientific disciplines for a research project at a foreign research institute. In some cases, there is an opportunity to apply for a research period at a research institute in the Kingdom of the Netherlands.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Postgraduates who are currently engaged in doctoral research or who have been awarded a doctorate in the twelve months preceding the relevant deadline can submit a proposal. Applicants who are still engaged in doctoral research may apply only if their supervisor provides a written declaration approving their thesis. Women especially are urged to apply. Applicants are eligible to apply if, before the start of the project, they have received a PhD from a research organisation mentioned under article 1.1 paragraph 1 of the NWO Grant Rules. The complete list of research organisations can also be found in the Call for Proposals. Applications must be submitted by individual researchers and not by pairs or teams of researchers. Applicants may only submit an application for a Rubicon grant once. You can find the complete list of the eligibility criteria in the call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A budget of 3,145,000 euro is available for this submission round. The available budget will be divided in proportion to the number of eligible applications between the units Science, SSH, AES and ZonMw. Applicants can apply for a period of up to 24 months at a research institute outside the Netherlands. The minimum duration is 12 months. The eligible costs are your salary including fringe benefits, travel expenses and a limited amount of research costs. Any overhead costs such as housing, computers, infrastructure etc. are not eligible for funding. You can find the standard amounts in chapter 7 of the Call for proposals. It is not possible to combine a stay at several institutes. Fieldwork is allowed, depending on the nature of the research proposed.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for this round is Tuesday, 2 September 2025 at 14:00:00 hours CEST (2 PM sharp) .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The quality of the applicant, The quality of the research proposal, The quality of the host institute in the context of the match between the applicant/proposal and the host institute, Scientific and/or societal impact. Procedure Each application will be assessed within the realm of the relevant discipline(s) by a multidisciplinary committee based on the criteria mentioned above. NWO announces which applications have been granted about four months after the deadline of this round.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: When writing your proposal, please bear in mind that each application will be assessed with the realm of the relevant discipline(s) by a multidisciplinary committee. Please use the application form that you can download from this page. Only the required annexes will be accepted.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Preparing an application Download the application form and fill it in; Save the application form as a pdf file and upload it in ISAAC (Science, SSH, AES) or MijnZonMw (ZonMw). The application form consists of: A research proposal written in English (max. 6 pages A4, 2,000 words). The word count includes footnotes, texts illustrations and tables, captions to illustrations and tables. References are excluded; A narrative CV of the applicant consisting of a narrative academic profile (400-700 words) and the key output (max. 10 items); One acceptance form of the intended host institute; Applications from applicants who have not yet been awarded a PhD must also be accompanied by a form approving their thesis. All forms will be made available on the websites of NWO and ZonMw at least two months before the deadline of this call.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Practorates are expertise platforms with a research component, organized as part of mbo-institutions. This call stimulates the development of (new) partnerships between practorates active in the green domain, lectorates, knowledge institutions and SME partners. This promotes the position of practorates in the knowledge chain, which helps them to professionalise further. It establishes a connection between the research infrastructure at universities of applied sciences on the one hand and professional practice on the other. The partnerships carry out joint activities in the field of (practice-oriented) research and innovation. Applications relate to applicable knowledge and solving practical issues. Improving and developing one's own practice are leading. Project outcomes include network activities, prototypes, tests, (business) models, research and work methods, demonstrators, processes, product concepts and service concepts. Project results have an impact on professional practice and on the education programme.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The applicant must be a government-funded MBO institution. The practor involved must be part of the CIV-koepelplan and be supervised by Stichting Ieder Mbo een Practoraat (SIMP). At least two practical partners are involved in the research consortium, at least one of which is an SME established in the Netherlands. In addition, a research group of a university of applied sciences that is affiliated with a Center of Expertise must participate in the consortium.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The maximum grant size is 40,000 per application. The practical partners in the research contribute at least 25% of the subsidy amount to the implementation of the project. This can be in cash or in kind. The duration of the subsidy period is a maximum of 12 months. The intended project must start within 3 months after the grant has been awarded.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: You can submit an application until September 9, 2025.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria All proposals are assessed by an Assessment Committee based on five assessment criteria: Fitting within the thematic delineation of the call Demand articulation Networking Innovation within the framework of the KIEM-groen pilotregeling practoraten Feasibility project plan For further explanation, consult the call for proposals section 4.3 'Criteria' (in Dutch). Procedure Regieorgaan SIA checks whether applications are eligible within 65 working days after submission. An independent assessment committee assesses each eligible application based on the assessment criteria. Each assessment criteria is scored as adequate or inadequate. An application only receives a positive assessment if it is adequate on all criteria. The board of Regieorgaan SIA assesses the procedure followed and decides whether or not to grant a subsidy based on the assessment committee's advice. This is done on a first come, first served basis. You will hear within 10 weeks after the date of eligibility whether your application has been granted.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: You can find more information on the website of Regieorgaan SIA

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: A complete application includes the following documents: the fully completed and signed application form; the project proposal; the budget in excel with the requested subsidy, co-financing and cost justification; the completed project participants form in excel with an overview of the project group members involved in the context of the NWO 'Code for dealing with personal interests'. If you have been authorized by the Executive Board to sign the application, you must also enclose a mandate form with the application.

title: Unconventional information processing technologies: Cooperation Japan – The Netherlands

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The programme aims to advance the frontiers of computational science by exploring unconventional approaches to information processing. The programme seeks to: Foster innovation in computational technologies that address complex global challenges, Strengthen research networks between the Netherlands and Japan, promote international talent mobility, and train early-career researchers who may become future leaders by connecting top researchers, Deliver societal and scientific impact by translating unconventional methods into practical solutions. Example of scientific research directions are: Quantum information processing: Investigating new methods and applications for quantum algorithms and systems, Neuromorphic computing: Developing systems that emulate neural architectures for energy-efficient and scalable computation, Bio-inspired algorithms: Drawing inspiration from biological processes to design novel computational paradigms, Integrated approaches: Combining interdisciplinary insights to tackle challenges in data processing, communication, and machine intelligence.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are prepared by a consortium consisting of at least one Japan-based applicant and one Netherlands-based applicant. The consortium works on one joint proposal, which the Netherlands-based applicant submits to the NWO web application ISAAC and the Japan-based main applicant submits to JST via e-Rad. Five categories of consortium participants are distinguished: Main applicant, Co-applicants, Research participants (Japan side only), Cooperation partners, Co-funders. The terms and conditions for each participant are detailed in the Call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: Consortium buildingApplications should be characterised by equitable partnership and sustainable collaboration among the partners in the consortium and with relevant stakeholders. Evidence of such active engagement will be an element in the assessment of project proposals. This includes inter-institutional cooperation, a balanced contribution to the proposed research, and frequent exchange between the partners, including exchange visits by both senior and junior researchers. The number of (co)applicants in both countries should be roughly equal. The workload on both sides should be equally balanced.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, the Netherlands-based applicant can apply for a grant amount of at least €800,000 and at most €1,150,000 from NWO. The Japan-based applicant can apply for a grant amount of at most 250,000,000 YP from JST. The main applicant and co-applicant(s) may apply for budget for personnel, material, knowledge utilization, and project management. To strengthen the transnational networks between the Netherlands and Japan, all early-career researchers working on the project (PhDs and postdocs) are required to spend at least 20% of their total employment time on the project in Japan for an extended research stay. Appropriate budget needs to be reserved to fund these extended research stays. The minimum duration of the proposed project is 48 months/ 4 years. The maximum duration of the project is 60 months/5 years. The conditions on the budget are further explained in the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Webinar 3 June 2025 Information webinar / matchmaking Statements of intent 31 July 2025 Deadline statements of intent (not mandatory) Proposals 14:00.00 CEST, 9 September 2025 14:00.00 JST, 9 September 2025 Deadline proposals at NWO Deadline proposals at JST November 2025 Outcome pre-selection (if applicable*) October/November 2025 Referees consulted December 2025 Applicants can submit a rebuttal January 2026 Assessment committee meeting

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Quality and relevance of the research content and plan, Quality of the consortium, Quality of stimulating researcher mobility and international collaborations, Scientific and/or societal impact. Criteria 1 and 3 each carry a weight of 30% and criteria 2 and 4 each carry a weight of 20%. The assessment criteria are further operationalised in the Call for proposals. Procedure An external, independent assessment committee will be assigned for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from science with knowledge of the field. The task of the assessment committee is to assess the applications and the relevant documents that are submitted, in conjunction with each other and with regard to both the respective merits of each application and the assessment criteria outlined in this Call for proposals (see section 4.3.1.). The Executive Board of NWO will decide on the submitted proposals.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: For questions regarding what can be applied for from JST, or other questions regarding the JST rules and conditions, please contact the JST ASPIRE coordination group at aspire- aspire-nl@jst.go.jp For other questions, please contact NWO at nwo-jst-joint-call@nwo.nl You can also find this Call for proposals on the website of JST via this link

write_proposal.title: Write proposals
write_proposal.text: Aanvraag bij NWO The Netherlands-based and Japan-based teams must submit their applications by parallel submission, using the designated application form to draft a joint proposal in English and submitting it to the application websites of their respective countries. Application to NWO The steps involved in writing your application are: Download the form for the declaration of intent from the website and fill it in (not compulsory). Send this form to aspire-nl@jst.go.jp and nwo-jst-joint-call@nwo.nl , download the application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from this NWO web page, complete the application form (adhere to the questions and word/page limits stated in the form), save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes, fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. Compulsory annexes: budget form, statement by the applicant, in case of co-funding; a declaration from the co-funder, in case it is needed (as specified in the Call for proposals): embedding guarantee.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The steps for writing and submitting your application are: download the application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from this NWO web page, complete the application form, save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes, fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. Compulsary annexes: Budget form, Declaration of the applicant, In case of co-funding; A statement from the co-funder, In case it is needed (as specified in the Call for proposals): an embedding guarantee. Annexes should be prepared in accordance with the template provided by NWO. Annexes should be uploaded separately from the application in ISAAC. All annexes, except the budget form, should be submitted as a pdf file (without security). The budget form should be submitted as an Excel file in ISAAC. Attachments other than those listed above are not allowed. It is mandatory to prepare your application in English, Submitting an application can only be done through the online application system ISAAC. Applications not submitted through ISAAC will not be considered, As the lead applicant, you are required to submit an application through your own personal ISAAC account. It is important to begin your application in ISAAC in a timely manner: if you do not already have an ISAAC account, it should be created in time to avoid any application problems, new organizations may still need to be added to IAAC by NWO, You still need to enter data online. This Call for Proposals is also announced on the JST website

title: NWO Talent programme Vici ZonMw 2025
target_groups: Diversity Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: Diversity Researchers from Abroad

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Vici target group consists of researchers at the stage of consolidation and further development of their leadership/research group. The goal of the Vici grant is to contribute to the researcher's development in this area.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Vici is aimed at senior researchers with academic qualities that clearly exceed what is customary within the international peer group, as well as established leadership- and mentorship skills. Researchers may only submit an application if they have obtained their PhD in the fifteen years preceding the benchmark date of this round, 1 March 2025. An extension to the above career-dependent submission period may be possible under specific circumstances (see Section 3.4.2 of the Call for proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What can be applied for
what_to_apply_for.text: The maximum grant is 1.5 million euros.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for pre-proposals is March 11, 2025, 14:00:00 hours CET . The deadline for proposals is September 9, 2025, 14:00:00 hours CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessment criterion for the pre-proposals is: Quality of the researcher The assessment criteria for the full proposals are: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%) Scientific and/or social impact (25%) Procedure The Vici competition will be assessed by four committees: four committees corresponding to the NWO domains: Social Sciences and Humanities (SSH), NWO-Science domain (Science), Applied and Engineering Sciences (AES), Health Research and Development (ZonMw). You are currently on the webpage for ZonMw. Vici applications are assessed in a domain-wide competition: all applications from different disciplines within one domain will be compared in one competition. ZonMw uses a broad-based assessment committee of academics to advise on the merits and prioritisation of the applications. Applicants must first submit a pre-proposal. These are assessed by the assessment committee, which then makes a recommendation on whether or not to submit a proposal. Proposals are sent to external referees. Applicants are given the opportunity to respond to the referees’ reports (‘rebuttal’). Next, the full proposals, the reviews and the rebuttals are assessed by the respective assessment committees. They invite a selection of the candidates for an interview. Based on the application, the referees’ reports, the rebuttal and the interview the assessment committee advises the accredited board of NWO. All proposals must be submitted via ISAAC, NWO's electronic application system. The only exceptions to this rule are proposals to the Health Research and Development domain. These proposals can only be submitted via My ZonMw.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applicants may only submit a full proposal after they have been informed that the board of the domain concerned has taken a positive decision on the applicant’s pre‐proposal. Applicants are not permitted to submit a proposal following a negative decision on the pre‐proposal. pre‐proposals must be written in English and must include: a description of the candidate’s academic profile (max. 1200 words); a description of the candidate’s key output (max. 10 items); the research idea (max. 150 words). NWO has recently updated the instruction for writing an EBCV . The updated instruction can be found in the application form for the VICI. We would also like to draw your attention to the existence of an international digital platform to support narrative style CV writing (PEP-CV) . Full proposals must be written in English and must include: an outline of the research proposal and a completed motivation for the choice of institution; a completed section scientific and/or societal impact; a completed data management section; a budget estimate.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Preparing a pre‐proposal download the pre-proposal form, complete the pre-proposal form. complete your My ZonMw account with up‐to‐date contact details (phone numbers and email address), make a list of relevant literature references (optional, to prepare for the search for reviewers), save the forms as pdf files and upload them separately in My ZonMw. Please use the link for the right domain in My ZonMw. Preparing a proposal download the proposal form, complete the proposal form, complete your My ZonMw account with up‐to‐date contact details (phone numbers and email address), save the form as a PDF file and upload it to My ZonMw. In the case of a contribution from the research institution and/or a third party, a co-funding statement from the contributing party must be enclosed with the submission of the proposal.

title: Take off phase 1 - Feasibility studies WO Autumn 2025
target_groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Take-off focuses on facilitating and stimulating activity and entrepreneurship from Dutch universities and research institutes recognized by NWO. This involves creating innovative new activities that follow knowledge development and utilization by researchers at these knowledge institutions. This may concern product, process, care or service innovation in the broadest sense of the word, within all scientific fields.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers from the following research institutes can submit applications: Dutch universities (or comparable positions at university medical centers); KNAW and NWO institutes; the Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; NCB Naturalis; Advanced Research Center for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center for Pediatric Oncology.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: NWO provides funding for Take-off feasibility studies in the form of grants with a maximum size of 60,000 euros for a maximum period of six months for the performance of a study into the feasibility for commercial application of specific innovative knowledge that has arisen within these research institutions.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: A proposal must be submitted via our ISAAC system before Tuesday 9 September 2025 14:00:00 CEST . As of this autumn round 2025, a pre-application is no longer required.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The ranking criteria for Take-off WO phase 1 - feasibility studies are: Knowledge base and innovativeness knowledge that forms the basis of the business idea; innovative elements; Commercial potential application possibilities and added value; market and commercial perspectives; Qualities of the team scientific expertise applicant(s) and support staff; entrepreneurial and commercial skills; motivation and level of ambition of the team involved; Quality of the project plan practical and economic approach; activity plan; project budget, broken down by project activity;

title: Doctoral Grant for Teachers, 2nd round 2025
target_groups: Teachers

characteristics.For specific groups: Teachers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Teachers from primary, secondary, further vocational, higher vocational and special education can receive a grant for a PhD study. The aim is to directly apply the knowledge and research experience acquired for the benefit of educational practice. A higher number of practising teachers with a PhD also increases the quality of education and strengthens the connections between universities and schools. You can apply for a PhD grant for any research subject. However, you must be able to indicate the importance.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: You must hold a tenured position and you must be employed at an educational institute funded by the Dutch government.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: With the PhD grant a replacement teacher can be appointed for a period of 5 years for half of your contract hours, up to a maximum of 0.4 fte.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for the submission of proposals is Tuesday 9 September 2025, 14:00 CEST . There are two submission rounds annually for the Doctoral Grant for Teachers, with submission deadlines in January and September.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Further information about the criteria can be found in the call for proposals. The quality of the candidate and the research proposal are assessed according to three criteria: Quality of the research proposal Impact Quality of the candidate Procedure A science-wide committee will assess the proposals and will compile a ranking. The most promising candidates will be invited for an interview. The candidate will present the research idea to the selection committee. The Domain Board Social Sciences and Humanities of NWO will take the final granting decision. Composition committee Please note that it is possible that not all committee or jury members mentioned on this page have assessed all pre-proposals or applications, for example in the context of the NWO Code for Dealing with Personal Interests. Please contact the round secretary if you have any questions about this. Doctor    L.    Aaldering Doctor    A.    Aar, van der Doctor    M.    Blaakman Doctor    M.L.V.    Bremmer Doctor Ing.    D.L.J.    Broere Professor    P.    Broomans Doctor    S.F.    Brouwer Professor    P.W.C.    Chan Doctor    A.M.L.    Collot d’Escury-Koenigs Doctor    J.    Copier Doctor    C.    Degener Doctor    Y.    Eski Doctor    A.H.    Gijlers Doctor    A.    Hemerik Professor    H.    Herk, van, Technical chair Doctor    J.    Huybrechts Doctor    W.G.S.J.    Isenia Doctor    J.P.    Kloeg Doctor    J.E.P.    Knapen Doctor    G.    Laan, van der Doctor    P.H.O.    Leijten Professor    C.J.C.    Lamoth , Technical chair Professor    N.P.    Landsman Doctor    T.    Laros Doctor    E.J.    Loo, van Doctor    B.    Paulle Doctor    S.    Ramaekers Doctor    W.G.    Reijnierse Professor Ir.    A.    Rensink Doctor    M.A.B.    Sande, van der Doctor    E.K.    Swart Professor    M.    Vermeulen Doctor    L.P.    Voogt Doctor    P.D.    Veen, van Professor    A.    Verhagen Doctor    J.C.M.    Wachelder Professor    I.    Wallenburg

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information about the Doctoral Grant for Teachers can be found on the programme-website .

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applicants are entirely free to choose the topic of their research; any research topic is eligible for consideration. The application will be assessed based on the criteria outlined in section 4.3 of the Call for Proposals. Applications may be written in either Dutch or English. In addition to the research proposal, the application form includes the applicant’s CV and a recommendation from the supervisor.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The application consists of : The application form (including a recommendation from the supervisor); A compulsory annex to the application: the employer’s declaration.

title: NWO Talent programme Vici Science 2025
target_groups: Diversity Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: Diversity Researchers from Abroad

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Vici target group consists of researchers at the stage of consolidation and further development of their leadership/research group. The goal of the Vici grant is to contribute to the researcher's development in this area.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Vici is aimed at senior researchers with academic qualities that clearly exceed what is customary within the international peer group, as well as established leadership- and mentorship skills. Researchers may only submit an application if they have obtained their PhD in the fifteen years preceding the benchmark date of this round, 1 March 2025. An extension to the above career-dependent submission period may be possible under specific circumstances (see Section 3.4.2 of the Call for proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The maximum grant is 1.5 million euros.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for pre-proposals is March 11, 2025, 14:00:00 hours CET . The deadline for full proposals is September 9, 2025, 14:00:00 hours CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessment criterion for the pre-proposal is: quality of the researcher. The assessment criteria for the full proposal are: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%) , Scientific and/or societal impact (25%). Procedure There is a single submission point for the Domain Science. Applications from the Earth Sciences, Astronomy, Chemistry, Computer Science, Life Sciences, Physics and Mathematics can be submitted to the Domain Science. Vici applications are assessed in a domain-wide competition: applications from different disciplines will be compared in competition. The domains use broad-based assessment committees of academics to advise on the merits and prioritisation of the applications. Depending on the number of submissions and the underlying disciplines the assessment procedure within the Domain Science can take place in different expert panels/clusters. Applicants must first submit a pre-proposal. These are assessed by the assessment committee, which then makes a recommendation on whether or not to submit a full proposal. Proposals are sent to external referees. Applicants are given the opportunity to respond to the referees’ reports (‘rebuttal’). Next, the proposals, the reviews and the rebuttals are assessed by the respective assessment committees. Based on the proposal, the referees’ reports and the rebuttal the assessment committee advises the accredited board of NWO. All proposals must be submitted via ISAAC, NWO's electronic application system. The only exceptions to this rule are proposals to the Health Research and Development domain. These proposals can only be submitted via Mijn ZonMw.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applicants may only submit a full proposal after they have been informed that the board of the domain concerned has taken a positive decision on the applicant’s pre‐proposal. Applicants are not permitted to submit a proposal following a negative decision on the pre‐proposal. pre‐proposals must be written in English and must include: a description of the candidate’s academic profile (max. 1200 words); a description of the candidate’s key output (max. 10 items); the research idea (max. 150 words). NWO has recently updated the instruction for writing an EBCV . The updated instruction can be found in the application form for the VICI. We would also like to draw your attention to the existence of an international digital platform to support narrative style CV writing (PEP-CV) . Full proposals must be written in English and must include: an outline of the research proposal and a completed motivation for the choice of institution; a completed section scientific and/or societal impact; a completed data management section; a budget estimate.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Preparing a pre‐proposal download the pre-proposal form, complete the pre-proposal form, complete your ISAAC account with up‐to‐date contact details (phone numbers and email address), make a list of relevant literature references (optional, to prepare for the search for reviewers), save the forms as pdf files and upload them separately in ISAAC. Please use the link for the right domain in ISAAC. Preparing a full proposal download the full proposal form, complete the full proposal form, complete your ISAAC account with up‐to‐date contact details (phone numbers and email address), save the form as a PDF file and upload it to ISAAC. In the case of a contribution from the research institution and/or a third party, a statement from the contributing party must be enclosed with the submission of the full proposal.
