title: NWO Talent programme Vici AES 2025
target_groups: Diversity Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: Diversity Researchers from Abroad

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Vici target group consists of researchers at the stage of consolidation and further development of their leadership/research group. The goal of the Vici grant is to contribute to the researcher's development in this area.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Vici is aimed at senior researchers with academic qualities that clearly exceed what is customary within the international peer group, as well as established leadership- and mentorship skills. Researchers may only submit an application if they have obtained their PhD in the fifteen years preceding the benchmark date of this round, 1 March 2025. An extension to the above career-dependent submission period may be possible under specific circumstances (see Section 3.4.2 of the Call for proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The maximum grant is 1.5 million euros.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for pre-proposals is March 11, 2025, 14:00:00 hours CET . The deadline for proposals is September 9, 2025, 14:00:00 hours CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: The assessment criterion for the pre-proposal is: quality of the researcher. The assessment criteria for the full proposal are: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%) , Scientific and/or societal impact (25%). Procedure The Vici competition will be assessed by four committees: four committees corresponding to the NWO domains: Social Sciences and Humanities (SSH), NWO-Science domain (Science), Applied and Engineering Sciences (AES), Health Research and Development (ZonMw). You are currently on the webpage for domain AES. Vici applications are assessed in a domain-wide competition: all applications from different disciplines within one domain will be compared in one competition. The domain AES uses broad-based assessment committees of academics to advise on the merits and prioritisation of the applications. Applicants must first submit a pre-proposal. These are assessed by the AES assessment committee, which then makes a recommendation on whether or not to submit a full proposal. Full proposals are sent to external referees. Applicants are given the opportunity to respond to the referees’ reports (‘rebuttal’). Interviews will then take place in which applicants can explain their application and answer questions from the committee. Based on the applications, the referees’ reports, the rebuttals and the interview the assessment committee advises the accredited board of NWO. All Vici proposals must be submitted via ISAAC, NWO's electronic application system. The only exceptions to this rule are applications to the Health Research and Development domain. These applications can only be submitted via Mijn ZonMw.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applicants may only submit a full proposal after they have been informed that the board of the domain concerned has taken a positive decision on the applicant’s pre‐proposal. Applicants are not permitted to submit a proposal following a negative decision on the pre‐proposal. pre‐proposals must be written in English and must include: a description of the candidate’s academic profile (max. 1200 words); a description of the candidate’s key output (max. 10 items); the research idea (max. 150 words). NWO has recently updated the instruction for writing an EBCV . The updated instruction can be found in the application form for the VICI. We would also like to draw your attention to the existence of an international digital platform to support narrative style CV writing (PEP-CV) . Full proposals must be written in English and must include: an outline of the research proposal and a completed motivation for the choice of institution; a completed section scientific and/or societal impact; a completed data management section; a budget estimate.

fill_in_forms.text: Preparing a pre‐proposal download the pre-proposal form, complete the pre-proposal form, complete your ISAAC account with up‐to‐date contact details (phone numbers and email address), make a list of relevant literature references (optional, to prepare for the search for reviewers), save the forms as pdf files and upload them separately in ISAAC. Please use the link for the right domain in ISAAC. Preparing a full proposal download the full proposal form, complete the full proposal form, complete your ISAAC account with up‐to‐date contact details (phone numbers and email address), save the form as a PDF file and upload it to ISAAC. In the case of a contribution from the research institution and/or a third party, a statement from the contributing party must be enclosed with the submission of the full proposal.

title: NGF: Strengthening the knowledge base for the high tech equipment sector
programme: Strengthening the knowledge position of the Dutch high-tech equipment sector Future-proof hightech equipment
target_groups: Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Strengthening the knowledge position of the Dutch high-tech equipment sector Future-proof hightech equipment

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The purpose of this Call for proposals is to make room for academic research within a number of prioritized key technologies relevant to multiple application domains in the scope of the National Growth Fund programme NXTGEN Hightech. In addition, it offers the opportunity for new parties (both companies and knowledge institutions) to contribute to knowledge development within the NXTGEN Hightech programme.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: A proposals can be submitted by a main applicant and one or more co-applicants. A proposal is developed up by a consortium in which other participants, including co-funders, are involved in addition to the applicants. A consortium always consists of researchers from two or more different research organizations (of which at least one is university), supplemented by one or more co-funders and possibly supplemented by one or more collaboration partners.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting proposals is 11 September 2025, at 14:00:00 hours CEST

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem posed and problem analysis Expected impact and route to impact Quality of the consortium Quality of the research Criteria 1 and 2 each carry a 25% weighting in the overall assessment, criterion 3, a weighting of 30% and criterion 4, a weighting of 20%. Procedure Applications are reviewed by referees (remote experts external to the assessment committee), after which applicants are given an opportunity to prepare a written rebuttal. The assessment committee then assesses the applications on the basis of the referees' reports and the written response. The NWO-AES domain board  makes a decision about awarding grants based on the advice of the assessment committee. The decision by the board is scheduled for January 2026.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: See section 3.3 of the Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: See section 3.3 of the Call for proposals.

title: KIC Future Plastics: Development of safe plastics for a sustainable future
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Future Plastics
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Future Plastics

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This programme aims to build knowledge about a new generation of bio-based or CO2-based plastics that are non-persistent and can no longer lead to harmful leakage. The definition of plastics is not limited to plastics but also includes, among other things, coatings, composites, paint, and rubbers. Additionally, knowledge is needed about the pathways that can be taken to introduce these plastics into society, and this knowledge should be incorporated into the design of new materials. Interdisciplinary collaboration is of great importance in this regard.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants other participants are involved as well. The main and co-applicant(s) should conduct the proposed research as a consortium that always includes, beside themselves, two or more co-funders, and potentially one or more cooperation partners. All consortiumpartners should play an active role in the formulation of the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In the period prior to the deadline for submitting pre-proposals NWO will facilitate matchmaking activities for this Call for proposals. For this call, matchmaking will take place on May 13. Your consortium’s participation in these activities is recommended, but not mandatory.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of €750,000 and a maximum of €2,000,000 of NWO funding can be applied for in. With this, NWO finances maximally 70% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding. NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments, knowledge utilization and project management. The available budget modules (including the maximum amounts) are listed below. Apply only for funding that is vital to realise the project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: 15 April 2025 Online information session 13 May 2025 Matchmaking NWO Utrecht Statements of intent Tuesday 24 June 2025 , before 14:00:00 CEST Deadline statements of intent Proposals Tuesday 16 September 2025 , before 14:00:00 CEST Deadline proposals October - December 2025 Referees consulted January 2026 Applicants can submit a rebuttal January – March 2026 Interview selection en interviews March 2026 Assessment committee meeting May 2026 Decision by the board

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: information session; matchmaking; submission of a statement of intent; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Write proposals
write_proposal.text: NWO will assess your application on the following conditions: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions; the main applicant has submitted a statement of intent in ISAAC before the set deadline; the applying consortium contains two or more cofunders; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in the Call for proposals; the required co-funding has been, after any request for addition or change, correctly and fully committed through declarations co-funding; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the application budget of the proposal is drawn up in accordance with the conditions for this Call for proposals; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; all of the required annexes are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call you need to fill out the application form and the following compulsory annexes: budget form: this must be submitted in ISAAC as an excel file and is set up according the conditions of the Call for proposals; declarations co-funding from co-funders (see Section 3.1.2 and 3.5.6); letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (if applicable, see Section 3.1); form ‘Statements and signature’.

title: Open Competition Domain Science – XS package 25-3

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The ENW-XS category is specifically intended to encourage curiosity-driven and bold research that involves the relatively rapid exploration of a promising idea. At the end of the ENW-XS project, researchers can decide for themselves whether or not to pursue this new line of research any further. One way in which this can be done is to submit a proposal within another funding programme in the Open Competition Domain Science (ENW-M and ENW-XL) or elsewhere.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers who have obtained a PhD can apply if they hold a salaried position for the duration of the application process and  the project. ENW-XS is open for postdocs. Read the conditions in section 3.1 of the call for proposals for the possibilities of requesting your own salary in ENW-XS. Applicants who had a proposal dealt with in the immediately preceding ENW-XS package (25-2; deadline 6 May 2025) may not make a submission

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For research proposals in ENW-XS, applicants may apply for sums of up to €50,000. There are no requirements concerning the type of research to be conducted. Also, there are very few restrictions on the subjects of proposals. For instance, they could involve the appointment of temporary staff, material costs, the costs involved in performing ‘citizen science’, and travel expenses for commuting to other locations to conduct experiments. Postdocs are permitted to request their own salary. The appointment of PhD students of physician assistants is subject to additional conditions. For details of these conditions, see Annex 7.1 to the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Three deadlines for submitting proposals for ENW-XS projects are specified for 2025. For the most up-to-date ENW-XS webpage please see www.nwo.nl/enw-xs-en Package Submission deadlines Decision anticipated by 25-1 18 February 2025, at 14:00:00 CET 17 April 2025 25-2 6 May 2025, at 14:00:00 CEST 10 July 2025 25-3 16 September 2025, at 14:00:00 CEST 20 November 2025 Indication of timetable with respect to next deadline: Deadline for submission of proposals 16 September 2025, at 14:00:00 CEST Assessment of admissibility 16 September – 2 October2025 Disclosure of personal interests 17-25 September 2025 Proposals sent to assessors 7 October 2025* NWO receives assessment reports 28 October 2025*, at 14:00:00 CEST NWO Domain Science Board reaches a decision 20 November 2025 * these dates are tentative and can differ with a few days, you will always get 10 working days for the assessment

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria High-risk/high-gain Is the proposed research innovative, highly speculative, and ground-breaking? Could the research proposal potentially have a scientific impact? Feasibility of the research plan The objective, rationale, and work plan must be clearly described. Can the project be carried out within the maximum period of one year and within the requested budget? Does the proposed research align with the aim of the Call, as detailed in Chapter 2? Proposals must be drawn up with a wide audience in mind, to enable assessors from all Domain Science disciplines to properly evaluate the research plan and the following assessment aspects. The assessors may award a lower ranking to any proposals that are drawn up in a way that is not accessible to those in other disciplines. Procedure All applications submitted for one of the deadlines are treated as a package. Per package the applications are separated into two clusters. Applicants choose in which of the following clusters their application is assessed: Life Sciences Physical and Exact Sciences Per cluster the applications are separated in group A and group B. The applications in groups A are assessed by the applicants in group B and vice versa. The NWO office will determine the final ranks of the applications based on the ranks submitted by the assessors. Two final rankings will be established per package; two for each cluster. There will be no opportunity for a rebuttal. The NWO Domain Science Board will decide whether to grant or refuse an application based on the results of the four rankings and the available financial means.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: When drawing up a proposal, be sure to make it anonymous: Applicants should include neither their own name nor those of any individuals in their research group; Applicants should not include details of any affiliations with institutes or knowledge institutions; Applicants should not include references to any publications that they have authored or co-authored; Applicants should not include the names of any partners (or potential partners). Applicants should be aware that they will be required to assess proposals from all Domain Science disciplines in the chosen cluster. Therefore, the own proposal must be written with a wide audience in mind.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Mandatory annexes: budget form (Excel) and information form. Optional annexes: declaration appointment PhD (see Annex 7.1 in the Call)

title: Large-Scale Research Infrastructure (LSRI)-Upgrade 2025
programme: National Roadmap for Large-Scale Research Facilities

characteristics.Research programme: National Roadmap for Large-Scale Research Facilities

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Via LSRI-upgrade 2025, NWO strategically invests into upgrades of operational LSRIs related to the national Roadmap LSRI, which are embedded in the applying research organisations. By means of this, the LSRI are brought to (beyond) state-of-the-art with the aim of extending the operational lifetime of this LSRI. Upgrading concerns investments in renewal of LSRIs and/or investments in (joint) strategic linking of LSRIs. Greening of the LSRI can be part of the investments. LSRIs for which upgrade investment is requested should be operational and embedded in the applying research organisations. The assessment procedure will test the extent to which this is the case.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: A main applicant submits the statement of intent and the proposal on behalf of the consortium. There are four possible categories of participants within a consortium: Main applicant, Co-applicants, Co-funder(s) (not compulsory), Cooperation partner(s) (not compulsory). Additional conditions Upgrade funding can only be applied for by consortia associated with LSRI for which the following cumulative conditions apply: For the realisation of (part of) the LSRI, an LSRI project has been funded in a Call for proposals related to the Roadmap 2008, 2012, 2016 or 2021 (see Annex 7); the LSRI is included in the LSRI landscape; the LSRI is long-term and future-proof embedded in the applying research organisation(s); the LSRI is operational (see threshold criteria in section 4.3.1). Other conditions You can find the full terms associated with this call in the call brochure on this page.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A consortium can request a minimum of € 1,000,000 and maximum of € 3,500,000 (NWO-contribution) for an upgrade project with a project duration of 5 years. A contribution of at least 25% of the total project budget (NWO-contribution + contribution of the applying consortium to the upgrade project) by the applying consortium (own-contribution) is mandatory. The total budget of the proposed LSRI project amounts to minimum € 1,333,333. Within the total investment, a distinction is made between capital investments and initial running costs. The following cost categories are possible: Investments, IT costs, Membership costs, Material costs, Personnel costs.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for statements of intent and letters of support is 12 June 2025 , before 14:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting a full proposal is 16 September 2025 , before 14:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals are assessed against four criteria with equal weighting: Scientific importance, Strategic importance and impact, Embedding and organisational aspects, Technical feasibility. Procedure The assessment procedure consists of the following steps: Submission of statements of intent and letters of support, deadline 12 June 2025; Submission of a full proposal, deadline 16 September 2025; Peer review by referees, October - December 2025; Submission of a rebuttal, January 2026; First meeting Assessment Committee (interview selection), March 2026; Interviews & Second meeting Assessment Committee, April 2026; Decision by the Executive Board of NWO, May - June 2026.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: NWO will test your statement of intent and application for conditions set out in the Call for proposals. Only if this meets the conditions it will be processed. Submitting a statement of intent is a requirement to submit an application.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Statement of intent & letter of support Prior to submitting an application, the main applicant must announce the initiative digitally by submitting a statement of intent in ISAAC. The statement of intent is not assessed. In addition to the statement of intent, a letter of support must be submitted signed by the project leader or his formal successor, from the funded LSRI project to which the upgrade is associated. Applications aimed at strategic linking of LSRIs require a letter of support per associated LSRI project. The letter of support must be emailed to LSRIupgrade@nwo.nl as a PDF file (without protection) separately from the statement of intent. Other attachments are not allowed. A mandatory format for this letter of support can be found on the NWO funding page for this Call for proposals. Application For this call, we ask you to submit the following attachments in addition to filling in the application form. For all attachments, a mandatory format can be downloaded from this page or from the online application system ISAAC. The completed budget format in Excel; The completed and signed submission letter from the highest administrative body of the institution or organisation that acts as the official secretary of the consortium (letter of commitment); For each applying institution, a signed letter of guarantee confirming the own contribution; For each co-funder, a Declaration of Co- funding ; If necessary, statement appointment and project supervision.

title: NWA Digital Technology and Work
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This Call for proposals aims to develop new knowledge on the impact of digital technology on work and workers, and on the collaboration and interaction that emerges between humans, technology and organisations when technology is deployed in the workplace. Such knowledge will help respond to opportunities and prevent or mitigate negative consequences. The programme aims to develop knowledge and action perspectives for the benefit of policymakers and organisations that want to deploy digital technologies in a healthy, safe, human-centric and inclusive manner. For organisations, the programme will offer insight into the process of deploying digital technologies and their impact, as well as an understanding of opportunities for the continuous development of technology in order to improve the suitability of work (and work processes). For policymakers, the programme will yield knowledge to formulate policies that will discourage the undesirable implementation of technology and encourage the desirable and effective implementation of technology.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: An application must be submitted by the main applicant on behalf of the consortium. There are four categories of participants within a consortium: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Cooperation partner(s); Co-funder(s) (optional). A consortium should consist of at least a main applicant, a co-applicant and cooperation partner. The conditions for each type of participant are explained in more detail in the Call for Proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: Collaborative workshops aim to bring together ideas and stimulate collaboration and submission of a limited number of research proposals by broadly-based (and possibly innovative) consortiums. The workshops also offer participants insights into the balance of forces around a theme. The respective expertise of the participants and their contribution to the call’s theme constitute the substantive basis of the workshops. There are two consecutive workshops in which NWO actively supports mutual exploration. In the end, however, the parties themselves decide whether they want to initiate a collaboration and, if so, with what other parties. After the workshops, the participants will have gained an overview of the possibilities and opportunities of clustering and collaboration. They will then elaborate their proposals. Further information on the collaborative workshops will be published on the NWO call page. Participation in both collaborative workshops is mandatory for main applicants. The collaborative workshops will be held on 22 April 2025 and 8 May 2025 . The deadline for registering for the collaborative workshops is 15 April 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST .

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of at least €2.750.000 and at most €2.865.000 can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 72 months/ 6 years. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. The available budget modules are listed in the Call for proposals. Apply only for funding that is vital to realise the project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for registering for the collaborative workshops is 15 April 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST . The deadline for submitting proposals is 16 September 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (weight 20%), Envisaged impact and route to impact ( weight 20%), Quality of the consortium (weight 30%), Quality of the research (weight 30%). Procedure The deadline for submitting proposals is 16 September 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST . For the assessment of the proposals, the committee will make use of pre-advices and interviews will be conducted. A final decision on funding by the NWO Executive Board is expected in December 2025.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: An application is prepared after the collaborative workshops, where research ideas were brought together, possible collaborations with other workshop participants were explored and where crucial initial agreements for joint development of the application were made.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: The (eventual) main applicant must have participated in both collaborative workshops.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in foms
fill_in_forms.text: To prepare your application, go through the following steps: download the application form from the online application system ISAAC or from the NWO website (on the website of the relevant funding instrument); fill in the application form; save the application form as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes in ISAAC; fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. Compulsory annex (es) : Budget form*; declarations of support from cooperation partner(s); statement of co-funder(s) (mandatory if applicable); guarantee for continuity in project supervision (obligatory if applicable, see section 3.1); form ‘Statement and signature’. *In July 2025, an updated version of the budget form will be made available. Please use this updated version when submitting your application.

title: Technology development for circular batteries
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this Call for Proposals is to contribute to the innovation programme of the NGF proposal ‘Material Independence and Circular Batteries’. This Call for Proposals is thus aimed at realising key technologies in the battery production and value chain, accelerating the energy transition, creating interdependence and increasing sustainable earning capacity. To achieve those key positions in the development of different parts of the battery value chain, this call comprises three separate programme lines: Program line 1: Selective recovery of materials from lithium iron phosphate batteries and associated mixed waste streams. Program line 2: Development of sustainable next generation sodium-ion batteries with lower environmental impact and improved performance. Program line 3: Development of innovative manufacturing technologies for battery systems suitable for Long Duration Energy Storage applications. In addition, research on techno-economic feasibility, impact assessments and life-cycle analysis are integral parts of this programme.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may act as main or co-applicants if they are employed by a Dutch research organisation such as universities, universities of applied sciences, TO2 institutes or other institutes. For research proposals, consortia must be formed consisting of at least one main applicant, one co-applicant and one cooperation partner. Cooperation partners can be other research institutions as well as companies or public organisations. The development of an impact plan is also a mandatory part of the proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: The consortia envisaged here must consist of at least one main applicant, one co-applicant and one cooperation partner, where main and co-applicants should be affiliated with research institutions eligible for funding. As cooperation partners, other research institutions, companies or public organisations, for example, may participate in projects. To facilitate consortium formation, a one-day workshop will be organised, for which interested parties have to register. The registration form as well as more information about the procedure can be found on the corresponding website.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: This Call for Proposals includes three programme lines with their own grant budget: For programme line 1, a maximum of one application will be awarded for a maximum of €1,440,000 grant, For programme line 2, a maximum of one application will be awarded for a maximum of €8,000,000 grant, For programme line 3, a maximum of one application will be awarded for a maximum of €5,000,000 grant. Subsidies can be applied for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. The intended duration of projects should be four years with a maximum of no more than six years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline registration for workshop: Thursday 15 may 2025, 14:00 CEST. Deadline application: Tuesday 16 september 2025, 14:00 CEST.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications submitted within this Call for Proposals will be assessed according to the following criteria: Criterion 1: Scientific quality of the research, Criterion 2: Scientific impact and expected contribution to the development of battery technologies, Criterion 3: Quality of the consortium. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: registration for workshop, participation in workshop (mandatory for main applicant), submission of the proposal, admissibility of the proposal, pre-advice by member of expert commission, interview and assessment committee meeting, decision-making.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: You can find more information about this call on Battery Competence Cluster - NL

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For this call, it is mandatory to prepare your proposal in English. To prepare your proposal, please go through the following steps: download the application form from the ISAAC online application system or from this website, fill in the application form, save the form as a pdf and submit it to ISAAC with any mandatory attachment(s), complete the information requested online in ISAAC. The main applicant submits the application via ISAAC, NWO's electronic submission system. During the assessment process, NWO communicates with the main applicant.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: Participation in the workshop for consortium formation is mandatory for main applicants or their substitutes.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the application form, we also ask you to include the following mandatory annexes: budget form, declaration of support from cooperation partner(s), ‘Statement and Signature’ form, if applicable: guarantee for continuity in project supervision.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The SSH Open Competition XS Call for proposals is specifically intended to encourage curiosity-driven and innovative research that involves the relatively rapid exploration of a promising idea. The characteristics of SSH-XS research are: the proposed research is innovative; it is not certain in advance whether the intended objectives will be achieved; the result of each project contributes to the advancement of existing science and new scientific insights.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers with a promising idea who obtained their PhD between 5 and 10 years ago may submit an application if they have a paid appointment for at least the duration of the application process and the duration of the proposed project. The submission deadline for the round is the reference date of the graduation date. The graduation date on the PhD diploma (degree) is leading. For round 3, the applicant must have obtained their PhD between 19 September 2015 and 18 September 2020. Extra conditions: an SSH-XS application has a single applicant and no co-applicants; an applicant may submit a maximum of one application in the SSH-XS, call round 2025; an applicant - when using the extension scheme - may not submit an application for multiple types of funding instruments within the NWO Open Competition programme during the same calendar year (2025); applicants may not be part of an XS project for which the end date has not yet passed at the time of submission. Please note: the extension scheme applies to the SSH Open Competition XS in 2025. For more information and the online application form for extension see: https://www.nwo.nl/en/extension-clause .

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals a maximum of 50,000 euro can be applied for. The minimum duration of the proposed project is 6 months and the maximum duration is 12 months. Applicants are required to draw up a realistic budget and explain why the spending choices are relevant to the project. This explanation must cover all of the personnel requested. Please only apply for funding that is vital to realise the project. A more detailed explanation of the budget modules can be found in the annexe to this Call for proposals (Section 7.1).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: For 2025, 3 deadlines for submitting applications are specified: Round 1: 6 March 2025, before 14:00:00 CET (closed) Round 2: 8 May 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST (closed) Round 3: 18 September 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST When you submit your application in ISAAC, you will also need to enter some details online. Therefore, please start submitting your application at least one day before the deadline of the round. Applications that are submitted after the deadline will not be taken into consideration.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessors who rank the applications will focus on the following criteria. These criteria are not weighted. At the discretion of an assessor, both of these aspects may be used for the purposes of ranking. However, that ranking must be accompanied by a short written explanation to substantiate the given rank based on the assessment criteria listed below. Innovative aspect Is the proposed research innovative and groundbreaking? Does the proposed research contribute to existing and new scientific insights? Does the research proposal potentially have a scientific impact? Feasibility of the research plan The objective and work plan must be clearly described. Does the methodology seem suitable to achieve the indicated project goal? Is the planning of the research plan appropriate and are the activities included in the planning with sufficient clarity? Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the relevant panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria described above. The SSH disciplines and associated (sub)disciplines are listed on the NWO website: https://www.nwo.nl/en/overview-panels-domain-social-sciences-and-humani… . The assessors may award a lower ranking to any applications that are drawn up in a way that is not accessible to those in other disciplines. Procedure The applicant determines by which panel the application will be processed by means of the SSH main discipline specified in the information form (see Section 4.2.3 of the Call for proposals). Applications processed per panel are divided into 2 groups, so that each panel consists of Group A and Group B. In principle, the classification takes place at the time of submission to NWO, taking into account the personal interests declared by the applicants. The assessors and applicants associated with a particular package of proposals do not meet. NWO will decide the final rankings of the applications, based on the rankings submitted by the assessors. Per round, 6 final rankings will be established – per panel one for Group A and one for Group B. There will be no opportunity to dispute the rankings. The SSH Domain Board will base its decision about awarding or rejecting the applications on the results of the 6 final rankings, the assessment reports and the available funding.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: For more information: FAQ

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The research idea needs to be drawn up anonymously, which means the application form meets the following conditions: applicants should not include their own name nor the name of any individuals in their research group; applicants should not include details of any affiliations with institutes or knowledge institutions; applicants should not include references to any publications that they have authored or co-authored; applicants should not include the names of any (potential) partners. Applications must be drawn up with a wide scientific audience in mind so that assessors from the relevant panel will be able to properly evaluate the research plan and the assessment criteria.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Compulsory annexes to a proposal in this Call for proposals are the Budget form (Excel) and the Information form (pdf).

title: KIC Nature-inclusive decision making in business
programme: Nature-inclusive decision making in business KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027 Knowledge and Innovation Covenant
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: Nature-inclusive decision making in business KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027 Knowledge and Innovation Covenant

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Companies active within the Agriculture, Horticulture, Water and Food (LWV) sectors can only work on restoring biodiversity if they know the expected impact of measures. A good understanding of this impact gives companies a choice of available courses of action. This requires researchers to develop integrated monitoring techniques and data management for biodiversity, specifically for business within the LWV sectors. The aim of the call is form an interdisciplinary research plan in cooperation with the business community in which decision-making is linked to biodiversity monitoring. The expected economic impact of the research includes enabling nature-inclusive business practices and new business models around biodiversity monitoring.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Pre-proposals and full proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. Full, associate and assistant professors, lectors and other researchers with similar positions may act as main or co-applicants if they have a tenured position (and therefore have a paid employment contract for an indefinite period of time) or have a tenure track agreement. A proposal  is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants co-funders are involved as well. Details on who may apply are described in section 3.1 of the call text.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of €750,000 a maximum of €1,750,000 can be applied for. The grant ceiling for the call for proposals totals € 7.000.000. With this, NWO finances maximally 90% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 6 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting pre-proposals is February 6, 2025, at 14:00:00 hours CET . The deadline for submitting full proposals is September 18, 2025, at 14:00:00 hours CEST .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem posed and problem analysis, contribution to solution Expected impact and route to impact Quality of the consortium Quality of the research Criteria 1 and 2 each carry a weighting of 20% in the overall assessment, and criteria 3 and 4 each carry a weighting of 30%. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: (Online) Matchmaking; 7 November 2024 (not required); submission of the pre-proposal; 6 February 2025; admissibility of the pre-proposal; advice assessment committee; March 2025; submission of the proposal; 18 September 2025; admissibility of the proposal; interview selection (if necessary); interview; October/November 2025; assessment committee meeting; decision-making by the NWO board. December 2025.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The application should indicate how the proposed research answers the Call for proposals, which societal problem/challenge the project addresses, as well as what the goals, hypotheses, research methods and scientific feasibility of the project are. Also, in the proposal needs to be described how the consortium is built up, and how the research is carried out to aid in the application of the research results. The Impact Plan approach needs to be applied and described in the proposal.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, we ask you to fill in the pre-application form and the following annex: Letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (if applicable).

programme: Towards a New Labour Market in 2050: Energy Transition and Digitalization in the Built Environment
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration

characteristics.Research programme: Towards a New Labour Market in 2050: Energy Transition and Digitalization in the Built Environment

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call is focused on developing knowledge on what the labour market of the built environment will look like in 2050. This research provides insight into the future-oriented challenges posed by the digital and energy transitions to the labour market of the built environment. Insights and knowledge from this call should contribute to the development of a future framework for the labour market of the built environment.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The main applicant submits the application on behalf of the consortium. There are four categories of participant within a consortium: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Cooperating partner(s); Co-funder(s) (optional). More information about the four categories of participants as well as specific requirements regarding who can apply, can be found in paragraph 3.1 of the call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The main applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. The available budget modules (including maximum amounts) are listed in the Call for proposals. Apply only for what is essential to carry out the project. The rates and an explanation of these budget modules are given in Annex 7.1 of the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline proposal: Thursday 25 September 2025 by 14:00:00 CEST

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis, contribution to a solution Envisaged impact and route to impact Quality of the consortium Quality of the research Each assessment criterion consists of several subcriteria. More information the criteria can be found in paragraph 4.3 of the call for proposals. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: Registering for and participating in collaborative workshops; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; preliminary advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; meeting of the assessment committee; decision-making. Detailed information about the procedure can be found in the Call for Proposals, Section 4.2. Composition committee/jury Please note that it is possible that not all committee members mentioned on this page have assessed all applications, for example in the context of the NWO Code for Dealing with Personal Interests. Please contact the round secretary if you have any questions about this. Dr. G. Borragán – Committee member Dr. W. van der Gaast – Committee member MSc. P. Hermans – Chairperson Dr. I. Kondratenko – Committee member Prof. R. Maalek – Committee member Dr. J. Talen – Committee member

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: An application is prepared (and submitted) after the collaborative workshops, where research ideas were brought together, possible collaborations with other workshop participants were explored and where crucial initial agreements for joint development of the application were made.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: The (eventual) lead applicant must have participated in both collaborative workshop.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To prepare your application, please follow the steps below: download the application form from the online application system ISAAC or from th NWO website (on the website of the relevant financing instrument); complete the application form; save the form as a PDF file and submit it to ISAAC with any mandatory attachments; complete the information requested online in ISAAC. Mandatory attachments: budget; support statements from collaboration partner(s) (mandatory if applicable); statement from co-financier(s) (mandatory if applicable); confirmation of contribution to investments (mandatory if applicable, see section 7.1); guarantee for continuity in project management (mandatory if applicable, see section 3.1); form ‘Statement and signature’.

title: NWA Loneliness
programme: Loneliness Dutch Research Agenda (NWA) Thematic Programming
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research
characteristics.Research programme: Loneliness Dutch Research Agenda (NWA) Thematic Programming

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: We know little about the different forms of loneliness experienced by different groups in society or the causes of loneliness and effective approaches for tackling it. Moreover, the translation of available knowledge into practice, the promotion of expertise among professionals and volunteers, and the development of policy in the field of loneliness are lagging behind. The NWA Loneliness programme aims to change all of this.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals should be submitted by a consortium in which the various types of research in the knowledge chain and civil society organisations must be represented. For this call it is important that societal partners participate in the consortium. There are four categories of participant within a consortium: Main applicant, Co-applicant(s), Cooperating partner(s), Co-funder(s) (optional). A consortium must consist of at least a main applicant, co-applicant and cooperating partner. More information about the four categories of participants as well as specific requirements regarding who can apply, can be found in section 3.1 of the call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For this call for proposals, a minimum of €800,000 and a maximum of €970,000 per target group project can be requested for an application in phase I. In phase II, a minimum of €700,000 and a maximum of €835,000 can be requested for the cross-cutting project. The maximum duration of a project in phase I is 54 months/4,5 years. The maximum duration of a project in phase II is 48 months/four years. An explanation of the budget modules available can be found in paragraph 3.2 of this Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting proposals in phase I is Thursday 29 June 2023, before 14:00:00 CEST . The deadline for submitting the revised proposal in phase II is Tuesday 14 October 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST . PLEASE NOTE: This call is open in phase II only to the consortia already granted in phase I.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Proposals in phase I and II will be assessed against the following four criteria: Problem definition and analysis, contribution to a solution (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Each criteria consists of several subcriteria, which differ in phase I and phase II. The exact criteria can be found in paragraph 4.2 of the Call for proposals. Procedure No referees will be used to evaluate the proposals. The assessment committee will assess the applications according to the criteria for applications in section 4.2 of the Call for Proposals. During an interview, consortia will be given the opportunity to present their proposal and respond to questions and comments from the Assessment Committee. The assessment committee discusses all proposals and interviews, assesses the proposals according to the assessment criteria and establishes a prioritisation for allocation within this call.

title: KIC Long-term programmes 2024
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Strategy 2024-2027 KIC 2024-2027 Langetermijnprogramma's - Strategiegedreven Consortia met Impact
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Strategy 2024-2027 KIC 2024-2027 Langetermijnprogramma's - Strategiegedreven Consortia met Impact

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: By funding the research projects that are the scientific foundation of the long-term programmes, NWO facilitates the development of strategic consortia consisting of researchers, public and private partners, that will set out or continue their collaboration throughout this ten-year programme. Tne ten-year collaboration within the long-term programme is the starting point of a long-term, forceful and sustainable stimulus of innovation and the national development of a scientific field in the Netherlands. A long-term programme is initiated with the aim and structure to continue after the initial funding of this LTP.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and two or more co-applicants. Full, associate and assistant professors, lectors and other researchers with similar positions may act as main or co-applicants if they have a tenured position (and therefore have a paid employment contract for an indefinite period of time) or have a tenure track agreement. A proposal  is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants co-funders are involved as well. Participation of research organisations is maximised, research organisations are allowed to apply as main applicant twice. More details on who can apply and additional conditions for eligibility can be found in the call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For this call for proposals, a consortium can apply for a minimum of €9.000.000 and a maximum of €25.000.000 of NWO funding. NWO funds a maximum of 30% of the total LTP budget. The other 70% is contributed in joint funding by the consortium partners.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submission of an (obligatory) initiative was 15 April 2025, 14:00 hours . The deadline for submission of an application is 14 October 2025, 14:00 hours . The deadline for submitting a (mandatory) initiative was 15 April 2025, 14:00. No more initiatives can be submitted now, but interested researchers can still join the consortia that registered before this deadline. Initiatives

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The application will be assessed on the criteria from the call for proposals: Problem statement and analysis, and strategic importance Expected impact and route to impact Quality of the consortium Quality of the research Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: submission of a statement of intent; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Given the characteristic collaboration that is required in the LTP, we recommend that consortiumpartners are included and involved in the writing process of the application. Please also note that the application will have to adhere to additional terms, as well as those that are customary in all NWO KIC calls. For these, we refer to chapter 3.4 in the call for proposals, please inform yourself about these.

title: Knowledge in Action – Towards Collective Water Management in East Brabant

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Knowledge-in-Action grant stimulates collaboration between science and practice to accelerate a just transition towards collective water management in East Brabant. This call challenges societal partners and researchers to develop concrete interventions in co-creation and learning that reduce water vulnerability and redefine the role of the water sector. Proposals can be submitted on specific sub-themes of the Knowledge-in-Action Agenda (chapter 2.2). The intended result is transformative knowledge and practical breakthroughs arising from action-oriented, cyclical processes of experimenting, researching and adjusting. This thematic call welcomes diverse approaches - from (trans)disciplinary, visual, design-oriented, entrepreneurial or very process-oriented, reflective or activist - as long as they unite practice and research to develop and share knowledge. The grant offers a unique opportunity to contribute to sustainable, just solutions for collective water management in East Brabant.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Who is this call for proposals for? Practitioner organisations experimenting with climate solutions Researchers who want to mobilise knowledge for transformative societal impact Pioneers who are already working on sustainable alternatives Anyone who wants to connect science and practice to drive climate transition Who can apply? Employees of knowledge institutions and societal organisations in the Kingdom of the Netherlands who want to contribute to transformative projects for collective water management in East Brabant. Collaboration is key. We believe that the best solutions arise when science and practice work together. Therefore, your team should consist of: at least one knowledge institution (such as universities, universities of applied sciences, university medical centres and other public and private knowledge institutions) at least one societal organisation (such as NGOs, non-profit foundations, municipalities, provinces, water authorities, safety regions, companies, self-employed persons and other public and private organisations) All project partners play an active role in developing the proposal and implementing the project. Everyone commits to the agreed time commitment and contributes their expertise.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Budget: Maximum € 50,000 per project What can be applied for: Salaries of project partners Materials and resources Temporary hiring of experts Other project activities Student assistants Not possible: Recruitment of new academic staff and basic facilities Creative approach welcome: We encourage non-traditional activities. Feel free to involve professionals from the creative sector. Desired duration: max. 9 months (longer duration must be well substantiated) Start: within 3 months of allocation More details can be found in chapter 3.1 of the call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Proposals can be submitted until 14 October 2025 14:00:00 CET . Please note! Start submitting your proposal in time in our submission system Isaac and ensure that project partners who are not in the Isaac database register at least 15 working days before the deadline via relatiebeheer@nwo.nl . For the assessment of additional knowledge institutions, the assessment documents should be sent by e-mail to programmas@hetkin.nl no later than 15 working days before the submission deadline (i.e. no later than 23 September 2025 before 14:00:00 CEST ). More information on preparation, mandatory annexes and submission requirements can be found in sections 3.3 and 3.4 of the call for proposals. Are you planning to submit a proposal? We encourage you to express your interest here . This way, everyone can see who wants to submit for which topic and new collaborations can potentially emerge.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals are evaluated in terms of content on four main criteria (with sub-criteria), each of which must score satisfactorily: Relevance: Connection to Knowledge-in-Action agenda and transition questions. Quality: Clear (learning) objectives, well-founded approach, learning and reflective work process, knowledge use and diffusion, ensuring justice and sustainability. Co-creation en knowledge development: Integration of different knowledge types, active involvement of all project partners, action perspective for actors involved. Feasibility: Appropriate activities and budget, realistic timeline (duration >9 months requires substantiation), proportional resources compared to activity plan. For the full assessment criteria read section 4.2 of the call for proposals. Procedure The assessment procedure consists of the following steps: submission of the proposal consideration of the proposal pre-advice of the evaluation committee; meeting of the evaluation committee selection (if applicable); decision-making. Assessment: An external, independent assessment committee of science and practice experts evaluates proposals on their own merits with satisfactory (+1) or unsatisfactory (-1) for each sub-criterion. To receive a positive advice, each main criterion must be assessed with a sufficient rating (Sum of all sub-criteria is >0). Selection: In case of more proposals with a positive advice than available budget, additional selection will take place. For more information about the assessment see chapter 4.1 of the call for proposals. Composition commission/jury Please note that it is possible that not all committee or jury members mentioned on this page have assessed all pre-proposals or applications, for example in the context of the NWO Code for Dealing with Personal Interests. Please contact the round secretary if you have any questions about this. M. Aalbersberg M. Taanman E.  Tromp L.  Squintani - Chair T.  van Hattum M.  van der Kruis F.  van Lamoen

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Do you have questions about this call for proposals? You can contact us via e-mail to programmas@hetkin.nl , or attend one of our online information sessions. These will take place on: Monday 8 September at 10:00-11:00 via this link Monday 29 September at 13:00-14:00 via this link You do not need to register; you can join via the link at the time of the session. This is an opportunity to ask your specific questions, listen to questions from others, or simply obtain some additional general information.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The Knowledge-in-Action grant focuses on joint experimentation, investigating and learning in and with the field. Choose one sub-theme from the agenda and develop a concrete, action-oriented, transformative intervention with a clear learning objective and reflective approach that contributes to the transition. What alternatives and questions need to be explored in order to bring about the desired change towards collective water management? Show transformative ambition: The committee is looking for projects that will bring about real change, not just optimalisation. Co-creation and knowledge development: Show how different types of knowledge come together, and how partners contribute to the proposal and its implementation. Be creative and concrete: Design-oriented, activist or creative approaches with clear impact are welcome. Write in an accessible style: Bear in mind that the committee is broadly composed of experts from different areas of science and practice.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, next to filling in the submission form, we also ask you to enclose a budget form, a signed commitment letter from all project partners and, if applicable, a signed declaration of knowledge institution without assessment and/or a de-minimis declaration.

title: High performance materials for Defence and Safety
programme: High performance materials for Defence and safety Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC - Missie 24/27 -
target_groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with Industry Collaboration with social partners
characteristics.Research programme: High performance materials for Defence and safety Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC - Missie 24/27 -

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The goal of this programme is to build knowledge on the development, applicability, and optimization of high-performance materials and advanced production technologies for defence and security-critical applications. For materials, it must be clear how they can be integrated into a defence or security context. The focus is on new materials that can ultimately be used in both civilian and defence applications (dual-use). Materials that are still in an early stage of development and were not explicitly designed for defence and security applications but could be utilized in this context after further development are also within the scope of this Call. However, material developments originating from the defence sector for application in civilian contexts fall outside the scope of this Call for proposals.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants other participants are involved as well. Four categories of participants can be distinguished in the consortium: 1. Main applicant, 2. Co-applicant(s), 3. Co-funder(s), 4. Cooperation partner(s). The co-funders jointly contribute a net minimum amount of 20% of the total budget for the proposal.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: On 22 May  2025, NWO will facilitate (online) matchmaking activities for this Call for proposals. Your consortium’s participation in these activities is recommended, but not mandatory. In the KIC, the aim of the matchmaking is to bring together and connect researchers from different scientific disciplines and organisations from everyday practice.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of €700,000 and a maximum of €2,000,000 of NWO funding can be applied for in. With this, NWO finances maximally 80% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding. NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting initiatives is Thursday 19 June 2025, before 14:00:00 CEST . The deadline for submitting proposals is Thursday 16 October 2025, before 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Problem definition and analysis (20%), Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%). Procedure The submission of an initiative is mandatory for this Call for proposals. With an initiative you indicate that you have a potential contribution to or an idea for a (sub)project within this Call for proposals and intend to submit an application for this Call for proposals. Applications will be provided with feedback by referees. Applicants will have the opportunity to submit a rebuttal to this. Subsequently, the applications are assessed by the assessment committee. The assessment committee offers its recommendation to the NWO Executive Board for decision-making. In April 2026, the Executive Board determines the final assessment of the applications.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: The KIC call ‘High performance materials for Defence and Safety’ requires submission of an initiative.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: You will find all of the documents that you need for your application here. Applications must be submitted through the ISAAC application and reporting system. If you do not have an ISAAC account yet, then please create one at least one workday in advance. More information about writing a proposal and which forms you need to use can be found below. Please download and use the forms below.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the application form when submitting the complete application, we also ask you to enclose the following mandatory attachments: budget form, declarations co-funding from co-funders (see Section 3.1.2 and 3.5.5), form ‘Statement and signature’, confirmation of institutional contribution to investment (compulsory if applicable, see Section 7.1 Call for proposals), letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (if applicable, see Section 3.1 Call for proposals). Kennisveiligheidsverklaring ENG Other types of attachments are not accepted in this phase of submitting full proposals. Attachments should be uploaded to ISAAC separately from the proposal. All attachments, with the exception of the budget form, should be uploaded as PDF files. The budget form can be uploaded to ISAAC as an Excel file.

title: Long-term Europe – Africa WEF Nexus (LEAWEF) Multilateral Research Programme
programme: South Africa Cooperation South Africa-The Netherlands
target_groups: International Collaboration Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad
characteristics.Research programme: South Africa Cooperation South Africa-The Netherlands

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this Call aligns with the objectives of the LEAWEF multilateral research programme. It focuses on two subthemes: 1. Climate Change Adaptation and 2. Just Energy Transitions. With this programme the 10 participating Science Granting Councils (SGCs) aim to enhance and strengthen sustainable research collaboration between their countries, by inviting consortia in which researchers from knowledge institutions will work with societal partners from the public, semi-public and private sector, in order to increase the societal relevance of their research. This programme, therefore, serves as an international research platform designed to provide the knowledge needed to support transformations towards sustainability. The programme seeks to: Develop and connect knowledge to increase the (societal) impact of research; Explore new development pathways; and Find new ways to accelerate transitions towards sustainable development.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Broad consortia consisting of researchers, knowledge institutions, societal stakeholders and practitioners from Sub Saharan Africa and the Netherlands are invited to apply. Each proposal must be submitted by a consortium consisting of at least three eligible applicants, from at least three different participating countries Each consortium should include at least one Co-operation Partner from one of the participating countries. You are encouraged to add additional co-operation partners. The Consortium Lead (project coordinator) must be eligible to be funded and request funding from its national participating Science Granting Council. A Consortium Lead and Co-Principal Investigator (Co-PI) can only participate in a maximum of one proposal, a Co-operation Partner in a maximum of two proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The total budget for this call amounts to 4 392 930 EUR (all funding organisations combined). With the available total budget, the partners aim to fund around seven projects for a maximum of 48 months. The funding organisations, as mentioned in section 1.2. in the call for proposals, will each fund researchers from their respective countries in a joint project. See section 3.2 for guidelines on budget allocation against eligible research activities. Further to this, researchers should consult Annex III: Individual National Eligibility and Funding Regulations for detailed information on the budget and what can be applied for.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for the submission of Partnership request forms (not mandatory), which are to be submitted to NWO, is 4 November 2024, at 14:00:00 hours CET . The deadline for the submission of Concept notes (mandatory), which are to be submitted to NWO, was 11 February 2025, at 14:00:00 hours CET . The deadline for the submission of Full proposals (mandatory) is 30 October 2025 , at 14:00:00 hours CEST . All applications must be submitted to NWO. Additionally, some Science Granting Councils require a submission via their national system (see national annexes).

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The Concept notes and Full proposals submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: I.              Quality of the research proposal II.             Potential for societal impact III.            Quality of the consortium A more detailed description of the (sub)criteria can be found in the call for proposals Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: Submission of a Partnership request form Submission of the Concept note Eligibility screening of the Concept note Assessment of the Concept note Submission of the Full proposal Eligibility screening of the Full proposal Pre-assessment by assessment committee Rebuttal Meeting of the assessment committee Decision-making by Science Granting Councils

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The steps in writing your application are: download the application form for the concept note, or for the full application, from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the relevant funding instrument); fill in the application form according to the instructions in the form; save the application form in Isaac as a PDF file and upload it with mandatory attachments; complete the requested data online in Isaac. The application must comply with the formal submission requirements mentioned in section 3.4 of this Call for Proposals and respond to the objectives of the programme. Applicants should use the instructions and explanations in the application form.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Standard forms for both the submission of the Concept note and the full application are available on the funding page of this Call for proposals on the NWO website. All attachments, except the budget form, should be uploaded as a pdf file. The budget form can be uploaded as an Excel file in Isaac. Please note that some Science Granting Councils may require their researchers to also submit nationally. Researchers are therefore advised to consult the National Annex of their respective Science Granting Council. Compulsory annexes: Budget (and separate national budget if requested by Science Granting Council); A letter of commitment from the organisations of the Consortium Lead, Co-Principal Investigators and Co-operation Partners, in which the institutions confirm that they agree to the conditions required for the execution of the project. The letter must be signed by the Dean of the faculty or director of the organisation and be printed on the letterhead of the institution or organisation; Draft consortium agreement; CVs of both Consortium Lead, all Co-Principal Investigators and Co-operations partners; In case of co-financing: a letter of guarantee from the co-financing organisation confirming the numeric amount that will be provided as co-financing in Euro. In case the organisation of a consortium member provides co-financing, this confirmation can be included in the letter of commitment. Letters of guarantee and commitment are unconditional and do not contain any opt-out clauses. If co-financing is provided at the application/project level (in kind or in cash) for projects were NWO is the funding agency, it is important that this co-financing is confirmed by the contributing organisations by means of a Co-funding declaration. A format can be downloaded from the programme webpage..
