title: Open Technology Programme 2025
target_groups: Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with Industry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Open Technology Programme (OTP) aims to stimulate free and unrestricted applied and technical-scientific research within the scope of the NWO Domain AES. The programme is characterised by the absence of disciplinary boundaries and promotes research that leads to societal and/or scientific impact. Researchers can, depending on the nature and objectives of their research, decide for themselves whether the focus is on scientific impact, societal impact, or a combination of both. Collaboration with relevant knowledge users from the public and/or private sectors is a key feature of the OTP, allowing for increased application of research results and impact opportunities. The OTP is specifically intended for research activities and is not intended for applications predominantly focus on other activities, such as investments, contract research or clinical trials.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations: universities as referred to in Article 1.8 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act and universities listed in the Policy Rules for Universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; university medical centres by which is meant academic hospitals as referred to in Article 1.13 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act; institutes affiliated to the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; Naturalis Biodiversity Center; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of at most €950,000 can be applied for. For projects with an investment character, where more than € 150,000 in investments are requested, a maximum of € 1,050,000 can be applied for. For both projects with and without an investment character, co-funding is required if the total project costs exceed €700,000. The minimum required co-funding is 25% of the project costs above €700,000, with no distinction between co-funding in cash or in kind. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 6 year. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. Apply only for funding that is vital to realise the project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Applications can be submitted on a continuous basis between 13 January 2025 until 12 December 2025 , as long as the grant ceiling or the maximum number of applications submitted has not yet been reached.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals are assessed for scientific quality (50%) as well as impact (50%). Procedure Applications will be submitted to a jury. Each jury will consider 18 applications in order of receipt, provided they meet the conditions for submission. Applications are assessed by international independent referees. The research proposal, the referee reports and the rebuttal are scored by an independent multidisciplinary jury consisting of 10 to 12 members on the criteria ‘scientific quality’ and ‘impact’. A ranking is determined based on these scores. The Board of NWO Domain Applied and Engineering Sciences takes the final award decision based on the prioritization of the proposals.

changes.title: Changes
changes.text: In comparison to the Call for Proposals of OTP 2024, a number of (administrative) changes have been implemented. These are listed below: The conditions in case of early closure of the Call have been clarified (see section 1.2); The maximum NWO contribution has been increased to €950,000 (€1,050,000 for projects with an investment character)(see Section 3.2). The ceiling above which co-funding is mandatory has been increased to €700,000 (see Section 3.5.6); The budget modules have been updated. The NWO domain AES cleanroom regulation has been discontinued and integrated into the Materials budget module (see Section 3.2); All applications are subject to the NWO Co-funding Scheme; this means, among other things, that cash co-funding is invoiced by the applicant rather than by NWO (see section 3.5.6); The deadline for making administration corrections to meet submission requirements has been shortened from four weeks to three weeks (see section 4.2.2); The deadline for submitting the rebuttal has been shortened from 10 working days to five working days (see section 4.2.4); The definition and role of users has been clarified (see Section 5.1.5). For a full explanation see the Call for proposals. Administrative The total number of pages in the application form for chapter 2 and 3 together must not exceed 13 pages, and the total number of pages for chapter 4 must not exceed 2 pages The total number of words in the rebuttal must not exceed 2000 words.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The application should specify which problem or challenge the project addresses, as well as its goals, hypotheses, research methods, and the scientific feasibility of the project. Additionally, at least four users who will be part of the user committee must be included, and in the application should describe how the research can lead to scientific and/or societal impact should be provided.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this Call, in addition to completing the application form, please upload the following attachments: budget: always compulsory; declaration and signature of main applicant: always compulsory; declaration co-funding: compulsory if co-funding is involved; statement appointment and project supervision: compulsory if the applicant’s appointment does not cover the entire duration of the project (e.g., in the case of a tenure-track agreement); motivation for resubmission in Dutch or English: compulsory if the application is a resubmission of an application that was rejected in a previous OTP assessment procedure.

title: Cellular Agriculture - towards larger scale and lower costs
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call aims to address key topics needed to cost-effectively and successfully produce at scale. Specifically, we seek research proposals that focus on advances in process and/or product technology and biology and fall within one of the two research lines: (A) precision fermentation, (B) cultured meat & seafood. A proposal should focus on and be limited to the topics indicated in the Call for proposals (see paragraph 2.1).

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may act as main or co-applicants if they are employed by a Dutch research organisation such as universities, universities of applied sciences, TO2 institutes or other institutes. For research proposals, consortia must be formed consisting of at least one main applicant and at least one co-funder.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: The consortia envisaged here must consist of at least one main applicant and at least one co-funder. Co-applicants and/or cooperation partners can join the consortium. To facilitate consortium formation, at 25 June 2025 a matchmaking meeting is organised, for which interested parties can register. The registration form as well as more information about the meeting can be found on the corresponding website. The matchmaking meeting is optional and therefore not a mandatory part of the application procedure.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum of €1,371,836 NWO budget can be applied for per project. At least 3 research positions must be applied for per project, of which at least 1 is a PhD student and at most 1 is an Engineering Doctorate (EngD). A postdoc position counts as a research position if it has a minimum duration of 2 years, 1.0 fte (or equivalent). The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment and knowledge utilisation. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 5 years. The co-funders jointly contribute at least 20% of the total project budget; this mandatory co-funding may be provided either in cash or in kind.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Matchmaking meeting (optional): 25 June 2025. Deadline for submitting proposals: 13 November 2025, by 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications submitted within this Call for Proposals will be assessed according to the following criteria: Criterion 1: Quality of the problem statement (20%), Criterion 2: Quality of the proposed research (30%), Criterion 3: Expected impact and route to impact (20%), Criterion 4: Quality of the consortium (30%). Procedure An external, independent assessment committee will be set up for this Call for proposals, consisting of representatives from science and practice with knowledge of the field. The task of the assessment committee is to assess the submitted applications and related documents in relation to each other and on their own merits on the basis of the assessment criteria given in the Call for proposals. Because of the expertise present in the assessment committee, the assessment procedure will be carried out without engaging referees. The procedure consists of the following steps: optional matchmaking event, submission of the proposal, admissibility of the proposal, initial advice from the assessment committee, including the possibility for interview selection, interview, assessment committee meeting, decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: For this call, it is mandatory to prepare your proposal in English. To prepare your proposal, please go through the following steps: download the application form from the ISAAC online application system or from this website, fill in the application form, save the form as a pdf and submit it to ISAAC with any mandatory attachment(s), complete the information requested online in ISAAC.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, in addition to completing the application form, we also ask you to include the following mandatory annexes: budget form, co-funding declaration for each co-funder, if applicable: declaration of support from cooperation partner(s), if applicable: guarantee for continuity in project supervision.

title: RAAK-publiek
target_groups: Applied Sciences Research

characteristics.For specific groups: Applied Sciences Research

downloads.name: Download call for proposals

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this instrument is to fund projects that will help to achieve the digitalization ambitions of the Thematic Digital Competence Centres (TDCCs) and the community they serve. The ambitions are described by the TDCCs in the TDCC roadmaps, and cover data, software and computing. They include ambitions such as: Improving the knowledge and skills of researchers and research support professionals (education and training); How to deal with data sharing bottlenecks (e.g., privacy laws); Establishing data sharing agreements (e.g., metadata standards); Knowledge of the use of software and platforms for sharing and analyzing data; Increasing knowledge of (the proper use of) available computing facilities. In most cases, this is not about developing new scientific knowledge, but about finding and disseminating the right knowledge, and agreeing on its joint application. The projects resulting from this Call for proposals will therefore mainly focus on support and networking. The project activities applied for under this Call for proposals should fit within the above ambitions and the definitions from the Community Framework for State aid for research, development and innovation. See section 2.1 in the Call for proposals for more information.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Individuals may apply if they are permanently employed (and therefore have a remunerated employment contract for an indefinite period of time) or have a tenure track agreement with one of the research organisations listed below: Universities and colleges as referred to in Article 1.8(1) of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act and the universities listed in the Policy Rule Universities in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; university medical centres, meaning academic hospitals as referred to in Article 1.13(1) of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act; KNAW and NWO institutes; The Netherlands Cancer Institute; The Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; NCB Naturalis; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Centre; TO2 federation: Deltares, Maritime Research Institute Netherlands (Marin), Netherlands Organisation for Applied Natural Science Research (TNO), Netherlands Aerospace Centre (NLR) and Wageningen University (WUR). National Institute of Public Health and the Environment (RIVM); Royal Netherlands Meteorological Institute (KNMI); IHE Delft Institute for Water Education; SURF; Netherlands eScience Center. Persons on zero-hours contracts are excluded from submission. It may happen that the applicant's tenure track agreement ends before the envisaged completion date of the project for which the grant is requested, or that the applicant's permanent employment ends before that date due to reaching retirement age. In such cases, the applicant shall attach a statement from his/her employer in which the research organisation concerned guarantees that the project and all persons working on the project for whom a grant is requested will be adequately supervised for the full duration of the project. Applicants with part-time employment must guarantee adequate supervision of the project and of all persons working on the project for whom funding is requested. Additional requirements Applicants affiliated to other research organisations as referred to in Article 1.1(4) of the NWO Grant Rules, the organisation must meet cumulative conditions, see Call for proposals section 3.1 for more information.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A grant of minimum €50,000 and maximum of €400,000 can be applied for per project. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 24 months. The applicant and co-applicants can claim costs for personnel, equipment and knowledge utilisation. The available budget modules (including maximum amounts) with further explanation can be found in the Call for proposals. Apply only for what is essential to carry out the project.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Applications can be submitted from 21 August 2025 until 20 November 2025, before 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantively assessed according 1. TDCC domain involvement in preparing the application (33%) a. Extent to which a transparent, community-driven process was followed to arrive at the project(s) proposed in the application in consultation with the TDCC domain, and to build support for them. Aspects assessed: Involvement of TDCC domain parties relevant to the project, such as those mentioned in the roadmaps. Public widely advertised meeting(s) where sufficient relevant parties are represented. 2. Relevance (33%) a. Extent to which the application aligns with the ambitions and topics of the TDCC roadmap. b. Impact of the project. Aspects assessed: Impact on digitalisation in general. Impact on the ambitions of the TDCC in particular. Application of FAIR principles, if relevant to the project. c. Mate waarin het TDCC domein wordt bediend. d. Urgentie en tijdgebondenheid van het project. Urgentie en tijdgebondenheid van het project. e. Aansluiting bij Kaderregeling inzake staatssteun voor Onderzoek, Ontwikkeling en Innovatie (O&O&I): Mate waarin de aanvraag bijdraagt aan de brede verspreiding van onderzoeksresultaten op een niet-exclusieve en niet-discriminerende basis, via bijvoorbeeld onderwijs, open access-databases, open access-publicaties of opensource-software; of Mate waarin de aanvraag aansluit bij activiteiten inzake kennisoverdracht, wanneer deze word uitgevoerd door de onderzoeksorganistie of onderzoeksinfrastructuur, enz. 3. Project design (33%) a. The application convincingly demonstrates that it builds on existing knowledge and projects. b. The proposed project design brings together initiatives in the same field, acts as a link and prevents fragmentation. c. The proposed project design maximises the chances of achieving the goal. d. The budget is realistic and supports the project design. e. If an application consists of multiple sub-projects, the coherence should be logical. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: submission of the application; processing the application; pre-advising the assessment committee; rebuttal; meeting of the assessment committee; decision-making. For this call for proposals, an external, independent (inter)national assessment committee will be established, consisting of representatives from the scientific community and practitioners with knowledge of the field. The task of the assessment committee is to assess the submitted applications and related documents in relation to each other and on their own merits based on the given selection criteria in this Call for proposals. Because of the expertise present in the assessment committee and because this Call for proposals is non-competitive and community-driven, NWO has decided not to use referees when evaluating applications. See Call for proposals for further explanation of the procedure.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Before you start preparing and submitting your application in the NWO online application system ISAAC, you should first contact the relevant TDCC (LSH, NES and SSH) about your project idea by sending an email via: https://tdcc.nl/contact/ To prepare your application, go through the following steps: download the application form from the online application system ISAAC or from the NWO website (on the website of the relevant funding instrument); fill in the application form; Save the form as a pdf and submit it with any mandatory attachment(s) in ISAAC; complete the data requested online in ISAAC. Mandatory attachment(s): budget; statement of support signed by mandated TDCC representative(s); letter of guarantee own contribution. The applicant consortium should provide its own cash or in-kind contribution of at least 25% of the total budget for the project application. One letter of guarantee per applicant institution should be attached to the application. Each application must be accompanied by a letter of support signed by mandated representative(s) of the submitting TDCC domain. NWO will review your application to check all the conditions of this Call for proposals. You are requested to be available after submitting an application to make any administrative corrections to meet the conditions for submission. See Call for proposals for further details.

title: KIC Consumer behaviour for a circular system transition
programme: Consumer behaviour for a circular system transition Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Consumer behaviour for a circular system transition Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Based on state-of-the-art research on system transitions, behavioural change and the circular economy, the aim of this Call for proposals is to gain insight into effective interventions, measures and strategies that promote circular consumer behaviour. This Call for proposals focuses on research that analyses, designs and applies interventions. The interaction between system transitions and behavioural change is central to this. T he research programme focuses on the impactful product groups from the Transition Agenda Consumer Goods (TAC): furniture, household appliances, clothing & textiles and disposables & packaging. Interventions around these product groups make the most impact in terms of circularity.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications are submitted by a main applicant and one or more co-applicants. An application is prepared by a consortium, which includes other participants in addition to the applicants. Four categories of participants in a consortium are distinguished: Main applicant Co-applicant(s) Co-funder(s) Cooperation partner(s) In this Call for proposals, two types of co-applicants are possible: 1) co-applicants affiliated to research organisations and 2) co-applicants affiliated to civil society organisations. Co-applicants linked to civil society organisations can only act as co-applicants under application of the de minimis regulation.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: For this Call for proposals, NWO is organising two collaborative workshops on 11 and 30 June 2025 at Cultuuren Congrescentrum Antropia (next to Driebergen-Zeist station). The two collaborative workshops build on each other in terms of content. Participation in both workshops is mandatory for main applicants. If necessary, it is possible to designate a substitute for this. More information can be found in the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The budget for this programme totals €7,000,000. Applications can request a minimum of €1,750,000 and a maximum of €2,250,000 in NWO budget. The maximum duration of awarded projects is 6 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The mandatory collaborative workshops take place on June 11 and 30, 2025 The deadline for proposals is November 11th, 2025, before 14:00:00 CET

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals will be assessed by the following four criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: collaborative workshops; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; pre-advice assessment committee; rebuttal; interview selection (if necessary); interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The following conditions for submission will be reviewed by NWO: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1; the main applicant must have attended both preparatory workshop days (Section 3.3.2) or, if this was not possible, they must have arranged for a colleague to attend on their behalf (Section 2.3, 3.3.1 and 4.2.1); the applying consortium contains two or more co-funders; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the proposed project has a duration of at most 6 years; the application budget of the proposal is drawn up in accordance with the conditions for this Call for proposals; the required co-funding has been, after any request for addition or change, correctly and fully committed through declarations co-funding (see Section 3.1.2 and 3.5.6); all of the required annexes are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, complete and filled out according to the instructions.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: For this Call for proposals, NWO is organising two collaborative workshops on 11 and 30 June 2025 at Cultuuren Congrescentrum Antropia (next to Driebergen-Zeist station). The workshops will bring together interested parties around the theme of the Call. Besides researchers at universities and colleges, representatives of social parties (public and private) are also expressly invited to these collaborative workshops. The expertise of the participants and their contributions to the desired impact on the Call theme form the basis for the conversation. The workshops provide an opportunity to combine ideas and form broad innovative consortia that generate scientific and societal impact by connecting research and practice. The two collaborative workshops build on each other in terms of content. Participation in both workshops is mandatory for main applicants. If necessary, it is possible to designate a substitute for this. More information on this can be found in the Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: With the proposal form, compulsory annexes are: declarations co-funding from co-funders (see paragraph 3.1.2 and 3.5.6 of the Call for proposals). A declaration needs to be filled in and signed by every co-funder (both in-cash and in-kind); budget form; form ‘Statements and signature’. The following annexes are compulsory if they are applicable: letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (see paragraph 3.1 of the Call for proposals); declaration de-minimis support.

title: KIC Reducing health inequalities – Technology for people in a lower socioeconomic position
programme: Reducing health inequalities Knowledge and Innovation Covenant
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: Reducing health inequalities Knowledge and Innovation Covenant

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Society is marked by large health inequalities among different groups of people, where lower socioeconomic position (SEP) limits the likelihood of good health. Health technology can help reduce these health inequalities Current technology products and services do not adequately address needs, desires and behaviors of people in lower SEP. They are often not integrated into everyday life or into the existing healthcare infrastructure. The (further) development of technology takes a long time and financing for companies is difficult. This Call for proposals therefore focuses on acquiring and putting into practice an understanding of associated technological as well as systemic preconditions to (further) develop health technology for groups in a lower SEP. It is crucial that all parties work together to develop and scale up health technology solutions in a sustainable way with a clear future perspective.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Full proposals are submitted by the main applicant and two or more co-applicants. The main applicant has participated in both collaborative workshops (see also sections 3.3.1 and 4.2.1) A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants other participants are involved as well. Besides the main applicant and co-applicant(s) a consortium always consists of two or more co-funders, possibly supplemented by one or more collaborating partners. For more information see sections 3.1 and 4.2.1 in the NWO Call for proposals

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: To encourage consortium formation, two consecutive collaborative workshops will be organised. These are mandatory for the main applicant to attend before submitting an application. Registration for these workshops should be done via the form on the programme page. For more information please refer to section 2.3.1 and 3.3.1 of the NWO Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of €2.800.000 and aa maximum of €3.500.000 of NWO funding can be applied for in. With this, NWO finances maximally 80%/70% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding (see Section 3.5.6). NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments, knowledge utilisation and project management. The rates and an explanation of these budget modules are given in annex 7 of the NWO call for proposals. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 6 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The online information session will be held on Friday 9 May 2025 Part 1 of the collaborative workshop will be on Friday 30 May 2025 Part 2 of the collaborative workshop will be on Friday 27 June 2025 The deadline for submission of full proposals is 2 December 2025, by 14:00:00 CET

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure After submission deadline the full proposals will be provided with feedback by (international) referees. Applicants will be given the opportunity to submit a rebuttal to this. The applications will then be assessed by the assessment committee and applicants will be given the opportunity to answer questions from the assessment committee during an interview. The assessment committee will present its advice to the NWO Executive Board for decision-making. In July 2026, the NWO Executive Board will adopt the final assessment of the applications.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: All the documents you need for your proposal can be found here. The proposal is developed after the collaborative workshops. The steps involved in writing your proposal are: download the proposal application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the funding instrument concerned); complete the application form; save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes; fill in the requested information online in ISAAC.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: To promote consortium formation collaborative workshops will be organised. The collaborative workshops aim to promote optimal networking around the topic and encourage collaboration. The expertise of the participants and their contributions to the call topic form the basis for the conversation during the workshops. The workshops provide an opportunity to combine ideas and form broad innovative consortia (joining forces). It is then up to participants to collaborate in interdisciplinary, public-private and innovative consortia and jointly develop the application. More information about the workshops will also be announced on the program page on NWO's website. The main applicant must have participated in both workshops. The collaborative workshops are explicitly open to companies and civil society parties as well. In this way, they will also have the opportunity to join consortia in the making that develop during the workshops.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In addition to filling in the application form when submitting the complete application, we also ask you to include a signed statement from the co-financiers as well as a complete budget form. If applicable, a guarantee for continuity in the project management (see section 3.1 in the call for proposals) must also be sent. At the time of submission, the enclosed letters of support must have pledged the full required co-financing in accordance with the conditions described in sections 3.1.2 and 3.5.6 in the call for proposals. Other types of annexes shall are not allowed at the stage of submission of complete applications. Attachments must be uploaded separately from the application in ISAAC. All attachments, with the exception of the budget form, must be uploaded as a pdf file. The budget form can be uploaded as an Excel file in ISAAC.

title: Rubicon 2025-3
target_groups: International Collaboration Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Starting Researchers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: International research experience is often essential for building up one’s scientific career. Rubicon offers talented researchers who have completed their doctorates in the past year the chance to gain experience at a research institution outside the Kingdom of the Netherlands, as international research experience is likely to be an advantage at a later stage in the applicant’s academic career. Rubicon is open to all scientific disciplines for a research project at a foreign research institute. In some cases, there is an opportunity to apply for a research period at a research institute in the Kingdom of the Netherlands.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: International research experience is often essential for building up one’s scientific career. Rubicon offers talented researchers who have completed their doctorates in the past year the chance to gain experience at a research institution outside the Kingdom of the Netherlands, as international research experience is likely to be an advantage at a later stage in the applicant’s academic career. Rubicon is open to all scientific disciplines for a research project at a foreign research institute. In some cases, there is an opportunity to apply for a research period at a research institute in the Kingdom of the Netherlands.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A budget of 3,145,000 euro is available for this submission round. The available budget will be divided in proportion to the number of eligible applications between the units Science, SSH, AES and ZonMw. Applicants can apply for a period of up to 24 months at an excellent research institute outside the Netherlands. The minimum duration is 12 months. The eligible costs are your salary including fringe benefits, travel expenses and a limited amount of research costs. Any overhead costs such as housing, computers, infrastructure etc. are not eligible for funding. You can find the standard amounts in chapter 7 of the Call for proposals. It is not possible to combine a stay at several institutes. Fieldwork is allowed, depending on the nature of the research proposed.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for this round is Tuesday 2 December 2025 at 14:00:00 hours CET (2 PM sharp) .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The quality of the applicant The quality of the research proposal The quality of the host institute in the context of the match between the applicant/proposal and the host institute Scientific and/or societal impact Procedure Each application will be assessed within the realm of the relevant discipline(s) by a multidisciplinary committee based on the criteria mentioned above. NWO announces which applications have been granted about four months after the deadline of this round.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: When writing your proposal, please bear in mind that each application will be assessed with the realm of the relevant discipline(s) by a multidisciplinary committee. Please use the application form that you can download from this page. Only the required annexes will be accepted.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Preparing an application: Download the application form and fill it in; Save the application form as a pdf file and upload it in ISAAC (Science, SSH, AES) or MijnZonMw (ZonMw). The application form consists of: A research proposal written in English (max. 6 pages A4, 2,000 words). The word count includes footnotes, texts illustrations and tables, captions to illustrations and tables. References are excluded; A narrative CV of the applicant consisting of a narrative academic profile (400-700 words) and the key output (max. 10 items); One acceptance form of the intended host institute; Applications from applicants who have not yet been awarded a PhD must also be accompanied by a form approving their thesis. All forms will be made available on the websites of NWO and ZonMw at least two months before the deadline of this call.

title: Computing Time on National Computing Facilities - round 2025
programme: Computing Time on National Computer Facilities

characteristics.Research programme: Computing Time on National Computer Facilities

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Advanced computer systems are used for technical-scientific research where major computational and/or data-intensive problems need to be solved. In the Netherlands, various computer systems are available for national use and managed by SURF: the Snellius supercomputer, Data Processing infrastructure (Grid, Spider), Cloud Research Consultancy en HPC Cloud (via SURF Research Cloud). Through the call for proposals Computing Time on National Computer Facilities, researchers can apply for access to computing time and associated data services on these national computer facilities. Capacity can also be requested on the Dutch part of the pre-exascale supercomputer LUMI in consultation with SURF should the Snellius system no longer be sufficient.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following research organisations. If the main applicant does not have a permanent employment contract, his/her appointment must be at least as long as the duration of the project for which an application is submitted: Universities and Universities of applied sciences, as referred to in section 1.8 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act (WHW) and the universities listed in the Policy Rule Universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; University medical centres, by which is meant academic hospitals as referred to in section 1.13 paragraph 1 of the Higher Education and Scientific Research Act (WHW); KNAW and NWO institutes; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; NCB Naturalis; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center; National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM); Royal Netherlands Meteorological Institute (KNMI); Wageningen University (WUR)/Dienst Landbouwkundig Onderzoek (DLO); Netherlands Aerospace Centre (NLR); Deltares; Maritime Research Institute Netherlands (Marin); Those with zero-hours contracts are excluded from submitting proposals. Additional requirements: Applicants affiliated to other research organisations as referred to in Article 1.1(4) of the NWO Grant Rules, the organisation must meet cumulative conditions, see Call for proposals section 3.1 for more information.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Within this call, these are applications for large amounts of computing time: More than 1,000,000 SBU on supercomputer Snellius (CPU and/or GPU). More than 500,000 CPU core hours and/or 14,000 GPU hours and/or 100,000 TB-hours of storage on the pre-exascale supercomputer LUMI. More than 1,000,000 CPU core hours or 10,000 GPU hours of Data Processing (Grid and/or Spider) and/or more than 200 TB of online data storage and/or more than 300 TB of Grid storage - tape (offline tape storage). More than 50,000 CPU core hours or 5,000 GPU hours on HPC Cloud (via SURF Research Cloud). More than 100,000 CPU core hours on Cloud Research Consultancy. The maximum duration of large computing time projects per application is two years. The maximum duration on the pre-exascale supercomputer LUMI is one year. Small amounts of computing time applications can be submitted directly to SURF via the portal https://www.surf.nl/en/small-compute-applications-nwo . The maximum duration of small computing projects per application is one year. More information on how to apply can be found in the call for proposals under section 3.2.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting proposals is 30 December 2025, before 14:00:00 hours CET . You can submit your proposal at any time. There are no intermediate deadlines.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications will be assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Project organization. Scientific aspects of the project. Need to access computer facilities and technical expertise. More information about the criteria can be found in the call for proposals. Procedure Applications are assessed in order of receipt based on a the application, referee reports and rebuttal. This will lead to an advice concerning the allocation or rejection by the permanent assessment Committee for the Scientific Use of Supercomputers (WGS) to the Board of NWO Domain Science. The WGS meets six times a year to consider the then completed application files. More information about the procedure can be found in the call for proposals. For more information about the WGS-committe the Computing Time programme page

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: For more information on the available computer systems, you are directed to SURF (scroll down to ‘Compute’). For small amounts of computing time, applications for access to the above mentioned computer systems can be made directly to SURF . Have a look at the page researchprogrammes on computing time national computer facilities , to see the allocated projects so far.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: More information on the conditions for applicants can be found in paragraph 3 of the call for proposals

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To prepare your application, please go through the following steps: download the application form that can be obtained from the online application system ISAAC or from the NWO website (on the website for this programme); you are expressly requested to use the most recent 2025 application form; application forms from previous years are outdated and will not be considered. The application form for 2025 can be found on the funding page for this programme on the NWO website. first consult one of SURF's advisers on the technical aspects of the application at servicedesk@surf.nl . complete the application form. select and complete the corresponding technical annexes of the application form; remove all irrelevant technical annexes of computer facilities that will not be used. save the form as a pdf file and submit it with any required annexe(s) in ISAAC. complete the information requested online in ISAAC. Mandatory attachment: If the application is a follow-up application, also submit a report from the previous project. Reports from previous projects are taken to the evaluation committee as additional information in the evaluation of the follow -up application. The report form can be found on the funding page for this program on the NWO website. The attachment should be prepared in accordance with the template provided by NWO. The attachment should be uploaded separately from the application in ISAAC. All attachments should be submitted as a pdf file (without security). Attachments other than those listed above are not permitted. It is mandatory to write your application in English. More information on the conditions for applicants can be found in section 3.3 of the call for proposals.

title: NWA Balancing agriculture and biodiversity in the Brazilian Cerrado: an integrated socio-ecological approach
programme: Agriculture and biodiversity in balance in the Brazilian Cerrado Dutch Research Agenda (NWA) Thematic Programming
target_groups: International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners
characteristics.Research programme: Agriculture and biodiversity in balance in the Brazilian Cerrado Dutch Research Agenda (NWA) Thematic Programming
characteristics.Themes: Biodiversity Climate Ruimtelijke en sociale transformaties Food Water

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Increasing deforestation rates driven by agricultural expansion and land use change are threatening the rich biodiversity of the Brazilian Cerrado, one of the world’s biodiversity hotspots. The Netherlands is a large importer of agricultural commodities from the Cerrado, while Brazilian climate and environmental policies and regulations are increasingly aimed at safeguarding the region’s rich biome. The aim of this Call for proposals is to develop strategies for a balanced approach towards conserving the biodiversity of the Cerrado, mitigating climate change and ensuring social justice, while at the same time driving food and nutrition security and economic development by safeguarding agricultural productivity. The Matopiba region in the northern part of the Cerrado, and its border areas with the Amazon, are the principal geographical focus of this Call for proposals. In this Call, NWO and CNPq invite knowledge institutions and societal partners to form consortia that jointly develop scientific and practice-oriented research proposals. The consortia are required to work in an inter and transdisciplinary manner and should have a knowledge-chain-wide composition. Knowledge-chain-wide implies that proposals connect fundamental, applied and practice-oriented research and that connection is sought with the knowledge needs of societal parties. The programme focuses on four themes, two of which should be addressed in the application: land-use design scenarios which support biodiversity and ecosystem services; social, economic and environmental impacts of land-use redesign strategies; developing agricultural systems more resilient to climate change while protecting biodiversity; co-creating governance approaches to support more sustainable land-use practices.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: A proposal must be submitted by a research consortium with participants from the Netherlands and Brazil who jointly develop the proposal. On both the Dutch and Brazilian sides, the consortium must consist of a main applicant and a co-applicant. On the Dutch side, the co-applicant must be affiliated with a different organisation than that of the main applicant. Furthermore, the consortium must include a cooperation partner from a practitioner organisation, such as a business, (local) government, or non-governmental organisation, on either the Brazilian or Dutch side. For Dutch applicants, full, associate and assistant professors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application (i.e. participate in a consortium and request NWO funding) if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the institutions listed in the Funding Organisation rules (NWO part) of the call for proposals. Persons with a zero-hour employment agreement or with a contract for a limited period of time (other than a tenure track appointment) may not submit a proposal. Researchers based in Brazil may be main applicants if they have their curriculum registered on the Lattes Platform, updated until the deadline for proposal submission and hold a doctoral degree. Applicants also need an employment contract (CLT) or statutory bond with the project executing institution or, if retired, prove that they maintain academic-scientific activity and present a declaration from the project executing institution agreeing with its execution. Each main applicant and consortium can only submit one proposal. A main applicant cannot participate as a co-applicant in other proposals. A co-applicant can participate in a maximum of two proposals. More information on the transnational eligibility criteria, can be found in the full Call for Proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: NWO organises an online information and matchmaking event. The aim of this event is to provide information about the Call and application process, and bring together potential applicants, researchers, cooperation partners and stakeholders from various relevant fields and sectors. In addition, ideas for (or needs for) research related to the Call theme and the described impact can be exchanged. In this way, additional expertise needed for the consortium can be identified. During the information and matchmaking event, participants are offered the opportunity to pitch their ideas and to network with other participants, in order to achieve the necessary breadth and higher quality of consortia and project proposals. It is up to the participants themselves to shape the follow-up to the results of the matchmaking. The matchmaking session will take place once. Further information about the information and matchmaking meeting will be published on the programme page of this Call for proposals. Participation in the matchmaking is not mandatory.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of at most € 715,000 can be applied for from NWO and a maximum of R$ 1,000,000 can be applied for from CNPq. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 4 years (i.e. 48 months). Reimbursable costs from NWO may include costs for personnel, equipment, investments, knowledge utilisation and project management. More detailed budget modules can be found in section 3.2.1 and Annex 7.1 of the Call for proposals. 70% of the CNPq budget of each project is allocated to the following scholarships modalities: Technological Development and Innovation in Brazil (DTI) and Abroad (DEJ and DES) from 1 to 12 months of duration each. The DEJ and DES are exclusively intended for Brazilian consortium members traveling to the Netherlands. A maximum of 30% of the CNPq budget can be allocated to Other Costs (please refer to section 3.2.1 of the Call for proposals for more information).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting full proposals is Tuesday 13 January 2026, before 14:00:00 CET at NWO. Your proposal needs to be submitted to NWO only. The deadline for submitting the partnership request form ( not mandatory ) is Thursday 20 November 2025, before 14:00:00 CET at NWO . The partnership request form ( not mandatory ) must be filled in and submitted via the form on the matchmaking page on the NWO website. The partnership request form is used for matchmaking purposes and its submission is optional. The deadline for applicants whose (foreign) affiliated organisations need to be assessed by NWO (Section 7.1.1) is Thursday 11 December 2025 before 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following substantive assessment criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure Applications should be submitted to NWO (via ISAAC ) only and the procedure is carried out through the NWO application system ISAAC. The NWO main applicant and the CNPq main applicant are both responsible for sharing the reviewer reports, information regarding the rebuttal and any further information with the rest of the consortium. The application procedure consists of the following steps: Submission of the proposal; Admissibility of the proposal; Peer review by referees; Rebuttal; Pre-advice of the International Assessment Committee (IAC); Meeting of the International Assessment Committee (IAC); Decision-making by the NWO Executive Board and the Executive Board of CNPq. For this Call for proposals, an external IAC will be jointly appointed by NWO and CNPq. The assessment committee will have a broad composition. This means that not only scientific expertise will be represented in the assessment committee, but also expertise from the entire knowledge chain, including societal stakeholders who are highly familiar with the subject and representatives from the target group. The task of the assessment committee is to assess the applications and the relevant documents that are submitted, in conjunction with each other and with regard to both the respective merits of each application and the assessment criteria outlined in this Call for proposals. Finally, the NWO Executive Board and the Executive Board of CNPq will assess the procedure followed as well as the advice from the assessment committee. They will subsequently determine the final qualifications and make a decision over awarding or rejecting the proposals. The decision is final when both organisations have come to the same decision. The Brazilian main applicants (project leaders) of the awarded projects will be contacted by CNPq in order to take the procedures needed for contracting the project. That will include the submission of the proposal to Plataforma Carlos Chagas, informing in the electronic form the institutions and members involved, as well as the budget to be supported to CNPq.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: In case of questions regarding what can be applied for from CNPq, please contact CNPq at conae@cnpq.br . In case of other questions, please contact the NWO policy officer, or send an e-mail to nwa-cerrado@nwo.nl .

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applications must be submitted through the Isaac application and reporting system. If you do not have an Isaac account yet, then please create one at least one workday in advance. More information about writing a proposal and which forms you need to use can be found in Isaac. Please download and use the forms in Isaac. Below you will find examples of the required forms

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The application form consists of nine sections and two annexes. Each section contains further clarification and notes on how to fill out the related questions. When writing your proposal, please take into account that it will be assessed by both expert reviewers as well as the International Assessment Committee with a broad range of expertise. Please only include the annexes specified in the Call for proposals and application form. Additional annexes are not allowed, and will need to be removed from the application.

title: Doctoral Grant for Teachers, 1st round 2026
target_groups: Teachers

characteristics.For specific groups: Teachers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Teachers from primary, secondary, further vocational, higher vocational and special education can receive a grant for a PhD study. The aim is to directly apply the knowledge and research experience acquired for the benefit of educational practice. A higher number of practising teachers with a PhD also increases the quality of education and strengthens the connections between universities and schools. You can apply for a PhD grant for any research subject. However, you must be able to indicate the importance.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: You must hold a tenured position and you must be employed at an educational institute funded by the Dutch government.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: With the PhD grant a replacement teacher can be appointed for a period of 5 years for half of your contract hours, up to a maximum of 0.4 fte.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for the submission of proposals is Tuesday 20 January 2026, 14:00 CET . There are two submission rounds annually for the Doctoral Grant for Teachers, with submission deadlines in January and June.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Further information about the criteria can be found in the call for proposals. The quality of the candidate and the research proposal are assessed according to three criteria: Quality of the research proposal Impact Quality of the candidate Procedure A science-wide committee will assess the proposals and will compile a ranking. The most promising candidates will be invited for an interview. The candidate will present the research idea to the selection committee. The Domain Board Social Sciences and Humanities of NWO will take the final granting decision.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: More information about the Doctoral Grant for Teachers can be found on the programme-website .

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Applicants are entirely free to choose the topic of their research; any research topic is eligible for consideration. The application will be assessed based on the criteria outlined in section 4.3 of the Call for Proposals. Applications may be written in either Dutch or English. In addition to the research proposal, the application form includes the applicant’s CV and a recommendation from the supervisor.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The application consists of: The application form (including a recommendation from the supervisor); a compulsory annex to the application: the employer’s declaration.

title: Senior Expert Programme (SEP) II - round 2
programme: NL-CGIAR Senior Expert Programme II The Netherlands (NL) - CGIAR research programme Senior Expert Programme (SEP) | NL-CGIAR
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Applied Sciences Research

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Applied Sciences Research
characteristics.Research programme: NL-CGIAR Senior Expert Programme II The Netherlands (NL) - CGIAR research programme Senior Expert Programme (SEP) | NL-CGIAR

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The overall goal of the Senior Expert Programme (SEP) is advancing knowledge and enhancing innovation (knowledge use) for global food and nutrition security (SDG2) through cooperation between the Netherlands and CGIAR. The objectives are: Leveraging Dutch knowledge and expertise for performance and impact of selected CGIAR (Science) Programs and Accelerators, Leveraging CGIAR expertise, science and networks for Dutch knowledge institutions, Partnering in research and innovation, cooperating in knowledge sharing and capacity strengthening in areas of mutual interest in CGIAR and Dutch policy on global food and nutrition security.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Senior Experts from Dutch knowledge institutes must comply with the following definition of Senior Expert: Senior Experts have at least a MSc/MA, Senior experts with a PhD should have at least ten years of demonstrable relevant working experience after the PhD. Reference date is 20 January 2026 so the PhD degree must be obtained no later than 20 January 2016, Senior experts with a MSc/MA should have at least fifteen years of demonstrable relevant working experience after the MSc/MA. Reference date is 20 January 2026 so the MSc/MA degree must be obtained no later than 20 January 2011.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The Senior Expert Programme (SEP) II Round 2 provides the possibility to fund part of the salary costs of Senior Experts from Dutch research organisations in order to spend (additional) time on the selected CGIAR (Science) Program and/or Accelerator. This means that the Dutch research organisation is compensated for the time that the Senior Expert will engage with CGIAR; and the CGIAR organisation will host a (part-time) Senior Expert without (extra) personnel costs. Per Senior Expert assignment, a grant amount of at most €550.000 for 3 years and for 0,5 fte (50%) can be applied for. The minimum duration of the proposed assignment is 1 year and the maximum duration of the proposed assignment is 3 years. The minimum number of fte that can be applied for is 0,2 (20%) and the maximum is 0,5 (50%). The applicant can include costs for salary, material, and knowledge utilisation.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting full proposals is 20 January 2026, before 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for SEP candidates will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Appropriateness of the Senior Expert to the SEP II, Quality of the proposed assignment, Added value of the proposed assignment. The criteria carry equal weight and each count for one-third of the final assessment. More information on the assessment criteria can be found in the Call for Proposals section 4.3. Procedure The procedure consists of the following steps: submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; Pre-assessment by the committee; Rebuttal; Assessment committee meeting; Decision by the NWO Executive Board. An external, independent assessment committee will be assigned for this Call for SEP candidates, consisting of representatives from science and practice with knowledge of the field. Detailed information about the procedure can be found in the Call for Proposals, Section 4.2.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: On the website site CGIAR Research Portfolio 2025–2030 - CGIAR , you can find more information about the following CGIAR (Science) Programmes and Accelerators: Climate Action; Food Frontiers and Security; Policy Innovation; Scaling for Impact; Capacity Sharing; Digital Transformation; Gender Equality and Inclusion.

write_proposal.title: Write proposals
write_proposal.text: The application complies with the formal submission conditions of this Call for SEP candidates and responds to the aims of the programme. Applicants have to make use of the instructions and explanatory notes in the application form.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The steps involved in writing your application are: download the application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the funding instrument concerned); complete the application form; save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes; fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. Compulsory annexes (please use the format provided on the grant page of the funding instrument on the NWO website): budget (needs to be submitted as an excel file in ISAAC); narrative CV of the Senior Expert; letter of support of the Dutch research organisation; letter of support of the hosting CGIAR Research Center. The host CGIAR Research Center depends on the assignment and is identified with the Science Director of the selected (Science) Program and/or Accelerator. letter of support from the Director of the selected CGIAR (Science) Program(s) and/or Accelerator(s).

title: NGF GroenvermogenNL: Hydrogen and green electrons for nitrogen-based chemistry
programme: GroenvermogenNL Hydrogen and green electrons for nitrogen-based chemistry
target_groups: Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry Knowledge Utilisation
characteristics.Research programme: GroenvermogenNL Hydrogen and green electrons for nitrogen-based chemistry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this Call for Proposals is to implement GroenvermogenNL Work Package 5: Hydrogen and green electrons for nitrogen-based chemistry. This will make an important contribution to making the Dutch chemical industry more sustainable. A consortium will be established through workshops that will focus on innovation in making nitrogen-based chemistry more sustainable. This innovation is aimed at developing and demonstrating affordable technology for producing nitrogen-containing chemicals. These production routes use renewable hydrogen and/or green electrons. Research areas are: Thermocatalytic synthesis of ammonia(c) and nitrogen-containing chemicals. Direct electrochemical, plasma-chemical and photochemical conversion to produce useful nitrogen-containing chemicals.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The proposal can be submitted by a consortium existing of: Researchers from universities, universities of applied and TO2 institutions. Employees of businesses and other public and private organisations provided that: They have a tenured position for at least 0.6 fte; Their organisation has demonstrable Research & Development (R&D) activities in the Netherlands; They hold a master’s degree.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: Consortium building takes places during two multiple-day workshops. The workshops are mandatory for the main applicant of the consortium. Co-applicants, cofinanciers and collaborating partners preferably also take part in the workshops. Signing up for the workshop can be done via ISAAC.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum total of €20,317,355 can be requested from NWO for the grant application in this Call for Proposals. NWO will fund a maximum of 85% of the project, and a minimum of 51%. You will therefore need to arrange other funding for this call, such as cash or in-kind co-funding from private parties. See chapter 3.2 of the call for proposals for more conditions. Projects may last for a maximum of six years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Workshop registration deadline : Thursday 3 July 2025, 14:00 CEST. Deadline for full proposal : Thursday 22 January 2026, 14:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The proposal will be assessed on the basis of two criteria, both of which are weighted equally: Quality of the consortium, Quality of the research plan. Procedure The procedure fort his Call for proposals consists of 3 steps: Registration of organisations for the workshops (deadline 3 Juli 2025, 14:00 CEST), Workshops (22 – 24 September/27 – 29 oktober 2025), Developing grant proposal (deadline 22 januari 2026, 14:00 CET). After submission, the proposal will be assessed by the selection committee, which will advise the NWO Domain Science Board about whether or not to award funding. The decision of the Domain Science board is expected in the second quarter of 2026.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Groenvermogen.nl

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Organisations that want to participate in the grant proposal within this Call for proposals are required to submit an Application form Workshops to participate in the procedure. For each organisation, a maximum of one Application form Workshops may be submitted. This means a maximum of one Application form Workshops for the entire organisation at the highest level (e.g. at university level and not at faculty or department level). The main applicant submits the proposal via the NWO web application ISAAC. During the assessment process, NWO will communicate with the main applicant.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: In this call for proposals casco-workshops are organised to guide the process of forming a consortium and defining the main lines of the grant application. Participants do not attend the workshop in a personal capacity, but represent their organisation.

title: Transformative practices and processes for climate transitions
programme: Transformatieve werkwijzen en processen voor klimaattransities

characteristics.Research programme: Transformatieve werkwijzen en processen voor klimaattransities
characteristics.Themes: Climate Resilient Society

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call for proposals supports projects that validate, further develop or make methods and processes more widely accessible in order to accelerate systemic and just climate transitions. The focus is on practices that promote transdisciplinary collaboration between science and practice, support transformative change, integrate sustainability principles and pursue climate justice. It is important that the practice forms the starting point and that methods are scalable and transferable. Initiatives that address less -explored aspects of transitions – such as transition pain, grief, chaos or imagination – are also very welcome. The call is part of KIN's Science in Transition programme and offers space, time and resources to strengthen and embed experimental forms of collaboration in practice. KIN aims to stimulate a new scientific practice in which knowledge production contributes to just system change and societal impact. Projects contribute to strengthening a transition mindset and ensure that valuable methods become more widely applicable for a climate-neutral and resilient society.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Who is this call for proposals for? Societal organisations, researchers and the creative sector that (wish to) use facilitating working methods, processes and in-between spaces that contribute to system change and a transition mindset. Pioneers who are already working on (sustainable) alternatives of collaborations for transformative societal impact. Anyone who wants to connect science and practice for just climate transitions. Who can apply? Employees of knowledge institutions and societal organisations in the Kingdom of the Netherlands who want to contribute to transdisciplinary collaboration between practice and science. More details can be found in chapter 3.1 of the call for proposals for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Who is this call for proposals for? Societal organisations, researchers and the creative sector that (wish to) use facilitating working methods, processes and in-between spaces that contribute to system change and a transition mindset. Pioneers who are already working on (sustainable) alternatives of collaborations for transformative societal impact. Anyone who wants to connect science and practice for just climate transitions. Who can apply? Employees of knowledge institutions and societal organisations in the Kingdom of the Netherlands who want to contribute to transdisciplinary collaboration between practice and science. More details can be found in chapter 3.1 of the call for proposals for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Proposals can be submitted until 27 January 2026 14:00:00 CET . Please note! Start submitting your proposal in time in our submission system Isaac and ensure that project partners who are not in the Isaac database register at least 15 working days before the deadline via relatiebeheer@nwo.nl . For the assessment of additional knowledge institutions, the assessment documents should be sent by e-mail to programmas@hetkin.nl no later than 15 working days before the submission deadline (i.e. no later than 6 January 2026 before 14:00:00 CEST ). More information on preparation, mandatory annexes and submission requirements can be found in sections 3.3 and 3.4 of the call for proposals.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals are assessed on two aspects: Relation to the call and quality easibility of the activity plan Please note, for this call for proposals, the main applicants are also the assessors. For the full assessment aspects, please read chapter 4.2 of the call for proposals. Procedure This call for proposals for proposals encourages the acceleration of climate transitions through open knowledge sharing and peer review. Main applicants assess each other's proposals in two groups, with consideration for conflict of interests and diversity. The assessment is based solely on the proposal, which must be widely accessible and clearly written. The chosen methodology utilises the expertise of applicants and promotes diverse feedback. Each assessor ranks the proposals they have received for assessment based on the assessment aspects (see section 4.2). The KIN bureau draws up a final ranking based on the priorities set by each group. The steering group reviews the procedure and decides on the allocation of grants. There is no possibility of appeal. If the number of proposals is too low or if conflicts of interest impede peer review, the assessment will be carried out by an independent committee.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: On 25 November and 9 December, KIN organised online information sessions about this call and the call 'Fund for more sustainable research', which also falls under the 'Science in Transition' programme line. Would you like to view the presentation from the information session? Or do you have another question about this call for proposals? Please contact programmas@hetkin.nl .

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The grant “Transformative practices and processes for climate transitions” focuses on validating, further developing and expanding the accessibility of experimental collaboration methods, facilitating practices, processes, in-between spaces and tools that: promote transdisciplinary collaboration between practice and science, contribute to system change, and strengthen the transition mindset among researchers and practitioners by empowering them and providing them with tools. Clearly describe what you will do, why, and how the results will be shared. Demonstrate transformative ambition: KIN is looking for methods and processes that truly change things, not just improve them. Co-creation & knowledge development: Show how different types of knowledge come together in the methods and processes. Be creative and concrete: Design-oriented, activist or creative practices with clear impact are welcome. Write accessibly: The main applicants are also the assessors and come from academia and practice with different areas of expertise.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, next to filling in the application form, we also ask you to enclose: Mandatory attachment(s): budget. Optional attachment(s): If applicable: de minimis declarations for societal organisations or additional knowledge institutions without assessment. If applicable: declaration of additional knowledge institution without assessment.

information_about_application_on_externa.title: Information about application on external website
information_about_application_on_externa.text: You can find more information about the programme ‘Science in transition’ here .
