title: NWO Talent Programme | Veni - Applied and Engineering Sciences (AES) 2025

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the NWO Talent Programme is to provide creative space for adventurous, talented, pioneering researchers to do research of their choice, develop their own line of research and further develop their talent.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications can be submitted by candidates who have obtained their PhD, irrespective of whether they hold tenured positions and of whether they come from the Netherlands or abroad. Candidates may only submit an application if they have obtained their PhD in the three years preceding the reference date of this round, 1 January 2025, or between 1 January 2025 and 2 September 2025. An extension to the above career-dependent submission period may be possible under specific circumstances (see Section 3.4.2 of the Call for proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a maximum of € 320,000 can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed project is three years. The duration of the project can be lengthened proportionally in case of part-time research by a maximum of one year (from three to four years). If the proposed research is of shorter duration, the maximum amount will be reduced accordingly. It is also possible to carry out the research part-time without extending the duration of the project. Part-time appointments of less than 0.4 FTE are not permitted.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting pre‐proposals is 2 September, 2025 , before 14:00:00 hours CEST. The deadline for submitting full proposals is 27 January, 2026 , before 14:00:00 hours CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The pre-proposal submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criterion: Quality of the researcher The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%) Scientific and/or societal impact (25%) Procedure submission of the pre-proposal; admissibility check of the pre-proposal; assessment of the pre-proposal; provisional advice assessment committee; possibility to submit a request for a correction of a factual inaccuracy in the negative provisional advice; decision-making pre-proposal; submission of the full proposal (only possible if you have received a positive decision on the pre-proposal); admissibility check of the full proposal; assessment of the full proposal; peer review by referees (for domains Science and AES only); review by committee (for domain ZonMw); rebuttal (for Science, AES and ZonMW only); provisional advice assessment committee (all domains); interview selection (ZonMw only); interview and meeting of the assessment committee (all domains); decision-making More explanation on the procedure and the timeline per domain can be found in the Call for proposals. The assessment of the Veni 2025 pre-proposals and applications of the domain AES take place on a domain-wide basis. A committee assesses the pre-proposals and selects candidates to submit a full proposal. The pre-application does not use external referees. In domain AES full applications and support letters are sent to referees and committee members. A separate forms for co-funding by universities and support letters for companies will become available. Interviews will take place on a domain-wide basis.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Embedding guarantee Since 2019 on NWO has asked candidates for an 'embedding guarantee' from the start of the application round for NWO Talent Programme Veni funding. Researchers can only submit applications for Veni funding with the support of the Dutch institution where they intend to conduct their research. The embedding guarantee can be found on the Veni financing webpage of each domain. Frequently asked questions On this page you will find an overview of the most frequently asked questions about the NWO Talent Programme, including questions about the set time limit to submit a proposal (and any exceptions to it) and the embedding guarantee.

write_proposal.title: Write proprosal
write_proposal.text: The pre-proposal contains an Evidence Based Curriculum Vitae of the applicant, consisting of: a description of the applicant’s academic profile (400-700 words); a description of the applicant’s key output (max. 700 words); short research idea (max. 100 words). NWO has recently updated the instruction for writing an EBCV . The updated instruction can be found in the application form for the VENI. We would also like to draw your attention to the existence of an international digital platform to support narrative style CV writing ( PEP-CV ). The full proposal contains: a description of the research proposal, a motivation for the choice of institution, and a description of the fit between the research proposal and the applicant’s academic profile (maximum 2000 words including in-text references); a section on scientific and/or societal impact (maximum 750 words); a section on data management; and a budget form.

Optional attachment(s) to the full proposal: declaration co-funding; statement exceeding maximum funding; embedding guarantee, only if amendments have been made to the embedding guarantee submitted with the pre-proposal.

title: NWA Opportunities and value chains for side streams in the protein transition
programme: Opportunities and value chains for side streams in the protein transition
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Opportunities and value chains for side streams in the protein transition

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this call is to develop knowledge about the use of plant-based side streams as a protein source for food applications and the adoption of proteins from side streams in the food consumption pattern of a large group of consumers. The program provides insight into the functioning of the entire value chain based on the assumption that this knowledge is essential to achieve the necessary system change and effective valorisation of side streams .

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are to be submitted by the main applicant on behalf of the consortium. There are four categories of participants within a consortium: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Cooperation partner(s); Co-funder(s) (optional). A consortium should consist of at least a main applicant, a co-applicant and cooperation partner. The conditions for each type of participant are explained in more detail in the call text.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: This Call for Proposals concerns complex and challenging societal issues. Research into answers to these questions requires collaboration, for example between different research disciplines, applied and fundamental research, and with societal organisations or companies. NWO encourages and supports the formation of these collaborations by organising meetings or workshops for consortium formation. These meetings and workshops can take various forms, depending on the specific objective. In this Call for Proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of matchmaking. Additional information about NWO's efforts in the field of consortium formation can be found on the website: Consortium formation | NWO.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Per project, a grant amount of at most €3,875,000 can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 6 years. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. The available budget modules (including the maximum amounts) are listed in more detail in the call text. Apply only for funding that is vital to realise the project

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting research ideas is Tuesday 16 September 2025 , before 14:00:00 CEST The Matchmaking will take place on Thursday 2 October 2025 , before 14:00:00 CET. The deadline for submitting a request for review of (foreign) organisations is 13 January 2026 before 14:00:00 CET. EN tekst The deadline for submitting full proposals is Tuesday 27 January 2026 , before 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) , Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%) , Quality of the research (30%) . Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: register a research idea; matchmaking meeting; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; pre advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; Decision-making.

write_proposal.title: Voorstel schrijven
write_proposal.text: NWO will assess your application against all the conditions set out in this Call for proposals, including the listed conditions. Your application will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. After submitting your application, NWO requests you to be available to implement any possible administrative corrections so that you can (still) meet the conditions for submission.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: In the period prior following the registration of research ideas, NWO will facilitate a matchmaking meeting for this call. The consortia who submitted a research idea will be invited. Matchmaking serves to make applicants aware of all ongoing research ideas around the Call for proposals and provides an opportunity for possible cooperation, merging or connection. These meetings also serve as a good opportunity for parties not yet involved in research ideas but interested to take part in a consortium to explore opportunities to connect with existing research ideas. These parties can therefore also register for this meeting so that they can come into contact with and possibly join a consortium. The matchmaking takes place once.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Prior to submitting an application, the intended main applicant is obliged to submit a research idea. Main applicants submit a research idea by completing the form on the website for this purpose. The main applicant then submits a full application by completing the application form and submits it via ISAAC with all necessary attachments. Submit a research idea

title: NWO Talent Programme | Veni - Social Sciences and Humanities (SSH) 2025

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the NWO Talent Programme is to provide creative space for adventurous, talented, pioneering researchers to do research of their choice, develop their own line of research and further develop their talen.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications can be submitted by candidates who have obtained their PhD, irrespective of whether they hold tenured positions and of whether they come from the Netherlands or abroad. Candidates may only submit an application if they have obtained their PhD in the three years preceding the reference date of this round, 1 January 2025, or between 1 January 2025 and 2 September 2025. An extension to the above career-dependent submission period may be possible under specific circumstances (see Section 3.4.2 of the Call for proposals).

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a maximum of € 320,000 can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed project is three years. The duration of the project can be lengthened proportionally in case of part-time research by a maximum of one year (from three to four years). If the proposed research is of shorter duration, the maximum amount will be reduced accordingly. It is also possible to carry out the research part-time without extending the duration of the project. Part-time appointments of less than 0.4 FTE are not permitted.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting pre‐proposals is 2 September, 2025, before 14:00:00 hours CEST. The deadline for submitting full proposals is 27 January, 2026 , before 14:00:00 hours CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The pre-proposal submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criterion: Quality of the researcher. The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%) , Scientific and/or societal impact (25%). Procedure submission of the pre-proposal; admissibility check of the pre-proposal; assessment of the pre-proposal; provisional advice assessment committee; possibility to submit a request for a correction of a factual inaccuracy in the negative provisional advice decision-making pre-proposal; submission of the full proposal (only possible if you have received a positive decision on the pre-proposal); admissibility check of the full proposal; assessment of the full proposal; review by committee (for domain ZonMw); peer review by referees (for domains Science and AES only); rebuttal (for Science, AES and ZonMW only); provisional advice assessment committee (all domains); interview selection (ZonMw only); interview and meeting of the assessment committee (all domains); decision-making More information on the procedure and the timeline per domain can be found in the Call for proposals. The assessment of pre-proposals and full applications within SSH takes place in six panels. The allocation of proposals to a panel is determined by the applicants themselves. Please note that candidates submit their proposal to one of these panels in ISAAC. You can find the panel structure under ‘prepare’. The panels assess the applications in all phases. In the SSH domain, NWO does not ask external reviewers to assess applications. Therefore, applicants are advised to take notice of the interdisciplinary character of the evaluation committees.

Freqeuntly asked questions On this page you will find an overview of the most frequently asked questions about the NWO Talent Programme, including questions about the set time limit to submit a proposal (and any exceptions to it) and the embedding guarantee.

We would also like to draw your attention to the existence of an international digital platform to support narrative style CV writing ( PEP-CV ). The full proposal contains: a description of the research proposal, a motivation for the choice of institution, and a description of the fit between the research proposal and the applicant’s academic profile (maximum 2000 words including in-text references); a section on scientific and/or societal impact (maximum 750 words); a section on data management; and a budget plan.

when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting pre‐proposals is 2 September, 2025 , before 14:00:00 hours CEST. The deadline for submitting full proposals is 27 January, 2026 , before 14:00:00 hours CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The pre-proposal submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criterion: Quality of the researcher The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Quality and innovative character of the research proposal (75%) Scientific and/or societal impact (25%) Procedure submission of the pre-proposal; admissibility check of the pre-proposal; assessment of the pre-proposal; provisional advice assessment committee; possibility to submit a request for a correction of a factual inaccuracy in the negative provisional advice; decision-making pre-proposal; submission of the full proposal (only possible if you have received a positive decision on the pre-proposal); admissibility check of the full proposal; assessment of the full proposal; peer review by referees (for domains Science and AES only); review by committee (for domain ZonMw); rebuttal (for Science, AES and ZonMW only); provisional advice assessment committee (all domains); interview selection (ZonMw only); interview and meeting of the assessment committee (all domains); decision-making More explanation on the procedure and the timeline per domain can be found in the Call for proposals. Applications from the Earth Sciences, Astronomy, Chemistry, Computer Science, Life Sciences, Physics and Mathematics will be submitted to the Science domain. The Science domain has a single submission point and seven panels in which applications are assessed. You can indicate the panel of your choice in the application form. Within the Science domain you could choose from seven panels : Earth Sciences; Astronomy; Life Sciences; Chemistry; Physics; Mathematics; Computer Science. Keep in mind that, when your field is for examples "Physics of life", this field is part of the research areas "Physics" and "Life Sciences". It is therefore important to consider what the main focus is of your proposal. The full proposals are assessed in a domain-wide competition. Interviews will take place in three broad clusters, which have different focus areas based on the discipline codes the applicants have indicated. Candidates will therefore be interviewed and assessed by committee members from their own discipline and other disciplines in the Science domain. Grants will be awarded to the Veni candidates with the highest ranking within the Science domain, independent of the discipline.

characteristics.Themes: Recognition and rewards Climate Sustainability

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call for proposals is for projects that focus on the development and sharing of knowledge and methods that researchers need to be able to make more sustainable choices from the design of a research. Researchers are partly dependent on the sustainability in their organization. That is why this call also offers room for sustainability issues that are widely discussed among research organisations and where a joint approach can accelerate the process. In addition, it is possible to grow, scale up or validate existing knowledge and initiatives and make them widely available. NWO wants to recognise and appreciate frontrunners for their efforts and encourage everyone to start making research practices more sustainable. The project can play a connecting role between the various stakeholders needed for anchoring and upscaling – such as researchers, research organisations, infrastructure providers, policymakers and other relevant parties in the research ecosystem.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Who is this call for proposals for? Research organisations and societal organisations that want to contribute to a more sustainable research practice. Who can apply? Employees of knowledge institutions and societal organisations in the Kingdom of the Netherlands who want to contribute to making research practice more sustainable. More details can be found in chapter 3.1 of the call for proposals for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Budget: Maximum € 50,000 per project What can be applied for: Salaries of project partners Materials and resources Temporary hiring of experts Other project activities Student assistants Not possible: Recruitment of new academic staff and basic facilities. Desired duration: max. 12 months Start: within 3 months of allocation More details can be found in chapter 3.2 of the call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Proposals can be submitted until 27 January 2026 14:00:00 CET . Please note! Start submitting your proposal in time in our submission system Isaac and ensure that project partners who are not in the Isaac database register at least 15 working days before the deadline via relatiebeheer@nwo.nl . For the assessment of additional knowledge institutions, the assessment documents should be sent by e-mail to programmas@hetkin.nl no later than 15 working days before the submission deadline (i.e. no later than 6 January 2026 before 14:00:00 CET ). More information on preparation, mandatory annexes and submission requirements can be found in sections 3.3 and 3.4 of the call for proposals.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Proposals are assessed on two aspects: Relation to the call and quality Feasibility of the activity plan Please note, for this call for proposals, the main applicants are also the assessors. For the full assessment aspects, please read chapter 4.2 of the call for proposals. Procedure This call for proposals for proposals encourages the transition towards sustainable science through open knowledge sharing and peer review. Main applicants assess each other's proposals in two groups, with consideration for conflict of interests and diversity. The assessment is based solely on the proposal, which must be widely accessible and clearly written. The chosen methodology utilises the expertise of applicants and promotes diverse feedback. Each assessor ranks the proposals they have received for assessment based on the assessment aspects (see section 4.2). The KIN bureau draws up a final ranking based on the priorities set by each group. The steering group reviews the procedure and decides on the allocation of grants. There is no possibility of appeal. If the number of proposals is too low or if conflicts of interest impede peer review, the assessment will be carried out by an independent committee.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: On 25 November and 9 December, KIN organised online information sessions about this call and the call 'Transformative Practices and Processes for Climate Transitions' which also falls under the 'Science in Transition' programme line. Would you like to view the presentation from the information session? Or do you have another question about this call for proposals? Please contact programmas@hetkin.nl .

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Projects must contribute to at least one of the following objectives: Adapting existing knowledge and/or methods Validating existing knowledge and/or methods Further development of existing knowledge and methods Developing new knowledge or methods Capacity building Projects should in any case focus on the following aspects: Optimising impact Knowledge utilisation and dissemination Knowledge transfer and embedding Scalability and portability An explanation of the above can be found in the call for proposals (H2.1) Clearly describe what you will do, why, and how the results will be shared. Co-creation & knowledge development: Show how different types of knowledge come together in the methods and processes. Be creative and concrete: Design-oriented, activist or creative approaches with clear impact are welcome. Write accessibly: The main applicants are also the assessors and come from academia and practice with different areas of expertise.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, next to filling in the application form, we also ask you to enclose: Mandatory attachment(s): budget. Optional attachment(s): If applicable: de minimis declarations for societal organisations.

information_about_application_on_externa.title: Information about application on external website
information_about_application_on_externa.text: You can find more information about the programme ‘Science in transition’ here .

title: NWA Slavery pasts: (understudied forms of) impacts and perspectives
programme: Slavery pasts: (understudied forms of) impacts and perspectives
target_groups: Diversity Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: Diversity Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Researchers from Abroad
characteristics.Research programme: Slavery pasts: (understudied forms of) impacts and perspectives

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The Call 'NWA Slavery pasts: (understudied forms of) impacts and perspectives’ aims to stimulate research into the often less-known consequences of the Dutch history of slavery. The programme seeks to contribute to the debate on how societies address injustices of the past and work towards a fairer future. It also aims to promote the development, sharing, and preservation of knowledge on this subject within the Kingdom of the Netherlands. The research focuses on the economic, social, cultural, and administrative impacts of slavery over the years. Additionally, it examines how societies today deal with this past and its lasting effects. The programme emphasizes an open approach that allows for diverse perspectives. This includes the use of new and innovative research methods and disciplines to gain deeper insights into these complex issues.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The consortium should consist of at least one lead applicant, co-applicant and collaboration partner. Main applicant Researchers may act as main applicants if they are permanently employed (and therefore have a paid employment contract for an indefinite period of time*) or have a tenure track agreement with one of the research organisations listed in the Call for proposals. Co-applicants Two types of co-applicants are possible in this Call for proposals: Co-applicants affiliated to research organisations; Co-applicants affiliated to civil society organisations. Co-applicants linked to civil society organisations can only act as co-applicants under application of the de minimis regulation (see the Call for proposals, section 3.1.1). Cooperation partners Cooperation partners are mandatory within this Call for proposals because active involvement of civil society stakeholders is of great importance in developing knowledge about challenges and possible solutions. Societal stakeholders include both public and private organisations, and where relevant also citizens or a representation thereof. The organisation of a societal stakeholder is not a main or co-applicant or co-financier. Co-funders Co-financing is not mandatory within this Call for proposals. Co-financiers are organisations that participate in the consortium and contribute cash and/or in kind to the project.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: This Call for proposals deals with complex and challenging societal issues. Research into answers to these issues requires collaboration, for example between different research disciplines, practical and fundamental research, and with civil society organisations and companies. NWO encourages and supports the formation of these collaborations by organising consortium formation meetings or workshops. These meetings and workshops can take different forms, depending on the specific objective, and are further explained in this Call for proposals. In this Call for proposals, consortium formation is stimulated by deploying a Sandpit. The aim of a Sandpit is to establish cooperation in new collaborations and joint research proposals. Sandpits focus on collaboration in a single consortium consisting of the selected parties. During a Sandpit, the selected participants work jointly on site to develop a research project, under the guidance of external facilitators and content supporters. For participation in a Sandpit, selection takes place through an Expression of Interest. Only individuals invited by the NWO Executive Board can participate in the Sandpit.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Within this programme, a maximum of €2,750,000 grant can be applied for one application. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 5 years. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. The available budget modules (including the maximum amounts) are listed in the Call for proposals. The rates and an explanation of these budget modules can be found in section 7.1.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting full proposals is 29 January 2026, before 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Substantive assessment criteria for Expressions of Interest Participants (researchers, civil society actors and companies) are subject to the evaluation criteria as specified below: Academic/professional attributes (40%), Personal competences and motivation (60%). Substantive assessment criteria Proposal The application submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%), Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%). More information on the substantive assessment criteria can be found in the Call for proposals. Procedure For this NWA Call for proposals, an assessment committee will be appointed by the NWO Executive Board. The assessment committee will have a broad composition. This means that not only scientific expertise will be represented in the assessment committee, but also expertise from the entire knowledge chain, including societal stakeholders who are highly familiar with the subject and representatives from the target group. The task of the assessment committee is to assess the applications and the relevant documents that are submitted, in conjunction with each other and with regard to both the respective merits of each application and the assessment criteria outlined in this Call for proposals.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: This Call for proposals has three phases: Submitting an Expression of Interest; Participation in the Sandpit workshop; Submitting a proposal. Within this programme, societal parties can also act as co-applicants, via the de minimis regulation. More information on who can apply can be found in the Call for proposals. NWO may request applicants to submit additional documents within 10 working days before the deadline to demonstrate that the set requirements for submission are met.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: In this Call for proposals, consortium formation is stimulated by deploying a Sandpit. The aim of a Sandpit is to establish cooperation in new collaborations and joint research proposals. Sandpits focus on collaboration in a single consortium consisting of the selected parties. For participation in a Sandpit, selection takes place through an Expression of Interest. Only individuals invited by the NWO Executive Board can participate in the Sandpit. Participation in the Sandpit is mandatory to act as a main applicant. More information on this form of Consortiuming can be found in the Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call, we ask you to complete and submit different forms for each phase. Phase 1 - Expression of Interest Form Expression of Interest Phase 3 - Proposal In addition to completing the application form, we also ask you to submit: budget; declarations of commitment of cooperation partners; declaration co-funding from co-funders (mandatory if applicable); letter guaranteeing the continuity of the project supervision (compulsory if applicable); Declaration de-minimissupport (compulsory if applicable); form 'Statement and signature'.

title: NWA Knowledge about unprotected species for sustainable fisheries policy
programme: Knowledge about unprotected species for sustainable fisheries policy
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge about unprotected species for sustainable fisheries policy
characteristics.Themes: Biodiversity Climate Ruimtelijke en sociale transformaties Food Water

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of this Call for proposals is to develop insights into the impact that the energy, food and nature transitions have on unprotected fish species in the North Sea. These insights are developed in cooperations with the fishing industry and form the basis for the establishment of a sustainable integrated North Sea policy, in which the developments in the North Sea and in the fishing industry go hand in hand. The programme should lead to insights that provide an initial framework for: Policy options for the management and protection of unprotected species, nature conservation, and measures to benefit fisheries; Insights that can help the fishing sector determine the direction in which it can develop to ensure sustainable and future-proof fishing; Opportunities arising from the achievement of the objectives of 'The North Sea Agreement' to support sustainable fishing for unprotected species and to mitigate undesirable developments for these target species and for the fishing communities concerned.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are to be submitted by the main applicant on behalf of the consortium. The main applicant has participated in both collaborative workshops (see Sections 2.4.1, 3.3.1 and 4.2.2 of the Call for proposals). There are four categories of participants within a consortium: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Cooperation partner(s); Co-financier(s) (optional). A consortium consist of at least a main applicant, co-applicant and collaboration partner. See section 3 of the Call for Proposals for conditions for consortium partners

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In this Call for proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through Collaborative workshops. The Collaborative workshops will take place on 8 and 29 October 2025. The two collaborative workshops build on each other in terms of content. Participation in both workshops is mandatory for lead applicants. If necessary, it is possible to designate a substitute for this. More information can be found in the Call for proposals. The aim of collaborative workshops is to bring together ideas, encourage cooperation and submission of a limited number of research proposals by broad (innovative) consortia, on a predefined societal issue. It also offers participants insight into the field of forces surrounding their topic. During the workshop, interested parties (researchers and public and/or private societal parties) around a specific theme are brought into contact with each other. For more information see Section 4.2 of the Call for proposals.. Additional information about NWO's efforts in the field of consortium formation can be found on the website: Consortium formation | NWO .

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A maximum grant amount of €4,780,000.‐ can be applied for. The applicant and co‐applicants may include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation among other things. Co-funding is not compulsory within this Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Registration for participation in the collaborative workshops is open until 1 October 2025 . Participation in both collaborative workshops is mandatory for the main applicant or service representative. Participation in both Collaborative workshops is mandatory for the main applicant or its replacement. The Collaborative workshops will take place on 8 and 29 October 2025 . The deadline for submitting the proposal is 29 January 2026, before 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem posed and problem analysis (20%) Expected impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) For this NWA Call for proposals, an assessment committee will be appointed by the NWO Executive Board. The assessment committee will have a broad composition. This means that not only scientific expertise will be represented in the assessment committee, but also expertise from the entire knowledge chain, including societal stakeholders who are highly familiar with the subject and representatives from the target group. The task of the assessment committee is to assess the applications and the relevant documents that are submitted, in conjunction with each other and with regard to both the respective merits of each application and the assessment criteria outlined in this Call for proposals. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: Registering for collaborative workshops; Participation in two collaborative workshops (8 and 29 October 2025; mandatory for main applicants; see section 3.3.1); Submission of the proposal (main applicant; deadline 29 January 2026, 14:00:00 CET); Admissibility check of the proposal (Bureau NWA; within two weeks after submission); Administrative corrections (main applicant within 5 working days after admissibility check); Processing the application (Bureau NWA); Preliminary advice (assessment committee); Interview selection (assessment committee); Interview with consortium; Advice of the assessment committee; Decision-making (NWO Executive Board. Expected decision date 31 March 2026).

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: An application is prepared and submitted after two collaborative workshops, where research ideas were brought together, possible collaborations with other workshop participants were explored and where crucial initial agreements for joint development of an application by a consortium were made. A consortium consist of at least a main applicant, co-applicant and collaboration partner. See section 3 of the Call for Proposals for conditions for consortium partners. The Impact Plan Approach applies to this Call for Proposals. More information about the Impact Plan Approach and how to describe Productive interactions and to draft an Impact Pathway is available from the NWO website.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: Participation in two collaborative workshops is mandatory for lead applicants. If necessary, it is possible to designate a substitute for this. To participate in the collaborative workshop you submit the application form. More information can be found in the Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To prepare your application, please follow the steps below: Download the application form from the online application system ISAAC or from the NWO website; Complete the application form; Save the form as a PDF file and submit it to ISAAC with the attachments below; Complete the information requested online from ISAAC. Attachments: Budget Declarations of commitment of co-applicant(s) and cooperation partner(s) Form 'Statement and signature' Letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision Declaration(s) co-financier(s) (if applicable);

title: Cooperation Germany - von Humboldt Stiftung (VHS)
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the research prizes is to encourage international collaboration between excellent researchers.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Eminent researchers (preferably professors) with a tenured position at one of the knowledge institutions listed below can nominate German candidates for a Von Humboldt Prize to NWO: Dutch universities, KNAW institutes, NWO institutes, NKI, MPI Nijmegen, researchers from the Dubble beamline at the ESRF in Grenoble, NCB Naturalis, and the Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL). German researchers can nominate Dutch candidates to the Von Humboldt Stiftung. The winners must satisfy substantial requirements. They must be at the top of their career and be in a leadership position, preferably as a professor. They must enjoy an outstanding international scientific reputation. And they must still be able to make a genuine contribution to science. A researcher can only receive this Prize once. In view of the Prize’s aim, it should not be combined with other prizes and stays. Scientists who in view of their scientific achievements have already received a prize in the past year will not be eligible for this Prize. On the date the nomination is received, candidates may not have been in the Netherlands for more than six months. If a person has been in the Netherlands for between six and twelve months then this person may no longer be staying in the Netherlands at the moment of nomination. If a person has been in the Netherlands for at least twelve months then he or she must have been outside of the Netherlands for at least one year before he/she can be nominated.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The Prize can be requested for research for a period of at least four months and at most one year. The research period can be split up if so desired. The following costs are reimbursed: subsistence costs for the research period, international travel costs (one return ticket), associated research costs (including congress visit and travel within the Netherlands).

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: You may submit at any time. There are no deadlines. The processing time is about six months. Nominations can be submitted until the reserved budget for the programmes Visitor’s Travel Grant, Cooperation Germany - Von Humboldt Stiftung, Cooperation Japan, Cooperation Taiwan and Cooperation China travel grants and seminars has been exhausted.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications will be assessed on the basis of: Scientific quality, originality and relevance of the research programme Quality of the researcher Knowledge utilisation Procedure The Von Humboldt Stiftung selects the Dutch candidates and awards the research prizes to the Dutch researchers. NWO selects the German candidates and awards the research prizes to the German researchers. In the case of an application for a visit of at most six months no external advice (peer review) is requested, in principle. For an application for a visit of between six and twelve months at least one external advice is requested. The NWO-wide grants committee assesses the applications and advises the NWO Governing Board about the award. The NWO Governing Board takes the award decision.

fill_in_forms.text: Download the application form and save the completed form as a PDF file.

title: Dutch Research Agenda - Research along Routes by Consortia 2025 (NWA-ORC 2025)
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Researchers from Abroad Collaboration with Industry

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The aim of the NWA ORC 2025 call for proposals is to encourage research that is set up and carried out by interdisciplinary consortia that include representatives from the full breadth of the knowledge chain as well as relevant societal (public and/or private) partners, including citizens. The research will address so called wicked problems, focused on themes that are proposed by NWA-route networks and based on one or more of the 140 cluster questions.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals should be submitted by a consortium in which the various types of research in the knowledge chain (fundamental, applied and practice-oriented) are represented. There are four categories of participant within a consortium: 1. Main applicant; 2. Co-applicant(s); 3. Cooperating partner(s); 4. Co-funder(s) (optional). The main applicant submits the application on behalf of the consortium. More information about the four categories of participants as well as specific requirements regarding who can apply, can be found in paragraph 3.1 of the call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In this Call for Proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of collaborative workshops. The main applicant must have participated in both collaborative workshops on the relevant theme, or a substitute must have been present.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: In this round, proposals can be submitted that are based on a theme as described in Annex 7.4 of the Call for proposals. Project duration should be 4-8 years, and maximum available budget is €7.250.000. The budget is built up using the NWO-wide standardised building blocks, the so-called modules. Seven modules are available in this call: Personnel; Material; Investments; Knowledge utilisation; Project management. Extensive information about the modules can be found in section 3.2 and annex 7.1 of the call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The NWA-ORC 2025 round has three phases: Registration for collaborative workshops (required for main applicants) Participation in collaborative workshops Between 14 April and 14 May 2025, NWO will organise collaborative workshops with the aim of bringing together interested parties (researchers and public and/or private social parties) around a specific theme, combining research ideas (joining forces) and forming broad innovative consortia. You can register for the workshops via the NWO website . Submitting a proposal The deadline for submission of proposals via ISAAC is 3 February 2026, at 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria Applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis, contribution to a solution (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Each assessment criterion consists of several subcriteria. More information the criteria can be found in paragraph 4.3 of the call for proposals. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: registration for collaborative workshops; participation in the collaborative workshops (mandatory for the main applicant or a deputy (see paragraph 3.3.2)); submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; rebuttal; pre advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making. Detailed information about the procedure can be found in the Call for Proposals, Section 4.2.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The application is submitted by a knowledge-chain-wide, inter- and transdisciplinary consortium (see section 3.1 of the Call for Proposals). Research is based on themes proposed by and with support from the broad network of the NWA route networks and involves innovative, demand-driven research with the aim of achieving scientific and social breakthroughs.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: In this Call for Proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of collaborative workshops. The main applicant must have participated in both collaborative workshops on the relevant theme, or a substitute must have been present. The aim of the workshops is bringing together interested parties (researchers and public and/or private social parties) around a specific theme, combining research ideas (joining forces) and forming broad innovative consortia. You can register for the workshops via the NWO website .

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To participate in the collaborative workshops, you must have read the Call for Proposals and registered via the NWO website . After the collaborative workshops, you will find the forms for your application on this page and in ISAAC.

title: Impact Explorer 2025
target_groups: Collaboration with social partners Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Collaboration with social partners Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The purpose of this Call for Proposals, identically to the earlier Impact Explorer round, is to explore opportunities for societal impact arising from curiosity-driven research. These are opportunities and ideas that were not anticipated in advance when the project plan was made, but have emerged during the lifetime of a project. The grant is intended for activities to validate the potential societal impact of discoveries from more curiosity-driven research from the Talent Line and Open Competitions of both NWO and ZonMw and to explore a route towards it.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Researchers with a running project in one of NWO or ZonMw's open competitions or talent line programmes can submit. An applicant may have one substantive submission within the Impact Explorer Call. Besides the main applicant Impact Explorer applications should be submitted with at least one Impact Partner. For specific conditions see chapter 3.1 in the call.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Researchers can apply for a minimum of 20,000 and a maximum of 30,000 euro. It is not possible to use it to extend or fund appointments of temporary academic staff.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Proposals can be submitted to NWO on a continuous basis from 4 February 2025 until 3 February 2026 before 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria An application will be assessed individually for: Appropriateness to the objective of the programme Quality and feasibility of the project plan Quality of the team See the call for details. Procedure Each application is assessed individually whether it is of sufficient quality to be granted. This is done by means of a jury without referees.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: The main changes of this extension from the earlier Impact Explorer round 2023 are: Addition of collection times for proposals in continuous submission, as described in chapter 1.3 and 4.2.3 in the call; An applicant may have one substantive submission assessed within the Impact Explorer Call.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Fill in the application form. There is relatively limited space to elaborate in details. The jury is not composed of experts from your own field, so keep this in mind when writing the application. In addition, make sure you look for an Impact Partner as early in the process as possible.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: You need to complete both the application form and obtain a letter of support from the Impact Partner. In the situation that your contract ends during the project, a statement appointment and project supervision is also required.

title: Venture Challenge Spring 2026
target_groups: Knowledge Utilisation

characteristics.For specific groups: Knowledge Utilisation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: Many scientific findings never reach the market. Researchers often do not have the experience and knowledge needed to set up a commercial organization and to come to a market-ready product or service. It is usually too early for private investors and experienced entrepreneurs to step in during this start-up phase, for example because the business case is not yet sufficiently clear to them. The Venture Challenge helps by developing a well-thought out business plan together with the researchers. This provides insight into what is needed to bring the invention to the market.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The Venture Challenge is intended for teams of at least three and a maximum of four people who are planning to bring a product or service in the field of life sciences & health to the market as a new start-up. The product or service is based on a new technological discovery or a new application of existing technology, grounded in scientific research. More specifically, it concerns a new therapy or medicine, diagnostics, medical device or a technology for improving existing - or accelerating the development of new - therapies or medicines. The team consists of the person who made the scientific discovery and the other founders of the start-up. If required, it can be supplemented with relevant advisors. For example, this can be a business developer from an involved Knowledge Transfer Office.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: An edition consists of two three-day bootcamps and regular coaching sessions. Participants work under the supervision of experienced coaches, to set up or improve their business plan. The supervision can possibly be expanded with experts and entrepreneurs with a background in life sciences & health. The entire process takes eight to ten weeks. At the end, the participants pitch their plans in front of a jury, including experienced entrepreneurs and investors. The selected candidates pay a personal contribution and receive funding for participating in the Venture Challenge, to be spent in the upcoming edition. The personal contribution for participation per team is: 2,000 euros when 3 team members participate; 2,500 euros when 4 team members participate. The following dates are important: 6-8 May 2026 – first bootcamp; 10-12 June 2026 – second bootcamp; 26 June 2026 – pitches for the jury.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Registration for the spring edition opens 1 January 2026 and closes on 3 February 2026 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria the commercial perspective of the new product or process or service; the innovative character of the new product or process or service; the perspective of the competitive position of the (future) Venture Challenge startup; the financial potential of the (future) Venture Challenge startup; the quality of the Venture Challenge team, considering the extent to which members complement each other, the members’ capacities and the allocation of roles within the team; the added value of participation in the Venture Challenge program for the (future) Venture Challenge startup. Procedure If NWO takes your application into consideration, then an interview can be part of the application process. In that case, you will be given the opportunity to explain your proposal to the advisory committee which NWO appoints to assess the applications. Based on the committee's advice, NWO will decide which VC teams will be awarded a grant. The timetable for the procedure is as follows: 3 February 2026 14:00:00 CET Deadline full proposals 23-24 March 2026 Interviews 17 April 2026 Decision awards and rejections 24 April Announcement awards

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: In the application form you are asked about the goal of the startup, the proposition and to what extent it has been validated, a market analysis, competitive position, and the team.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Download the application form and complete the form. The maximum size of the application is 10 pages (excluding the ‘Innovatieadviesdienstverklaring’ mentioned below). If the application is submitted by a start-up, then a so-called ‘Innovatieadviesdienstverklaring’ from the start-up should be appended to the application. If the application is submitted by a team without a start-up, then each team member should complete and add an innovation advisory service statement. Please note: there are two different forms; which one you need to fill in depends on whether there is already a company established when you register for the Venture Challenge. You can read more in the ‘Toelichting’ (see the appendix to the form). Please note: The ‘innovatieadviesdienstveklaring’-forms are changed compared the last Venture Challenge round.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The SSH Open Competition L funding instrument (hereinafter: SSH OC L) for thematically free research focuses on research projects with a primary social and/or humanities research question. Proposals with an interdisciplinary or cross-domain character can also be submitted to this instrument, provided they contain a clear social and/or humanities component.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The SSH OC L target group consists of experienced researchers who obtained their doctorate at least fifteen years ago with a great fascination for conducting challenging and groundbreaking research. The focus is on curiosity-driven research. Given the aim of the programme, applicants must be able to independently formulate and supervise research. When submitting your proposal, please ensure that it is submitted to the correct panel. Read more about the six panels within the Social Sciences and Humanities domain.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: This instrument offers the opportunity to apply for various types of personnel costs, material budget, investments in equipment or databases, and knowledge utilization. In addition, funding can also be requested for international cooperation through Money Follows Corporation . See the paragraphs 3.2.1.3 and 7.1.1 in the Call for Proposals. The domain board expressly invites researchers to make the best possible use of the various possibilities offered by this funding instrument. It is up to the researchers themselves to determine which type of funding best suits their research ambitions. For an application in this call for proposals, a maximum of 800,000 euros can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed project is six years. Request only what is essential to carry out the project. A further explanation of the budget modules can be found in the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The pre-proposal deadline is 9 September 2025, at 14:00:00 CEST. The full proposal deadline is 3 February 2026 , at 14:00:00 CET. The deadline for submitting a request for review of (foreign) organisations (see section 3.1 and section 7.1.1 in the Call for Proposals) is Tuesday 26 August 2025 , by 14:00:00 CEST. When submitting your application in ISAAC, you must also enter information online. Therefore, start submitting your application at least one day before the deadline of this call for proposals. Applications submitted after the deadline will not be considered.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The pre-proposals submitted within this Call for proposals are substantively assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Research idea (60%) Appropriateness of the research team (40%) The full proposals submitted within this Call for proposals are substantively assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Research idea, design and impact (80%) Team, embedding and budget (20%) Please note that the abovementioned criteria also contain subcriteria. The full criteria including the sub criteria for both the pre-proposals as well as the full proposals can be found in the Call for proposals. Procedure The assessment takes place in six disciplinary panels in both the pre-proposal and the full proposal phase of the round. In the pre-proposal phase, the assessment committee composes a written assessment in the form of a preliminary advice, after which the applicants are given the opportunity to respond to the assessments. The final advice on the pre-proposals is submitted to the Domain Board Social Sciences and Humanities, which then takes a decision on the proposals submitted in the pre-proposals phase. Based on this decision, the applicant may or may not submit a full application. In the full proposal phase, the applications are first assessed by external referees, after which the committee also provides the applications with a preliminary advice. The committee then discusses the applications, referee reports and preliminary advice in an assessment meeting. It then advises the domain board on the basis of a ranking of applications drawn up during the meeting. Finally, the domain board reviews the procedure followed and the advice of the assessment committee, determines the final qualifications and decides whether to grant or reject the applications. A specific timeline for the round can be found in the Call for proposals. Composition committee of the pre-proposals Please note that it is possible that not all committee members assessed all proposals, because the NWO Code for Dealing with Personal Interests is applicable to all people involved in formal decisions. This concerns both personal interests as well as (appearance of) bias. Because of this, it can be decided that a committee member will not assess a proposal if there are personal interests or (appearance of) bias. Please note that the composition of the assessment committee can change between the first and second phase of the assessment procedure. Because of this, committee members who assessed proposals in the first phase, might not participate in the committee in the second phase, and vice versa. echnical chairs Professor Jansz – Technical chair Professor Lietaert Peerbolte – Technical chair Professor Plantenga – Technical chair CTW Professor A. write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: NWO will assess your application against the conditions listed below. Your application will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. After submitting your application, NWO requests you to be available to implement any possible administrative corrections so that you can (still) meet the conditions for submission. These conditions are: the main applicant and possible co-applicant meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application form is, after a possible request to make additions or changes, complete and filled out according to the instructions; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the application budget is drawn up in accordance with the conditions for this Call for proposals (budget form only to be submitted in the full proposal phase); the proposed project has a duration of at least one year and at most six years; all of the required annexes are, after a possible request to make additions or changes, complete and filled out according to the instructions.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The steps involved in writing your application are: download the application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the funding instrument concerned); complete the application form; save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes; f ill in the requested information online in ISAAC. Compulsory annexe(s): Pre-proposal phase: Statement appointment and project supervision (if applicable). Full proposal phase: budget form; statement co-funding (if applicable). In case NWO has made a template available, the annexes should be drawn up according to the NWO template. Annexes must be uploaded in ISAAC separately from the application. All of the annexes, except for the budget, must be submitted as PDF files (without encryption). The budget must be submitted in ISAAC as an Excel file. Any annexes other than those above-mentioned are not permitted.

title: Future proof large rivers in the Netherlands
programme: Future-proof large rivers in the Netherlands
target_groups: International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: International Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Future-proof large rivers in the Netherlands

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The goal of this programme is to provide perspectives for potential policy choices concerning the large Dutch rivers. Research financed by this program should investigate the impact which various policy choices might have and how river functions may play into these choices. As such this programme aims to contribute to possible answers to the question of how the Netherlands can best prepare itself for future challenges concerning river systems. These challenges concern the effects of climate change, such as rising sea levels and larger changes in river water levels and quality. In addition, aspects concerning the whole system of the river basin, the socio-economic effects of policy choices and the role of waterborne transport play a significant role. The Directorate-General for Water and Soil Affairs of the Dutch Ministry of Infrastructure and Water Management is the initiator and is supported in this by the Directorate-General Rijkswaterstaat (DGRWS).

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: The main applicant submits the application on behalf of the consortium. There are four categories of participant within a consortium: Main applicant; Co-applicant(s); Cooperating partner(s); Co-funder(s) (optional). This Call for proposals has three phases: Registering for collaborative workshops (mandatory for main applicants) Participating in collaborative workshops (mandatory for main applicants or substitution) Submitting a proposal. More information about the four categories of participants as well as specific requirements regarding who can apply, can be found in paragraph 3.1 of the call for proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: For this programme NWO organizes two subsequent collaborative workshops. Attendance is mandatory for the main applicant or a replacement (see section 3.3.2 of the call for proposals for more information). Registration for the collaborative workshops is submitted via a form on the NWO website. NWO explicitly invites both researchers and societal parties to participate in the workshops and to jointly develop proposals. The aim of collaborative workshops is to bring together ideas, stimulate collaboration and submit a limited number of research proposals by broad (innovative) consortia. The workshops also give participants an overview of the forces at play around a topic. The expertise of the participants and their contribution to the impact on the call topic form the substantive basis of the workshops. The workshops consist of two consecutive meetings in which NWO actively supports the exploration of new collaborations, but the parties ultimately decide for themselves whether and with whom they wish to collaborate. After the workshops, participants will have insight into the possibilities and opportunities for clustering expertise and collaboration, and will further develop their proposals. Further information about the collaborative workshops can be found here: Collaborative Workshops ‘Future-proof large rivers in the Netherlands’ (NWA) | NWO

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: Within this Call for proposals one project can be funded. A total of € 2.880.000 is available. The main applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments, knowledge utilisation and project management. The available budget modules (including maximum amounts) are listed in the Call for proposals. Apply only for what is essential to carry out the project. The rates and an explanation of these budget modules are given in Annex 7.1 of the Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline proposals: 10 February 2026, 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%), Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%), Quality of the consortium (30%), Quality of the research (30%). Each assessment criterion consists of several subcriteria. More information the criteria can be found in paragraph 4.3 of the call for proposals. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: Registering for and participating in collaborative workshops; submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; preliminary advice assessment committee; interview selection; interview; meeting of the assessment committee; decision-making. Detailed information about the procedure can be found in the Call for Proposals, Section 4.2.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Representatives of the DGWB and DGRWS of the Ministry of Infrastructure and Water Management and Rijkswaterstaat will also be present at the workshops.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: An application is prepared (and submitted) after the collaborative workshops, where research ideas were brought together, possible collaborations with other workshop participants were explored and where crucial initial agreements for joint development of the application were made.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: The (eventual) lead applicant must have participated in both collaborative workshop.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: To prepare your application, please follow the steps below: download the application form from the online application system ISAAC or from th NWO website (on the website of the relevant financing instrument); complete the application form; save the form as a PDF file and submit it to ISAAC with any mandatory attachments; complete the information requested online in ISAAC. Mandatory attachments: budget; declarations of commitment of cooperation partners; declaration co-funding from co-funders (mandatory if applicable); letter guaranteeing the continuity of the project supervision (compulsory if applicable, see Section 3.1); form 'Statement and signature'.

title: KIC Medicines from medicines - knowledge development for circular redesign of the pharmaceutical supply chain
programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Mission 2024-2027 Medicines from medicines
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration
characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge and Innovation Covenant Mission 2024-2027 Medicines from medicines
characteristics.Themes: Circulaire economie Health Climate Health Care Innovation

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call for proposals focuses on inter- and transdisciplinary knowledge development that contributes to redesigning the pharmaceutical supply chain. The emphasis is on developing business cases and use cases that involve the entire chain. The intended societal impact is a change among all key stakeholders to achieve a circular pharmaceutical supply chain that meets the same high standards for quality, efficacy, and safety as the current linear chain.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period*) or a tenure track agreement at a research organisation. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants two or more co-funders, and potentially one or more cooperation partners, are involved as well. All consortiumpartners should play an active role in the formulation of the research questions and in the design and implementation of the project. For more information: see section 3.1 in the Call for Proposals.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: This Call for proposals stimulates the formation of consortiums by means of collaborative workshops. Attending the collaborative workshops is mandatory for main applicants. The objective of the collaborative workshops is to bring together potential applicants and their ideas, to encourage collaboration within broad, innovative consortia, and to limit the number of proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of €750.000 and aa maximum of €2.300.000 of NWO funding can be applied for. With this, NWO finances maximally 80% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding (see Section 3.5.6). NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 6 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The dates of the collaborative workshops are 23 September 2025 and 7 October 2025 . The deadline for submission of full applications is 10 February , 2026, by 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) Procedure For this Call for proposals, attending the collaborative workshops by main applicant (or substitute) is mandatory. Full applications will be provided with peer review by referees. Applicants will be given the opportunity to submit a rebuttal to this. The applications will then be assessed by the assessment committee. The assessment committee will present its advice to the NWO Executive Board for decision-making. In September 2026, the NWO Executive Board will adopt the final assessment of the applications.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: All the documents you need for your application can be found here. The submission of an application is done via the application and reporting system ISAAC. If you do not yet have a personalISAAC account, please create one at least one working day earlier. Below you will find more information about writing a proposal and which forms you should use. To do so, you will need to use the forms that you can download below.

mandatory_consortium_building_activities.title: Mandatory consortium-building activities
mandatory_consortium_building_activities.text: For this Call for proposals, attending the collaborative workshops by the main applicant (or substitute) is mandatory.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: In addition to filling in the application form when submitting the complete application, we also ask you to include a signed statement from the co-financiers. If applicable, a confirmation of contribution to investments (see section 7.1 in the call for proposals) and a guarantee for continuity in the project management (see section 3.1 in the call for proposals) must also be sent. At the time of submission, the enclosed letters of support must have pledged the full required co-financing in accordance with the conditions described in sections 3.1.2 and 3.5.6 in the call for proposals. The appendix "confirmation of contribution to investments" is mandatory if the application requests funding for investments of € 150,000 or more (see also section 7.1 in the call for proposals). Other types of annexes shall are not allowed at the stage of submission of complete applications. Attachments must be uploaded separately from the application in ISAAC. All attachments, with the exception of the budget form, must be uploaded as a pdf file. The budget form can be uploaded as an Excel file in ISAAC.
