title: KIC Biochemical diversity
programme: Biochemical diversity Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027
target_groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry

characteristics.For specific groups: Interdisciplinary Collaboration Collaboration with social partners Applied Sciences Research Collaboration with Industry
characteristics.Research programme: Biochemical diversity Knowledge and Innovation Covenant KIC 2024-2027 Mission 2024-2027

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The increasing pressure on natural resources due to the rapidly growing global population necessitates fundamental changes to production processes to ensure the continued viability of life on our planet. This Call for proposals focuses on increasing the sustainability of production processes by developing and refining key enabling technologies. This will involve expanding and improving sustainable production processes by exploring the vast range of biochemical substances and biochemical pathways in nature and making them accessible for production processes. The many biochemical substances and processes in nature offer opportunities to achieve this objective. However, the existing technological capabilities are insufficient to effectively explore, analyse and utilise that diversity. This Call for proposals therefore focuses not just on knowledge development, but first and foremost on developing and refining technologies that facilitate the exploration, analysis and application of biochemical diversity.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: This Call for proposals is intended to promote collaboration between different scientific fields, social partners and business. As such, proposals are invited from researchers from both academic institutes and universities of applied sciences. Close collaboration with private and public organisations is essential. Proposals are submitted by the main applicant and one or more co-applicants. Researchers may submit an application if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period*) or a tenure track agreement. A proposal is written by a consortium in which besides the applicants other participants are involved as well. Details on who can apply can be found in section 3.1 of the call text.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: In this Call for proposals, consortium formation is encouraged through the use of matchmaking. Participation in the matchmaking meeting is encouraged, but not mandatory. The objective of matchmaking is to bring together potential applicants, researchers, collaborators and stakeholders from various fields and sectors to exchange ideas about, or the need for, research related to the topic and impact described in the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a minimum of €600,000 and a maximum of €1,360,000 of NWO funding can be applied for. With this, NWO finances maximally 80% of the total project budget; the rest of the funding must be contributed by the compulsory co-funding. NWO never funds less than 50% of the total project budget. The applicant and co-applicants can include costs for personnel, equipment, investments, knowledge utilization and project management.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: When you submit your application in ISAAC, you will also need to enter some details online. Therefore please start submitting your application at least one day before the deadline of this Call for proposals. Applications that are submitted after the deadline will not be taken into consideration. The deadline for submitting full proposals is 12 February 2026, before 14:00:00 CET . The deadline for submitting a request for review of foreign organisations (see section 7.1.1) is 26 January 2026, by 14:00:00 CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Problem definition and analysis Envisaged impact and route to impact Quality of the consortium Quality of the research Criteria 1 and 2 will each count for 20% of the final verdict, criteria 3 and 4 will each count for 30%. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: matchmaking; around September (optional); submission of the proposal; Thursday 12 February 2026, before 14:00 CET; admissibility of the proposal; peer review by referees; February-April 2026; rebuttal; May 2026; pre-advice assessment committee; interview selection (if neccesary); interview; June/July 2026 assessment committee meeting; June/July 2026 decision-making by the Board of directors of NWO; September 2026.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: The application should indicate how the proposed research relates to the Call for proposals and on which societal problem/challenge and which key technology(ies) the application focusses. It should also indicate the goals, hypotheses and scientific feasibility of the project. In addition, the application should show how the consortium is composed and will work together to increase the applicability of the research results. For this, the Impact Plan approach should be followed and described in the proposal.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this call you need to fill out the application form and the following annexes: form ‘Statements and signature’ (required); declarations co-funding from co-funders (required); budget form (required); letter guaranteeing the continuity of project supervision (if applicable); Research Organisation Declaration (if applicable).

title: PhDs in the Humanities 2026
target_groups: Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: Starting Researchers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: PhDs in the Humanities offers talented researchers a paid PhD position.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Applications can only be submitted for candidates who have been selected by the deans of the humanities faculties from the following universities: Erasmus University Rotterdam Leiden University Maastricht University Open University Protestant Theological University Radboud University Nijmegen Theological University Apeldoorn Theological University Utrecht Tilburg University University of Amsterdam University of Aruba University of Curaçao University of Groningen University of Humanistic Studies University of St. Martin Utrecht University VU University Amsterdam For further information about the pre-selection within the faculties, please consult the contact persons named in the call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: A grant can be applied for to cover a standard PhD research project (4 years, 1 FTE or 5 years, 0.8 FTE). The grant covers the personnel costs of the PhD. Material costs for research are remunerated to a limited extent.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: This funding round takes place once a year. The deadline for submitting an application is 17 February 2026 14:00 hrs . Applications received after the deadline are automatically disqualified.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The assessment criteria for the proposals are: Quality of the PhD candidate (30%) Quality of the research proposal (70%). Procedure The assessment of the proposals is done by one committee. Candidates are interviewed and assessed by committee members from their own discipline and neighbouring disciplines within the humanities.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Only proposals of which the main discipline is part of the humanities are eligible for this grant. Apart from the requirement that the proposal must align with a main discipline within the humanities, the subject of the research project is entirely open. The evaluation of the proposal will be based on the criteria mentioned in section 4.3 of the Call for Proposals. The proposal must be written in English and should consist of the following components: (Academic) profile of the candidate ; Description of proposed research (total max. 3000 words) consisting of a) description of the research idea and b) a section on the anticipated scientific and/or societal impact (max. 750 of the 3000 words); Work plan (max. 1 A4); Description of institutional embedding and supervision (max. ½ A4); Budget section; Data management section.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: For this Call, we ask you to fill in the application form as well as a budget form according to the provided template. If applicable, you are also required to submit a signed letter of confirmation from the master-thesis supervisor containing the expected graduation date of the candidate and/or a statement of appointment and project supervision.

title: Knowledge security: scientific research in a geopolitical context – Phase 2
programme: Knowledge security: scientific research in a geopolitical context

characteristics.Research programme: Knowledge security: scientific research in a geopolitical context

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: The NWA programme 'Knowledge security: scientific research in a geopolitical context' promotes research into developments around knowledge security and inherent dilemmas in the context of an evolving geopolitical landscape. The programme aims to contribute to the development of a conceptual framework for the study of knowledge security and yield insights into the international context and threats, the organisation and vulnerability of the knowledge sector, and the balance between opportunities and risks in knowledge security policies. These insights, together with tangible action perspectives, should help relevant stakeholders within the knowledge sector and government to develop balanced policies and deal with dilemmas. In this way, this programme aims to increase the resilience of the Dutch knowledge sector and bolster national security, in such a way that core academic values are being protected and promoted and society can continue to benefit from international cooperation.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Please note: this is a closed application. Only one of the main applicants who submitted a preproposal that was selected by NWO may submit a proposal. The application has to be submitted by a main applicant, on behalf of a consortium. A consortium should include at least a main applicant, co-applicant and cooperation partner. This Call for proposals consists of two phases. In phase 1, consortia submit a preproposal that addresses one of the four themes identified in the Call. In phase 2, the applicants of the selected preproposals jointly develop a proposal by participating in synergy workshops, after which the proposal is submitted by a main applicant who has been appointed in phase 2. All consortium members should play an active role in formulating the research questions and in preparing and executing the project. The requirements per consortium member are explained in further detail in paragraph 3.1 of the Call for proposals.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: In this Call for proposals, a total of €6,600,000 is available, and a maximum of one proposal will be awarded funding. In phase 2, the applicants of the four preproposals selected in phase 1 of the procedure jointly submit one application, composed of an overarching work package and four thematic work packages. At least €2,000,000 is available for the overarching work package. A maximum of €1,150,000 is available for each thematic work package. Depending on the number of preproposals selected, any remaining budget will be added to the overarching work package (phase 2). The budget for the preproposals (phase 1) is released  once the research proposal of the application (phase 2) has been approved. The proposed project has a duration of a minimum of 48 months/4 years and a maximum of 72 months/6 years.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting preproposals is Tuesday 24 June 2025 , before 14:00:00 CEST. The deadline for submitting a preproposal has passed. Please note that only main applicants of preproposals that have been selected in phase 1, are permitted to submit a proposal in phase 2. The deadline for submitting the proposal is Thursday 26 February 2026 , before 14:00:00 CET.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The application submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantively assessed according to the following criteria: Phase 2 – Proposal Problem definition and analysis  (20%) Envisaged impact and route to impact (20%) Quality of the consortium (30%) Quality of the research (30%) For a detailed description of the specific aspects that are distinguished within these substantive assessment criteria, please refer to paragraph 4.3.1 of the Call for proposals. Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps, divided into two phases: Phase 1 – Preproposals information meeting (online); submission of the preproposal; admissibility of the preproposal; assessment and selection of the preproposal. Phase 2 – Drafting and submitting a proposal participation in synergy workshops submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; pre-advice assessment committee; interview; assessment committee meeting; decision-making by NWO Executive Board. For a detailed description of the procedural steps, please refer to paragraph 4.2 of the Call for proposals.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: Please note: only main applicants of preproposals that were selected by NWO may submit a proposal in phase 2 of the procedure. In this Call for proposals, four complementary themes have been identified within which knowledge gaps exist with regards to knowledge security: International context and threats Organisation and vulnerability of knowledge practice Balance (between opportunities and risks) and policy Action perspectives: increasing resilience In phase 1 of this Call for proposals, it is mandatory to submit a preproposal. A preproposal is a concise application addressing one of the four themes described above. The preproposal describes which knowledge questions the applicant and co-applicants want to address and how this contributes to the overall objectives and impact described in the Call for proposals. In addition, the preproposal describes which opportunities and leads the applicants anticipate for cooperation with the consortia working on the other themes. In phase 2, one application is developed and submitted by a consortium consisting of parties that have submitted the selected preproposals. In this phase, the selected preproposals serve as starting point and are integrated into a single proposal. The proposal consists of an overarching work package in which an interdisciplinary conceptual framework is developed, which forms the basis to coherently address the four themes and the objectives and impact described in the Call for proposals. NWO will assess your application against all the conditions set out in the Call for proposals, including the conditions listed below. Your application will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. After submitting your application, NWO requests you to be available to implement any possible administrative corrections so that you can (still) meet the conditions for submission. These conditions are: the main applicant and co-applicant(s) meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the application budget is drawn according to the terms of this Call for proposals (using the format made available which includes the most recent rates); the proposed project has a duration of at least 48 months/4 years and maximum 72 months/6 years; all of the required annexes, after a possible request for additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and in accordance with the terms of this Call of proposals; if applicable: the co-financing has been correctly and fully committed through co-financing statements, after any supplement or modification request.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: The steps involved in writing your application are: download the application form from the NWO web application ISAAC or from the NWO web page (on the grant page of the funding instrument concerned); complete the application form; save the application form in ISAAC as a PDF file and upload it with any compulsory annexes; fill in the requested information online in ISAAC. Compulsory annexes in phase 2: budget; declarations of commitment of cooperation partners; declaration co-funding from co-funders (if applicable); letter guaranteeing the continuity of the project supervision (if applicable, see paragraph 3.1); form 'Statement and signature'

title: Quantum Delta NL Quantum Technology 2025
target_groups: Applied Sciences Research Starting Researchers

characteristics.For specific groups: Applied Sciences Research Starting Researchers

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This call aims to stimulate, solidify and strengthen fundamental and applied research of high scientific quality. This forms the basis for new innovations in quantum technology that are connected to the Catalyst (“CAT”-) programmes as described in the Quantum Delta NL Growth Fund proposal. These are: CAT-1: Quantum Computing and Simulation, CAT-2: National Quantum Network and CAT-3: Quantum Sensing Applications. In other words, fundamental and applied research from this call is feeding into technology development within one or more of these CATs.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Full, associate and assistant professors and other researchers with a comparable position may submit an application if they have a position at one of the Dutch knowledge organisations as mentioned in the call for proposals. In addition, applications for a personal postdoc appointment can be submitted by candidates who have obtained their PhD (before the deadline of this call), irrespective of whether they hold tenured positions and of whether they come from the Netherlands or abroad.

consortium_building.title: Consortium building
consortium_building.text: Applications can be submitted by: a single main applicant without co-applicants, or a consortium consisting of; a single main applicant and maximum one co-applicant, or a single main applicant with at least one co-funder, optionally complemented with one co-applicant. The main applicant and co-applicant may be employed by the same research organisation, as long as their expertise is complementary and creates added value.

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: The available budget for this Call for proposals is €10,588,522. Per project, maximum € 750,000 grant can be applied for. The maximum duration of the proposed project is 6 years. The grant must be used for exactly one single (in case of a single applicant) or exactly two (in case of a main applicant with a co-applicant) junior research position(s) (such as PhD student, postdoc, or equivalent position at a university of applied sciences or TO2 institute) in combination with other budget modules available. The applicant and any co-applicant may include costs for personnel, equipment, investments and knowledge utilisation. Co-funding is not mandatory in this Call for proposals. However, it is possible to add co-funders in the proposal. Co-funding can be contributed by parties that do not apply for funding under this Call for proposals.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: Deadline application: 3 March 2026, 14:00:00 CET

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Connection to the relevant CAT programmes as described in section 2.1 of the Call for proposals (20%) Scientific quality (60%) Scientific and/or societal impact (20%) Procedure Admissible proposals are assessed by independent external experts. The referees' reports are sent to the applicant for a written rebuttal. On the basis of the assessment criteria, the assessment committee ranks the proposals with accompanying referees' reports. The assessment committee agrees on a prioritisation of the proposal and advises the NWO Domain Science Board about the quality of the proposals. Based on this advice and the available funding, the Domain Science Board decides whether to grant or reject the proposals.

more_information.title: More information
more_information.text: Quantum Delta NL provides applicants with the opportunity to contact the Catalyst programs in advance to obtain further insight into these programs. This can be arranged via the following email addresses: For CAT-1: Quantum Computing and Simulation: cat1@quantumdelta.nl For CAT-2: National Quantum Network: cat2@quantumdelta.nl For CAT-3: Quantum Sensing Applications: cat3@quantumdelta.nl

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Download the application form, the budget table and other required templates; Prepare the application in English; Use the application form and the budget table for the NGF Quantum Delta NL Quantum Technology 2025 round; Save the budget table as Excel and the other forms as pdf files and upload them in ISAAC. For every proposal submitted, NWO assumes that the applicant has informed the host institution and that the university or institute has accepted the funding conditions of this programme.

title: Hestia - Impulse for Refugees in Science
programme: Hestia - Impulse for Refugees in Science
target_groups: Diversity International Collaboration Researchers from Abroad

characteristics.For specific groups: Diversity International Collaboration Researchers from Abroad
characteristics.Research programme: Hestia - Impulse for Refugees in Science

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.text: This programme aims to make research and the academic labour market in the Kingdom of the Netherlands accessible to researchers living in the Kingdom of the Netherlands with a demonstrable refugee status. In doing so, NWO enables these academics to start or continue their career that was interrupted in their home country and simultaneously enrich the research landscape with the experience and expertise they bring with them. By making short-term funding available, NWO provides researchers with refugee status in the Kingdom of the Netherlands the opportunity to join already funded NWO or ZonMw research projects in all fields of research and build a network. At the same time these researchers will be able to actively share knowledge and expertise, and to familiarise themselves with the research landscape. This programme therefore functions as point of entry into academia in the Kingdom of the Netherlands and expands the opportunities for these academics in the long term. The involvement of knowledge institutes is vital for long-term success. NWO therefore encourages these organisations to look at options for extending the appointments of competitively successful candidates, or to seek other relevant advancement opportunities.

who_can_apply.title: Who can apply
who_can_apply.text: Project leaders of current NWO or ZonMw-funded research projects can submit an application, provided their project will overlap with at least the first ten months of the Hestia appointment and if they have a tenured position (and therefore a paid position for an indefinite period) or a tenure track agreement at one of the following organisations: universities located in the Kingdom of the Netherlands; university medical centres; institutes affiliated to the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences (KNAW) or NWO; Netherlands Cancer Institute; the Max Planck Institute for Psycholinguistics in Nijmegen; Naturalis Biodiversity Center; Advanced Research Centre for NanoLithography (ARCNL); Princess Máxima Center. Please see the Call for proposals (chapter 3.1) for a more specific explanation of the requirements for applicants. Conditions for refugee researchers: Only people who fit the situations described in chapter 3.1.1 of the Call for proposals can be eligible as candidates. On the submission date of 3 March 2026, the potential candidate should possess the residence permit and/or if applicable, the associated documentation for the commencement date of the right to residence. Researchers with refugee status who are already working as full, associate or assistant professor or a comparable position in academia in the Kingdom of the Netherlands can be applicants, but are not eligible as candidates in this Call for proposals. Please see the Call for proposals (chapter 3.1.1) for a more specific explanation of the requirements for candidates. In the period leading up to your application, please do not send copies of documents containing personal data (such as a residence permit) to NWO in order to answer questions about your eligibility for Hestia. Please note: It is necessary to send proof of your refugee status (e.g. a copy of the front and back of your residence permit) when you officially submit your application. Please send this proof via a password-protected SURFdrive, sending the link and password to hestia@nwo.nl .

what_to_apply_for.title: What to apply for
what_to_apply_for.text: For an application in this Call for proposals, a maximum amount of €200,000.00 can be applied for. The minimum duration of the proposed project is 12 months and the maximum duration is 18 months. In the case of a part-time appointment of the candidate, the duration can be spread over a maximum of 24 months, and the appointment must be at least 0.6 fte, and can be maximum 0.8 fte.

when_to_apply.title: When to apply
when_to_apply.text: The deadline for submitting full proposals is March 3, 2026, at 14:00:00 hours CET .

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.text: Criteria The applications submitted within this Call for proposals will be substantially assessed on the basis of the following criteria: Quality and feasibility of the proposed project and relevance to the funded NWO or ZonMw project Quality of the candidate Motivation of the candidate and relevance to the candidate Guidance plan and future prospects of the candidate Procedure The application procedure consists of the following steps: submission of the proposal; admissibility of the proposal; provisional assessment; rebuttal; initial advice from the assessment committee; assessment committee meeting; decision-making. For a more detailed explanation of the procedure, see chapter 4 of the Call for proposals.

write_proposal.title: Write proposal
write_proposal.text: NWO will assess your application against all the conditions set out in this Call for proposals, including the conditions listed below. Your application will only be admitted to the assessment procedure if it meets these conditions. After submitting your application, NWO requests you to be available to implement any possible administrative corrections so that you can (still) meet the conditions for submission. These conditions are: the main applicant and candidate meet the conditions stated in Section 3.1; in case the candidate is a junior researcher they obtained a master’s degree or equivalent; in case the candidate is a senior researcher they obtained a doctorate or equivalent; the proposed project has a duration of at least 12 and at most 18 months full time, or 24 months part-time (0.6 - 0.8 fte); (at least) the first ten months of the project fall within the funded NWO or ZonMw project it relates to; the application complies with the DORA guidelines as described in Section 4.1; the application is submitted via the main applicant’s ISAAC account; the application is received before the deadline; the application is written in English; the application form is, after a possible request to make additions or changes, correct, complete and filled out according to the instructions; the application budget is drawn according to the terms of this Call for proposals; all required documents and annexes, after a possible request for additions or changes, have been completed and submitted completely and according to the instructions and in accordance with the terms of this Call for proposals.

fill_in_forms.title: Fill in forms
fill_in_forms.text: Download the application form and save the completed form as a PDF file.
